# SRS

> This is the canonical System Requirements Specification backlog. `UPGRADE_BACKLOG.md` remains as a compatibility alias.

Purpose: track future upgrades without mixing them into active implementation threads.

Status legend:
- `queued` not started and ready for triage
- `in_progress` actively in progress
- `blocked` waiting on dependency/decision
- `done` completed

## Metakernel v0.1 Intake (ChatGPT Draft, 2026-03-08)

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V7-META-001 | existing-coverage-validated | Core primitive contract registry (`node/cell/task/capability/object/stream/journal/budget/policy/model/supervisor/attestation`) | The architecture needs one canonical primitive surface before additional profile-specific work can be trusted. | Delivered authoritative registry contract (`planes/contracts/metakernel_primitives_v1.json`) and enforcement runtime (`core/layer0/ops/src/metakernel.rs`) with unknown-primitive usage scanning over `client/runtime/config/**/*.json`; verified via `npm run -s ops:metakernel:registry`. | 10 | 0/1/2 |
| V7-META-002 | existing-coverage-validated | Invariant gate for the first eight metakernel invariants | Safety guarantees must be executable and fail-closed, not descriptive text only. | Delivered `ops:metakernel:invariants` lane (`protheus-ops metakernel invariants --strict=1`) evaluating all 8 metakernel invariants with deterministic receipts; verified via `npm run -s ops:metakernel:invariants`. | 10 | 0/1/2 |
| V7-META-003 | existing-coverage-validated | `CellBundle` manifest schema + validator lane | Portable deploy units need a strict manifest contract for capability/budget/provenance correctness. | Delivered strict CellBundle schema + example (`planes/contracts/cellbundle.schema.json`, `planes/contracts/examples/cellbundle.minimal.json`) and strict manifest validator (`protheus-ops metakernel manifest --strict=1`); verified via `npm run -s ops:metakernel:manifest`. | 10 | 0/1/2 |
| V7-META-004 | existing-coverage-validated | WIT world registry + wasm component ABI contract | Pluggable substrate execution requires a stable component boundary independent of TS wrappers. | Delivered world registry contract (`planes/contracts/wit/world_registry_v1.json`) and compatibility validation lane (`protheus-ops metakernel worlds --strict=1`) that validates manifest-declared worlds/capabilities against registry; verified via `npm run -s lane:v7-meta-004:run`. | 10 | 0/1/2 |
| V7-META-005 | existing-coverage-validated | Effect-typed capability taxonomy + risk class policy defaults (R0-R4) | Capability safety depends on typed effects and deterministic risk-tier handling rules. | Delivered capability taxonomy policy (`planes/contracts/capability_effect_taxonomy_v1.json`) and strict gating lane (`protheus-ops metakernel capability-taxonomy --strict=1`) for effect/risk derivation with high-risk policy-gate checks + receipts; verified via `npm run -s lane:v7-meta-005:run`. | 10 | 0/1/2 |
| V7-META-006 | existing-coverage-validated | Budget admission controller across CPU/RAM/storage/network/tokens/power/privacy/cognitive-load | Budgets must bind before execution to prevent post-hoc overrun behavior. | Delivered admission policy (`planes/contracts/budget_admission_policy_v1.json`) and fail-closed admission lane (`protheus-ops metakernel budget-admission --strict=1`) that emits bounded `reason_codes` on denial; verified via `npm run -s lane:v7-meta-006:run`. | 10 | 0/1/2 |
| V7-META-007 | existing-coverage-validated | Epistemic object schema v1 (value/schema/provenance/confidence/policy/retention/export/rollback) | AI-native state needs typed uncertainty and lineage metadata, not byte-only storage semantics. | Delivered `planes/contracts/epistemic_object_v1.schema.json` + example object and strict metakernel validator lane (`protheus-ops metakernel epistemic-object --strict=1`); verified via `npm run -s lane:v7-meta-007:run`. | 10 | 0/1/2 |
| V7-META-008 | existing-coverage-validated | Effect journal commit-before-actuate enforcement | External side effects must be journaled deterministically to preserve replay and audit guarantees. | Delivered effect-journal policy + example (`planes/contracts/effect_journal_policy_v1.json`, `planes/contracts/examples/effect_journal.minimal.json`) and strict enforcement lane (`protheus-ops metakernel effect-journal --strict=1`); verified via `npm run -s lane:v7-meta-008:run`. | 10 | 0/1/2 |
| V7-META-009 | existing-coverage-validated | Substrate descriptor registry + degrade matrix | Portable execution across MCU/edge/cloud/quantum/neural nodes requires machine-readable substrate contracts. | Delivered substrate descriptor registry (`planes/contracts/substrate_descriptor_registry_v1.json`) with required descriptor fields + degrade matrix checks and strict validator lane; verified via `npm run -s lane:v7-meta-009:run`. | 10 | 0/1/2 |
| V7-META-010 | existing-coverage-validated | Radix policy guard (binary baseline, ternary optional class-scoped lowering) | Radix experimentation must not weaken core security invariants. | Delivered radix guard policy (`planes/contracts/radix_policy_guard_v1.json`) and strict guard lane (`protheus-ops metakernel radix-guard --strict=1`) enforcing binary-required critical paths + ternary overlap rejection; verified via `npm run -s lane:v7-meta-010:run`. | 10 | 0/1/2 |
| V7-META-011 | existing-coverage-validated | Quantum broker domain contract (`compile/estimate/submit/session/batch/measure`) | Quantum execution is hybrid and queue-bound; it needs explicit broker semantics and fallback guarantees. | Delivered `planes/contracts/quantum_broker_domain_v1.json` and strict lane `protheus-ops metakernel quantum-broker --strict=1` validating required operations + classical fallback receipt policy; verified via `npm run -s lane:v7-meta-011:run`. | 10 | 0/1/2 |
| V7-META-012 | existing-coverage-validated | Neural consent kernel scaffold with separated authorities (`observe/infer/feedback/stimulate`) | Neural interfaces require strict authority boundaries and highest-risk controls for stimulation paths. | Delivered `planes/contracts/neural_consent_kernel_v1.json` and strict lane `protheus-ops metakernel neural-consent --strict=1` enforcing separated authorities and dual-control/consent/audit stimulate policy; verified via `npm run -s lane:v7-meta-012:run`. | 10 | 0/1/2 |

| V7-META-015 | existing-coverage-validated | Execution profile matrix harness (`mcu`, `edge`, `cloud`) | Profile claims need reproducible execution evidence, not architecture-only assertions. | Delivered `planes/contracts/execution_profile_matrix_v1.json` and strict lane `protheus-ops metakernel execution-profiles --strict=1` verifying required profile IDs and harness declarations; verified via `npm run -s lane:v7-meta-015:run`. | 10 | 0/1/2 |

## LLMN Mode + Smart Memory Regression Shield (2026-03-09)

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-LLMN-001 | existing-coverage-validated | Canonical LLMN mode registry + alias normalization (`REQ-35`) | Operator-facing modes drifted across wrappers and aliases (`standard/deep thinking/hyper creative`) causing runtime mismatch risk. | Canonical alias normalization is active in runtime mode resolver surfaces; verified by `tests/client-memory-tools/llmn_mode_conformance.test.js` and `tests/client-memory-tools/strategy_resolver.test.js`. | 8 | 1/2/client |
| V6-LLMN-002 | existing-coverage-validated | Router/strategy parity for all LLMN modes (`REQ-35`) | Mode behavior can silently diverge between router and strategy surfaces without one parity contract. | Router + strategy parity contract is enforced and normalized mode receipts are stable; verified by `tests/client-memory-tools/model_router_routing_features.test.js` and `tests/client-memory-tools/model_router_variant_policy.test.js`. | 8 | 1/2/client |
| V6-LLMN-003 | existing-coverage-validated | Mode-critical CLI entrypoint path restoration (`REQ-35`) | Recent refactors left stale wrapper/path references (`client/systems/*`, missing TS peer files) that break mode operations. | Mode-critical wrappers resolve through active runtime paths and stale alias roots are guarded; verified by `tests/client-memory-tools/legacy_path_alias_adapters.test.js`. | 8 | 1/2/client |
| V6-LLMN-004 | existing-coverage-validated | LLMN conformance smoke pack + CI gate (`REQ-35`) | Mode regressions recur because they are not continuously validated as one contract. | Deterministic end-to-end conformance pack is active and passing (`llmn_mode_conformance`, router/strategy/path tests) with receipt-backed execution through core-authoritative lanes. | 8 | 1/2/client |
| V6-MEMORY-013 | done | Index-first and node-only retrieval enforcement (`REQ-36`) | Token burn regresses whenever retrieval bypasses index traversal and reads broad files. | Enforced in Rust core via `core/layer1/memory_runtime/src/recall_policy.rs` + `core/layer0/memory_runtime/src/main.rs` (`enforce_index_first`, `enforce_node_only`) with behavior coverage in `core/layer0/memory_runtime/src/main.rs` tests and CLI integration `core/layer0/memory_runtime/tests/memory_policy_cli_integration.rs`; client fail-closed preflight enforced in `client/runtime/systems/memory/policy_validator.ts` + `session_isolation.ts` with regression checks in `tests/client-memory-tools/{memory_security_gate_integration,memory_uid_enforcement}.test.js`. | 8 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-MEMORY-014 | done | Lean dynamic hydration policy hardening (`REQ-36`) | Eager hydration of memory protocol/index files reintroduces cold-start token tax. | Bootstrap lazy-hydration fail-closed guard enforced in Rust core (`enforce_hydration_guard` in `recall_policy.rs`, wired in `query_index_payload`), validated by `memory_policy_tests::bootstrap_guard_blocks_eager_hydration` and CLI evidence `core/local/artifacts/batch3_memory_cli_evidence_20260313T081955Z.jsonl`; client regression coverage in `tests/client-memory-tools/v6_memory_013_019_client_regression.test.js`. | 8 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-MEMORY-015 | done | Memory-query burn SLO gate (`<200`) (`REQ-36`) | Low-burn behavior previously achieved regressed without executable SLO enforcement. | Burn SLO (`<200` default) is enforced in `query_index_payload` via Layer-1 token telemetry (`evaluate_burn_slo`) with fail-closed reject mode; validated by `memory_policy_tests::query_fail_closed_when_burn_slo_exceeded` and CLI evidence artifact, plus client-side guard regression in `tests/client-memory-tools/memory_recall_context_budget.test.js`. | 8 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-MEMORY-016 | done | Recall budget default contract + fail-closed cap mode (`REQ-36`) | Query defaults can drift toward high-burn expansion if budget/expand settings are not contract-locked. | Fail-closed budget contract (`reject`/`trim`) enforced by `enforce_recall_budget` and wired into `query_index_payload`; validated by unit tests in `recall_policy.rs`, `memory_policy_tests::{query_rejects_when_budget_exceeds_in_reject_mode,query_trims_budget_when_trim_mode_enabled}`, CLI integration, and client-side regression in `tests/client-memory-tools/memory_recall_context_budget.test.js`. | 10 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-MEMORY-017 | done | Matrix/sequencer/auto-recall ranking invariants (`REQ-36`) | Smart memory quality degrades if ranking math or sequencing order drifts silently. | Ranking invariants (`enforce_descending_ranking`) enforced for matrix and auto-recall in `core/layer0/memory_runtime/src/wave1.rs`; validated by `wave1::tests::auto_recall_produces_sorted_matches_with_invariant_receipt` and client regression coverage in `tests/client-memory-tools/v6_memory_013_019_client_regression.test.js`. | 8 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-MEMORY-018 | done | Index freshness + stale-read gate (`REQ-36`) | Stale indexes force expensive fallback reads and degrade retrieval precision. | Freshness fail-closed gate (`enforce_index_freshness`) is enforced in query/get/auto-recall paths with stale override flags; validated by `memory_policy_tests::query_fail_closed_when_index_is_stale`, `wave1::tests::auto_recall_blocks_stale_matrix`, CLI evidence artifact, and client guard regression in `tests/client-memory-tools/memory_index_freshness_gate.test.js`. | 8 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-MEMORY-019 | done | LensMap memory annotation schema (tags/nodes/jots in docs comments) (`REQ-36`) | Future code-intent retrieval can regress into full-file scans without structured annotation in LensMap/doc surfaces. | Extended Layer-1 schema with document-level validator (`validate_lensmap_document`) and duplicate-node safeguards in `core/layer1/memory_runtime/src/lensmap_annotations.rs`; validated by `cargo test --manifest-path core/layer1/memory_runtime/Cargo.toml` and client-side schema regression in `tests/client-memory-tools/v6_memory_013_019_client_regression.test.js`. | 8 | 1 |

| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-RESEARCH-001.1 | existing-coverage-validated | Multi-level stealth fetcher engine | Current research/assimilate flows are fragile on anti-bot protected targets and do not adapt fetch strategy by protection level. | Add fast HTTP + stealth fetcher + dynamic browser fetch modes with automatic selector based on protection signals; protected target scrape runs show fallback switching and bounded detection failures. | 8 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V6-RESEARCH-001.2 | existing-coverage-validated | Adaptive element tracking and selector recovery | Site layout churn breaks brittle selectors and causes repeated manual parser maintenance. | Implement adaptive element recovery (CSS/XPath/text/regex + similarity fallback) so parser contracts survive representative site redesign fixtures without manual selector edits. | 8 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V6-RESEARCH-001.3 | existing-coverage-validated | Smart scaling + resilience lane for crawling | Large crawl jobs need deterministic queueing/retry/checkpoint behavior to avoid partial-loss failures. | Ship concurrent crawler controls with per-domain throttle, checkpoint pause/resume, and exponential backoff retries; prove 100+ concurrent request profile with graceful recovery receipts. | 8 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V6-RESEARCH-001.4 | existing-coverage-validated | MCP extraction integration for pre-structured research payloads | LLM burn remains high when raw pages are forwarded without pre-extraction contracts. | Provide MCP-callable research endpoint that returns structured pre-extracted artifacts (content + metadata + provenance receipts) prior to model invocation. | 8 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V6-RESEARCH-001.5 | existing-coverage-validated | Safety-plane routed stealth research execution + deterministic receipts | Research tooling must not bypass conduit/policy enforcement even when running stealth/browser fetch paths. | Enforce research execution through Layer-0 conduit gate with policy/budget checks and deterministic per-action receipts; no direct bypass paths from client wrappers. | 8 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V6-RESEARCH-001.6 | existing-coverage-validated | Developer-facing stealth research DX (`protheus research --stealth`) | Adoption friction remains high without one-command CLI/API defaults and thin-wrapper conventions. | Add thin client wrappers and CLI flags for stealth mode (`protheus research --stealth`, `protheus assimilate --stealth`) with zero-config defaults and operator-visible diagnostics. | 8 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |

## Scrapy Core Crawling Architecture Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1TV9jmVwCtNvClWsQ5fbDFZD_gQ1Qoj5ffHl3aGjYBJg`, 2026-03-11)

Source references:
- https://docs.google.com/document/d/1TV9jmVwCtNvClWsQ5fbDFZD_gQ1Qoj5ffHl3aGjYBJg/edit?usp=sharing
- https://github.com/scrapy/scrapy

Notes:
- Primitive-first normalization: this intake extends the existing research/crawl lane (`V6-RESEARCH-001.*`) with production crawler architecture contracts; it does not create a separate crawl subsystem.
- Net-new focus from Scrapy patterns:
  - class/rule-based crawl graph traversal (`CrawlSpider`/`Rule`/`LinkExtractor`)
  - layered downloader/spider middleware contracts
  - item pipeline + feed exporter contracts
  - crawl lifecycle signal/extension hooks + live inspection controls
- Default placement:
  - Layer `0`: conduit-routed crawl request/response/item/export actions + deterministic receipts
  - Layer `1`: URL graph/item schema policy, exporter policy (destinations/retention), extension safety controls
  - Layer `2`: spider/rule engine, middleware orchestration, pipeline/export runtime, signal bus
  - `client`: thin `crawl run/status/export` surfaces only
  - `app`: optional `/apps/crawl_studio/` UX only
  - `adapter`: provider/storage-specific backends (S3/FTP/etc.)

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-RESEARCH-002.1 | existing-coverage-validated | Rule-Based CrawlSpider + LinkExtractor Contract | Current crawling lanes lack a canonical rule-driven traversal engine for large structured crawls, causing repeated bespoke crawl logic. | Add governed spider class contract supporting allow/deny link rules, callback routing, depth/domain constraints, and deterministic per-link traversal receipts; `protheus crawl spider <name> --rules <config>` executes rule-based crawls over the same conduit-governed research pipeline. | 8 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V6-RESEARCH-002.2 | existing-coverage-validated | Downloader + Spider Middleware Stack Contract | Production crawler hardening is fragmented without one pluggable middleware lifecycle around request/response and spider processing. | Implement ordered middleware stacks (downloader + spider phases) with deterministic lifecycle hooks (`before_request`, `after_response`, `on_error`, retry) and policy-scoped install/enable controls; all middleware decisions emit deterministic receipts. | 8 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V6-RESEARCH-002.3 | existing-coverage-validated | Item Pipeline + Feed Exporter Contract | Scraped data handling lacks a standardized chain for transform/validate/enrich/export, reducing reuse and data-quality consistency. | Add chainable item pipeline stages plus governed exporters (e.g., JSON/CSV/XML/JSONL and policy-approved storage backends) with deterministic stage/export receipts and schema validation outcomes. | 8 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V6-RESEARCH-002.4 | existing-coverage-validated | Crawl Signals + Extension Hook Bus Contract | Crawl observability/extensibility remains ad hoc without a first-class event bus for lifecycle hooks. | Add crawl signal contracts (`spider_opened`, `request_scheduled`, `response_received`, `item_scraped`, `spider_closed`, etc.) and extension hook registration under policy-gated capabilities with deterministic signal/handler receipts. | 8 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V6-RESEARCH-002.5 | existing-coverage-validated | Authenticated Live Crawl Control/Inspection Console Contract | Operators need runtime introspection and intervention during long crawls, but uncontrolled debug surfaces can bypass policy boundaries. | Provide authenticated local-first crawl inspection/control console (stats, queues, middleware state, pause/resume/stop) routed through conduit with deterministic control receipts and explicit fail-closed authorization gates. | 10 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V6-RESEARCH-002.6 | existing-coverage-validated | Curated Scrapy-Compatible Template Pack Governance Lane | High-quality crawler templates/middleware presets need human review and signed governance to avoid unsafe/legal-risk defaults. | Maintain signed, curated scrapy-compatible template pack installable via `protheus assimilate scrape://scrapy-core` with human-reviewed update cadence, provenance metadata, and policy conformance checks before enablement. | 8 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |

## Firecrawl Agent Crawler Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1GNCzMkKKyHNpxNwkWncd6YkM3kQl7272xiBH3kYYTis`, 2026-03-11)

Source references:
- https://docs.google.com/document/d/1GNCzMkKKyHNpxNwkWncd6YkM3kQl7272xiBH3kYYTis/edit?usp=sharing
- https://github.com/firecrawl/firecrawl

Notes:
- Primitive-first normalization: this extends existing research crawl lanes (`V6-RESEARCH-001.*`, `V6-RESEARCH-002.*`) with agent-mode, prompt/schema extraction, and longitudinal monitoring contracts.
- Net-new focus from Firecrawl patterns:
  - seedless goal-driven crawl planning (`intent -> discover -> crawl -> extract`)
  - first-class site-map + crawl graph artifact contract for depth-limited domain traversal
  - prompt-or-schema extraction contract for clean markdown + JSON outputs
  - built-in change tracking/monitoring with historical deltas
- Default placement:
  - Layer `0`: conduit-routed crawl/extract/monitor actions + deterministic receipts
  - Layer `1`: extraction schema/prompt policy, delta retention policy, provenance/confidence contracts
  - Layer `2`: goal planner, site discovery mapper, crawl executor, monitor scheduler/diff engine
  - `client`: thin `crawl/map/monitor` wrappers only
  - `app`: optional `/apps/crawl_agent/` and persona presets only
  - `adapter`: provider-specific fetch/auth/storage backends

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-RESEARCH-004.1 | existing-coverage-validated | Goal-Driven Seedless Crawl Agent Contract | Current crawl flows still assume operator-provided seeds/config rather than intent-first autonomous discovery for broad web research. | Add `protheus crawl "<goal>"` contract that plans targets from intent, discovers/crawls relevant site surfaces without seed URLs, and emits deterministic plan/discovery/page receipts with policy-bounded scope controls. | 8 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V6-RESEARCH-004.2 | existing-coverage-validated | Depth-Controlled Site Mapping + Graph Artifact Contract | Domain-wide crawl coverage is hard to reason about without explicit URL graph artifacts tied to traversal depth and exclusion policy. | Add `protheus map <domain> --depth N` contract that outputs deterministic site graph artifacts and per-page extraction bundles, with bounded parallelism/rate controls and coverage receipts. | 8 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V6-RESEARCH-004.3 | existing-coverage-validated | Prompt-or-Schema Structured Extraction Contract | "LLM-ready" output quality is inconsistent when extraction depends on ad hoc parsing flows per crawl target. | Provide unified extraction contract supporting either explicit schema input or natural-language extraction prompt, returning normalized markdown + structured JSON with deterministic validation/provenance receipts. | 8 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V6-RESEARCH-004.4 | existing-coverage-validated | Crawl Change Tracking + Delta Notification Contract | Long-running research workflows lose value when site changes are not tracked and surfaced as first-class deltas over time. | Add `protheus monitor <url | 8 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V6-RESEARCH-004.5 | existing-coverage-validated | Firecrawl-Compatible Template/Preset Governance Lane | Reusable crawler goals/schemas/presets need curation and policy review to avoid unsafe or low-quality defaults. | Maintain signed, curated crawl-agent templates/presets installable through governed assimilation lanes (for example `crawl://...`) with human review cadence, provenance metadata, and policy conformance checks before enablement. | 8 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V6-RESEARCH-004.6 | existing-coverage-validated | Conduit-Only Agent Crawler Enforcement + Thin Client Boundary | Agentic crawl/extract/monitor surfaces can drift into client-owned authority paths without explicit boundary enforcement. | Enforce conduit-only routing for goal planning, crawling, extraction, and monitoring actions with fail-closed policy checks and deterministic receipts; keep client/app layers non-authoritative and test for bypass rejection. | 10 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |

## Python-Scraping Pattern Source Coverage Intake (Doc `10zk6RreqZjknjQFYv8rPx75OD1W_exBricQ2lWCJ2CU`, 2026-03-11)

Source references:
- https://docs.google.com/document/d/10zk6RreqZjknjQFYv8rPx75OD1W_exBricQ2lWCJ2CU/edit?usp=sharing
- https://github.com/REMitchell/python-scraping

Notes:
- Primitive-first normalization: this intake extends existing research/crawler lanes and does not introduce a separate scraping engine.
- Already covered:
  - Scrapy-style crawler contracts: `V6-RESEARCH-002.1..002.6`
  - Agentic crawl and extraction flows: `V6-RESEARCH-004.1..004.6`
  - Stealth/adaptive crawling baseline: `V6-RESEARCH-001.1..001.6`
- Net-new focus from book-pattern workflows:
  - governed Selenium/JS-render specialization profile
  - authenticated form/login + session-jar lifecycle contract for scraping workflows
  - explicit anti-bot trap/rotation response matrix with policy-safe behavior
  - curated "book patterns" template pack governance lane
- Default placement:
  - Layer `0`: conduit-routed scrape/browser/session actions + deterministic receipts
  - Layer `1`: session/privacy/rate-limit policy and pattern-template governance
  - Layer `2`: JS-render scrape executor, proxy/session rotation orchestration, parallel worker scheduler
  - `client`: thin `scrape` command surfaces only
  - `app`: optional `/apps/scrape_workbench/` UX only
  - `adapter`: browser/proxy/provider-specific adapters

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-RESEARCH-005.1 | existing-coverage-validated | Governed JS-Rendered Scrape Profile Contract | JavaScript-heavy targets require browser-rendered extraction, but this path needs explicit policy-governed scraping semantics rather than ad hoc browser automation. | Add a governed `scrape js` profile (headless/non-headless policy modes, waits, form actions, screenshots) that routes through conduit with deterministic action receipts and bounded runtime/resource controls. | 8 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V6-RESEARCH-005.2 | existing-coverage-validated | Authenticated Form/Login Session Lifecycle Contract | Complex scraping targets require durable login/session handling; without a contract, credentials/session state handling can become unsafe and brittle. | Add session lifecycle primitives for login forms/cookie jars/token refresh with per-worker isolation controls, deterministic session-state receipts, and fail-closed auth/expiry behavior. | 10 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V6-RESEARCH-005.3 | existing-coverage-validated | Proxy Rotation + Anti-Bot Trap Response Matrix Contract | Existing stealth lanes need explicit, policy-safe response logic for proxy churn, trap detection, and fallback decisions under scrape pressure. | Add policy-scoped rotation/trap-response matrix (backoff, proxy quarantine, retry classing, manual-handoff triggers) with deterministic per-attempt receipts; prohibited bypass/evasion patterns are denied by policy. | 8 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V6-RESEARCH-005.4 | existing-coverage-validated | Parallel Session-Aware Scrape Worker Contract | High-throughput scraping needs parallel execution with predictable session/account isolation and deterministic retry coordination. | Provide parallel scrape worker contract with bounded concurrency, shared orchestration state, isolated session contexts, and deterministic worker/queue receipts for run, retry, and completion events. | 8 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V6-RESEARCH-005.5 | existing-coverage-validated | Curated "Book Patterns" Scrape Template Governance Lane | Canonical scraping patterns from the source should be reusable, but unmanaged templates increase risk and drift. | Maintain signed template pack installable via governed URI (for example `scrape://book-patterns`) with compatibility metadata, legal/ethical review cadence, and deterministic install/activate receipts. | 8 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V6-RESEARCH-005.6 | existing-coverage-validated | Conduit-Only Scrape Runtime Boundary Enforcement | Scrape/browser/session logic can drift into ungoverned client code paths without explicit boundary contracts. | Enforce conduit-only routing for scrape crawl/js/session/proxy actions with fail-closed policy checks and bypass-rejection tests; keep client/app surfaces non-authoritative. | 10 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |

## Google News URL Decoder Source Coverage Intake (Doc `14LDfaNqkqACV7ZYZTeclBGIoHFHrY33CNmY14jTx6XQ`, 2026-03-11)

Source references:
- https://docs.google.com/document/d/14LDfaNqkqACV7ZYZTeclBGIoHFHrY33CNmY14jTx6XQ/edit?usp=sharing
- https://github.com/SSujitX/google-news-url-decoder

Notes:
- Primitive-first normalization: this intake extends existing research ingestion primitives; it does not create a standalone news subsystem.
- Already covered:
  - Stealth/proxy crawl baseline and policy routing: `V6-RESEARCH-001.*`, `V6-RESEARCH-005.*`
  - Structured extraction and provenance contracts: `V6-RESEARCH-004.*`, `V6-PARSE-001.*`
- Net-new focus here:
  - deterministic Google News obfuscated URL decode primitive (news.google.com/read/articles/rss forms)
  - decode-path fallback contract (`articles` primary -> `rss/articles` fallback)
  - batch decode with per-item failure isolation + summary receipts
  - curated signed decoder template lane for rapid enablement without client authority sprawl
- Default placement:
  - Layer `0`: conduit-routed decode actions + deterministic receipts
  - Layer `1`: proxy/rate-limit policy, decode error taxonomy, source provenance labeling
  - Layer `2`: decoder runtime, fallback resolver, batch orchestrator
  - `client`: thin `research decode-news-url(s)` wrappers and observability rendering only
  - `app`: optional `/apps/news_intel/` workflow composition only
  - `adapter`: provider/network transport adapters (proxy stacks, fetch providers)

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-RESEARCH-006.1 | existing-coverage-validated | Native Google News Obfuscated URL Decode Primitive | Research pipelines still ingest unresolved Google News wrapper URLs, degrading citation quality and downstream extraction fidelity. | Add `protheus research decode-news-url <url>` primitive that returns structured `{status, decoded_url, message}` outputs with deterministic receipts and provenance linkage to the input wrapper URL. | 8 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V6-RESEARCH-006.2 | existing-coverage-validated | Proxy-Aware Decode Rate-Limit Governance Contract | Large decode runs are brittle without first-class proxy and interval controls that are policy-governed and receipt-visible. | Support policy-approved proxy configs (HTTP/HTTPS/SOCKS) plus interval/backoff controls for decoder calls, with deterministic per-attempt proxy/rate-limit receipts and fail-closed policy denial for disallowed proxy modes. | 10 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V6-RESEARCH-006.3 | existing-coverage-validated | Dual-Path Decode Fallback Contract | Google News wrapper formats are not uniformly resolvable through a single endpoint path, causing avoidable decode failures. | Implement deterministic resolver order (`/articles` primary then `/rss/articles` fallback) with explicit error taxonomy and fallback-reason receipts; regression fixtures prove improved success without silent format drift. | 8 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V6-RESEARCH-006.4 | existing-coverage-validated | Batch Decode + Per-Item Failure Isolation Contract | Assimilation pipelines require high-throughput decode runs that continue on individual failures instead of aborting whole jobs. | Add `protheus research decode-news-urls <file\ | 8 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V6-RESEARCH-006.5 | existing-coverage-validated | Curated Google News Decoder Template Governance Lane | Decoder behavior can drift as upstream URL formats evolve; unmanaged templates increase breakage and policy risk. | Maintain signed template package installable via governed URI (for example `research://google-news-decoder`) with quarterly human format review, compatibility metadata, and deterministic install/activate receipts. | 8 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V6-RESEARCH-006.6 | existing-coverage-validated | Conduit-Only Decoder Boundary + Thin Client Enforcement | URL decoding can become an ungoverned sidecar if implemented directly in client/app logic. | Enforce conduit-only routing for all decode/fallback/batch actions with bypass-rejection tests; keep client/app surfaces non-authoritative and limited to UX wrappers. | 10 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |

## doc2dict Structured Parser Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1ssSBNU2J52VcoFj6LiwNorpF4PxTvXf5kJ-H0ls5rt0`, 2026-03-11)

Source references:
- https://docs.google.com/document/d/1ssSBNU2J52VcoFj6LiwNorpF4PxTvXf5kJ-H0ls5rt0/edit?usp=sharing
- https://github.com/john-friedman/doc2dict

Notes:
- Primitive-first normalization: this extends ingestion/research primitives with a governed document-structure parsing lane; it does not create a separate memory/store subsystem.
- Overlap handled explicitly:
  - Crawl/retrieval ingestion lanes: `V6-RESEARCH-001.*`, `V6-RESEARCH-002.*`, `V6-RESEARCH-004.*`
  - Existing structured extraction surfaces in those lanes are augmented here with deep document mapping-rule contracts and table/postprocessing specialization.
- Default placement:
  - Layer `0`: conduit-routed parse/transform/export actions + deterministic receipts
  - Layer `1`: mapping-rule registry, parser template governance, transformation policy/provenance contracts
  - Layer `2`: parser runtime pipeline, table cleanup/postprocessing, flatten/unnest transforms
  - `client`: thin parse CLI and visualization wrappers only
  - `app`: optional `/apps/document_parser/` UI/workbench only
  - `adapter`: document-format backends (HTML/PDF/DOCX parsers)

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-PARSE-001.1 | existing-coverage-validated | Modular Mapping-Rule Parser Contract | Domain documents (10-K/reports/complex HTML) need deterministic structure extraction controlled by reusable rule maps rather than brittle one-off parsers. | Add versioned mapping-rule contract (levels, regex selectors, hooks, pre/post sections) with policy-scoped load/apply path; `protheus parse doc <file> --mapping <id | 7 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V6-PARSE-001.2 | existing-coverage-validated | Instruction-Stage Parse Pipeline + Visualization Contract | Parsing failures are hard to debug when intermediate parse instructions are not inspectable before final dict emission. | Add inspectable staged pipeline (`source -> instructions -> structured dict`) with thin visualization surfaces (`protheus parse visualize`) and deterministic stage receipts for each transformation step. | 7 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V6-PARSE-001.3 | existing-coverage-validated | Advanced Table Postprocessing Pipeline Contract | Real-world filings contain malformed tables/footnotes that degrade extraction quality without specialized cleanup/normalization stages. | Add configurable table postprocessing stages (fake-table detection, cleanup, merge/simplify, row/column pruning, footnote handling) with before/after evidence artifacts and deterministic stage receipts. | 7 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V6-PARSE-001.4 | existing-coverage-validated | Flatten/Unnest Transformation Contract | Downstream LLM/research workflows require normalized flat outputs without losing lineage/metadata from nested parse artifacts. | Add governed `flatten`/`unnest` transforms with configurable depth/format profiles (markdown/text/flat rows), preserving metadata/provenance and emitting deterministic transformation receipts. | 7 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V6-PARSE-001.5 | existing-coverage-validated | Curated Parser Template + Mapping Library Governance Lane | Parser quality/safety drifts without curated template packs for common document domains and periodic review. | Maintain signed parser template/mapping library installable via governed URIs (for example `parse://...`) with compatibility metadata, benchmark artifacts, and human review cadence before enablement. | 7 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V6-PARSE-001.6 | existing-coverage-validated | Conduit-Only Parse Boundary Enforcement + Thin Client Surface | Document parsing can bypass policy/audit controls if format parsers or transform tools run directly from client/app code paths. | Enforce conduit-only routing for parse/apply/visualize/postprocess/flatten/export actions with fail-closed policy checks and bypass-rejection tests; keep client/app layers non-authoritative. | 10 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |

| V6-SKILLS-001.3 | existing-coverage-validated | Composable, versioned skill chaining + test validation | Cross-skill workflows are brittle without composition/version/test primitives. | Add composition primitives enabling chained skills (`research -> summarize -> format`), version contract metadata, and deterministic skill test-case runner with pass/fail receipts. | 7 | 1/2/client/app |
| V6-SKILLS-001.4 | existing-coverage-validated | Conduit-only skill execution + deterministic audit receipts | Skill execution must remain safety-plane governed and non-bypassable. | Route all skill run/install/share actions through Layer-0 conduit gates with deterministic receipts and explicit fail-closed policy checks for capability/budget violations. | 10 | 0/1/2/client/app |
| V6-SKILLS-001.5 | existing-coverage-validated | Developer/user skill DX + observability surfacing | Adoption depends on low-friction CLI/SDK and visibility into skill execution behavior. | Ship thin wrappers for `protheus skill create | 7 | 1/2/client/app |
| V6-SKILLS-001.6 | existing-coverage-validated | Curated skill gallery ingest + one-click pattern loader | Curated external skill patterns (ReAct/ToT/Reflexion/Plan-and-Execute/Toolformer) are not yet first-class import targets, so discovery and reuse remain manual. | Add governed ingest/index lane for curated skill repositories and local caches, with one-command import/list/load behavior that materializes patterns as versioned skill objects, validates schema/signature policy, and emits deterministic import/activation receipts. | 7 | 1/2/client/app |
| V6-SKILLS-001.7 | existing-coverage-validated | First-party minimal ReAct reference profile + TAO state contract | Users need one canonical, minimal Thought-Action-Observation baseline profile for learning/debugging and deterministic trace comparison across skills. | Ship a first-party `react-minimal` skill profile that runs bounded TAO loops over existing routing/tool primitives, persists stepwise state (`thought`, `action`, `observation`, `final`) as governed objects, and links each step to deterministic trace receipts for replay/debug without introducing new core authority lanes. | 7 | 1/2/client/app |
| V6-SKILLS-001.8 | existing-coverage-validated | First-party Tree-of-Thoughts reference profile + branch search contract | Teams need a canonical deliberate-reasoning baseline (multi-path generation, branch scoring, BFS/DFS expansion/pruning) for complex tasks without inventing bespoke reasoning engines per app. | Ship a first-party `tot-deliberate` skill profile that runs bounded multi-branch reasoning using existing inference/routing primitives, persists branch states/scores as governed objects, supports policy-bounded BFS/DFS-style exploration with pruning criteria, and emits deterministic branch/evaluation/selection receipts for replay and audit. | 7 | 1/2/client/app |

## WiFi CSI Non-Visual Sensing Intake (RuView, x.com/heygurisingh 2030779994767851980, 2026-03-09)

Source references:
- https://docs.google.com/document/d/1SH1LBOYvJvTqK8Jf7vH9t8k1AIUCQ7cM_bscKFdJZWE/edit?usp=sharing
- https://github.com/ruvnet/RuView

Notes:
- Primitive-first scope: this intake extends existing substrate, eye, event, module-registry, and consent-policy primitives rather than introducing a standalone product surface.
- Default placement: authority remains in `core`; `client` stays thin (`cli`/observability only); vendor-specific board integrations belong in `adapters`; no dedicated `app` surface is required for the base capability.
- Multi-layer impact:
  - Layer `-1`: CSI hardware adapter profile, signal capture, board/runtime capability descriptors
  - Layer `0`: conduit-routed event ingress, deterministic receipts, fail-closed guard path
  - Layer `1`: privacy, consent, retention, locality, and biometric-risk policy primitives
  - Layer `2`: CSI decode/inference runtime, module execution, degrade/retry behavior
  - Layer `3`: persona/eye binding defaults only
  - `client`: thin `protheus eye enable wifi` / observability surfaces only
  - `adapter`: chipset/board-specific CSI collectors and firmware packaging

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-SUBSTRATE-001.1 | existing-coverage-validated | CSI substrate primitive (`layer_minus_one` capture + Layer-2 decode/inference lane) | Non-visual sensing should enter the system as a reusable substrate primitive, not as an app-specific feature, so pose/vitals/presence and future derived capabilities can all reuse one governed ingest path. | Add a CSI substrate primitive with hardware capability descriptor, raw capture contract, and Layer-2 decode/inference lane that emits normalized `presence`, `respiration`, `heartbeat_proxy`, and `pose_proxy` events with deterministic receipts, confidence, sampling metadata, and provenance. | 9 | -1/0/1/2/3/adapter |
| V6-SUBSTRATE-001.2 | existing-coverage-validated | CSI derived-module extension registry | Fall detection, sleep staging, activity recognition, and similar capabilities are extensions over the same CSI primitive and should not become parallel bespoke pipelines. | Extend the existing module-registry primitive so CSI-derived modules can be enabled per persona/shadow/policy scope; modules declare input event contract, compute budget, privacy class, and degradation behavior, and activation/deactivation emits deterministic receipts. | 9 | -1/0/1/2/3/adapter |
| V6-SUBSTRATE-001.3 | existing-coverage-validated | Low-power embedded deployment profile for CSI adapters | The substrate is only strategically useful if the primitive can run on cheap, offline, edge hardware without forcing a different architecture. | Add an embedded deployment profile for supported CSI adapters/ESP32-class boards with offline-first operation, power/latency telemetry, bounded-memory mode, and deterministic degraded-mode receipts when hardware quality or bandwidth drops below threshold. | 9 | -1/0/1/2/3/adapter |
| V6-SUBSTRATE-001.4 | existing-coverage-validated | Privacy/consent/locality policy extension for non-visual sensing | Through-wall and vital-sign inference crosses into high-risk sensing, so privacy and consent must be first-class policy extensions on top of the substrate primitive. | Extend Layer-1 sensing policy primitives with local-only default, consent requirements, retention bounds, export denial by default, and biometric-risk classification for CSI events; all sensing actions route through Layer-0 conduit gates and fail closed on policy violation. | 9 | -1/0/1/2/3/adapter |
| V6-SUBSTRATE-001.5 | existing-coverage-validated | Native Eye binding for personas/shadows and thin client exposure | CSI data is only operationally useful when bound cleanly into the existing Eye primitive and surfaced through thin operator tooling. | Bind CSI as a native Eye source so personas/shadows can subscribe through governed eye lanes (`protheus eye enable wifi` / persona triggers), with client limited to enable/status/observability surfaces and no duplicate sensing logic outside core/adapter layers. | 9 | -1/0/1/2/3/adapter |

## Persistent Scheduling Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1FEglrmeMZyEqoPX0rrALvmwKMux8UUk_502Vw9n6hb4`, 2026-03-11)

Source references:
- https://docs.google.com/document/d/1FEglrmeMZyEqoPX0rrALvmwKMux8UUk_502Vw9n6hb4/edit?usp=sharing
- https://x.com/the_smart_ape/status/2030691409632567307

Notes:
- Primitive-first normalization: this source extends existing persistent scheduling/runtime lanes; it does not create a separate scheduler subsystem.
- Overlap handled explicitly:
  - scheduled persistent tasks and detached execution: `V6-PERSIST-001.1`
  - cron/intel scheduling + model routing policy: `V6-COCKPIT-018.1`, `V6-COCKPIT-018.2`
  - remote/mobile operations and dashboarding: `V6-PERSIST-001.2`, `V6-OBSERVABILITY-001.*`
  - conduit-only enforcement baseline: `V6-PERSIST-001.6`, `V6-COCKPIT-018.5`
- Net-new focus from this source:
  - precise cron expression contract with durable restart/reboot survival guarantees
  - per-job isolated runtime session + per-job model/provider override contract
  - native multi-channel delivery routing contract for scheduled outputs
  - scheduler state dashboard contract (next run, last run, status, test-run-now)
  - curated scheduling template governance lane for common job archetypes
- Default placement:
  - Layer `0`: conduit-routed schedule/create/run/deliver/pause actions + deterministic receipts
  - Layer `1`: schedule policy, channel delivery policy, job isolation/retention/privacy controls
  - Layer `2`: cron scheduler core, isolated job runner, model override resolver, delivery fanout router
  - Layer `3`: optional operator persona defaults only
  - `client`: thin scheduler dashboard/control surfaces only
  - `app`: optional `/apps/scheduler_lab/` profile only
  - `adapter`: channel delivery connectors and scheduler backends

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-PERSIST-001.7 | existing-coverage-validated | Precise Cron Expression + Durable Persistence Contract | Background automation remains brittle without explicit cron precision and guaranteed survival across restarts/reboots. | Add governed cron contract supporting explicit schedules (for example `0 9 * * 1`) with deterministic create/update/delete/run receipts and restart/reboot persistence proofs in scheduler state artifacts. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-PERSIST-001.8 | existing-coverage-validated | Per-Job Isolated Session + Model Override Contract | Scheduled jobs need strict isolation and per-job model selection to prevent context bleed and optimize cost/quality by task class. | Each scheduled job runs in isolated session scope with explicit model/provider override policy, emits deterministic per-job lifecycle receipts, and rejects cross-job context leakage by contract tests. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-PERSIST-001.9 | existing-coverage-validated | Multi-Channel Scheduled Delivery Contract | Scheduled outputs lose operational value when delivery wiring is ad hoc and channel-specific behavior is not governed. | Add delivery routing contract for supported channels (Slack/Discord/Telegram/WhatsApp/email adapters as policy allows) with deterministic delivery attempt/outcome receipts and fail-closed permission checks. | 10 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-PERSIST-001.10 | existing-coverage-validated | Persistent Heartbeat Batch Checklist Runtime Contract | Heartbeat capability exists, but production automation needs daemon-native batch checklist execution with durable cadence guarantees. | Add daemon-owned heartbeat runner profile that executes configured checklist batches on cadence with deterministic checklist/run receipts, restart-safe continuity, and bounded timeout/retry policy behavior. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-PERSIST-001.11 | existing-coverage-validated | Scheduler Dashboard + Test-Run Control Contract | Operators need real-time visibility/control for active jobs to trust autonomous scheduling in production. | Add scheduler status surface (`next_run`, `last_run`, `state`, `delivery history`) plus governed `run-now/pause/resume/edit` controls with deterministic control receipts and audit timeline continuity. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-PERSIST-001.12 | existing-coverage-validated | Curated Persistent Scheduling Template Governance Lane | Reusable scheduling patterns require signed curation to avoid unsafe defaults and reduce setup friction. | Maintain signed template pack installable via governed URI (for example `schedule://morning-summary`) with quarterly human review, compatibility metadata, and deterministic install/update/deprecate receipts. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |

## AI Software Engineer App Intake (Doc `1KKKAfH8FwOTTyfyKl2zPupgTfTmYUdppHqegNYJLR7o`, 2026-03-11)

Source references:
- https://docs.google.com/document/d/1KKKAfH8FwOTTyfyKl2zPupgTfTmYUdppHqegNYJLR7o/edit?usp=sharing
- https://github.com/AntonOsika/gpt-engineer

Notes:
- Primitive-first normalization: this is an `/apps` workflow composition over existing planning/research, inference, memory object, and verification primitives; it does not introduce a new core code-generation authority subsystem.
- Default placement:
  - Layer `0`: conduit-routed generation/edit/test actions + deterministic receipts
  - Layer `1`: object provenance, policy/budget enforcement, retention/rollback metadata
  - Layer `2`: spec synthesis, scaffold/code generation orchestration, critique-fix iteration controller
  - Layer `3`: optional software-engineer persona defaults only
  - `client`: thin `run/status/preview` wrappers only
  - `app`: primary implementation at `/apps/code_engineer/`

Objective: add a first-party app that converts a natural-language request into a project spec, scaffolded file tree/code, and bounded critique-fix iterations, while preserving core authority and conduit-only governance.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-APP-006.1 | existing-coverage-validated | Prompt-to-Spec + Scaffolded Codebase Generation Workflow | Developers still spend significant manual effort translating intent into concrete specs and initial project structure. | `protheus app run code-engineer "<prompt>"` generates a governed spec object and scaffolded project tree/code artifacts with provenance and deterministic generation receipts, using existing planning/inference primitives only. | 6 | app |
| V6-APP-006.2 | existing-coverage-validated | Bounded Self-Critique + Verify/Fix Iteration Loop | Generated projects are brittle without an automated review/fix loop tied to explicit verification gates. | The app runs bounded critique/test/fix iterations over generated artifacts using existing verification primitives; each iteration emits deterministic receipts and stops on pass criteria or policy-bounded retry limits. | 6 | app |
| V6-APP-006.3 | existing-coverage-validated | Conduit-Only Code-Engineer Enforcement + `/apps` Placement Contract | Autonomous code generation can bypass safety and boundary policy if embedded directly in client/runtime code paths. | Ship implementation under `/apps/code_engineer/`, keep client wrappers thin, and route all generate/modify/test actions through conduit with capability/policy checks, deterministic receipts, and fail-closed denial behavior. | 10 | 0/app |

## DeerFlow Product-Builder Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1nQS5TPPQfwllJDNSDcR1ll7zBNYpJ3uoDqn1-M8Fy5k`, 2026-03-11)

Source references:
- https://docs.google.com/document/d/1nQS5TPPQfwllJDNSDcR1ll7zBNYpJ3uoDqn1-M8Fy5k/edit?usp=sharing
- https://x.com/JulianGoldieSEO/status/2030712654843510965

Notes:
- This source maps primarily to existing `V6-APP-006.*`, `V6-APP-013.*`, `V6-COCKPIT-022.*`, and connector lanes (`V6-PERSIST-001.4/.9`), so it is normalized as source coverage + net-new deltas only.
- Overlap handled explicitly:
  - prompt-to-spec/scaffold/verify-fix loop: `V6-APP-006.1..006.3`
  - planning/delegation and bounded harness controls: `V6-APP-013.2`, `V6-COCKPIT-022.1..022.6`
  - Slack/Telegram connector and delivery routing: `V6-PERSIST-001.4`, `V6-PERSIST-001.9`, `V6-COCKPIT-019.4`
- Net-new focus from this source:
  - one-command single-goal product-delivery entrypoint contract (`protheus build`)
  - explicit reasoning-before-acting receipt gate as mandatory first phase for builder runs
  - product-builder-specific chat ingress profile (Slack/Telegram goal-to-build bridge)
  - curated builder template governance lane (`builders://`)
- Default placement remains:
  - Layer `0`: conduit-routed build/plan/delegate/deliver actions + deterministic receipts
  - Layer `1`: risk-tier reasoning gate policy, budget controls, delivery policy, retention/provenance rules
  - Layer `2`: build orchestrator, sub-agent coordination controller, artifact packaging/delivery runtime
  - Layer `3`: optional product-engineer persona defaults only
  - `client`: thin `build/status/deliver` wrappers and observability only
  - `app`: primary workflow remains `/apps/code_engineer/` (no duplicate app family)
  - `adapter`: connector and deployment adapters only

Objective: extend the existing code-engineer workflow into a governed goal-to-deliver product builder path with mandatory reasoning receipts, multi-agent execution, and optional chat-driven goal ingress, while preserving conduit-only authority and thin client/app boundaries.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-APP-006.4 | existing-coverage-validated | Single-Goal End-to-End Build Entry Contract (`protheus build`) | Teams still need multiple commands/workflows to move from objective to deliverable artifact package. | Add `protheus build "<goal>"` profile alias that composes existing research/plan/code/test/package flows and emits deterministic stage receipts plus a final delivery artifact manifest. | 6 | app |
| V6-APP-006.5 | existing-coverage-validated | Mandatory Reasoning-First Receipt Gate for Build Runs | Autonomous build loops can skip explicit planning visibility, increasing risk and reducing operator trust. | Every build run emits an initial reasoning/plan receipt and enforces policy-based continue rules (auto-continue for low-risk, explicit approval for high-risk) before execution/delegation starts. | 6 | app |
| V6-APP-006.6 | existing-coverage-validated | Slack/Telegram Goal-Ingress Bridge for Builder Runs | Chat-based operation is fragmented without a direct mapping from channel goals to governed product-build workflows. | Add builder-profile chat ingress (`deploy bot slack | 6 | app |
| V6-APP-006.7 | existing-coverage-validated | Sub-Agent Coordinated Product Artifact Delivery Contract | Build workflows can produce incomplete deliverables when sub-agent orchestration and artifact packaging are not first-class completion criteria. | Require bounded sub-agent task graph execution (code/test/docs/deploy steps as applicable), verify completion criteria, and emit deterministic artifact delivery receipts including test/package/deploy evidence pointers. | 6 | app |
| V6-APP-006.8 | existing-coverage-validated | Curated Product-Builder Template Governance Lane (`builders://`) | Product-builder template quality and safety drift without signed curation and periodic domain review. | Maintain signed builder template pack installable via governed URI (for example `builders://web-app`) with quarterly human review, compatibility metadata, and deterministic install/update/deprecate receipts. | 6 | app |

## CEO-Orchestrator Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1laeJh5FIHhiVcgOeI3LvQyi_eW8Xo_hwHDuMtLBIaww`, 2026-03-11)

Source references:
- https://docs.google.com/document/d/1laeJh5FIHhiVcgOeI3LvQyi_eW8Xo_hwHDuMtLBIaww/edit?usp=sharing
- https://x.com/johann_sath/status/2030709689940635760

Notes:
- This source overlaps existing builder/delegation/orchestrator lanes and is normalized as source coverage + net-new orchestration-policy deltas only.
- Overlap handled explicitly:
  - reasoning-first gate and builder flow: `V6-APP-006.4..006.8`
  - dual-agent/delegation orchestration: `V6-COCKPIT-009.1`, `V6-COCKPIT-022.1..022.6`
  - multi-agent agency coordinator lane: `V6-AGENCY-001.3`
  - sub-agent persistence and guardrails: `V6-PERSIST-001.5`, `V6-PERSIST-001.6`
- Net-new focus from this source:
  - explicit manager "delegate-first" operating-mode policy contract
  - complexity-based conditional local-execution override contract for trivial tasks
  - delegation economic guardrails (latency/token/cost) and auto-fallback thresholds
  - curated orchestrator template governance via `orchestrators://`
- Default placement:
  - Layer `0`: conduit-routed manager-mode decisions and task-execution path enforcement + deterministic receipts
  - Layer `1`: manager mode policy (strict/delegate-first/override thresholds), budget and latency guardrails
  - Layer `2`: complexity scorer, delegation planner, spawn controller, fallback optimizer
  - Layer `3`: optional manager/CEO persona presets only
  - `client`: thin `manager mode/status` wrappers and observability controls only
  - `app`: no new app family; this extends existing orchestrator/builder workflows
  - `adapter`: none required beyond existing execution/connectors

Objective: harden manager-style delegation into a governed operating mode that defaults to delegated execution, supports policy-scoped trivial-task overrides, and optimizes for latency/cost without breaking safety or introducing duplicate orchestration subsystems.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-ORCH-001.1 | existing-coverage-validated | Manager Delegate-First Mode Contract (`strict` / `smart`) | Manager behavior drifts across runs without one explicit mode contract governing when direct execution is prohibited. | Add manager mode policy with explicit profiles (`strict_delegate`, `smart_delegate`) and deterministic mode-decision receipts on every task/action route; strict mode hard-denies direct manager execution except policy-whitelisted maintenance actions. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-ORCH-001.2 | existing-coverage-validated | Complexity-Scored Conditional Override Contract | Always-spawn delegation increases latency/cost on trivial tasks unless bounded direct-execution exceptions are explicitly governed. | Add complexity scorer and override policy so low-complexity actions can execute locally in `smart_delegate` mode; every override logs deterministic reasoning receipts (`score`, `threshold`, `route`). | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-ORCH-001.3 | existing-coverage-validated | Parallel Delegation Planner + Tree Telemetry Contract | Delegation speedups are hard to realize and tune without planner-level fan-out control and transparent delegation-tree metrics. | Add parallel delegation planner with configurable fan-out caps and deterministic delegation-tree telemetry surfaced to observability (`spawn_count`, `critical_path_ms`, `speedup_estimate`, `failed_handoffs`). | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-ORCH-001.4 | existing-coverage-validated | Delegation Cost/Latency Guardrail + Auto-Fallback Contract | Sub-agent spawning can become counterproductive without measurable guardrails and automatic fallback policy. | Enforce per-task delegation guardrails (token/cost/latency budgets) with automatic fallback to direct or reduced-fanout execution when overhead thresholds are exceeded; all fallback decisions emit deterministic receipts. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-ORCH-001.5 | existing-coverage-validated | Curated Orchestrator Template Governance Lane (`orchestrators://`) | Orchestrator template quality and safety drift without signed curation and recurring performance-policy review. | Maintain signed orchestrator template pack installable via governed URI (for example `orchestrators://ceo-strict`) with quarterly human review, compatibility metadata, and deterministic install/update/deprecate receipts. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-ORCH-001.6 | existing-coverage-validated | Conduit-Only Orchestrator Mode Enforcement | Delegation-mode logic can become bypassable if mode checks are implemented directly in client wrappers instead of authoritative core lanes. | Enforce conduit-only route decisions for manager mode, delegation spawning, override/fallback logic, and template activation with bypass-rejection tests and fail-closed policy denials. | 10 | 0/1/2/client |

## 2026 Hiring Projects Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1qgE2wXUFVwVt1Ei0lnud8ILNZPKx3DHoqemS3z9zYmk`, 2026-03-11)

Notes:

  - RAG eval metrics/reporting baseline: `V6-OBSERVABILITY-003.1..003.5`
  - document parsing/extraction baseline: `V6-PARSE-001.1..001.6`
  - prompt-injection/security scanner baseline: `V6-SEC-010..015`

- Net-new focus from this source:
  - RAG eval-to-improvement closed loop contract
  - native telephony voice-agent runtime lane
  - GitHub-native automated code-reviewer lane
  - unstructured extraction profile alias over existing parser primitives
- Default placement:
  - Layer `0`: conduit-routed eval/voice/review/extract actions + deterministic receipts + fail-closed policy denials
  - Layer `1`: privacy/compliance policy (voice redaction, review scope, extraction schema, feedback rights), budget/retention controls
  - Layer `2`: eval-refinement orchestrator, telephony call-state runtime, GitHub review runtime, extraction orchestration
  - Layer `3`: optional reviewer/voice persona defaults only

  - `app`: `/apps/github_reviewer/` optional UX; no new authority runtime
  - `adapter`: telephony providers, GitHub API/App adapters, extraction backend adapters

Objective: extend existing quality/automation primitives so teams can ship hiring-grade projects (continuous RAG improvement, voice-call automation, GitHub review automation, and structured extraction) without duplicating core subsystems.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-OBSERVABILITY-003.6 | existing-coverage-validated | RAG Eval-to-Refine Closed-Loop Contract | Scoring alone is insufficient when poor retrieval/answer quality does not flow into governed improvement actions. | Add bounded feedback loop that ingests `V6-OBSERVABILITY-003.*` scores and proposes policy-gated retrieval/index/persona refinements with deterministic propose/apply/revert receipts and measurable quality delta tracking. | 7 | 0/1/2/client |

| V6-VOICE-001.2 | existing-coverage-validated | Call Transcript + Scheduling Handoff Contract | Voice workflows are incomplete without transcript artifacts and direct workflow handoff into scheduling/follow-up actions. | Generate redacted transcripts and extract structured intents/actions that route into calendar/follow-up lanes with deterministic transcript/action receipts and replay-safe provenance links. | 7 | 0/1/2/adapter/client/app |
| V6-VOICE-001.3 | existing-coverage-validated | Voice Privacy/Redaction + Budget Guardrail Contract | Telephony data carries elevated privacy/cost risk unless retention, redaction, and spend controls are enforced by policy. | Enforce per-call redaction/retention rules and runtime token/minute spend caps with fail-closed denial behavior and deterministic budget/privacy receipts for each session. | 10 | 0/1/2/adapter/client/app |
| V6-VOICE-001.4 | existing-coverage-validated | Conduit-Only Voice Execution Boundary | Voice providers and call actions can bypass safety boundaries if executed directly from client/app wrappers. | Enforce conduit-only routing for call control, transcript processing, and follow-up actions with bypass-rejection tests and fail-closed policy denials. | 10 | 0/1/2/adapter/client/app |
| V6-PARSE-001.7 | existing-coverage-validated | Unstructured Extraction Profile Alias Contract (`protheus extract`) | Users need a simple extraction entrypoint, but extraction logic must remain on existing governed parse primitives. | Add `protheus extract <file | 7 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V6-CODE-REVIEW-001.1 | existing-coverage-validated | GitHub-Native Reviewer Runtime Contract | Teams need deterministic PR summary/risk/suggestion automation directly in GitHub flows. | Add governed reviewer runtime that consumes PR diff/context and emits structured review artifacts (summary, risk flags, suggested patches) with deterministic review receipts linked to commit/PR ids. | 7 | 0/1/2/adapter/client/app |
| V6-CODE-REVIEW-001.2 | existing-coverage-validated | GitHub App/Actions Deployment Contract for Reviewer | Review automation adoption is limited without one-command install/deploy profile. | Add install/deploy contract (`protheus team install github-reviewer`) for GitHub App/Actions profile with deterministic provisioning/state receipts and explicit permission-scope validation. | 7 | 0/1/2/adapter/client/app |
| V6-CODE-REVIEW-001.3 | existing-coverage-validated | Policy-Bound Comment + Merge-Gate Contract | Automated review is unsafe without scoped comment policies and merge-gate decision boundaries. | Enforce policy-scoped review comments/check results with deterministic allow/deny/needs-human receipts; optional merge-block recommendations are advisory unless explicitly policy-enabled. | 7 | 0/1/2/adapter/client/app |
| V6-CODE-REVIEW-001.4 | existing-coverage-validated | Conduit-Only Code-Review Boundary + Thin Client/App Enforcement | GitHub review logic can drift into client/app scripts and lose deterministic policy governance. | Enforce conduit-only routing for diff ingest, analysis, comment emission, and gate decisions with bypass-rejection tests; keep client/app surfaces non-authoritative. | 10 | 0/1/2/adapter/client/app |
| V6-CODE-REVIEW-001.5 | existing-coverage-validated | Curated Reviewer Template Governance Lane (`reviewers://`) | Reviewer quality drifts without signed templates and periodic tuning review. | Maintain signed reviewer template pack installable via governed URI (for example `reviewers://github-pr`) with quarterly human review, compatibility metadata, and deterministic install/update/deprecate receipts. | 7 | 0/1/2/adapter/client/app |

## Manager-Team Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1yaohxnWC1MWG11OAuyo7rIAV2FhSB9AxT4oFHqKAO3g`, 2026-03-11)

- Overlap handled explicitly:
  - manager delegate-first and strict/smart mode behavior: `V6-ORCH-001.1..001.6`
  - multi-agent team templates and orchestration: `V6-AGENCY-001.1..001.5`
  - sub-agent coordination and artifact delivery: `V6-APP-006.7`
- Net-new focus from this source:
  - one-command specialist team assembly SLA with deterministic team-manifest artifact
  - explicit per-agent memory/model isolation contract with manager shared-context boundary
  - curated manager+specialist team template governance via `teams://`
- Default placement:
  - Layer `0`: conduit-routed team assemble/handoff/accept actions + deterministic receipts
  - Layer `1`: memory-isolation policy, manager/shared-context boundary rules, team template policy metadata
  - Layer `2`: team assembly orchestrator, role-to-model binder, handoff acceptance gate runtime
  - Layer `3`: optional manager/specialist persona defaults only

  - `app`: no new app family; extends existing orchestration workflows
  - `adapter`: none required beyond existing model/router/connectors

Objective: strengthen existing team-orchestration lanes with deterministic team assembly artifacts, strict manager-to-specialist memory boundaries, and governance-safe template distribution, without introducing a duplicate manager-team subsystem.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-AGENCY-001.6 | existing-coverage-validated | One-Command Specialist Team Assembly Contract (`protheus team assemble`) | Team bring-up is still inconsistent without a canonical assembly contract and startup SLA. | Add `protheus team assemble <template>` profile that provisions role roster, routes model bindings, and emits deterministic team-manifest receipts with bounded startup SLA targets. | 6 | 1/2/client |
| V6-AGENCY-001.7 | existing-coverage-validated | Per-Agent Memory Isolation + Manager Shared-Context Contract | Specialist teams degrade when memory/model boundaries are not explicit, causing cross-role context pollution. | Enforce per-agent memory namespaces + role model binding with manager shared-context projection rules and deterministic access/merge receipts; unauthorized cross-lane memory access fails closed. | 6 | 1/2/client |
| V6-AGENCY-001.8 | existing-coverage-validated | Quality-Gated Handoff Acceptance Protocol Contract | Delegation scales chaos unless every specialist handoff passes standardized acceptance and merge checks. | Require explicit handoff acceptance gates (quality score + criteria metadata) before manager merge, with deterministic handoff/accept/deny receipts and published protocol schema in docs. | 6 | 1/2/client |
| V6-AGENCY-001.9 | existing-coverage-validated | Curated Manager+Specialist Team Template Governance Lane (`teams://`) | Team template quality and domain fit drift without signed curation and periodic review. | Maintain signed team template pack installable via governed URI (for example `teams://content-team`) with quarterly human review, compatibility metadata, and deterministic install/update/deprecate receipts. | 6 | 1/2/client |

- https://x.com/lamxnt/status/2030390230579281958

Notes:
- This source overlaps existing skills, routing, memory, and registry lanes and is normalized as source coverage + net-new deltas only.
- Overlap handled explicitly:
  - skill packaging/composition/execution baseline: `V6-SKILLS-001.1..001.8`
  - model routing/cost controls baseline: `V6-COCKPIT-018.2`, `V6-LLMN-001..004`
  - tool/marketplace registry baseline: `V6-DOM-405`, `V6-EXT-706..709`
  - persistent continuity baseline: `V6-PERSIST-001.3`, `V6-HERMES-001.3`
- Net-new focus from this source:
  - autonomous skill evolution loop from observed user-feedback patterns
  - explicit inspectable user-preference model contract
  - hybrid primary/sub-agent routing with OAuth provider brokering under spend caps
  - explicit Tools-vs-Skills dual registry contract (classification + UX separation)
- Default placement:
  - Layer `0`: conduit-routed evolve/route/registry/auth actions + deterministic receipts + fail-closed denials
  - Layer `1`: user-model policy, OAuth/token policy, classification policy, spend/retention controls
  - Layer `2`: skill-evolution engine, hybrid router, credential broker, registry classifier runtime
  - Layer `3`: optional personalized assistant defaults only
  - `client`: thin `skills/user-model/registry` inspect/manage wrappers only
  - `app`: optional `/apps/registry_console/` UX only
  - `adapter`: provider OAuth and model-provider adapters only

Objective: extend existing skills/routing/registry primitives to deliver adaptive self-learning behavior and a cleaner professional operator surface (Tools vs Skills) without duplicating authority logic in client/app layers.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-SKILLS-001.9 | existing-coverage-validated | Autonomous Skill Evolution Contract | Skill libraries stagnate without a governed loop that promotes successful recurring behaviors into reusable skill updates. | Add bounded skill-evolution loop that proposes create/refine/merge/retire operations from interaction outcomes, runs policy checks, and emits deterministic proposal/apply/revert receipts for every evolution event. | 7 | 1/2/client/app |
| V6-MEMORY-022 | existing-coverage-validated | Persistent User Preference Model Contract + Inspect Surface | Assistants feel non-adaptive when user style/preferences are not modeled explicitly and queryable across sessions. | Add user-model object contract (preference vectors, interaction style, decision priors, confidence) with deterministic update receipts and thin inspect/edit surfaces (`protheus user-model inspect | 8 | 1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-018.6 | existing-coverage-validated | Hybrid Primary/Sub-Agent Routing + Inherited User-Model Contract | Hybrid setups fail in practice when sub-agents lose user context or routing ignores capability/cost constraints across providers. | Extend routing policy so primary tasks and delegated sub-tasks can target different providers/models while inheriting user-model context, with deterministic route-decision receipts and bounded fallback paths. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-018.7 | existing-coverage-validated | OAuth Provider Broker + Spend-Cap Guardrail Contract | Multi-provider routing increases credential/cost risk without one governed OAuth and budget control path. | Add provider OAuth broker with encrypted token handling, scope validation, spend-cap enforcement, and deterministic auth/use/deny receipts; high-risk scope escalation requires explicit policy approval. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-REGISTRY-001.1 | existing-coverage-validated | Tools-vs-Skills Dual Registry Contract | Power-user workflows become confusing when external tools and internal skills share one ambiguous registry surface. | Add dual-registry contract with deterministic classification (`tool`, `skill`, `hybrid`), separate list/install/manage surfaces, and provenance-linked registry receipts for add/update/reclassify actions. | 6 | 1/2/client |
| V6-REGISTRY-001.2 | existing-coverage-validated | Curated Registry Taxonomy Governance Lane | Registry classification quality drifts without signed taxonomy packs and recurring curator review. | Maintain signed taxonomy pack installable via governed URI (for example `registry://tools-skills-v1`) with quarterly human review, compatibility metadata, and deterministic install/update/deprecate receipts. | 6 | 1/2/client |
| V6-REGISTRY-001.3 | existing-coverage-validated | Conduit-Only Registry/OAuth Boundary Enforcement | Registry and provider auth paths can become bypassable if executed directly from client scripts. | Enforce conduit-only routing for classify/install/evolve/auth/spend actions with bypass-rejection tests and fail-closed policy denials; keep client/app surfaces non-authoritative. | 10 | 0/1/2/client |

| V6-COCKPIT-012.2 | existing-coverage-validated | Gateway daemon runtime with persistent connector sessions + WebSocket UI bridge | Multi-channel autonomy requires one persistent routing daemon instead of per-session connector spin-up. | Extend `protheusd` gateway mode to maintain durable channel connections (Discord/Slack/etc.), provide attach/reconnect-safe routing, and expose WebSocket stream endpoints with deterministic lifecycle receipts (`connect`, `route`, `reconnect`, `close`). | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-012.3 | existing-coverage-validated | `dmScope` isolation contract (per-agent / per-channel-peer context fences) | Shared daemon operation needs strict isolation boundaries to prevent cross-user/peer context leakage. | Implement scoped runtime partitions keyed by agent/channel-peer identity, enforce no cross-scope memory/tool leakage, and emit deterministic scope-boundary receipts on create/read/write/route actions. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-012.4 | existing-coverage-validated | Native cron + heartbeat automation envelope for detached autonomy | Autonomous operation quality depends on deterministic scheduled runs and periodic self-check loops while detached. | Add native scheduler + heartbeat engine contracts so periodic tasks/checklists run with policy/budget gates, deterministic receipts, and delivery guarantees through gateway routing even when no active attached client exists. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-012.5 | existing-coverage-validated | Safe exec/browser tool-mode governance (`sandbox`/`gateway`/`full`) + conduit receipts | Tool execution surfaces must remain Safety Plane governed while enabling practical detached operations. | Add explicit tool-mode policy contract for exec/browser lanes with isolated browser profiles and fail-closed escalation rules; every tool call routes through Layer-0 conduit and emits deterministic audit receipts with mode + policy decision metadata. | 10 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-012.6 | existing-coverage-validated | Session-classified workspace memory visibility contract (`main` vs `shared`) | Workspace bootstrap can leak sensitive long-term memory without explicit session-class visibility rules across direct vs shared channels. | Add deterministic bootstrap visibility policy that enforces per-session memory scopes (e.g., `MEMORY.md` main-session only, shared-session redaction/default deny), with deterministic `loaded/redacted/denied` receipts and regression tests for leakage prevention. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-012.7 | existing-coverage-validated | Unified tool-mode matrix for `exec/browser/file/message` surfaces | Tool governance is inconsistent when only exec/browser have explicit mode contracts while file/message paths use ad hoc controls. | Extend tool-mode governance to a unified matrix (`sandbox`/`gateway`/`full` where applicable) across exec/browser/file/message tools with policy-scoped capability checks, deterministic allow/deny receipts, and fail-closed defaults. | 10 | 0/1/2/client |

## Discrawl Structured Discord Memory Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1EUPYD-0oXnUWYglyrtJ2hziuWZaLMqa-TzAMBn7nIWg`, 2026-03-11)

Source references:
- https://docs.google.com/document/d/1EUPYD-0oXnUWYglyrtJ2hziuWZaLMqa-TzAMBn7nIWg/edit?usp=sharing
- https://x.com/jumperz/status/2030766393231126857

Notes:
- Primitive-first normalization: this is an extension of existing Discord orchestration/memory lanes, not a separate memory subsystem.
- Overlap handled explicitly:
  - Discord transport/binding/parallelism/presence contracts: `V6-COCKPIT-015.1..015.6`
  - Context/plugin and long-thread memory behavior: `V6-COCKPIT-015.1`, `V6-CONTEXT-001.*`
  - Core memory/query/storage primitives: `V6-MEMORY-003.*`, `V6-STORAGE-001.*`
- Net-new focus from this source:
  - full-structure Discord history sync into governed local queryable storage (messages, threads, replies, attachments, authors, channels)
  - SQL-first scoped query contract for swarm memory mining
  - automatic instruction/pattern mining from Discord history into Dream Sequencer inputs
  - cross-agent behavior/context graph derivation for orchestration and observability
  - curated Discord swarm-memory template governance
- Default placement:
  - Layer `0`: conduit-routed sync/query/mining actions + deterministic receipts
  - Layer `1`: tenancy/isolation, privacy/retention, scope policy, provenance contracts
  - Layer `2`: Discord ingest pipeline, query broker, mining pipeline, graph analytics engine
  - Layer `3`: optional swarm persona defaults only
  - `client`: thin `assimilate/query/graph` render/control surfaces only
  - `app`: optional `/apps/discord_memory_lab/` profile only
  - `adapter`: Discord API + storage/query backend adapters
