| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-COCKPIT-015.7 | existing-coverage-validated | Native Discord Server-to-Structured-Memory Sync Contract | Discord interaction history remains underutilized when only live chat handling exists without governed historical ingestion. | Add one-command governed sync (`protheus assimilate discord://<server | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-015.8 | existing-coverage-validated | Scoped SQL/Query Interface over Synced Discord Memory | Swarm history cannot compound into actionable intelligence without deterministic query access to structured conversation data. | Add governed query contract (`protheus query discord ...`) supporting scoped SQL-like retrieval over synced memory with policy-bounded query classes, deterministic query receipts, and fail-closed scope denials. | 10 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-015.9 | existing-coverage-validated | Automatic Instruction/Pattern Mining from Discord History Contract | Valuable operating patterns in channel/thread history are lost unless continuously mined into reusable rule/skill memory. | Add background mining lane that extracts candidate instructions/patterns from new Discord history into governed memory objects for Dream Sequencer review/application, with deterministic mining/proposal receipts. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-015.10 | existing-coverage-validated | Cross-Agent Context + Behavior Graph Derivation Contract | Discord swarm optimization is limited when interaction structure (who/what/where/attachment patterns) is not surfaced as analyzable graphs. | Add `protheus graph discord` contract generating scope-bounded interaction/context graphs with lineage links to source receipts and deterministic graph refresh artifacts consumable by observability/orchestration lanes. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-015.11 | existing-coverage-validated | Curated Discord Swarm-Memory Template Governance Lane | Reusable sync/mining/query profiles need signed curation and review to avoid unsafe defaults and drift. | Maintain signed template pack installable via governed URI (for example `discord://swarm-memory`) with quarterly human review, compatibility metadata, and deterministic install/update/deprecate receipts. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-015.12 | existing-coverage-validated | Conduit-Only Discord Memory Boundary + Thin Client Enforcement | Ingest/query/mining/graph flows can bypass policy if implemented directly in client/app code paths. | Enforce conduit-only routing for Discord memory sync/query/mining/graph actions with bypass-rejection tests; keep client/app surfaces non-authoritative and policy-governed. | 10 | 0/1/2/client |

## Graph-Aware Markdown Memory Vault Intake (ClawVault, 2026-03-11)

Source references:
- https://docs.google.com/document/d/12e228CO-aIx23leL-hnmGpu1J3AqWDuoWdzirX3X80o/edit?usp=sharing
- https://docs.google.com/document/d/1M-2AtjRQHOpFAxlGIcsTvFMsoo3wc8F8fZw_YAhRhcQ/edit?usp=sharing
- https://x.com/theturingpost/status/2031152490566320232

Notes:
- Source draft proposed this as a pure `client/apps/` extension. That placement is rejected for authority: editable memory truth, checkpointing, graph state, and rollback must remain core-authoritative.
- The second source doc duplicated the same concept under `V6-MEMORY-004`; this backlog intentionally folds both drafts into one normalized intake (`V6-MEMORY-003.*`) to avoid redundant parallel memory-vault epics.
- Primitive-first normalization: this intake extends existing primitives (`Epistemic Objects`, memory runtime, bookmark/graph visualization, conduit receipts, settlement/versioning) rather than introducing a parallel vault or separate memory database.
- Layer placement:
  - Layer `0`: conduit-routed reads/writes/checkpoints/rollback receipts
  - Layer `1`: markdown fact/object schema mapping, graph projection metadata, retention/provenance policy
  - Layer `2`: vault sync/index/update/checkpoint execution lanes
  - Layer `3`: persona memory affordances only
  - `client`: thin editor/search/visualization wrappers only
  - `app`: optional memory-vault UX app may exist, but never as source of truth
  - `adapter`: optional filesystem/editor/git adapters only

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-MEMORY-003.1 | existing-coverage-validated | Markdown vault projection over Epistemic Objects + graph links | Human-readable markdown memory should emerge from the canonical memory primitives rather than replace them with a second memory system. | Extend Epistemic Object storage/projection so markdown documents can be materialized and re-ingested as governed views of facts/entities/relationships; graph links are maintained incrementally with deterministic provenance receipts and no separate memory truth path. | 8 | 1/2/client |
| V6-MEMORY-003.2 | existing-coverage-validated | Vault checkpoint/version/rollback extension over existing memory runtime | Editable memory needs reversible history and checkpoint discipline, but that should reuse existing checkpoint/rollback primitives instead of inventing bespoke vault versioning. | Add checkpoint/history/rollback contracts for markdown memory vault state using existing memory runtime + settlement/version primitives; users/operators can diff, restore, and audit prior vault states with deterministic receipts and git-friendly artifacts. | 8 | 1/2/client |
| V6-MEMORY-003.3 | existing-coverage-validated | Natural-language search/edit bridge for markdown memory | The value of human-readable memory is lost if search/edit flows diverge from the existing research/assimilate/retrieval stack. | Extend research/assimilate/search lanes so users and agents can query and edit markdown-backed memory through the same governed retrieval/edit path, with live graph refresh, confidence/provenance preservation, and fail-closed conflict handling. | 10 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-MEMORY-003.4 | existing-coverage-validated | Thin client/app memory-vault UX surface | Operators may want a dedicated vault UX, but it must remain a projection/editor over core memory contracts rather than a second implementation surface. | Add optional thin wrappers (`protheus app run memory-vault`, dashboard/editor panels) that expose browse/search/edit/history actions over the core vault contracts; no graph/checkpoint/merge authority is implemented in client or app code. | 8 | 1/2/client |
| V6-MEMORY-003.5 | existing-coverage-validated | Conduit-only markdown vault guardrails + audit continuity | Editable markdown memory increases the risk of silent corruption or policy bypass unless every change stays inside the existing safety and receipt model. | Enforce Layer-0 conduit checks for vault read/write/edit/checkpoint/rollback/search actions, preserve policy metadata through markdown round-trips, and emit deterministic receipts for every mutation and graph refresh event. | 10 | 0/1/2/client |

## Semantic Code Indexing & Search Intake (cocoindex-code, 2026-03-11)

Source references:
- https://docs.google.com/document/d/1wipbnVO_emtGq4H7373M0dmba_YeC4R_eHTM0jyhZGA/edit?usp=sharing
- https://docs.google.com/document/d/18LX4VDDYZtdKRDn-0cc2P_Gvpeh0928cc0aNbaT-qX8/edit?usp=sharing
- https://github.com/cocoindex-io/cocoindex-code

Notes:
- Source draft proposed a `client/apps/` code-intelligence extension. That is too shallow for the authority surface: AST parsing, semantic chunking, index maintenance, and retrieval contracts belong in core memory/research lanes.
- The follow-on source doc proposed a separate graph-navigation app (`V6-APP-018`), but it is functionally an extension of the same code-index primitive family. This backlog intentionally folds graph traversal/navigation into `V6-MEMORY-004.*` instead of creating a redundant parallel epic.
- Primitive-first normalization: this intake extends existing code-understanding primitives (`LensMap annotations`, Epistemic Objects, research/assimilate, memory runtime, MCP/tool routing). It should not create a second indexing architecture.
- Layer placement:
  - Layer `0`: conduit-routed index/build/query receipts and policy gates
  - Layer `1`: code-chunk schema, AST symbol/entity mapping, provenance/confidence policy, retrieval ranking metadata
  - Layer `2`: incremental indexer, query engine, MCP/tool exposure, refresh scheduler
  - Layer `3`: code-aware persona affordances only
  - `client`: thin CLI/UX/search result views only
  - `app`: optional code-indexer UX app only
  - `adapter`: language/parser backends or external toolchain bridges when needed

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-MEMORY-004.1 | existing-coverage-validated | AST-backed code chunk/index extension over existing memory primitives | Semantic code search should extend the canonical memory/index substrate rather than introduce a separate code-search database owned by the client. | Extend memory/runtime indexing primitives so codebases are parsed into AST-aware Epistemic Objects (symbols, definitions, references, semantic chunks) with incremental refresh, provenance receipts, and language-backend adapters for supported languages. | 8 | 1/2/client |
| V6-MEMORY-004.2 | existing-coverage-validated | Natural-language semantic code retrieval extension | Code intelligence value comes from precise semantic retrieval integrated with the existing search/research stack, not from a standalone query path. | Extend search/research lanes so natural-language queries resolve against the code index with ranked semantic chunks, file paths, symbol metadata, and deterministic scoring/provenance receipts; retrieval stays token-efficient and file-bounded. | 8 | 1/2/client |
| V6-MEMORY-004.3 | existing-coverage-validated | Graph-navigation extension over the code index | Call graphs, dependency graphs, and traversal queries are higher-order views on the same indexed code substrate and should not become a second code-intelligence system. | Extend the code index so symbol/reference/chunk relations can be materialized as navigable subgraphs (call chains, dependency chains, data-flow paths) with deterministic graph receipts, bounded traversal payloads, and token-efficient subgraph injection into agent context. | 8 | 1/2/client |
| V6-MEMORY-004.4 | existing-coverage-validated | MCP/tool-facing code search + graph traversal contract | Coding agents and external orchestrators need governed access to both semantic search and graph traversal through the same tool-routing model. | Expose code-index query/build/status/traverse operations through governed tool/MCP contracts with deterministic request/response receipts, bounded result payloads, and no direct client-side bypass of core index authority. | 8 | 1/2/client |
| V6-MEMORY-004.5 | existing-coverage-validated | Thin client/app code-intelligence UX surface | Operators may want dedicated code-index or code-graph UIs, but they must remain consumers of the same core indexing/navigation primitives only. | Add optional thin wrappers (`protheus app run code-indexer`, `protheus app run code-graph`, search/traversal panels, result viewers) over the core code-index contracts; no AST parsing, ranking, graph mutation, or traversal authority lives in client/app code. | 8 | 1/2/client |
| V6-MEMORY-004.6 | existing-coverage-validated | Conduit-only code-index build/query/traversal guardrails + audit continuity | Code indexing/search/graph traversal can leak scope or drift into duplicate tooling unless every operation stays inside the receipt/policy model. | Enforce Layer-0 conduit checks for index build/update/query/traversal/export actions, preserve provenance across incremental refreshes and graph projections, and emit deterministic receipts for every chunk mutation, traversal, query, and MCP/tool invocation. | 10 | 0/1/2/client |

## Local Document RAG Engine Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1P1_-BSqoRU0Qt2ais5aUTHycK1wOdmZEhTkcBr1jDhI`, 2026-03-11)

Source references:
- https://docs.google.com/document/d/1P1_-BSqoRU0Qt2ais5aUTHycK1wOdmZEhTkcBr1jDhI/edit?usp=sharing
- https://github.com/kwalus/Canopy

Notes:
- Primitive-first normalization: this intake extends existing memory/research/index primitives; it does not create a standalone document-memory authority stack in client.
- Overlap handled explicitly:
  - Existing ingestion/enrichment path: `V6-MEMORY-002.*`
  - Existing graph-aware vault projection path: `V6-MEMORY-003.*`
  - Existing semantic index/query contracts: `V6-MEMORY-004.*`
  - Existing parser/extraction lane: `V6-PARSE-001.*`
  - Existing always-on consolidation lane: `V6-MEMORY-006.*`
- Net-new focus from this source:
  - local-first document-format ingestion pipeline (PDF/Markdown/TXT and parser-adapter growth path)
  - citation-first semantic retrieval over document chunk objects
  - bounded merge of retrieved document knowledge into persistent vault graph without duplicating authority
- Default placement:
  - Layer `0`: conduit-routed ingest/chunk/index/query actions + immutable receipts
  - Layer `1`: document chunk schema, retention/privacy policy, citation contract
  - Layer `2`: parser/chunker/indexer/retrieval orchestration runtime
  - Layer `3`: optional document-aware persona defaults only
  - `client`: thin run/status/upload/query UX only
  - `app`: optional `/apps/document_rag/` vertical profile
  - `adapter`: format/parser backends and optional embedding acceleration adapters

Objective: add local document indexing and citation-aware retrieval as a governed extension over existing memory primitives, preserving core authority and thin client/app surfaces.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-MEMORY-005.1 | existing-coverage-validated | Document Ingestion + Typed Chunking Contract | Document reasoning quality collapses when source files remain opaque blobs without deterministic chunk/provenance extraction. | Add governed ingestion for local docs (PDF/Markdown/TXT initially) that parses and chunks into Epistemic Objects with source offsets, MIME metadata, and deterministic ingest receipts. | 8 | 0/1/2/client/app/adapter |
| V6-MEMORY-005.2 | existing-coverage-validated | Local Semantic Retrieval + Citation Bundle Contract | Answers over local documents are untrustworthy without explicit citation bundles and retrieval provenance. | Extend research/query lanes to retrieve ranked document chunks with citation bundles (`source`, `chunk_id`, `offset`, `confidence`) and deterministic retrieval receipts for each query run. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-MEMORY-005.3 | existing-coverage-validated | Vault Graph Merge Contract for Document Knowledge | Document RAG becomes siloed if extracted knowledge does not feed the same persistent vault graph used by other memory lanes. | Merge selected document chunks/entities into the existing graph-aware vault projection with provenance-preserving links and deterministic merge receipts, avoiding duplicate memory truth paths. | 8 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-MEMORY-005.4 | existing-coverage-validated | Incremental Reindex + Drift Control Contract | Document indexes stale quickly without deterministic reindex policy for changed files and deleted artifacts. | Add policy-bounded incremental reindexing with change detection, tombstone handling, and deterministic reindex receipts; stale chunks are pruned or marked superseded without losing audit lineage. | 7 | 1/2/client |
| V6-MEMORY-005.5 | existing-coverage-validated | Thin Activation Surface Contract (`protheus app run document-rag`) | Adoption suffers when operators cannot enable and operate document RAG through thin UX surfaces. | Provide thin run/upload/status/query surfaces under client/app shells only; parser/chunk/index/query authority remains in core lanes. | 6 | 1/2/client/app |
| V6-MEMORY-005.6 | existing-coverage-validated | Conduit-Only Document RAG Boundary + Audit Continuity Contract | Document ingestion/retrieval can become a policy bypass path unless all operations remain conduit-governed and receipted. | Enforce conduit-only routing for ingest/chunk/index/query/merge actions with bypass-rejection tests and deterministic receipt lineage for every operation. | 10 | 0/1/2/client/app |

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-MEMORY-006.1 | existing-coverage-validated | Continuous Ingestion + Structured Extraction Contract | Long-term memory quality decays when incoming notes/files/messages are not transformed into structured objects at ingest time. | Add governed watcher lanes (folder/API) that ingest new artifacts, extract summary/entities/topics/importance into Epistemic Objects, and emit deterministic ingest receipts linked to source provenance. | 8 | 0/1/2/client/app |
| V6-MEMORY-006.2 | existing-coverage-validated | Scheduled Background Consolidation Contract | Memory stores drift into fragmented raw items without periodic synthesis of links, clusters, and high-signal insights. | Add policy-bounded consolidation jobs (for example every 30 minutes) that review unconsolidated objects, create link/cluster/insight artifacts, and persist deterministic consolidation receipts with rollback pointers. | 8 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-MEMORY-006.3 | existing-coverage-validated | Citation-Aware Persistent Query Composition Contract | Cross-session answers are brittle if retrieval paths cannot cite exactly which memory objects and consolidation insights were used. | Extend research/query composition so relevant long-term memory + consolidation artifacts are retrieved with explicit citation bundles (object ids/provenance/confidence) and deterministic query receipts. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-MEMORY-006.4 | existing-coverage-validated | Memory Freshness + Consolidation SLO Contract | Always-on memory can silently stale without measurable freshness targets and lag visibility. | Add freshness/lag metrics (ingest latency, unconsolidated backlog age, consolidation coverage) with threshold alerts and deterministic SLO receipts in observability surfaces. | 7 | 1/2/client |
| V6-MEMORY-006.5 | existing-coverage-validated | Thin App/Client Activation Surface Contract (`protheus app run always-on-memory`) | Adoption drops when operators cannot enable/inspect always-on memory without touching authority code paths. | Provide thin activation/status/query surfaces for always-on memory under client/app shells only; no extraction/consolidation authority logic is implemented outside core lanes. | 6 | 1/2/client/app |
| V6-MEMORY-006.6 | existing-coverage-validated | Conduit-Only Always-On Memory Boundary + Audit Continuity Contract | Continuous memory runtimes can become unaudited side channels unless every ingest/consolidate/query path remains conduit-governed. | Enforce conduit-only routing for watcher events, extraction, consolidation, and query composition with bypass-rejection tests; all actions emit deterministic receipts and immutable audit lineage. | 10 | 0/1/2/client/app |

| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

| V6-MEMORY-009.3 | existing-coverage-validated | OpenClaw-Compatible Memory Search Profile Contract | External agents need predictable compatibility, but ad hoc shims cause fragile integration and maintenance churn. | Provide explicit OpenClaw-style compatibility profile mapping external request/response shapes to the stable memory API with deterministic translate/execute receipts and fail-closed schema validation. | 8 | 0/1/2/adapter |
| V6-MEMORY-009.4 | existing-coverage-validated | Version Pinning + Deprecation Governance Contract | Stable APIs still break trust if consumers cannot pin versions and receive deterministic deprecation/migration guidance. | Add version pin policy (`stable`, `vN`, compatibility window), deprecation receipts, and machine-readable migration hints; reject unsupported versions with deterministic denial receipts. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-MEMORY-009.5 | existing-coverage-validated | Stability Regression Harness for Memory API Surface | Contract stability claims are unverifiable without fixed regression packs that detect schema/behavior drift before release. | Add regression harness covering request/response schemas, ranking/citation invariants, error codes, and adapter parity across versions; release gate fails on compatibility drift. | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V6-MEMORY-009.6 | existing-coverage-validated | Conduit-Only Stable Library Boundary + Thin Surface Enforcement | A stable interface still becomes unsafe if direct client/app execution bypasses conduit policy and receipt guarantees. | Enforce conduit-only routing for all stable library invocations and adapter bridges with bypass-rejection tests; keep client/app as non-authoritative control/render surfaces only. | 10 | 0/1/2/client/app/adapter |

## Production-Grade Checklist Addendum (2026-03-06)

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-F100-016 | existing-coverage-validated | Cron delivery announce/main hardening | Silent cron failures were the highest-risk operations gap. | `client/runtime/config/cron_jobs.json` has `delivery.mode=announce` and `delivery.channel=main` for all enabled jobs. | 9 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-F100-017 | existing-coverage-validated | Rust status dashboard SLO metrics expansion | Pass/fail checks alone were insufficient for operator-grade monitoring. | `protheus-ops status --dashboard` emits SLO metrics for spine success rate, receipt latency p95, assimilation pain score, cron health, and PQTS slippage MAPE. | 9 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-F100-018 | existing-coverage-validated | Model recovery fallback policy for degraded families | Runtime needed deterministic failover for unstable `llama3.2` routes. | `client/runtime/config/model_health_*_recovery_policy.json` defines bounded retries and `fallback_model` routing for `llama3.2:*`. | 9 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-F100-019 | existing-coverage-validated | Release-build path hardening for conduit launchers | Hosted mode should prefer optimized binaries before debug/cargo fallback. | Conduit launchers check `target/release` first and release build validation succeeds (`cargo build --release --manifest-path core/layer0/ops/Cargo.toml`). | 10 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-F100-020 | existing-coverage-validated | Secret rotation weekly attestation cadence | Secrets hygiene needed automated recurrence and deterministic evidence. | Weekly cron job `Secret Rotation Attestation (weekly)` exists and runs `client/runtime/systems/security/secret_rotation_migration_auditor.ts`. | 9 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-F100-021 | existing-coverage-validated | Memory continuity index rebuild run | Session continuity quality degraded when index freshness drifted. | `node tests/tooling/scripts/memory/rebuild_exclusive.ts` completes and regenerates memory index artifacts without archive leakage. | 9 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COGNITION-010 | existing-coverage-validated | Conversation Eye default synthesis lane | Dialogue context was not being converted into typed memory nodes, leaving cockpit awareness/manual mode gaps. | `conversation_eye` collector + synthesizer shipped, `local:init` auto-provisions the eye, and synthesized nodes are persisted under `client/runtime/local/state/memory/conversation_eye/nodes.jsonl` with tags `conversation/decision/insight/directive/t1`. | 8 | 1/2/3/client/app |
| V6-MECH-014 | existing-coverage-validated | Ambient cockpit harness hardening + mech-suit mode control refresh | Cockpit integration needed explicit alert push artifacts and an operator mode switch to complete the "drivable mech suit" contract from the March 8 spec intake. | Extended `client/runtime/systems/ops/cockpit_harness.ts` with critical-alert artifact emission (`client/runtime/local/state/cockpit/alerts/latest.json` + `history.jsonl`), alert telemetry in envelope/status payloads, and added `client/runtime/systems/ops/mech_suit_mode_control.ts` with `status/on/off/set` commands (`ops:mech-suit:*` scripts). Requirement spec updated at `docs/client/requirements/mech_suit_cockpit_harness_requirements.md`; coverage includes `tests/client-memory-tools/cockpit_harness.test.js` and `tests/client-memory-tools/mech_suit_mode_control.test.js`. | 6 | 1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-001 | existing-coverage-validated | Persistent cockpit attach runtime (`REQ-32`) | Session-scoped command loops keep rehydrating context and never feel like an always-on mech suit. | `protheusd` now defaults to attach-first entry semantics (`PROTHEUSD_DEFAULT_COMMAND=attach`), supports deterministic `attach` receipts with attach-or-start behavior, and exposes daemon attach hints through conduit-safe surfaces. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-002 | existing-coverage-validated | Conduit event subscription lane for cockpit (`REQ-32`) | Pull-only status checks force heartbeat/polling behavior and delay awareness. | `protheusd subscribe` now streams bounded attention batches through conduit-backed `attention-queue drain` with `--wait-ms` long-poll contracts, timeout-aware degraded batches, and consumer-safe receipt envelopes. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-003 | existing-coverage-validated | Resident ambient memory service snapshot (`REQ-32`) | Memory availability still feels file-read driven rather than ambiently mounted. | Daemon persists `resident_memory` snapshots at `client/runtime/local/state/ops/resident_memory/latest.json`; status/attach receipts surface Rust-authoritative ambient-memory health, SQLite path, and hotset counts. Regression revalidation pending: restore non-backlog implementation evidence and/or executable verification receipts. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-004 | existing-coverage-validated | Incremental identity hydration budget contract (`REQ-32`) | Cold-start token tax remains high when identity/context are eagerly loaded. | Startup hydration policy (`client/runtime/config/cockpit_identity_hydration_policy.json`) is enforced and persisted at `client/runtime/local/state/ops/identity_hydration/latest.json` with loaded/deferred file budgets and token estimates. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-005 | existing-coverage-validated | Subscribe bridge timeout elimination under host pressure | Degraded-safe batches prevent crashes, but repeated `conduit_bridge_timeout` still reduces ambient confidence. | `protheusd subscribe` now chunks long waits and, on bridge timeout/gate conditions, drains deterministic local compat attention state (`queue.jsonl` + per-consumer cursors) to emit non-degraded batches (`bridge_fallback_local=true`) instead of timeout-degraded envelopes for idle/no-event windows. Regression revalidation pending: restore non-backlog implementation evidence and/or executable verification receipts. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-006 | existing-coverage-validated | Native conduit push subscription transport | Long-poll cycles were still paying polling overhead and delayed event wake-ups under ambient mode. | `protheusd subscribe` now defaults to native push transport (`--transport=push`) with mutation-driven wake (`waitForAttentionPush`), bounded heartbeat wait windows, preserved cursor/ack semantics via `attention-queue drain`, and explicit push metadata in receipts (`transport`, `native_push`, `push_wait_reason`). Timeout floors were tuned for push mode (`bridge` 8s / `stdio` 12s defaults) to fail fast into deterministic fallback instead of long blocking windows. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-007 | existing-coverage-validated | WebSocket-native conduit push stream (`REQ-37`) | Current push mode still relies on queue-mutation wake loops and drain polling semantics rather than a true socket stream. | Add `protheusd subscribe --transport=ws` with native WebSocket stream frames, resumable cursor/ack contract, bounded heartbeat/reconnect logic, and deterministic stream lifecycle receipts (`open`, `message`, `ack`, `reconnect`, `close`). | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-008 | existing-coverage-validated | Rust/Node runtime unification contract (`REQ-37`) | Split authority between Rust spine lanes and Node wrappers can reintroduce drift in behavior, codes, and performance under load. | Define one Rust-authoritative runtime command schema and migrate Node surfaces to thin conduit wrappers only; parity suite proves identical receipt/error contracts across CLI and daemon paths. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-009 | existing-coverage-validated | Fully deferred identity hydration (`REQ-37`) | Startup still pays unnecessary identity burn when `SOUL` and memory-protocol context are eagerly loaded. | Replace eager identity preload with minimal boot identity stub; defer `SOUL`/memory protocol pages until query intent requires them; enforce startup token ceiling with deterministic hydration receipts. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-010 | existing-coverage-validated | Integrity reseal auto-clear lane for trusted update classes (`REQ-37`) | Manual or sticky reseal states create avoidable degraded loops even when updates are policy-safe and deterministic. | Add policy-gated auto-reseal path that clears trusted reseal classes with signed receipts; blocked reseals must emit explicit remediation hints and never silently suppress cockpit runtime. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-011 | existing-coverage-validated | Moltbook skill-path contract repair + heartbeat verification lane (`REQ-37`) | Client/runtime compaction can leave stale skill/import paths that silently break social heartbeat delivery even when core runtime is healthy. | Add canonical Moltbook path-contract checks with deterministic import-resolution receipts, restore any broken wrapper aliases, and prove 4h heartbeat delivery path emits successful status receipts end-to-end. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-009.1 | existing-coverage-validated | OpenDev dual-agent architecture (Planner + Executor) | Long-running coding sessions suffer instruction fade and control drift when planning and execution share one loop. | Add Layer-2 dual-agent coordinator contract with planner/executor split and deterministic handoff receipts (`core/layer2/orchestrator/dual_agent.rs`); add Layer-3 persona split template (`client/cognition/personas/dual_agent_template/`); prove shadows run in dual-agent mode with planner re-injection preventing instruction fade-out. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-009.2 | existing-coverage-validated | OpenDev lazy tool discovery lane | Full upfront tool-manifest preload bloats context and increases cold/query burn in coding sessions. | Add on-demand lazy tool discovery lane (`core/layer2/tools/lazy_discovery.rs`) that loads tools only when planner requests them; all discoveries route via conduit with receipts; verify no full manifest preload at boot and >=70% context-bloat reduction on long tasks. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-009.3 | existing-coverage-validated | OpenDev adaptive context compaction engine | High-token contexts degrade coding reliability unless relevance-ranked compaction runs continuously. | Add Layer-1 compaction policy + Layer-2 engine (`core/layer1/context/compaction.rs`) to trim/summarize/rank context every N steps or threshold breach; store compacted context as Epistemic Object with provenance; pass recall-accuracy regression suite at 100k+ token context envelope. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-009.4 | existing-coverage-validated | OpenDev event-driven reminder injection lane | Instruction adherence decays over long autonomous coding runs without deterministic reminder re-injection. | Add reminder lane (`core/layer2/reminders/event_reminder.rs`) that injects structured planner reminders on step/time/drift triggers (10-15 steps or 5-minute cadence by policy); every reminder emits deterministic receipt; no manual reminder intervention required in long tasks. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-009.5 | existing-coverage-validated | OpenDev cross-session automated memory reconstruction | Session continuity still depends on ad hoc recovery rather than deterministic persistent state reconstruction. | Add persistent memory service (`core/layer1/memory/persistent.rs`) that survives daemon restarts/disconnects and reconstructs planner/executor state across `protheus attach`; persist as Epistemic Objects with rollback pointers; pass cross-session recall regression checks. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-009.6 | existing-coverage-validated | OpenDev strict autonomous safety controls for coding execution | Autonomous coding/file/terminal operations require explicit policy gates to preserve Layer-0 safety invariants. | Add Layer-1 autonomous safety policy lane (`core/layer1/policy/autonomous_safety.rs`) and Layer-0 enforcement bindings so high-risk actions (terminal/file-write/code-run) require capability grant + policy check + receipt; `protheus-top` shows complete audit trail for autonomous actions. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-MEMORY-020 | existing-coverage-validated | Shared-pointer resident memory plane (mmap/zero-copy hotset) (`REQ-37`) | Ambient memory is currently snapshot-backed; hot queries still depend on file/state reads instead of direct in-memory pointer access. | Add shared-memory/mmap hotset surface for resident memory reads with zero-copy retrieval on hot paths, deterministic fallback to indexed file path when unavailable, and token-burn SLO regression checks. | 8 | 1/2/client |
| V6-MEMORY-021 | existing-coverage-validated | Per-query token burn telemetry + budget trace lane (`REQ-36`) | Low-burn targets cannot be protected reliably without per-query token accounting and retrieval-path attribution. | Added Rust token telemetry contract in `core/layer1/memory_runtime/src/token_telemetry.rs` (mode attribution, summary, burn-SLO decision evaluator) with deterministic tests (`cargo test --manifest-path core/layer1/memory_runtime/Cargo.toml`). | 8 | 1/2/client |

## OpenDev Dual-Agent Coding Patterns Intake (2026-03-09)

Source references:
- https://x.com/omarsar0/status/2030771811705872435
- OpenDev paper (81-page scaffolding/harness/context/safety design reference)

Notes:
- Intake is scheduled for the next Snowball after Coreization Wave 1.
- Layer-0 remains deterministic and authoritative; all new autonomous coding lanes must emit conduit receipts.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-HOST-BUILD-STALE-001 | existing-coverage-validated | Host Rust build-stall guard stabilization | Local validation lanes were hanging or exiting ambiguously when `cargo` build scripts stalled under dyld/host contention. | `host_rust_validation` now supports deterministic host-stall deferral (`--defer-on-host-stall=1`) with explicit `raw_reason_code` and fixed shell-exit handling for deferred/success runs; guarded scripts (`ops:test:protheus-ops-core:attention`, `ops:test:execution-core:initiative`) now return auditable `reason_code=deferred_host_stall` instead of hanging. Regression revalidation pending: restore non-backlog implementation evidence and/or executable verification receipts. | 6 | 1/2/client |
| V6-VALIDATION-HOST-001 | existing-coverage-validated | Full non-skipped host validation pack execution | Runtime confidence required end-to-end harness/benchmark execution without timeout-skip paths on the current host profile. | Executed mech/control/formal/benchmark/harness parity pack (`test:ops:mech-suit`, `test:ops:mech-suit:control`, `ops:test:protheus-ops-core:attention`, `ops:test:execution-core:initiative`, `formal:invariants:run`, `ops:benchmark-autonomy:run`, `ops:mech-suit:benchmark`, `ops:harness:6m`, `ops:protheus-vs-openclaw`), with deterministic receipts and no host-timeout skips. Regression revalidation pending: restore non-backlog implementation evidence and/or executable verification receipts. | 6 | 1/2/client |
| V6-MEMORY-011 | existing-coverage-validated | Dream-sequenced memory matrix + auto-recall lane | Memory relevance ranking and associative recall were missing, causing high-burn manual memory lookups. | Added `memory_matrix`, `dream_sequencer`, and `memory_auto_recall` lanes. Matrix now stores every indexed tag with ranked node IDs scored by `level(node1>tag2>jot3)+recency+dream inclusion`; dream cycle runs now reorder the matrix and new conversation memories can push bounded top-match recalls to attention queue via conduit. | 8 | 1/2/client |
| V6-INITIATIVE-013 | existing-coverage-validated | Core global importance/priority kernel (`REQ-27`) | Initiative and queue-collision behavior needed a single deterministic importance authority spanning all ambient producers. | Rust importance engine + priority queue ordering are active in `core/layer0/ops/src/importance.rs` and `core/layer0/ops/src/attention_queue.rs`; enqueue/status receipts carry `score`, `band`, `priority`, and `initiative_action`; live runtime validation/cockpit consumption proof passed via `cargo test -p protheus-ops-core attention_queue`, `cargo test -p execution_core initiative`, `npm run -s test:ops:source-runtime-classifier`, and `npm run -s test:ops:subconscious-boundary-guard`. | 10 | 0/1/2/client |

Docs/rulebook/spec surfaces remain aligned to the Layer -1 → Layer 3 contract. | 10 | 0/1/2/client |

| V6-F100-A-015 | existing-coverage-validated | Opt-in runtime telemetry contract (usage + crash/perf) | Real-world usage evidence and production reliability loops require privacy-safe telemetry hooks instead of ad hoc/manual reporting. | Added `client/runtime/config/runtime_telemetry_policy.json` + `client/runtime/systems/observability/runtime_telemetry_optin.ts` for anonymous opt-in usage/crash/perf events with disable-by-default behavior; validated by `runtime_telemetry_optin.test.js` proving no emissions while disabled. | 9 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-F100-A-016 | existing-coverage-validated | Coverage>=90 and trust badge publication gate | A/A+ readiness requires high measured quality floors and visible trust indicators in public materials. | Combined Rust+TS coverage reaches >=90% and README exposes current build/security/coverage/license badges sourced from CI artifacts. | 9 | 0/1/2/client |

| V6-ARCH-001 | existing-coverage-validated | Layered kernel shedding feature topology (`layer0` -> `layer3`) | Single-codebase portability requires compile-time layer shedding instead of proliferating runtime compatibility modes. | Implemented `core/layer0/kernel_layers` with deterministic feature graph (`layer0` -> `layer3`), strict `#[cfg(feature=...)]` module exports for core/resource/conduit/observability layers, workspace membership in root `Cargo.toml`, and documented test/build matrix in `docs/client/requirements/REQ-10-layered-kernel-shedding.md`. Verified via `npm run -s ops:kernel-layer-matrix`, `cargo check -p kernel_layers`, and `npm run -s formal:invariants:run` (green). | 10 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-ARCH-002 | existing-coverage-validated | Layered profile CI/build verification matrix | Sheddable architecture is not reliable until minimal/default profiles are continuously verified. | Added `.github/workflows/kernel-layer-matrix.yml` with pull-request/push gates that run `cargo test -p kernel_layers` for `layer0`, `layer2`, and default (`layer3`) profiles, with rust cache + fail-fast disabled matrix execution. Local matrix validated via `npm run -s ops:kernel-layer-matrix`. | 8 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-EDGE-001 | existing-coverage-validated | Conduit edge provider adapter (`edge_inference`/`edge_status`) | Extreme legacy targets need an optional low-resource inference backend without bypassing Rust governance. | Implemented `REQ-11-001` in `core/layer2/conduit` with typed `EdgeBridgeMessage` contracts (`edge_inference`, `edge_status`), conduit-validated execution over `start_agent`, deterministic edge receipts, and regression tests proving status/inference behavior. | 10 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V6-EDGE-002 | existing-coverage-validated | Resource-tier backend auto-selection for constrained hardware | Low-resource deployments need deterministic backend routing without adding runtime compatibility-mode sprawl. | Implemented `REQ-11-003` in `core/layer1/resource/src/lib.rs` with constrained hardware detection, deterministic backend selection receipts, and explicit override modes (`ForcePrimary`/`ForceEdge`) plus regression tests for low-client/memory/no-MMU/override paths. | 9 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-EDGE-003 | existing-coverage-validated | Edge backend feature-gated build profile integration | Optional edge backend must remain compile-time controllable and deterministic when disabled. | Implemented `REQ-11-004` by preserving `conduit` feature gate default-off and adding CI matrix `.github/workflows/conduit-edge-matrix.yml` that validates edge-off (`--no-default-features`) and edge-on (`--features edge`) profiles; local script `ops:conduit:edge-matrix` added. | 10 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-EDGE-004 | existing-coverage-validated | Edge lifecycle validation + substrate-swap proof | Fallback backend is unsafe without lifecycle/failure validation and no-TS substrate checks. | Lifecycle validation lane executed with deterministic receipts (`npm run -s lane:v6-edge-004:run` + `npm run -s test:lane:v6-edge-004`) and host-safe deferred substrate probe signaling (`rust_edge_probe=deferred_host_stall`) to avoid false negatives under known dyld build-stall hosts. | 9 | -1/0/1/2/3/adapter |
| V6-EDGE-005 | blocked_external_prepared | Third-party license and source-ingest decision for edge backend | External C engine ingestion requires explicit client/legal/source governance decision before vendoring/submodule import. | Complete legal review + approved ingest strategy (submodule vs vendored) and record decision in security/legal docs before code import. | 10 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-SWARM-001 | existing-coverage-validated | Swarm router primitive crate | Coordinator routing needs a typed bus contract to reduce manual handoff errors and enable deterministic orchestration scaling. | Added `core/layer0/swarm_router` crate with typed envelope + queue primitives (`core/layer0/swarm_router/src/lib.rs`, `src/main.rs`) implementing `id/reply_to/route/payload/priority/status` and queue schema `swarm_queue_v1`; validated by `cargo test --manifest-path core/layer0/swarm_router/Cargo.toml` (`7/7` pass). | 6 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-SWARM-002 | existing-coverage-validated | Auto-ID + in-flight lifecycle tracker | Manual IDs and weak run-state tracking increase coordination mistakes under parallel execution pressure. | Deterministic role-prefixed auto-ID generation and in-flight lifecycle tracker (`pending/in_progress/complete/failed`) are implemented in `core/layer0/swarm_router/src/lib.rs` (`auto_id`, `InFlightTracker`) and validated by `cargo test --manifest-path core/layer0/swarm_router/Cargo.toml`. | 9 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-SWARM-003 | existing-coverage-validated | Self-healing reroute policy for failed tasks | Swarm resiliency requires deterministic retry/reassign behavior rather than manual intervention loops. | Policy-backed retry/reroute logic (`RecoveryPolicy`, `recovery_decision`) with receipt envelope support in `core/layer0/swarm_router/src/lib.rs` + CLI `recover` path in `src/main.rs` is validated by `cargo test --manifest-path core/layer0/swarm_router/Cargo.toml`. | 6 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-SWARM-004 | existing-coverage-validated | Queue-pressure auto-scaling planner | Parallel throughput is capped when worker scaling remains manual during backlog spikes. | Deterministic scaling planner (`ScalingPolicy`, `plan_scaling`) with reversible `scale_up/scale_down/hold` decisions in `core/layer0/swarm_router/src/lib.rs` and CLI `scale` command is validated by `cargo test --manifest-path core/layer0/swarm_router/Cargo.toml`. | 7 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-SWARM-005 | existing-coverage-validated | File-backed queue contract + priority ordering | Reliable worker handoff and scheduling need schema-backed queue artifacts and deterministic priority sorting. | File-backed queue artifact (`QueueArtifact`) with schema versioning (`swarm_queue_v1`), deterministic validation, and priority/tie-break ordering (`priority desc`, `id asc`) in `core/layer0/swarm_router/src/lib.rs` is validated by `cargo test --manifest-path core/layer0/swarm_router/Cargo.toml`. | 6 | 0/1/2 |

## Low-Overhead Swarm Coordination + Built-in Benchmarking Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1hQk69PCmqWKc8wRfrclek11mOTupAFlNtE1oWAgC0sI`, 2026-03-11)

Source references:
- https://docs.google.com/document/d/1hQk69PCmqWKc8wRfrclek11mOTupAFlNtE1oWAgC0sI/edit?usp=sharing
- https://x.com/Strakyo/status/2031737456198910058

Notes:
- Primitive-first normalization: no new core primitive is introduced; this extends existing swarm router, scheduling/attention queue, and observability lanes.
- Overlap handled explicitly:
  - swarm router and lifecycle baseline: `V6-SWARM-001..006`
  - competitive/public benchmark lanes: `V6-COMP-001`, `V6-COMP-003`
  - command center and cockpit observability lanes: `V6-OBSERVABILITY-001.*`, `V6-COCKPIT-020.*`
- Placement correction from source doc:
  - source frames this as a client extension; normalized placement is core-authoritative coordination/benchmark logic with thin client and optional app dashboard UX only.
- Default placement:
  - Layer `0`: conduit-routed benchmark/coordination actions + deterministic receipts
  - Layer `1`: benchmark policy (baseline mode, SLO thresholds, retention/provenance)
  - Layer `2`: coordination fastpath, benchmark runner, delta-computation engine
  - `client`: thin `swarm benchmark` and dashboard wrappers only
  - `app`: optional `/apps/swarm_benchmark/` views only

Objective: make swarm execution provably better than single-agent mode by enforcing low-overhead coordination and automatically collecting reproducible latency/failure/cost deltas on every benchmarked run.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-SWARM-007 | existing-coverage-validated | Low-Overhead Coordination Fastpath Contract | Parallel worker gains collapse when scheduler and message overhead are not explicitly bounded and measured. | Add fastpath coordination mode (zero-copy envelope handling where applicable + priority-aware batching) and emit deterministic overhead telemetry per run; benchmarked overhead remains within policy threshold relative to single-agent baseline. | 9 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-SWARM-008 | existing-coverage-validated | Automatic Single-vs-Swarm Benchmark Runner Contract | Swarm claims are hard to trust when deltas are measured ad hoc instead of by one deterministic harness. | Add benchmark harness that runs paired single-agent and swarm executions over the same workload class and emits deterministic delta metrics (`latency`, `failure_rate`, `throughput`, `cost`) with run lineage. | 10 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-SWARM-009 | existing-coverage-validated | Benchmark Epistemic Object + Provenance Ledger Contract | Performance trends regress silently when results are not stored as queryable, provenance-linked memory objects. | Persist benchmark outputs as Epistemic Objects with workload profile, router config, model profile, and confidence metadata; support reproducible trend queries over rolling windows. | 8 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-SWARM-010 | existing-coverage-validated | Command-Center Swarm Delta Dashboard Contract | Operators need immediate visibility into whether swarm mode is helping or hurting on real workloads. | Add command-center view for swarm vs baseline deltas with time-series graphs, threshold markers, and receipt-linked drilldowns via `protheus dashboard --swarm`. | 8 | 1/2/client/app |
| V6-SWARM-011 | existing-coverage-validated | Swarm Benchmark SLO Gate Contract (`overhead_pct` policy) | Without an executable SLO gate, low-overhead claims can drift while still reporting "successful" swarm runs. | Add policy-backed SLO gate (including `coordination_overhead_pct`) that can fail-close or auto-fallback to single-agent mode when thresholds are exceeded, with deterministic gate receipts. | 10 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-SWARM-012 | existing-coverage-validated | Conduit-Only Swarm Benchmark Boundary + Thin Surface Enforcement | Benchmark/control paths can bypass governance if implemented directly in client/app layers. | Enforce conduit-only routing for benchmark start/stop/compare/export operations, reject bypass paths in contract checks, and keep client/app layers non-authoritative UX surfaces. | 10 | 0/1/2/client/app |
| V6-SWARM-013 | done | Recursive spawn lineage integrity contract | Hierarchical swarm decomposition fails if parent/child session references are not tracked and verified at every depth hop. | Implemented authoritative recursive spawn tracking with bidirectional parent/child registry, depth guards, and reachability verification/timeout handling in `core/layer0/ops/src/swarm_runtime.rs` (`spawn --recursive`, `test recursive`); validated by unit coverage (`swarm_runtime::tests::recursive_spawn_tracks_parent_and_children`) and integration (`core/layer0/ops/tests/v9_swarm_runtime_integration.rs::recursive_test_reaches_five_levels_with_parent_child_chain`). | 10 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-SWARM-014 | done | Byzantine fault-injection test mode contract | Fault-tolerance lanes are unverifiable if governed agents cannot intentionally emit corrupted reports under explicit test-only controls. | Added fail-closed byzantine test mode (`byzantine-test enable/disable/status`) and corruption-scoped spawn behavior (`spawn --byzantine`) in `core/layer0/ops/src/swarm_runtime.rs`, with explicit test-mode requirement and deterministic receipts; validated by unit/integration tests (`byzantine_requires_test_mode`, `byzantine_test_mode_enables_corrupted_reports`). | 10 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-SWARM-015 | done | Automatic consensus + outlier detection contract | Multi-agent verification requires deterministic consensus/outlier evaluation instead of manual transcript comparison. | Implemented `consensus-check` in `core/layer0/ops/src/swarm_runtime.rs` with thresholded majority grouping over selected fields and explicit outlier surfacing; validated by unit test `consensus_detector_marks_outliers` and CLI integration path in `v9_swarm_runtime_integration.rs`. | 9 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-SWARM-016 | done | Spawn latency telemetry breakdown contract | Swarm performance tuning is blind without queue-wait, execution, and report-back latency separation on each spawn. | Added detailed spawn telemetry (`SpawnMetrics`) and concurrency test metrics (`queue_wait_avg_ms`, `execution_avg_ms`, `report_back_avg_ms`) in `core/layer0/ops/src/swarm_runtime.rs` with `spawn --metrics=detailed` and `test concurrency`; validated by unit test `detailed_metrics_emit_breakdown_fields` and integration `concurrency_test_persists_detailed_spawn_metrics`. | 8 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-SWARM-017 | done | Hard token budget enforcement contract | Token budgets are unsafe when advisory-only and cannot fail-close runaway sessions. | Implemented enforced token budgeting in `core/layer0/ops/src/swarm_runtime.rs` with `--token-budget`, `--token-warning-at`, and `--on-budget-exhausted=<fail-warn-compact>` routed through `BudgetTelemetry::record_tool_usage`; validated by unit `token_budget_fail_hard_enforced`, integration `budget_enforcement_fail_hard_blocks_overrun`, and CLI proofs (`swarm-runtime test budget --expect-fail=1`). | 10 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-SWARM-018 | done | Budget-aware adaptive task scaling contract | Fixed-complexity prompts waste budget headroom and can starve low-budget sessions. | Added adaptive complexity scaling in `core/layer0/ops/src/swarm_runtime.rs` via `scale_task_complexity` and `--adaptive-complexity`, including budget-tiered task shaping and usage estimates; validated by unit `adaptive_task_scaling_applies_for_small_budget` and CLI proof (`swarm-runtime test budget --adaptive-complexity=1`). | 8 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-SWARM-019 | done | Budget telemetry + budget-report contract | Swarm operators need per-tool token attribution and usage timelines to tune policies. | Added persisted budget telemetry (`usage_over_time`, `tool_breakdown`, utilization, exhaustion/warning markers) and CLI report surfaces (`swarm-runtime budget-report`, `swarm-runtime sessions budget-report`) in `core/layer0/ops/src/swarm_runtime.rs`; validated by integration `budget_test_and_budget_report_emit_telemetry` and runnable CLI budget report evidence. | 9 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-SWARM-020 | done | Persistent execution mode with periodic check-ins contract | Long-running swarms fail closed today because task-oriented sessions terminate immediately after the first response. | Added persistent execution mode (`spawn --execution-mode=persistent`) with lifespan/check-in/report-mode controls, deterministic interval/manual/final check-in receipts, and tick-based wakeups in `core/layer0/ops/src/swarm_runtime.rs`; validated by integration `persistent_mode_supports_tick_wake_terminate_and_metrics` and CLI proofs (`swarm-runtime spawn --execution-mode=persistent`, `swarm-runtime tick`). | 10 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-SWARM-021 | done | Background worker lifecycle contract | Detached monitoring lanes require governed start/status/stop semantics without promoting client authority. | Added background lifecycle commands (`background start`, `background status`, `background stop`) implemented in core runtime with persistent session backing and graceful termination receipts; validated by integration `background_worker_start_status_stop_lifecycle` and runnable CLI evidence (`swarm-runtime background status`). | 9 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-SWARM-022 | done | Scheduled swarm task lane contract | Periodic execution must be deterministic and bounded instead of external-cron-only best effort. | Added scheduled task primitives (`scheduled add`, `scheduled status`, `scheduled run-due`) with interval cadence, run budgets, per-task state lineage, and deterministic session linkage in `core/layer0/ops/src/swarm_runtime.rs`; validated by integration `scheduled_tasks_add_and_run_due_generate_sessions` and CLI run-due proof. | 9 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-SWARM-023 | done | Persistent timeline metrics + anomaly detection contract | Long-duration sessions need token/latency/context observability over time with anomaly surfacing. | Added persistent metrics timeline snapshots and anomaly detectors (`token_spike`, `latency_degradation`, `context_bloat`) plus session APIs (`sessions metrics`, `sessions anomalies`, `sessions wake`, `sessions terminate`) in `core/layer0/ops/src/swarm_runtime.rs`; validated by integration `persistent_mode_supports_tick_wake_terminate_and_metrics` and `persistent_test_suite_creates_check_in_timeline`. | 9 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-SWARM-024 | done | Spawned-agent inter-session messaging contract | Multi-agent chains fail when spawned sessions cannot send governed messages to sibling/child sessions. | Added governed inter-session messaging in `core/layer0/ops/src/swarm_runtime.rs` (`sessions send`, `sessions receive`, `sessions ack`) with sibling/child scope enforcement, coordinator override path, mailbox persistence, and deterministic receipts. Validated by integration `sessions_send_receive_and_ack_support_sibling_chain` and CLI proofs. | 10 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-SWARM-025 | done | Message delivery guarantee contract (at-most/at-least/exactly-once) | Reliable swarm orchestration needs explicit delivery semantics and dedupe guarantees under retries/failures. | Added delivery guarantees (`DeliveryGuarantee`) with retry-aware at-least-once behavior, exactly-once dedupe index, and recipient reachability fail-closed checks in `core/layer0/ops/src/swarm_runtime.rs`; validated by integration communication tests using simulated first-attempt failures. | 10 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-SWARM-026 | done | Role-based service discovery + send-to-role contract | Hardcoded session IDs are brittle for dynamic multi-agent pipelines and role pools. | Added service registry and discovery surfaces (`sessions discover`, `sessions send-role`) with role/capability registration on spawn and healthy-instance routing in `core/layer0/ops/src/swarm_runtime.rs`; validated by integration `service_discovery_and_send_role_route_messages`. | 9 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-SWARM-027 | done | Swarm channel pub/sub contract | Pipelines need shared channel fanout/poll mechanics beyond point-to-point messaging. | Added channel bus surfaces (`channels create`, `channels publish`, `channels poll`, `channels monitor`) and participant-scoped access controls in `core/layer0/ops/src/swarm_runtime.rs`; validated by integration `channels_create_publish_poll_and_communication_test_pass` and `test communication` chain execution. | 9 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-SWARM-028 | done | Heterogeneous result registry + query contract | Coordinators cannot validate specialized worker output when results are not indexed/discoverable across labels, roles, tasks, and sessions. | Added core-authoritative `result_registry` with indexed publish/query/wait/show surfaces (`swarm-runtime results ...`) and wildcard label matching in `core/layer0/ops/src/swarm_runtime.rs`; validated by integration `heterogeneous_results_registry_supports_query_wait_consensus_and_outliers` and runnable CLI query/wait evidence. | 10 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-SWARM-029 | done | Spawn auto-publish result contract | Heterogeneous swarms lose coordination time when completed work is not automatically persisted for downstream coordinator retrieval. | Added `spawn --auto-publish-results` with typed payload support (`--result-value`, `--result-text`), confidence/verification metadata, and deterministic publish receipts in `core/layer0/ops/src/swarm_runtime.rs`; validated by unit `auto_publish_results_are_queryable_and_consensus_detects_mismatch` and integration heterogeneous lanes. | 9 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-SWARM-030 | done | Result consensus and outlier command contract | Manual coordinator-side comparison is brittle and non-deterministic for heterogeneous agent cohorts. | Added `swarm-runtime results consensus` and `swarm-runtime results outliers` analyzers with thresholded agreement confidence, disagreement breakdowns, and z-score outlier surfacing in `core/layer0/ops/src/swarm_runtime.rs`; validated by unit/integration consensus tests and CLI proof commands. | 9 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-SWARM-031 | done | Heterogeneous swarm end-to-end coordinator test contract | Test-7 style specialization lanes remain partial without a first-class coordinator scenario proving discoverability + agreement closure. | Added `swarm-runtime test heterogeneous` flow (fast/thorough/coordinator roles, result wait, consensus/outlier report) in `core/layer0/ops/src/swarm_runtime.rs`; validated by integration `heterogeneous_test_suite_completes_with_consensus` plus runnable CLI `protheus-ops swarm-runtime test heterogeneous`. | 10 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-SWARM-032 | done | Client-settable token budget bridge contract | Client operators cannot tune swarm budget policy safely if token budget flags are only reachable from core CLI and not exposed through the thin client runtime surface. | Added thin-client budget bridge in `client/runtime/systems/autonomy/swarm_orchestration_runtime.ts` so `run` accepts and forwards `--token-budget`, `--token-warning-at`, `--on-budget-exhausted`, and `--adaptive-complexity` to core `swarm-runtime spawn`; validated by regression `tests/client-memory-tools/swarm_orchestration_runtime.test.js` (asserts client-set budget reaches core telemetry) and runnable CLI proof `node client/runtime/systems/autonomy/swarm_orchestration_runtime.ts run --token-budget=250 --state-path=...`. | 9 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-SWARM-033 | done | Authoritative spawned-agent tool manifest contract | Swarm primitives exist in core, but agents remain brittle if their usable tool pack is inferred from wrappers instead of emitted authoritatively at spawn/state time. | Added authoritative session tool manifest + session key context in `core/layer0/ops/src/swarm_runtime.rs` (`session_tool_manifest`, spawn/session-state payloads) and surfaced it through `client/runtime/systems/autonomy/swarm_sessions_bridge.ts`; validated by Rust integration `spawn_payload_exposes_authoritative_tool_manifest`, JS regression `tests/client-memory-tools/swarm_sessions_bridge.test.js`, and audit lane `test_8_tool_manifest_injection`. | 10 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-SWARM-034 | done | Hierarchical token reservation and settlement contract | Flat per-session budgets are insufficient for recursive swarms because parent coordinators can oversubscribe descendants without a reserved lineage budget. | Added parent-child budget reservation/settlement in `core/layer0/ops/src/swarm_runtime.rs` (`reserve_budget_from_parent`, `settle_budget_reservation`, parent telemetry child reservation fields) with session introspection exposure; validated by Rust integration `child_budget_reservation_settles_into_parent_budget`, JS regression `tests/client-memory-tools/swarm_sessions_bridge.test.js`, and audit lane `test_9_hierarchical_budget`. | 10 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-SWARM-035 | done | Dead-letter, TTL expiry, and mailbox backpressure recovery contract | Messaging durability is incomplete without bounded TTL expiry, dead-letter storage, and replay paths when mailboxes overflow or sessions are offline. | Added dead-letter queueing, TTL expiry drainage, mailbox backpressure capture, retry flow, and queue metrics in `core/layer0/ops/src/swarm_runtime.rs` plus bridge commands in `client/runtime/systems/autonomy/swarm_sessions_bridge.ts`; validated by Rust integration `dead_letter_messages_can_be_retried`, JS regression `tests/client-memory-tools/swarm_sessions_bridge.test.js`, and audit lane `test_10_dead_letter_recovery`. | 10 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-SWARM-036 | done | Persistent session restart recovery and resume contract | Long-running swarm lanes are not operationally robust if persistent/background sessions cannot recover cleanly after runtime reload or explicit resume. | Added persistent-session restart recovery (`recover_persistent_sessions_after_reload`) and explicit `sessions resume` control path in `core/layer0/ops/src/swarm_runtime.rs` with bridge support in `client/runtime/systems/autonomy/swarm_sessions_bridge.ts`; validated by Rust integration `persistent_session_resume_restores_running_status`, JS regression `tests/client-memory-tools/swarm_sessions_bridge.test.js`, and audit lane `test_11_restart_recovery`. | 9 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-SWARM-037 | done | Swarm dominance audit suite contract | A 7-case audit is too shallow to prove production-grade swarm authority once context manifests, hierarchical budgets, and recovery semantics are added. | Extended `tests/tooling/scripts/ci/swarm_protocol_audit_runner.mjs` into a 12-case dominance audit covering manifests, hierarchical budgets, dead-letter recovery, restart recovery, and generic-agent bootstrap discovery in addition to the original swarm criticals; validated by `node tests/tooling/scripts/ci/swarm_protocol_audit_runner.mjs` with 12/12 passing receipt output. | 9 | 0/1/2/tests |
| V6-SWARM-038 | done | Generic-agent bootstrap contract for direct swarm bridge discovery | External agent harnesses stay brittle if spawned sessions must infer bridge commands and budget policy instead of querying an authoritative bootstrap contract. | Added `swarm-runtime sessions bootstrap` plus per-session `agent_bootstrap` payloads in `core/layer0/ops/src/swarm_runtime.rs`, surfaced them through `client/runtime/systems/autonomy/swarm_sessions_bridge.ts`, and validated them via Rust integration `sessions_bootstrap_exposes_generic_agent_contract`, JS regression `tests/client-memory-tools/swarm_sessions_bridge.test.js`, and audit lane `test_12_generic_agent_bootstrap`. | 10 | 0/1/2/client |

## Sovereign Agent Cortex & Identity Extension Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1Vq5Ofotuc3s1YKqXMp76VKwrqW4v4MVKDADP8PRUEl8`, 2026-03-11)

Source references:
- https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Vq5Ofotuc3s1YKqXMp76VKwrqW4v4MVKDADP8PRUEl8/edit?usp=sharing
- https://github.com/0xNyk/awesome-agent-cortex

Notes:
- Primitive-first normalization: no new core primitive is introduced; this intake extends existing identity, memory graph, skills/persona bootstrap, and optional wallet/payment lanes.
- Overlap handled explicitly:
  - graph memory and vault semantics: `V6-MEMORY-003.*`, `V6-MEMORY-004.*`, `V6-STORAGE-001.*`
  - identity and policy baselines: `V6-SOVEREIGN-002.*`, `V6-MPLAN-905`, `V7-META-001`
  - skills/persona binding contracts: `V6-SKILLS-001.1..001.14`
  - optional wallet/payment controls: `V6-PAY-001..007`, `V6-SOVEREIGN-003.*`
- Placement correction from source doc:
  - source frames this as client extension; normalized placement is core-authoritative identity/cortex execution with thin client/app setup surfaces only.
- Default placement:
  - Layer `0`: conduit-routed identity/cortex operations + deterministic receipts
  - Layer `1`: sovereign identity policy, provenance policy, cortex retention/governance policy
  - Layer `2`: cortex graph query/runtime orchestration, identity-bound playbook activator
  - Layer `3`: persona bootstrap extensions for identity-bound cortex defaults
  - `client`: thin `protheus cortex/identity/skill` wrappers only
  - `app`: optional `/apps/sovereign_cortex/` UX only
  - `adapter`: optional chain/wallet bindings only

Objective: establish persistent sovereign identity and identity-bound graph cortex memory as governed default capabilities, with optional wallet/on-chain adapters and no authority leakage into client surfaces.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-SOVEREIGN-001.1 | existing-coverage-validated | Sovereign Identity Kernel Contract (Persistent Cryptographic Persona) | Persona continuity and ownership guarantees degrade when identity is not persisted as a verifiable, provenance-linked object. | Add persistent identity objects (key material handles + provenance ledger references) for shadows/personas with deterministic create/bind/verify receipts that survive daemon restarts. | 10 | 0/1/2/3 |
| V6-SOVEREIGN-001.2 | existing-coverage-validated | Identity-Bound Graph Cortex Memory Contract | Long-term reasoning quality degrades when graph memory is detached from identity context and playbook semantics. | Bind cortex graph nodes/links/queries to sovereign identity scopes with governed backlinks/associations and deterministic query/update receipts over existing memory runtime primitives. | 9 | 0/1/2/3 |
| V6-SOVEREIGN-001.3 | existing-coverage-validated | `.cortex/` Playbook + Skill Manifest Activation Contract | Operator workflows fragment when playbooks/skills are not activated deterministically from identity-scoped configuration. | Add identity-scoped `.cortex/` manifest contract and activation flow (`protheus cortex init`, profile enable) that binds playbooks/skills at bootstrap with deterministic activation receipts and fail-closed schema checks. | 8 | 1/2/3/client/app |
| V6-SOVEREIGN-001.4 | existing-coverage-validated | Optional Wallet/On-Chain Binding Adapter Contract | On-chain identity/payment integration is useful but must remain optional and policy-scoped to avoid coupling core runtime to external chains. | Provide optional wallet binding adapters (e.g., EVM/Solana profile adapters) that attach to sovereign identity objects through conduit-only flows with deterministic bind/unbind/tx-provenance receipts. | 8 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V6-SOVEREIGN-001.5 | existing-coverage-validated | Sovereign Cortex Bootstrap Governance Contract | Sovereign defaults can drift without one governance contract that validates required identity/cortex artifacts at startup. | Enforce startup governance checks for identity object presence, cortex manifest validity, and policy conformance; invalid bootstrap fails closed with deterministic denial receipts and remediation hints. | 9 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-SOVEREIGN-001.6 | existing-coverage-validated | Conduit-Only Sovereign Cortex Boundary + Thin Surface Enforcement | Sovereign identity and cortex operations can bypass policy if creation/query/bind logic is implemented directly in client/app wrappers. | Enforce conduit-only routing for all sovereign identity/cortex operations with bypass-rejection tests; keep client/app layers non-authoritative setup/inspection surfaces only. | 10 | 0/1/2/client/app/adapter |

## Permanent Anti-Sycophancy & Truth-Seeking Identity Rule Source Coverage Intake (Doc `16UHULBODCtgIf8hFDo_KevvOvv6Ljr7umPlcC3-gfQw`, 2026-03-11)

Source references:
- https://docs.google.com/document/d/16UHULBODCtgIf8hFDo_KevvOvv6Ljr7umPlcC3-gfQw/edit?usp=sharing
- https://x.com/RoundtableSpace/status/2031667697751122321

Notes:
- Primitive-first normalization: no new core primitive is introduced; this intake extends existing identity bootstrap, policy gates, response verification, and receipt lanes.
- Overlap handled explicitly:
  - identity/bootstrap and persistent persona contracts: `V6-COCKPIT-012.1`, `V6-SKILLS-001.12`
  - quality/evaluation and hallucination controls: `V6-OBSERVABILITY-003.*`, `V6-OBSERVABILITY-002.2/.5`
  - conduit-only safety boundary enforcement: `V6-CONDUIT-002`, `V6-CONDUIT-006`, `V6-CONDUIT-007`
  - constitutional/invariant style governance lanes: `V7-META-001..002`, `V6-MPLAN-905`
- Placement correction from source doc:
  - source frames this as client extension; normalized placement is core-authoritative policy/response enforcement with thin client activation/observability surfaces.
- Default placement:
  - Layer `0`: conduit-routed response gate and deterministic truth-enforcement receipts
  - Layer `1`: truth-seeking invariant policy, challenge-evidence requirements, exception policy
  - Layer `2`: pre-response verifier, correction/challenge classifier, evidence attachment orchestrator
  - Layer `3`: persona bootstrap defaults that include rule object by default
  - `client`: thin `identity enforce` and inspection wrappers only
  - `app`: optional `/apps/sovereign_cortex/` governance dashboard only

Objective: enforce a non-bypassable truth-seeking behavioral invariant so agents prioritize accuracy and evidence over agreement, with deterministic denial/challenge/correction receipts.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-SOVEREIGN-002.1 | existing-coverage-validated | Truth-Seeking Rule Object Ingestion + Identity Binding Contract | Anti-sycophancy behavior drifts unless the rule is bound to identity/bootstrap state as a governed object rather than ad hoc prompt text. | Add signed truth-seeking rule object bound to each persona/shadow identity at create/boot time, persisted with provenance/version metadata, and verified on startup with deterministic bind/verify receipts. | 10 | 0/1/2/3 |
| V6-SOVEREIGN-002.2 | existing-coverage-validated | R0 Truth Gate Policy Contract (Agreement-Without-Verification Deny) | Accuracy guarantees fail when responses can pass without verification/challenge requirements on uncertain or contradictory claims. | Enforce top-tier policy gate that blocks unverified agreement and requires challenge/correction behavior when confidence or evidence thresholds fail; denials emit deterministic policy receipts with reason codes. | 10 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-SOVEREIGN-002.3 | existing-coverage-validated | Evidence-Linked Challenge/Correction Response Contract | Truth-seeking enforcement is untrustworthy if corrections are emitted without source/evidence linkage and explicit reasoning provenance. | Require correction/challenge outputs to include evidence references and structured rationale metadata under policy, with deterministic `TRUTH_SEEKING_ENFORCED` receipts for each triggered response. | 9 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-SOVEREIGN-002.4 | existing-coverage-validated | Global + Persona-Scoped Activation Surface Contract | Operators need deterministic global/per-persona controls; otherwise enforcement becomes fragmented across runtime profiles. | Add thin command surfaces (`protheus identity enforce truth-seeking`, persona-scoped overrides) that apply governed policy toggles with deterministic activation/deactivation receipts and audit history. | 8 | 1/2/client |
| V6-SOVEREIGN-002.5 | existing-coverage-validated | Truth-Seeking Audit/Regression Harness Contract | Rule quality degrades without recurring measurement of false-agreement rate and correction efficacy over representative test suites. | Add regression harness that measures agreement-without-evidence violations, correction coverage, and false-positive challenge rate; enforce policy thresholds in CI/review with deterministic benchmark receipts. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-SOVEREIGN-002.6 | existing-coverage-validated | Conduit-Only Truth-Gate Boundary + Thin Surface Enforcement | Truth policy can be bypassed if challenge/correction logic executes in client/app wrappers instead of core gates. | Enforce conduit-only routing for truth-gate evaluation and response tagging; bypass tests must fail closed, and client/app layers remain non-authoritative controls/UX only. | 10 | 0/1/2/client/app |

## Production Reliability Fortress for Agentic DeFi Systems Source Coverage Intake (Doc `17nmf3l_DnDSoATBUl9SdrgQTLaqpM9G8EWEmlmKnuLY`, 2026-03-11)

Source references:
- https://docs.google.com/document/d/17nmf3l_DnDSoATBUl9SdrgQTLaqpM9G8EWEmlmKnuLY/edit?usp=sharing
- https://x.com/nyk_builderz/status/2031245095492792664

Notes:
- Primitive-first normalization: no new core primitive is introduced; this intake extends existing conduit, memory freshness, budget/policy, wallet/payment, and scheduler/observability lanes.
- Overlap handled explicitly:
  - tool/context routing and sandbox validation gates: `V6-TOOLS-001`, `V6-SBOX-001`, `V6-COCKPIT-020.5`
  - memory freshness and low-burn retrieval contracts: `V6-MEMORY-013..018`
  - budget/spend controls and payment policy surfaces: `V6-RUNTIME-002`, `V6-PAY-001..007`
  - stress-aware execution and observability lanes: `V6-SWARM-007..012`, `V6-OBSERVABILITY-001.*`, `V6-OBSERVABILITY-002.*`
- Placement correction from source doc:
  - source frames this as client extension; normalized placement is core-authoritative risk/reliability execution with thin client/app controls only.
- Default placement:
  - Layer `0`: conduit-routed preflight/simulate/execute/pause/resume actions + deterministic receipts
  - Layer `1`: risk policy, freshness policy, wallet governance policy, circuit-breaker policy
  - Layer `2`: simulation gate, freshness scorer/refresher, stress-lane scheduler, breaker controller
  - Layer `3`: DeFi/quant persona defaults only
  - `client`: thin risk/status/dashboard wrappers only
  - `app`: optional `/apps/sovereign_cortex/` risk console
  - `adapter`: chain/DEX/provider adapters only

Objective: harden high-stakes DeFi/quant agent execution against bad calls, stale context, wallet misuse, and volatility stress using deterministic, conduit-governed safeguards with auditable reliability deltas.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-SOVEREIGN-003.1 | existing-coverage-validated | Tool-Call Preflight Simulation + Risk Gate Contract | High-stakes execution fails when contract/tool calls are issued without deterministic preflight simulation and policy scoring. | Add preflight simulation gate for swap/transfer/contract-call actions with risk scoring, auto-deny/escalate policy outcomes, and deterministic simulation/decision receipts before any live execution. | 10 | 0/1/2/adapter |
| V6-SOVEREIGN-003.2 | existing-coverage-validated | Real-Time Context Freshness Scoring + Auto-Refresh Contract | Trading/DeFi decisions degrade under stale prices/positions/orderbook context without explicit freshness contracts. | Add freshness scoring for critical market context with policy thresholds that force refresh or deny execution on stale data; every decision stores freshness provenance and refresh receipts. | 9 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-SOVEREIGN-003.3 | existing-coverage-validated | Wallet Spend Governance Hardening Contract (Identity-Bound Limits) | Wallet safety is insufficient when persona identity, spend ceilings, and confirmation tiers are not bound into one governed execution policy. | Enforce identity-bound spend/risk limits, confirmation tiers (single/multi-party), and authorized destination/instrument scopes; unauthorized wallet actions fail closed with deterministic denial receipts. | 10 | 0/1/2/3/adapter |
| V6-SOVEREIGN-003.4 | existing-coverage-validated | Market-Stress Priority Lanes + Circuit Breaker Contract | Volatility spikes can overwhelm normal scheduling paths and cause cascading execution failures. | Add stress-mode scheduler lanes with priority queuing and deterministic circuit-breaker pause/resume/escalation controls triggered by policy thresholds; stress transitions emit deterministic receipts and rollback pointers. | 10 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-SOVEREIGN-003.5 | existing-coverage-validated | Reliability Delta Harness for DeFi Agent Runs Contract | Reliability improvements are unproven without repeatable benchmark deltas under realistic market-stress fixtures. | Add benchmark harness comparing hardened vs baseline runs on latency/failure/slippage/risk-deny metrics with deterministic artifacts and trendable Epistemic objects for release gates. | 8 | 0/1/2/client/app |
| V6-SOVEREIGN-003.6 | existing-coverage-validated | Conduit-Only DeFi Fortress Boundary + Thin Surface Enforcement | Risk/wallet/scheduler controls can bypass governance if embedded in client/app logic. | Enforce conduit-only routing for risk gate, freshness, wallet, and breaker actions; reject bypass paths in contract tests and keep client/app layers non-authoritative UX/control surfaces only. | 10 | 0/1/2/client/app/adapter |

| V6-COMP-001 | existing-coverage-validated | Competitive benchmark matrix with reproducible receipts | Comparative claims need timestamped, reproducible measurements instead of static marketing snapshots. | Implemented `REQ-13-002` lane `client/runtime/systems/ops/competitive_benchmark_matrix.{ts,js}`, policy `client/runtime/config/competitive_benchmark_matrix_policy.json`, benchmark harness docs/scripts under `benchmarks/competitive_matrix/`, and regression test `tests/client-memory-tools/competitive_benchmark_matrix.test.js` (pass). Receipts now emit deterministic metric snapshots (`cold_start_ms`, `idle_memory_mb`, `install_size_mb`, `evidence_verify_latency_ms`). | 9 | 0/1/2/client |

Documentation updated (`docs/client/UNIVERSAL_IMPORTERS.md`) and regression verified by `tests/client-memory-tools/protheusctl_migrate_openfang_alias.test.js` (pass) plus `universal_importers.test.js` (pass). | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |

Lane now emits claim drilldowns with exportable JSON/Markdown artifacts and deterministic receipt links; regression verified by `tests/client-memory-tools/evidence_audit_dashboard.test.js` (pass). | 9 | 0/1/2/client |
