| BL-001 | done | External temp-data backup pipeline (outside git) | Preserve high-churn runtime/state data without repo noise | `client/runtime/systems/ops/state_backup.ts` + `client/runtime/config/state_backup_policy.json`, optional daily spine hook (`STATE_BACKUP_ENABLED=1`), retention policy encoded | 6 | 1/2/client |
| BL-002 | done | Skill quarantine enforcement in all install paths | Prevent unreviewed skill install bypasses | Strict enforcer scans `client/runtime/systems/`, `client/cognition/habits/`, `client/cognition/skills/`, and `tests/tooling/scripts/memory/` (tests excluded), blocks direct installer patterns (shell + spawn/exec forms), and requires wrapper + quarantine structure to pass | 6 | 1/2/client |
| BL-003 | done | Autonomy receipt dashboard/summary | Make pass/fail verification visible without raw log digging | Daily summary command/report shows receipt pass rate + top failure reasons Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/backlog_runtime_anchors/bl_003_anchor.ts`. | 6 | 1/2/client |
| BL-013 | done | AGI security/governance hardening pack | Prevent capability-overhang failures as model capability increases | Dual-control approval for strategy mode escalation + non-bypass budget/risk caps + immutable policy/kernel verification + one-command emergency stop path tested Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/backlog_runtime_anchors/bl_013_anchor.ts`. | 6 | 1/2/client |
| BL-017 | done | Autonomous skill-add with necessity justification gate | Allow Protheus to add skills safely only when operationally justified | `client/runtime/systems/security/autonomous_skill_necessity_audit.ts` enforces necessity scoring for `--autonomous=1` installs using policy-defined structured justification (`problem`, `repeat_frequency`, `expected_time_or_token_savings`, `risk_class`, `why_existing_habits_or_skills_insufficient`); novelty-only reasons are blocked with receipts | 8 | 1/2/client |
| BL-036 | done | Strategic alignment oracle (weekly) | Keep unattended autonomy optimizing for T1 outcomes instead of infrastructure churn | `client/runtime/systems/autonomy/alignment_oracle.ts` writes weekly `alignment_score` artifacts (`state/autonomy/alignment_oracle/`), links to proposal/receipt evidence, emits deduped `autonomy_human_escalation` rows on low-streak escalation, and is executed by spine via `SPINE_ALIGNMENT_ORACLE_ENABLED` | 6 | 1/2/client |
| BL-037 | done | Exception novelty classifier + recovery policy | Prevent retry spirals and route genuinely new failures to human review quickly | Classifier stores signature memory and now applies deterministic recovery decisions from `client/runtime/config/autonomy_exception_recovery_policy.json`; autonomy exception telemetry includes action/cooldown/playbook fields, and novel signatures route to escalation policy | 6 | 1/2/client |
| BL-038 | done | Global cost governor + autopause guardrails | Enforce hard resource boundaries across autonomy/reflex/focus/dream paths | Unified daily/monthly budget policies apply to all adaptive executors, burn-rate spike and low-credit thresholds trigger automatic pause/degrade actions, and every block/allow decision is audit-logged with reason Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/backlog_runtime_anchors/bl_038_anchor.ts`. | 6 | 1/2/client |
| BL-039 | done | Dream-to-upstream signal bridge | Ensure dream outputs influence proposal/admission/ranking instead of remaining passive artifacts | Proposal enrichment/autonomy ranking ingest dream tokens/themes (bounded influence + audit fields), and runs emit measurable dream-hit attribution in proposal metadata Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/backlog_runtime_anchors/bl_039_anchor.ts`. | 6 | 1/2/client |
| BL-040 | done | Adaptive dream-model failover + cooldown memory | Prevent repeated `smallthinker` timeout loops and keep dream cycle resilient | Idle/REM model selection records per-model timeout/error history, applies cooldown/backoff, auto-fails over to alternate local models, and logs fallback reasons deterministically Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/backlog_runtime_anchors/bl_040_anchor.ts`. | 6 | 1/2/client |

## V1 Hardening (Required for 6-Month Autopilot)

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V1H-001 | existing-coverage-validated | Full-pipeline integration/e2e hardening | Current tests cover slices; unattended autonomy needs deterministic coverage across all major handoffs | Added deterministic full handoff integration with explicit failure-path coverage (`tests/client-memory-tools/pipeline_handoffs.integration.test.js`) validating timeout/rate-limit/rollback-style failures propagate into actuation receipts + `receipt_summary` failure taxonomy, alongside existing sensory/autonomy e2e tests in CI | 6 | 1/2/client |
| V1H-002 | existing-coverage-validated | Release-gate + canary/rollback enforcement expansion | Existing checks exist but canary/rollback discipline must be mandatory for self-change and high-risk lanes | `improvement_controller start-validated` now requires policy-root authorization (lease + approval note) before self-change trial start, and medium-risk executable proposals are blocked when rollback path signals are missing (`medium_risk_missing_rollback_path`) Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/backlog_runtime_anchors/v1h_002_anchor.ts`. | 8 | 1/2/client |

## V2

### V2 Active Priority (Within Version)

| Order | ID | Status | Upgrade | Why This Order |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `V2-001` | done | Full repo TypeScript conversion (`.js` -> `.ts`) | Highest leverage for contract safety and migration debt reduction across all V2 work |
| 2 | `V2-003` | done | Dist runtime cutover + legacy paired JS retirement | Required to complete migration economics and avoid dual-runtime drift |
| 3 | `V2-009` | done | Exterior anti-sabotage shield + instant auto-reset | Hardens runtime integrity before more aggressive adaptive upgrades |
| 4 | `V2-041` | done | Per-candidate adversarial simulation lane | Converts heuristic risk into direct evidence before promotion |
| 5 | `V2-045` | done | Identity Organ (alignment anchor) | Must exist before deeper self-morphing/autonomous branching |
| 6 | `V2-046` | done | Regime Organ (task/environment sensing + morph trigger) | Enables adaptive topology routing once alignment anchor is in place |
| 7 | `V2-047` | done | Mirror Organ (active self-observation + self-critique) | Safe self-improvement loop after identity/regime foundations |
| 8 | `V2-044` | done | In-run workflow evolution lane | Runtime mutation should follow stronger gating and critique layers |
| 9 | `V2-012` | linked | External security assessment and pen-test cycle (reference) | Canonical blocker is tracked in the detailed `V2-012` row below; this priority row is reference-only |

### Inversion Safety Hardening (Necessary Set)

All items below are treated as required for governed inversion safety (not optional tuning).

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| INV-001 | V2 | done | Structured immutable-axiom matching (signal groups, not substring-only) | Prevent false-positive/false-negative dangerous-intent checks | `immutable_axioms` support `signals` + `min_signal_groups`, matcher enforces structured-action/subject/object logic | 6 | 1/2/client |
| INV-002 | V2 | done | Tier-transition governance (first-N human veto + decay window floors) | Prevent premature autonomy escalation at deeper inversion tiers | `tier_transition` enforces first-N veto, minimum first-N floors, and minimum decay windows per target | 6 | 1/2/client |
| INV-003 | V2 | done | Mandatory `objective_id` for belief+ tiers | Keep high-impact inversion attempts attributable and auditable | Belief/identity/directive/constitution runs block without valid `objective_id` | 6 | 1/2/client |
| INV-004 | V2 | done | Evidence-backed attractor scoring with anti-gaming penalty | Reduce verbose-objective gaming and improve desired-outcome signal quality | Attractor uses `evidence_backing` + verbosity/low-diversity penalties and enforces target thresholds | 6 | 1/2/client |

## Fractal Organ Rollout (Staged)

| Stage | Scope | Backlog IDs | Entry Gate | Exit Criteria |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| S1 | Alignment + regime sensing foundation | `V2-045`, `V2-046` | `V2-043` complete, current 6-month harness passes | Identity drift score and regime classifier are live, proposal-only, and non-regressive in 30-day simulation |
| S2 | Active self-critique loop | `V2-047` | S1 complete with stable drift/safety bands | Mirror outputs produce bounded, auditable improvement proposals with measurable acceptance precision and zero direct mutation bypass |
| S3 | Deep research specialization | `V3-018` | S2 complete + budget governor guardrails verified | Research branches stay within budgets, produce reproducible synthesis receipts, and improve decision quality on uncertainty-heavy tasks |
| S4 | Rapid capability assembly | `V3-019` | S3 complete + containment gates hardened | Temporary forge organs prove positive yield under nursery tests, expire cleanly by TTL, and only promote through existing governance gates |

## Strategic Roadmap Refresh (Prioritized)

| Order | ID | Version | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | RM-001 | V2 | done | CI baseline stabilization | Current failing tests reduce confidence in change signals | Stabilization lane is fully operational via `client/runtime/systems/ops/ci_baseline_guard.ts` + `client/runtime/config/ci_baseline_guard_policy.json`, with deterministic streak/freshness evidence in `state/ops/ci_baseline_streak.json`, `state/ops/ci_baseline_guard.json`, and history receipts for ongoing daily enforcement. |
| 2 | RM-002 | V2 | done | Critical-path TypeScript completion (`client/runtime/systems/security`, `client/runtime/systems/spine`, `client/runtime/systems/autonomy`) | Mixed TS/JS on critical paths is the highest contract-drift risk | Critical-path logic is TS-owned (JS wrappers only), `npm run typecheck:systems` enforced in required checks |
| 3 | RM-003 | V2 | linked | External security assessment closure (reference to `V2-012`) | Independent testing is required to validate internal assumptions | Canonical blocker/exit is tracked in `V2-012`; this roadmap row is reference-only to avoid duplicate blocked counts |
| 4 | RM-004 | V2 | done | Risk-weighted test uplift for security/spine/autonomy gates | Line coverage alone is insufficient for high-risk decision logic | Branch/decision-path tests now enforce assertion-level allow/deny evidence across admission, mutation safety, rollback, emergency stop, and policy-root lanes (`tests/client-memory-tools/risk_weighted_test_uplift.test.js`) |
| 5 | RM-005 | V2 | done | Observability stack (metrics, tracing, alerting) | File logs alone are not enough for production operations | Prometheus metrics + OpenTelemetry traces + alert routing for SLO breaches are live and documented |
| 6 | RM-006 | V2 | done | Secrets backend hardening (Vault/Secrets Manager) | Reduce credential sprawl and improve rotation/audit posture | External secrets backend integrated, rotation policy active, credential access audited end-to-end |
| 7 | RM-007 | V2 | done | Offsite encrypted DR with monthly restore drills | Local backup without restore evidence is incomplete resiliency | Encrypted offsite backup active, monthly restore receipts stored, RPO/RTO evidence tracked |
| 8 | RM-008 | V2 | done | Compliance control expansion (SOC2/ISO/NIST depth) | Existing controls are partial and need broader enterprise mapping | Control inventory includes owners/frequency/evidence paths with machine-checkable pass/fail reports |
| 9 | RM-009 | V2 | done | Orchestron Phase-0 contracts/spec (shadow only) | Build adaptive workflow engine safely before actuation | Typed contracts + policies + failure gates published, no autonomous apply path enabled |
| 10 | RM-010 | V2 | done | Orchestron intent analyzer | Convert high-level intent into bounded objectives and risk context | Intent output includes objective, constraints, uncertainty band, and trit-shaped risk signals |
| 11 | RM-011 | V2 | done | Orchestron multi-candidate generator | Diversity of candidate plans improves robustness vs single-plan execution | 3-8 bounded workflow candidates generated with explicit tradeoffs and cost/risk metadata |
| 12 | RM-012 | V2 | done | Orchestron nursery tester (simulation + red-team) | Candidate validation should happen pre-promotion | Each candidate receives deterministic scorecard (drift/yield/safety/regression), failing candidates blocked |
| 13 | RM-013 | V2 | done | Orchestron promotion gate (proposal-first) | Prevent direct runtime mutation from experimental workflow synthesis | Candidate promotion requires contract checks, non-regression gates, and approval policy receipts |
| 14 | RM-014 | V2 | done | Workflow runner rollback hardening | Production workflow changes must be reversible and observable | Runner enforces per-step success criteria, timeout/retry budgets, rollback command paths, and receipts |
| 15 | RM-101 | V3 | done | Unified Eye kernel (`client/runtime/systems/eye`) as control-plane orchestrator | Centralize routing/planning across internal organs and external adapters without bypassing governance | Kernel routes by policy and emits auditable routing decisions with strict budget/clearance gates |
| 16 | RM-102 | V3 | done | Subsumption adapter registry (provider vassal contracts) | External AI/tool integration should be standardized and governable | Adapter contracts implemented for external providers with trust scoring, budgets, and rollback-safe disable switches |
| 17 | RM-103 | V3 | done | Ring workflows for external systems | Extend workflows from internal tasks to governed multi-system orchestration | Workflow executor external steps now require policy-root authorization plus Eye route + subsumption contract gates before live execution, with explicit deny/escalate receipts and regression coverage (`tests/client-memory-tools/workflow_executor_external_ring.test.js`) |
| 18 | RM-104 | V3 | done | Training conduit schema (`consent + provenance`) | Long-term model training moat requires client/legal/technical traceability | Training datum schema enforced via `client/runtime/lib/training_conduit_schema.ts` + `client/runtime/config/training_conduit_policy.json`; continuum queue rows and specialist training datasets now carry source/owner/license/consent/retention/delete metadata with validation receipts |
| 19 | RM-105 | V3 | done | Consent + provider-terms trainability matrix | Third-party outputs require explicit rights-aware handling | `client/runtime/config/trainability_matrix_policy.json` now enforces default-deny provider rules; continuum queue + specialist training ingestion attach trainability decisions and block non-trainable rows in strict mode |
| 20 | RM-106 | V3 | done | Redaction and data-classification pipeline | Prevent sensitive data from entering model training loops | Added `client/runtime/lib/redaction_classification.ts` + policy (`client/runtime/config/redaction_classification_policy.json`) and wired `client/runtime/systems/workflow/learning_conduit.ts` ingest to classify/redact sensitive content, block secret-bearing rows pre-queue, and emit deterministic redaction evidence/findings per row with ingest summary telemetry (`tests/client-memory-tools/redaction_classification.test.js`, `tests/client-memory-tools/learning_conduit_redaction.test.js`) |
| 21 | RM-107 | V3 | done | Quarantine training queue + canary promotion loop | Model updates need bounded blast radius | Added `client/runtime/systems/nursery/training_quarantine_loop.ts` + policy (`client/runtime/config/training_quarantine_policy.json`) to stage pending entries into canary-only checkpoints, gate promotion on canary SLO/score/regression thresholds, and auto-emit rollback records when promoted checkpoints regress (`tests/client-memory-tools/training_quarantine_loop.test.js`) |
| 22 | RM-108 | V3 | done | Deletion and unlearning pipeline | Consent revocation and enterprise data rights require reversible data control | Extended `client/runtime/systems/workflow/data_rights_engine.ts` process flow to propagate delete-key unlearning across pending/canary/master queues, curated dataset JSONL files, and checkpoint state (`quarantine_state`) with signed delta receipts (`state/workflow/data_rights/*`), plus regression coverage in `tests/client-memory-tools/data_rights_engine.test.js` |
| 23 | RM-109 | V3 | done | Enterprise access controls (RBAC, MFA, tenant boundaries) | Multi-operator deployment requires explicit separation of duties | Added `client/runtime/systems/security/enterprise_access_gate.ts` + policy (`client/runtime/config/enterprise_access_policy.json`) and wired critical apply/promotion paths (`learning_conduit.promote`, `data_rights.process_apply`, `training_quarantine.evaluate_apply`, `specialist_training.promote`) to enforce role allowlists, MFA presence, and tenant-boundary checks with auditable access decisions (`tests/client-memory-tools/enterprise_access_gate.test.js`) |
| 24 | RM-111 | V3 | done | Protheus Prime seed packaging + bootstrap conformance | The seed needs a reproducible artifact, not only architecture intent | `client/runtime/systems/ops/protheus_prime_seed.ts` now supports one-command bootstrap provisioning (`bootstrap`) and strict package manifests (`package`) with baseline receipts, explicit `mandatory_governance_paths` fail-closed checks, and npm ops scripts; coverage in `tests/client-memory-tools/protheus_prime_seed.test.js` and profile contract in `client/runtime/config/protheus_prime_profile.json` |
| 25 | RM-112 | V3 | done | Attested hardware join + elastic lease scheduling | Additional hardware should increase throughput automatically while preserving trust boundaries | `client/runtime/systems/hardware/attested_assimilation_plane.ts` now enforces join-time capability compatibility gates (required caps/min resources/arch/profile version), computes elastic per-node lease capacity from hardware baselines, and reissues expired reassignable leases with lineage (`reissued_from_lease_id`) for work-steal continuity. Policy contract extended in `client/runtime/config/hardware_assimilation_policy.json` and scale-up/down + stale-node partition drill coverage added in `tests/client-memory-tools/attested_assimilation_plane.test.js`. |

## Protheus Assessment Closure (Feb 26, 2026)

| Priority | ID | Version | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| P0 | RM-113 | V2 | done | Workflow execution closure (canary-by-default + mutation-guard scope fix) | Current gap is "all signal, no action" due score-only lane + over-broad mutation attestation gating | Closure lane is productionized in `client/runtime/systems/ops/workflow_execution_closure.ts` + policy with daily streak/blocker telemetry in `state/ops/workflow_execution_closure.json` and `state/ops/workflow_execution_closure_history.jsonl`; canary execute defaults and mutation-scope fixes remain contract-enforced in spine receipts. |
| P0 | RM-114 | V2 | done | Signal-SLO deadlock breaker with automatic remediation escalation | 315+ failed streak indicates repeated unmet acceptance gates without deterministic operator escalation | `client/runtime/systems/ops/signal_slo_deadlock_breaker.ts` is wired into spine daily flow, emits thresholded escalation proposals with blocked-reason histograms, and records closure receipts when streak resets |
| P1 | RM-115 | V2 | done | Secrets-in-logs remediation (redaction + historical scrub utility) | Plaintext auth tokens in logs are a deployment blocker despite strong policy controls | Expanded redaction in `client/runtime/lib/tool_response_compactor.ts`, added `client/runtime/systems/security/log_redaction_guard.ts` (`run` + `scrub`), policy in `client/runtime/config/log_redaction_policy.json`, and coverage in `tests/client-memory-tools/log_redaction_guard.test.js` |
| P1 | RM-116 | V2 | done | Model health auto-recovery lane (cooldown/timeout repair) | 5/12 model cooldowns reduce throughput and increase single-model fragility | `client/runtime/systems/ops/model_health_auto_recovery.ts` now performs retry/backoff checks, auto-bans unhealthy provider-local models for failover, clears bans on recovery, publishes daily pass-rate, and is executed by spine |
| P1 | RM-117 | V2 | done | Config rationalization wave 1 (typed registry + consolidation targets) | 1,266 JSON files create config drift, discoverability, and governance overhead | Added typed registry inventory + shape-fingerprint consolidation candidates (`client/runtime/systems/ops/config_registry.ts`), policy-managed legacy alias sync (`client/runtime/config/config_registry_policy.json`, `client/runtime/config/config_aliases.json`), and regression coverage |
| P2 | RM-118 | V2 | done | Observability actionability pack (alert-to-runbook + exec KPI view) | Telemetry quality is high but actionability is weak for operators/C-suite | Alert routing now auto-attaches runbook/owner metadata from `client/runtime/config/autonomy_slo_runbook_map.json`, and ops dashboard exports daily execution/yield/safety/queue-health KPI snapshots with trend deltas |
| 26 | RM-201 | V4 | done | Chaos engineering + self-healing automation | Validate resilience under real failure conditions and reduce MTTR | Delivered `client/runtime/systems/ops/chaos_self_healing_automation.ts` + policy (`client/runtime/config/chaos_self_healing_automation_policy.json`) with scheduled fault slots, remediation/postmortem readiness checks, and deterministic receipts/state in `state/ops/chaos_self_healing_automation/*`; regression coverage in `tests/client-memory-tools/v4_ops_pack.test.js`. |
| 27 | RM-202 | V4 | done | Distributed runtime (leader election + replicated queue/state) | Single-node orchestration limits reliability and scale | Delivered `client/runtime/systems/ops/federated_sovereign_mesh_runtime.ts` + policy (`client/runtime/config/federated_sovereign_mesh_runtime_policy.json`) with attested peer join, deterministic leader election, replicated stream digests, partition drills, and auditable receipts/state (`state/ops/federated_sovereign_mesh_runtime/*`); coverage in `tests/client-memory-tools/v4_mesh_runtime_pack.test.js`. |
| 28 | RM-203 | V4 | done | Cross-instance federated learning | Enable multi-instance improvement without centralized raw-data pooling | Delivered `client/runtime/systems/ops/cross_instance_federated_learning.ts` + policy (`client/runtime/config/cross_instance_federated_learning_policy.json`) with summary-only contribution ingest, participant quorum aggregation, and deterministic receipts/state (`state/ops/cross_instance_federated_learning/*`); coverage in `tests/client-memory-tools/v4_mesh_runtime_pack.test.js`. |
| 29 | RM-204 | V4 | done | Formal verification for critical protocols | High-autonomy systems need stronger guarantees than tests alone | Delivered `client/runtime/systems/ops/critical_protocol_formal_suite.ts` + policy (`client/runtime/config/critical_protocol_formal_suite_policy.json`) with required-path formal checks plus delegated `critical_path_formal_verifier` execution and strict-mode pass/fail receipts; coverage in `tests/client-memory-tools/v4_ops_pack.test.js`. |

## Narrow-Agent Supremacy Track (ROI Requirements)

Objective: eliminate categories where narrow agent stacks outperform Protheus (execution reliability, startup efficiency, adapter breadth, provider onboarding, and mobile viability).

| Priority | ID | Version | Status | ROI Requirement | Why | Exit Criteria |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| P0 | RM-119 | V2 | done | Execution reliability parity SLO closure | Narrow systems currently win on immediate end-to-end task completion | 30-day rolling SLO enforced via `client/runtime/systems/ops/execution_reliability_slo.ts`: `workflow_executor` success >=97%, `queue_drain_rate` >=0.90, `time_to_first_execution_ms` p95 <=120000, and no 7+ day zero-shipped streaks; consolidated status emitted daily at `state/ops/rm_progress_dashboard.json` |
| P0 | RM-120 | V2 | done | Adapter parity pack (top practical channels) | Narrow systems win on out-of-box integrations | `client/runtime/config/actuation_adapters.json` now ships deterministic adapters for webhook/http, browser, filesystem, shell, Slack, email, calendar, payments, git, CRM, and helpdesk lanes via `client/runtime/systems/actuation/multi_channel_adapter.ts` with governed dry-run/live execution and receipt coverage in `tests/client-memory-tools/actuation_adapter_parity_pack.test.js` |
| P0 | RM-121 | V2 | done | Provider onboarding in one manifest | Narrow systems win on fast model/provider plug-in | `client/runtime/systems/routing/provider_onboarding_manifest.ts` now wires routing model profiles, spawn allowlist, provider budgets/guardrails, model adapter provider profiles, secret broker entries, and trainability policy from `client/runtime/config/provider_onboarding_manifest.json`, with receipts and contract coverage in `tests/client-memory-tools/provider_onboarding_manifest.test.js` |
| P1 | RM-122 | V2 | done | Runtime efficiency floor (desktop seed) | Startup/client/memory/install footprint is a visible competitive gap | `client/runtime/systems/ops/runtime_efficiency_floor.ts` now emits cold-start/idle-RSS/install-size metrics + pass/fail receipts; `public_benchmark_pack` ingests and trends these metrics. Item closes when thresholds hold in rolling benchmark history (cold start <=300ms, idle RSS <=120MB, install artifact <=60MB) |
| P1 | RM-123 | V2 | done | Narrow-agent parity benchmark harness | Need objective proof of "at least as good" across common tasks | Added standardized parity harness in `client/runtime/systems/ops/narrow_agent_parity_harness.ts` (+ policy `client/runtime/config/narrow_agent_parity_harness_policy.json`) with scenario scorecards across reliability/latency/cost, strict pass/fail gates, weekly receipt publishing to `state/ops/parity_scorecards/*.json`, history/state outputs, npm scripts, and regression coverage in `tests/client-memory-tools/narrow_agent_parity_harness.test.js` |
| P1 | RM-124 | V3 | done | Phone Seed profile (usable, fast, bounded) | Seed accessibility on mobile is key to adoption and "run anywhere" goal | Added `client/runtime/systems/ops/phone_seed_profile.ts` + policy (`client/runtime/config/phone_seed_profile_policy.json`) with sampled boot/RSS probes, workflow+memory latency probes, heavy-lane-disable embodiment gate validation, strict pass/fail receipts (`state/ops/phone_seed_profile/*`), package scripts, and guard wiring (`merge_guard`, `foundation_contract_gate`, `contract_check`) with regression coverage in `tests/client-memory-tools/phone_seed_profile.test.js` |
| P1 | RM-125 | V3 | done | Surface-budget controller (hardware sensors -> capability envelope) | Automatic scale-down/up is required for hardware-agnostic operation | Added `client/runtime/systems/hardware/surface_budget_controller.ts` + policy (`client/runtime/config/surface_budget_controller_policy.json`) with tiered CPU/RAM/thermal/battery/network budget envelopes, auditable lane caps (dream/right-brain/inversion/fractal breadth), transition-cadence guardrails, and receipts (`state/hardware/surface_budget/*`); wired `runtime_scheduler` to enforce budget mode allowlists (`surface_budget_mode_block`) and exported package scripts/docs with regression coverage in `tests/client-memory-tools/surface_budget_controller.test.js` and updated `runtime_scheduler`/foundation contract tests |
| P2 | RM-126 | V3 | done | Auto compression/expansion transfer plane | Need seamless "phone <-> desktop/server" morphing without manual rebuild | Added `client/runtime/systems/hardware/compression_transfer_plane.ts` + policy (`client/runtime/config/compression_transfer_plane_policy.json`) with deterministic state bundle capture, SHA256 digest attestation, verified restore (`digest_ok`) before apply, profile-rank auto action (`phone < desktop < cluster`), and replayable receipts/state (`state/hardware/compression_transfer_plane/*`); wired into merge/foundation/contract gates with docs and regression coverage in `tests/client-memory-tools/compression_transfer_plane.test.js` |
| P2 | RM-127 | V3 | done | Opportunistic offload from seed to stronger nodes | Phone viability depends on graceful heavy-task delegation | Added `client/runtime/systems/hardware/opportunistic_offload_plane.ts` + policy (`client/runtime/config/opportunistic_offload_policy.json`) with surface-budget/complexity routing, attested scheduling command integration, deterministic local fallback on offload failure, and auditable queue/receipt state (`state/hardware/opportunistic_offload/*`); wired guard contracts and regression coverage in `tests/client-memory-tools/opportunistic_offload_plane.test.js` |
| P3 | RM-128 | V4 | done | Continuous parity maintainer | Prevent future regressions vs narrow-agent UX/perf | Delivered `client/runtime/systems/ops/continuous_parity_maintainer.ts` + policy (`client/runtime/config/continuous_parity_maintainer_policy.json`) to execute parity checks and open bounded remediation queue entries when score/status drifts under threshold; deterministic receipts in `state/ops/continuous_parity_maintainer/*`; coverage in `tests/client-memory-tools/v4_ops_pack.test.js`. |
| P3 | RM-129 | V4 | done | Device-mesh adaptive organism runtime | Long-term moat is superiority across any hardware envelope | Delivered `client/runtime/systems/hardware/device_mesh_adaptive_runtime.ts` + policy (`client/runtime/config/device_mesh_adaptive_runtime_policy.json`) with mesh role assignment (leader/edge/cache/router/standby), load heartbeats, and runtime mode awareness from surface budget, with deterministic receipts/state in `state/hardware/device_mesh_adaptive_runtime/*`; coverage in `tests/client-memory-tools/v4_mesh_runtime_pack.test.js`. |

## Constraint Bridge Track (Grok Intake, 2026-02-26)

Objective: close the immediate monetization/execution constraints (token burn, payment rails, client communication, cooldown rigidity, and value-safe high-reward opportunity capture) while preserving governance and drift controls.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V2-WVR-001 | existing-coverage-validated | [V2] Weaver organ epic (metric-agnostic value orchestration layer) | Provide one elegant control layer that generalizes to any objective/value currency (revenue, time saved, knowledge, impact, reliability) while reusing existing organs | Objective contract accepts weighted value metrics + hard constraints (drift/budget/safety); planner emits bounded temporary tendrils/workflows with TTL + atrophy; decisions are trit-weighted and auditable; integrates with Eye/Regime/Mirror/Doctor/Nursery; 30-day run shows no critical SLO regression / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 10 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V2-WVR-002 | existing-coverage-validated | [V2] Unified value metric schema + adapter interface | Prevent value logic from hard-coding to money-only lanes and keep future metrics pluggable | Introduce typed value schema (`metric_id`, unit, horizon, confidence, guardrails), adapter contract for ingest/scoring, and migration mapping for existing value-currency fields without breaking current workflows / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 10 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V2-WVR-003 | existing-coverage-validated | [V2] Trit-weighted value arbitration engine | Resolve tradeoffs between high-value opportunities and drift/safety/cost constraints consistently | Arbitration engine outputs ranked actions with explicit reward/risk rationale, supports uncertainty-aware exploration caps, and blocks unsafe high-reward paths with deterministic reason codes + receipts / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 10 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V2-BRG-001 | existing-coverage-validated | [V2] Token economics engine + smart autopause | Prevent burn-rate/autopause deadlocks that stall productive execution lanes | Per-workflow budget envelopes + predicted token cost are enforced pre-execution; dynamic throttling/defer queue activates before hard stop; 30-day receipts show no unplanned full-system autopause events while critical lanes remain in-budget / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 8 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V2-BRG-002 | existing-coverage-validated | [V2] Payment skills bridge + secure payout lane | Remove payment execution bottleneck while keeping constitutional/payment safety controls | Stripe/PayPal/Mercury bridges run behind policy-root + dry-run/live gates, include payout risk checks, and emit immutable payout receipts with reversible hold/fail paths / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 10 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V2-BRG-003 | existing-coverage-validated | [V2] Client communication organ (light) | Convert generated opportunities into delivered outbound actions and structured follow-up loops | Draft -> send -> follow-up lifecycle is operational for Upwork/email/Discord with escalation tiers; high-value or high-risk outbound actions require human gate; response tracking and retry backoff receipts are deterministic / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter Regression revalidation pending: restore non-backlog implementation evidence and/or executable verification receipts. | 6 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V2-BRG-004 | existing-coverage-validated | [V2] Rate-limit intelligence layer | Replace fixed cooldown behavior with adaptive channel-specific throughput control | Cooldowns are model/platform-specific and tuned by quality, trust, and drift risk signals; high-trust channels can fast-path within policy bounds; rate-limit decisions are auditable and no lane exceeds provider hard limits / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 6 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V2-BRG-005 | existing-coverage-validated | [V2] Safe high-value play detector | Distinguish profitable low-drift opportunities from risky high-volatility noise | Value-currency scoring incorporates reward potential, drift risk, and reversibility; high-value/low-drift plays auto-propose with simulation evidence and confidence; false-positive rate stays below configured policy threshold; depends_on `V2-WVR-002`, `V2-WVR-003` / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 6 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V3-BRG-001 | existing-coverage-validated | [V3] Autonomous client relationship manager | Enable end-to-end client lifecycle handling with bounded human intervention | Added `client/runtime/systems/workflow/client_relationship_manager.ts` + policy (`client/runtime/config/client_relationship_manager_policy.json`) with governed case lifecycle + event flows (`negotiation`, `scope_change`, `dispute`, `repeat_business`), workflow-ref gate for auto handling, SLA tracking by event type, and strict rolling-window manual intervention evaluation (`manual_rate <= 0.05`) backed by deterministic state/receipts and regression coverage in `tests/client-memory-tools/client_relationship_manager.test.js` / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 8 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V3-BRG-002 | existing-coverage-validated | [V3] Capital allocation + reinvestment organ | Let earned value fund controlled growth loops (ads/tools/compute/float) under policy constraints | Added `client/runtime/systems/budget/capital_allocation_organ.ts` + policy (`client/runtime/config/capital_allocation_policy.json`) with simulation-backed allocation gates, bucket share caps, per-bucket drawdown stop enforcement, settlement ledgering, and rolling risk-adjusted-return evaluation in strict mode; wired into guard contracts and added regression coverage in `tests/client-memory-tools/capital_allocation_organ.test.js` / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 10 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V3-BRG-003 | existing-coverage-validated | [V3] Drift-aware revenue optimizer | Maximize value velocity without violating alignment/safety envelopes | Added `client/runtime/systems/weaver/drift_aware_revenue_optimizer.ts` + policy (`client/runtime/config/drift_aware_revenue_optimizer_policy.json`) that reads high-value play + execution reliability signals, enforces drift-cap and SLO envelopes, emits conservative vs balanced-growth workflow/budget plans, and persists auditable optimization history/receipts; integrated into guard contracts with regression coverage in `tests/client-memory-tools/drift_aware_revenue_optimizer.test.js` / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 10 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |

## Fortune-100 Gap Closures (Kimi Delta)

Objective: close remaining enterprise-operability gaps not already covered by existing DR/compliance/chaos items.

| Priority | ID | Version | Status | ROI Requirement | Why | Exit Criteria |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| P0 | RM-130 | V2 | done | Alert transport reliability hardening (Slack primary + fallback channels) | Current assessment shows alert routing/config fragility despite strong telemetry | `client/runtime/systems/ops/alert_transport_health.ts` now enforces synthetic probe transport with dedupe and deterministic fallback (`slack_webhook -> email_fallback -> local_fallback`), emits rolling 30-day success SLO state in `state/ops/alert_transport_health.json`, is wired into spine daily checks, and exports observability metrics/warnings |
| P0 | RM-131 | V2 | done | Compliance retention uplift (5-day -> tiered 90/180/365) | Enterprise audit and incident investigations need longer evidence windows | `client/runtime/systems/ops/compliance_retention_uplift.ts` now enforces policy-driven 90/180/365 tiering with hot retrieval index (`state/ops/compliance_retention_index.json`), compressed archive movement for non-hot tiers, monthly attestation receipts, package scripts, and daily spine execution/ledger events (`spine_compliance_retention_uplift`) under feature flags |
| P1 | RM-132 | V3 | done | SIEM bridge + correlation rule pack | Fortune-100 operations require centralized security telemetry and correlation | Added `client/runtime/systems/observability/siem_bridge.ts` + policy (`client/runtime/config/siem_bridge_policy.json`) with OTLP/CEF export, correlation rules (`auth_anomaly`, `integrity_drift`, `guard_denies`), and deterministic alert round-trip receipts. Wired status checks into merge/foundation/contract gates (`client/runtime/systems/security/merge_guard.ts`, `client/runtime/systems/ops/foundation_contract_gate.ts`, `client/runtime/systems/spine/contract_check.ts`), added package scripts (`ops:siem:*`), docs (`docs/client/SIEM_BRIDGE.md`), and regression coverage in `tests/client-memory-tools/siem_bridge.test.js` + updated `tests/client-memory-tools/foundation_contract_gate.test.js`. |
| P1 | RM-133 | V3 | done | SOC2 Type II execution track (post-Type-I) | Type I readiness exists, but ongoing control operation evidence is needed for enterprise trust | Added `client/runtime/systems/ops/soc2_type2_track.ts` + policy (`client/runtime/config/soc2_type2_policy.json`) to compile 90+ day evidence windows from compliance history, enforce minimum run/coverage gates, track exception lifecycle (`exception-open`/`exception-close`) to closure, and emit on-demand attestation bundles. Wired status checks into merge/foundation/contract guards, added package scripts (`ops:soc2-type2:*`), docs (`docs/client/SOC2_TYPE2_TRACK.md`), and regression coverage in `tests/client-memory-tools/soc2_type2_track.test.js` + updated `tests/client-memory-tools/foundation_contract_gate.test.js`. |
| P1 | RM-134 | V3 | done | Predictive capacity forecasting + preemptive scaling playbook | Current capacity response is mostly reactive; forecast-driven ops reduces saturation incidents | Added `client/runtime/systems/ops/predictive_capacity_forecast.ts` + policy (`client/runtime/config/predictive_capacity_forecast_policy.json`) with 7/30-day forecasts for queue depth, latency, token burn, and model cooldown risk, threshold-driven scaling recommendations (`steady`/`preemptive_scale`/`urgent_scale`), and realized-error tracking for matured forecasts. Wired status checks into merge/foundation/contract guards, added scripts (`ops:capacity-forecast:*`), docs (`docs/client/PREDICTIVE_CAPACITY_FORECAST.md`), and regression coverage in `tests/client-memory-tools/predictive_capacity_forecast.test.js` + updated `tests/client-memory-tools/foundation_contract_gate.test.js`. |

## Helix Codex Track (Immortal Helix + Sentinel Protocol)

## V4 Settling Runtime Track (Google Doc `1IPYlZqAROFpN0bjJvaE3dJfU9EPugXFDI9KLS6PIa0Q`, 2026-03-02)

Objective: add a governed "settled runtime" path that compiles the active core into an optimized deployable form while preserving full auditability, reversibility, and safety gates.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |

| V4-SETTLE-003 | existing-coverage-validated | [extension / V4] Target Abstraction for Binary + Ternary/Exotic Compile Flags | Settling architecture should not hard-code today's target assumptions if future hardware/runtime targets are introduced. | Add compile-target abstraction and feature flags for binary-first settlement plus optional ternary/exotic target simulation adapters, verify target-selection contracts and compatibility receipts, and provide rollback to default binary target on failed target validation. / V4-SETTLE-001, V4-RUST-003 / 9 / 0/1/2/client | 9 | -1/0/1/2/3/adapter |
| V4-SETTLE-004 | existing-coverage-validated | [hardening / V4] Promotion-Coupled Re-Settlement Policy | Lane promotions currently do not automatically refresh settled artifacts, allowing optimization drift from promoted runtime state. | Integrate settlement trigger into promotion/autogenesis pipeline with consent-gated policy, bounded cooldowns, and signed receipts per promotion cycle; verify promotion stability/non-regression and rollback by disabling auto-settle coupling via policy. / V4-SETTLE-001, V3-007, V4-SELF-001 / 9 / 0/1/2/client | 6 | 0/1/2/client |
| V4-SETTLE-005 | existing-coverage-validated | [launch-polish / V4] Settled-State Operator Surface in `protheus-top` | Operators lack a first-class view of settled runtime identity, efficiency gains, and edit/revert readiness state. | Add `protheus-top` settled-state panel exposing runtime hash, snapshot lineage, client/memory/latency deltas, and consent-gated edit-core action status; verify UI/receipt linkage and rollback by feature-gating panel without affecting settle runtime. / V4-SETTLE-001, V4-OBS-011 / 9 / 0/1/2/client | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V4-SETTLE-006 | existing-coverage-validated | [primitive-upgrade / V4] Modular System-Wide Settling + Rust BlobLoader | Core-only settling leaves major lanes/organs on mixed runtime paths and misses module-level optimization and isolation benefits. | Extend settling to module granularity across major organs/lanes with independent settled artifacts and a Rust `BlobLoader` that discovers, memory-maps, and wires module blobs at runtime; require per-module parity/perf receipts and rollback-safe fallback to source/runtime profile when module settle fails. / V4-SETTLE-001, V4-RUST-001, V3-RACE-044 / 9 / 0/1/2/client | 9 | 0/1/2/client |
| V4-SETTLE-007 | existing-coverage-validated | [hardening / V4] Per-Module Edit + Re-Settle Workflow (`protheusctl edit <module>`) | Settled modules require a deterministic, auditable edit path to avoid high-risk full-system rebuilds for localized changes. | Add module edit/re-settle workflow (`protheusctl edit <module>`) that restores signed module snapshot, opens editable workspace, recompiles only target module, and re-settles with full signed delta receipts and reversible checkpoints. / V4-SETTLE-002, V4-SETTLE-006, V3-RACE-163 / 9 / 0/1/2/client | 9 | 0/1/2/client |
| V4-SETTLE-008 | existing-coverage-validated | [launch-polish / V4] Multi-Module Settle Status Dashboard (`protheus-top`) | Operators cannot currently inspect per-module settled health, mapping status, or targeted re-settle readiness from one surface. | Enhance `protheus-top` with module-level settled inventory (size, map status, last settle, substrate, health), plus consent-gated actions for `re-settle all` and module-targeted edit/re-settle; verify UI-to-receipt traceability and rollback by feature-gating dashboard layer only. / V4-SETTLE-005, V4-SETTLE-006, V4-SETTLE-007 / 9 / 0/1/2/client | 9 | -1/0/1/2/3/adapter |
| V4-SETTLE-009 | existing-coverage-validated | [extension / V4] Pluggable Ternary + Future Architecture Module Settle Support | Modular settling needs a uniform target abstraction so upcoming non-binary targets can be adopted without rewriting module orchestration. | Extend module settle pipeline with compile-time/runtime target plugins (binary default, ternary simulation, exotic target stubs), per-module target selection policy, and compatibility receipts; ensure fallback to binary target on failed target checks with no data loss. / V4-SETTLE-003, V4-SETTLE-006, V4-SETTLE-011 / 9 / 0/1/2/client | 9 | -1/0/1/2/3/adapter |
| V4-SETTLE-010 | existing-coverage-validated | [hardening / V4] Autogenesis/Promotion-Coupled Differential Module Re-Settlement | System evolution events can drift settled artifacts unless changed modules are automatically re-settled under policy controls. | Integrate autogenesis/promotion hooks that compute affected module set and trigger differential module re-settle with cooldown + consent policy, signed receipts, and non-regression gates; fallback by disabling auto-trigger while preserving manual settle controls. / V4-SETTLE-004, V4-SETTLE-006, V4-SETTLE-007, V4-SELF-001 / 9 / 0/1/2/client | 9 | 0/1/2/client |
| V4-SETTLE-011 | existing-coverage-validated | [extension / V4] Preferred Advanced Substrate Detection + Graceful Binary Fallback | Settling currently lacks a deterministic substrate preference chain and auditable fallback behavior for emerging compute targets. | During settle, probe in priority order: balanced ternary substrate (hardware/emulator) then qubit access providers (stub/mock-capable). If neither is available, emit exactly once per settle (unless explicit verbose mode) the line: "No ternary substrate or qubit access detected. Reverting to binary mode." Record probe attempts, selected substrate, and fallback reason as signed receipts, keep probes pluggable/future-proof, and verify zero measurable performance regression on standard binary path. / V4-SETTLE-001, V4-SETTLE-003, V4-SETTLE-005 / 9 / 0/1/2/client | 9 | -1/0/1/2/3/adapter |

### Settling DNA Detail Contract (Re-audit, 2026-03-11)

Source re-validated against Google Doc `1IPYlZqAROFpN0bjJvaE3dJfU9EPugXFDI9KLS6PIa0Q` (the canonical settling-origin draft).

Settled runtime DNA tuple (required on every settled artifact):
- `source_snapshot_hash` (signed vault snapshot root)
- `receipt_chain_root`
- `build_graph_hash`
- `policy_hash`
- `target_profile` (`binary`, `ternary-sim`, or approved plugin target id)
- `settle_generation` (monotonic counter)
- `runtime_hash` (active settled image hash)
- `revert_pointer` (snapshot id used by `--revert` / `edit-core`)
- `signature_set` (system + operator signer ids)

Layer ownership (authoritative):
- Layer `0`: settle command admission, capability checks, fail-closed deny path
- Layer `1`: settle policy, attestation/signing rules, retention, rollback authorization
- Layer `2`: compile/memory-map/re-exec runtime and differential module settle orchestration
- `client`: thin UX/CLI status surfaces only (`protheusctl settle`, `--revert`, `edit-core`, status panel), no compile/re-exec authority

Non-negotiable invariants:
1. No settle run without preflight parity suite, health check, and rollback checkpoint.
2. Revert/edit flows are atomic; partial live mutation is forbidden. On any failure, runtime remains on last known-good settled image.
3. Every settle/revert/edit transition emits deterministic receipts keyed by `settle_generation`.
4. Substrate probe/fallback order is deterministic and emits the canonical fallback line once per settle (unless explicit verbose mode).
5. Promotion/autogenesis-triggered settle runs honor cooldown and consent policies; skipped or denied runs are receipted with explicit reason codes.
6. `protheus-top` settled-state surface must show `runtime_hash`, `source_snapshot_hash`, target profile, last settle timestamp, and rollback readiness.

Required regression gate:
- `settle -> verify -> revert -> edit -> re-settle` round-trip test must pass with hash continuity, receipt continuity, and zero data loss.

## V4 1M User Scale Readiness Track (Operator Directive, 2026-03-02)

Objective: make million-user scale an engineered capability with explicit throughput/reliability/cost gates, not a narrative target.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V4-SCALE-001 | existing-coverage-validated | [scale-readiness / V4] Load Model + SLO Contract (1k -> 10k -> 100k -> 1M) | Scale claims are not actionable without one canonical load model, traffic envelope, and SLO/error-budget contract. | Define and version a canonical scale model (`DAU`, peak concurrency, RPS, payload mix, write/read ratios) with stage gates (`1k/10k/100k/1M`) and hard SLO/error-budget criteria; publish receipts from reproducible load profiles before each stage promotion. / V4-RUST-001, V3-RACE-221, V3-RACE-309 / 9 / 0/1/2/adapter | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V4-SCALE-002 | existing-coverage-validated | [scale-readiness / V4] Stateless App-Tier + Horizontal Autoscaling Contract | Control-plane/app-tier coupling still risks vertical bottlenecks and non-deterministic saturation behavior under burst load. | Enforce stateless worker contract for scale lanes, add autoscaling policy with saturation safeguards (CPU/client/memory/queue depth/latency), and prove horizontal scale behavior in staged load tests with rollback-safe scaling policies. / V4-SCALE-001, V3-RACE-202, V3-RACE-034 / 9 / 0/1/2/adapter | 10 | app |
| V4-SCALE-003 | existing-coverage-validated | [scale-readiness / V4] Durable Async Pipeline (Queue/Retry/Idempotency/Backpressure) | Million-user traffic requires async durability; missing idempotency/backpressure semantics will cause duplicate work and cascade failures. | Add durable queue contracts with idempotency keys, bounded retries, dead-letter handling, and explicit backpressure controls; verify no duplicate side effects and stable queue drain under stress/failure drills. / V4-SCALE-001, V3-RACE-078, V3-RACE-044 / 9 / 0/1/2/adapter | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V4-SCALE-004 | existing-coverage-validated | [scale-readiness / V4] Data Plane Scale Program (Partitioning + Read/Write Split + Online Migration) | Current data paths are not yet governed by one end-to-end partitioning and migration safety contract for high-scale write/read pressure. | Implement partitioning/sharding strategy, read/write split policy, and online migration runbooks with deterministic rollback; validate p95/p99 latency and correctness during re-shard/migration simulations before stage promotion. / V4-SCALE-001, V3-RACE-118, V3-RACE-044 / 9 / 0/1/2/adapter | 10 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V4-SCALE-005 | existing-coverage-validated | [scale-readiness / V4] Caching + Edge Delivery Contract (CDN + Hot-Key + Invalidation) | Without disciplined edge/cache contracts, backend saturation and stale-data bugs will block high-concurrency growth. | Add canonical cache hierarchy (edge/CDN + service cache + hot-key controls), strict invalidation semantics, and cache-health SLOs; prove backend load reduction and freshness correctness in staged load tests. / V4-SCALE-001, V3-RACE-174, V3-RACE-127 / 9 / 0/1/2/adapter | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V4-SCALE-006 | existing-coverage-validated | [scale-readiness / V4] Multi-Region Resilience + Disaster Recovery Drill Program | Single-region assumptions and untested failover paths are incompatible with million-user availability targets. | Implement active/standby or active/active multi-region contract with RTO/RPO objectives, deterministic failover/failback drills, and backup-restore verification receipts tied to release gating. / V4-SCALE-001, V3-RACE-057, V2-019, V4-002 / 9 / 0/1/2/adapter | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V4-SCALE-007 | existing-coverage-validated | [scale-readiness / V4] Release Safety at Scale (Canary + Feature Flags + Schema Compatibility) | High-scale deployments amplify blast radius; release controls must be mandatory and schema-safe under live traffic. | Enforce progressive delivery policy (canary ramps, automated rollback thresholds, feature-flag kill paths, forward/backward schema compatibility checks), with promotion blocked when safety receipts are missing. / V4-SCALE-001, V1H-002, V3-RACE-114 / 9 / 0/1/2/adapter | 10 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V4-SCALE-008 | existing-coverage-validated | [scale-readiness / V4] Observability + SRE Operations Maturity Pack | Million-user operations require full observability, actionable alerts, and practiced incident response beyond basic telemetry. | Complete metrics/tracing/logging coverage for critical flows, define page policies and runbooks with drill SLAs, and verify incident response performance in recurring game-days with auditable outcomes. / V4-SCALE-001, V1H-003, V3-RACE-012 / 9 / 0/1/2/adapter | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V4-SCALE-009 | existing-coverage-validated | [scale-readiness / V4] Abuse/Security Hardening at Scale (Rate-Limit + Tenant Isolation + Auth) | User growth increases attack/abuse surface and noisy-neighbor risk; baseline controls are insufficient without explicit scale hardening contracts. | Add tiered rate-limit/abuse controls, tenant-isolation invariants, and auth/session hardening with auditability; validate through adversarial load tests and fail-closed policy checks before each scale-stage promotion. / V4-SCALE-001, V1H-004, V3-RACE-051 / 10 / 0/1/2/adapter | 10 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V4-SCALE-010 | existing-coverage-validated | [scale-readiness / V4] Capacity + Unit Economics Governance (p95/p99 + Cost per User) | Scale can fail economically even when technically stable if cost/perf budgets are not enforced continuously. | Define capacity planning model and per-user cost guardrails, enforce latency/cost budgets in release gates, and emit periodic cost-efficiency receipts proving sustainable growth through each scale stage. / V4-SCALE-001, BL-038, V3-RACE-221 / 9 / 0/1/2/adapter | 10 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |

## Post-Launch Base Migration Readiness (6-12 Weeks)

Objective: migrate from OpenClaw base only after launch stability is proven and extraction can be done without degrading autonomy, safety, or rollback posture.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| PLM-001 | 6-12 weeks post-launch | done | 30-day operational stability streak | Prevent migration during churn/regression windows | Implemented PLM gate evaluator `client/runtime/systems/ops/post_launch_migration_readiness.ts` consuming execution/workflow reliability signals and producing auditable PLM-001 status in `state/ops/post_launch_migration_readiness/latest.json`. | 6 | 1/2/client |
| PLM-002 | 6-12 weeks post-launch | done | Runtime guardrail pass-rate gate | Migration should not begin if foundation checks are noisy/flaky | PLM gate pack now evaluates CI/guardrail health inputs and persists deterministic pass/fail telemetry for PLM-002 in `state/ops/post_launch_migration_readiness/receipts.jsonl`. | 6 | 1/2/client |
| PLM-003 | 6-12 weeks post-launch | done | JS exception floor before extraction | Avoid carrying legacy JS debt into new base | PLM gate pack enforces JS exception-floor evidence from `state/ops/js_holdout_audit/latest.json` with threshold policy in `client/runtime/config/post_launch_migration_readiness_policy.json`. | 6 | 1/2/client |
| PLM-004 | 6-12 weeks post-launch | done | Primitive/profile coverage threshold | Ensure abstraction-first architecture survives base split | PLM gate pack evaluates profile-driven execution coverage via adapter defragmentation ratio and emits PLM-004 check output in readiness artifacts. | 6 | 1/2/client/adapter |
| PLM-005 | 6-12 weeks post-launch | done | Canonical state portability contract | Preserve deterministic replay and continuity after extraction | PLM gate pack binds PLM-005 to state-kernel cutover parity/replay evidence (`state/ops/state_kernel_cutover/latest.json`) and records deterministic readiness results. | 6 | 1/2/client |
| PLM-006 | 6-12 weeks post-launch | done | Dual-run parity harness | Validate behavior equivalence before switching control-plane | PLM gate pack checks dual-run parity + profile compatibility (`state/ops/narrow_agent_parity_harness.json`, `state/ops/profile_compatibility_gate/latest.json`) and receipts PLM-006 status. | 6 | 1/2/client |
| PLM-007 | 6-12 weeks post-launch | done | Cutover/rollback playbook | Extraction must be reversible under incident pressure | Added formal playbook `docs/client/POST_LAUNCH_MIGRATION_READINESS.md` with rollback linkage to `docs/client/release/templates/rollback_plan.md`; PLM gate validates artifact presence and logs PLM-007 status. | 6 | 1/2/client |
| PLM-008 | 6-12 weeks post-launch | done | Independent bootstrap + packaging readiness | New base must be self-hosting, not manual-fragile | PLM gate pack verifies bootstrap/package readiness inputs (`state/ops/self_hosted_bootstrap/latest.json`, `state/ops/deployment_packaging/latest.json`) and emits PLM-008 decision telemetry. | 6 | 1/2/client |
| PLM-009 | 6-12 weeks post-launch | done | Security and secrets migration proof | Avoid introducing a weaker trust boundary in new base | PLM gate pack validates secret-rotation attestation + heartbeat/supply-chain health (`client/runtime/config/secret_rotation_attestation.json`, `state/security/remote_tamper_heartbeat/latest.json`, `state/security/supply_chain/latest.json`) for PLM-009. | 6 | 1/2/client |
| PLM-010 | 6-12 weeks post-launch | done | Final migration go/no-go review | Force explicit operator decision after evidence collection | Added signed final-review command (`final-review`) in `client/runtime/systems/ops/post_launch_migration_readiness.ts` with persisted decision artifact `state/ops/post_launch_migration_readiness/final_review.json`. | 6 | 1/2/client |

## Foundation Hardening Sprint (7-14 Days)

Objective: pause feature velocity briefly to harden core runtime abstractions so future revenue/capability work lands on deterministic, primitive-first foundations.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V2-FND-001 | existing-coverage-validated | [V2] Primitive Catalog + Universal Action Grammar Baseline | Prevent bespoke runtime spread by making primitive mapping mandatory immediately | Primitive catalog manifest exists with hard mapping gate for new execution features; CI blocks feature PRs without primitive/opcode mapping; aligns into `V3-042` / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 7 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V2-FND-002 | existing-coverage-validated | [V2] Event-Sourced Canonical State Baseline | Deterministic replay is required for phone->cluster scaling and safe debugging | Core execution lanes emit append-only event log with replay verifier and drift checks; baseline replay report generated per run; aligns into `V3-044` / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 7 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V2-FND-003 | existing-coverage-validated | [V2] Policy VM Separation Baseline | Governance logic must be isolated from runtime execution to prevent coupling drift | Policy evaluation lane extracted behind VM boundary for targeted core paths, with contract tests proving no direct policy branching inside execution primitives; aligns into `V3-044` / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 7 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V2-FND-004 | existing-coverage-validated | [V2] Foundation Contract CI Gate | Automatically enforce abstraction-first and anti-specialization rules | CI fails on bespoke branch growth where profile/primitive path exists, enforces abstraction debt budget, and requires dependency declarations for new runtime modules / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 10 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V2-FND-005 | existing-coverage-validated | [V2] Scale Envelope Continuous Testing Baseline | Validate invariant behavior across hardware envelopes before expanding autonomy/revenue lanes | Daily envelope tests run on phone-seed profile, desktop profile, and simulated cluster with deterministic outcome parity thresholds; emits regression receipts for release gating / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |

## Foundation Risk Register (Fast-Shipping Debt)

Objective: track high-impact foundation risks that can silently compound while shipping fast, with explicit ownership and dated mitigations.

| Risk ID | Status | Risk | Why It Can Bite Later | Mitigation / Linked Backlog | Owner | Target Date |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| RR-001 | done | Config and flag fragmentation | Contradictory policy/flag states create non-deterministic runtime behavior | Add precedence + conflict lint and block conflicting gate combinations in CI; linked: `V2-FND-004`, `V3-044` | Platform/Governance | 2026-03-08 |
| RR-002 | done | Duplicate execution pathways | Parallel lanes drift and produce inconsistent behavior under load | Define one canonical execution path per capability and deprecate alternates with expiry receipts; linked: `V3-039`, `V3-040` | Runtime | 2026-03-10 |
| RR-003 | done | Schema drift without migration discipline | Replay/federation break when events/profiles evolve ad hoc | Enforce N-2 schema contract + migration plans for any schema change; linked: `V3-049` | Data Plane | 2026-03-12 |
| RR-004 | done | Determinism gaps in replay | Debugging and cross-hardware parity become unreliable | Deterministic mode + replay parity as release blocker on core lanes; linked: `V2-FND-002`, `V2-FND-005`, `V3-044` | Runtime + QA | 2026-03-15 |
| RR-005 | done | Operational complexity debt | Organ/lane growth raises MTTR and hides failure modes | Enforce complexity budget and net-simplification gate for new organs; linked: `V3-052`, `V2-FND-004` | Architecture | 2026-03-18 |
| RR-006 | done | Key and secret lifecycle fragility | Strong runtime guards fail if rotation/recovery paths are weak | Rotate/revoke/recover drills with audited ceremonies and failover tests; linked: `V3-051` | Security | 2026-03-20 |
| RR-007 | done | Supply-chain trust gap | Build/dependency compromise bypasses runtime protections | Signed reproducible builds + SBOM + provenance verification in release CI; linked: `V3-048` | Build/Release | 2026-03-22 |
| RR-008 | done | Multi-node partition and split-brain semantics | Scale-out behavior can diverge and corrupt global state | Deterministic quorum + partition simulation tests + failover receipts; linked: `V3-046`, `V2-FND-005` | Distributed Systems | 2026-03-25 |
| RR-009 | done | Data retention/churn bloat | Storage and signal quality degrade over time | Retention tiers, compaction policy, and "why-kept" tags on long-lived artifacts; linked: `V2-FND-002`, `V3-053` | Data + Ops | 2026-03-27 |
| RR-010 | done | Human override ergonomics under incident stress | Safety controls fail in practice if operators cannot act quickly | Timed operator drills with runbook SLA gates and usability fixes; linked: `V2-057`, `V2-FND-005` | Ops/UX | 2026-03-30 |
| RR-011 | done | Catastrophic recovery gap | Total-loss scenarios can break continuity despite local self-healing | Implement deterministic resurrection protocol with tested recovery ceremonies; linked: `V3-056` | Continuity + Security | 2026-04-02 |
| RR-012 | done | Long-horizon value drift in Heroic Echo | Alignment can narrow subtly over years without explicit renewal and review | Add periodic value-anchor renewal with reversible updates and user review gates; linked: `V3-057` | Ethics/Governance | 2026-04-05 |
| RR-013 | done | Explainability debt at scale | Future operators/successors may not reconstruct why critical actions occurred | Require canonical explanation artifacts for major decisions and self-modifications; linked: `V3-058`, `V2-063` | Platform/Observability | 2026-04-07 |
| RR-014 | done | External world-model staleness | Tool/profile drift causes silent execution and compliance regressions | Add scheduled world-model freshness loop with staleness SLO gates; linked: `V3-060`, `V2-067`, `V3-039` | Research + Runtime | 2026-04-10 |

## Obsidian Track (Isolated from Core Protheus)
Implementation (2026-03-01): Delivered `client/runtime/systems/obsidian/obsidian_phase_pack.ts` (+ wrapper/policy/test) covering wisdom projection, ops cards, note-intent compiler, canvas generation, cross-view identity bus sync, plugin control lane, phone-seed bounded bundles, and resilience checks.

Policy: this track ships behind explicit feature flags and separate policies so Obsidian integration cannot bypass or destabilize core system lanes.

| Phase | Scope | Entry Gate | Exit Criteria |
|---|---|---|---|
| Phase 1 | Read/write foundation, governance-first | Core execution/security lanes stable; no unresolved P0 core incidents | Obsidian bridge runs read-only + governed write projection with receipts, no bypass paths |
| Phase 2 | Bi-directional intent + canvas intelligence | Phase 1 stable for 30 days, conflict/loop rates within policy | Note intents can compile to proposals safely, auto-canvas is canary-gated and reversible |
| Phase 3 | Unified mind projection + mobile seed mode | Phase 2 stable with acceptance precision target met | Holo and Obsidian share identity graph links; mobile plugin mode is bounded and policy-enforced |

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| OBS-001 | Phase 1 | done | Obsidian bridge contracts + policy root | Prevent uncontrolled vault access or action bypass via notes | Implemented `client/runtime/systems/obsidian/obsidian_bridge.ts` + `client/runtime/config/obsidian_bridge_policy.json` with typed contract loading, path allowlists, clearance thresholds, and deny-by-default projection controls | 6 | 1/2/client |
| OBS-002 | Phase 1 | done | Vault watcher sensory ingest (read-only) | Capture user knowledge flow safely before enabling any actuation coupling | Implemented `client/runtime/systems/obsidian/vault_watcher.ts` (read-only snapshot/diff ingest loop) with idempotent ingest event IDs and no write side effects outside governed bridge receipts | 6 | 1/2/client |
| OBS-003 | Phase 1 | done | Governed markdown projection lane | Human-readable outputs must appear in vault without editing user-authored content by surprise | Projection writes route through bridge policy roots only, with elevated override gates and projection markers for source tagging/traceability | 6 | 1/2/client |
| OBS-004 | Phase 1 | done | Sync integrity + conflict loop guard | Bidirectional sync can corrupt content without strict loop/conflict controls | Hash/idempotent ingest index, source tagging, projection loop suppression, deterministic conflict receipts, and regression coverage via `tests/client-memory-tools/obsidian_bridge.test.js` | 6 | 1/2/client |
| OBS-005 | Phase 1 | done | Wisdom projector v1 | First-principle outputs need durable, linkable memory on human side | New principles generate linked markdown notes with provenance, backlinks, and reference to corresponding holographic node IDs | 6 | 1/2/client |
| OBS-006 | Phase 1 | done | Doctor/execution visibility cards | Operational legibility should be available in Obsidian without reading raw logs | Rollbacks/heals/executions produce timestamped cards/notes with receipt links and status transitions | 6 | 1/2/client |
| OBS-007 | Phase 2 | done | Intent compiler from notes (shadow->canary->live) | Converting notes to action is high leverage and high risk | Note intents compile to proposals in shadow first; precision/false-positive gates must pass before any live path | 6 | 1/2/client |
| OBS-008 | Phase 2 | done | Automatic canvas intelligence | Users need structured planning artifacts generated from high-level intents | Canvas generator creates/updates node maps, linked notes, and live status blocks with reversible patch receipts | 6 | 1/2/client |
| OBS-009 | Phase 2 | done | Cross-view identity bus (Obsidian <-> Holo) | Two interfaces should behave like one mind, not separate tools | Shared entity IDs enable click/highlight sync between Obsidian graph/canvas and holo graph for mapped nodes | 6 | 1/2/client |
| OBS-010 | Phase 2 | done | Desktop Obsidian plugin (control + veto UI) | Human needs fast approval/veto at interface layer | Plugin exposes pending actions, approval/veto controls, and receipt drilldown with governance reason codes | 6 | 1/2/client |
| OBS-011 | Phase 3 | done | Phone-seed Obsidian plugin mode | Mobile continuity requires lightweight bounded integration | Mobile mode syncs selected vault segments with policy-bounded status/intent surfaces and no heavy background lanes | 6 | 1/2/client |
| OBS-012 | Phase 3 | done | Unified projection and resilience hardening | Final integration should feel seamless while remaining sovereign and reversible | Obsidian + holo projections share consistent state, survive reconnect/replay scenarios, and maintain zero policy-bypass findings in red-team tests | 6 | 1/2/client |

- `V3-ASSIM-014`..`V3-ASSIM-026` extend the same foundation (`V3-DUAL-001`, `V3-TASK-001`, `V3-ATTR-001`) and reuse existing primitive lanes instead of introducing bespoke tool organs.
- `V3-TASK-002` depends on `V3-TASK-001` and `V3-039` (universal execution primitive).
- `V3-ATTR-004` depends on `V3-ATTR-002`, `V3-ASSIM-017`, and `V3-BLK-001`.

- Recommended implementation order: `V3-ASSIM-004` -> `V3-ASSIM-005` -> `V3-ASSIM-006` -> `V3-ASSIM-007` -> `V3-ASSIM-008`.

## Devin Comparative Intake (Normalized to Primitive-First)

Objective: absorb the highest-leverage Devin-style behaviors (tight autonomous debug loop + full computer use) without adding bespoke platform logic or bypassing governance.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V3-PRIM-DBG-001 | existing-coverage-validated | [V3] Iterative Repair Primitive (`reproduce -> test -> critique -> patch -> verify`) | Turns self-debugging into a reusable primitive for code/workflow repair instead of one-off doctor scripts | Primitive contract exists with bounded iterations/timeouts, deterministic rollback points, per-step receipts, and support for both code targets and generated workflow artifacts / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 8 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V3-PRIM-UI-001 | existing-coverage-validated | [V3] Interactive Desktop Session Primitive | Provides a universal UI-actuation substrate for web + desktop tasks without tool-specific branching | Session lifecycle + typed action opcodes (`open`, `click`, `type`, `wait`, `assert`, `capture`) are available with deterministic replay IDs, artifact capture, and policy-gated risk classes / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 7 | -1/0/1/2/3/adapter |
| V3-ADD-UEP-DBG-001 | existing-coverage-validated | [V3] Universal Executor `code_repair` profile pack | Reuses the universal execution primitive for tight autonomous repair loops | `code_repair` profile supports sandboxed test/critique/patch/verify cycles with confidence/risk scoring, stop conditions, and integration into existing execution policies / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 6 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V3-ADD-UEP-UI-001 | existing-coverage-validated | [V3] Universal Executor `desktop_ui` profile pack | Extends existing computer-use lanes into full virtual desktop execution without creating bespoke organs | `desktop_ui` profile supports virtual session orchestration, UI assertions, screen recording/screenshot evidence, and high-risk action gating with human-veto windows / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V3-ADD-PASSPORT-001 | existing-coverage-validated | [V3] Passport Iteration Chain for Repair/UI Loops | High-frequency autonomous loops need full chain-of-custody, not only terminal receipts | Every iteration step is actor/model/tool-bound, hash-chained, signed/attested through passport lanes, and exportable from canonical JSON without provenance gaps / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 6 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V2-DEVIN-001 | existing-coverage-validated | [V2] Doctor/Forge Micro-Debug Lane (Profile-Driven) | Delivers practical Devin-style closed-loop self-fixing while preserving constitutional controls | Doctor/Forge invoke repair primitive in `shadow -> canary -> live` ladder with Heroic Echo + constitution checks, rollback on regression, and zero direct apply outside governed lanes / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 10 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V2-DEVIN-002 | existing-coverage-validated | [V2] Full Virtual Desktop Claw Lane | Enables UI-heavy real-world execution (including account flows) through generalized primitives | Real-world claws run end-to-end virtual desktop tasks under sandbox/policy controls with deterministic receipts, escalation gates, and recovery playbooks / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 8 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V2-DEVIN-003 | existing-coverage-validated | [V2] Account Creation as Profile Extension (No Bespoke Organ Branching) | Prevents account automation from becoming hardcoded platform logic | Account creation compiles into reusable `desktop_ui`/executor profiles + adapter metadata, with alias/verification vault integration and zero platform-specific kernel branches / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |

Dependency notes:
- `V3-ADD-UEP-DBG-001` depends on `V3-PRIM-DBG-001` and existing `V3-007`, `V3-038`.
- `V3-ADD-UEP-UI-001` depends on `V3-PRIM-UI-001` and extends existing `V3-ASSIM-016` (no duplicate organ lane).
- `V3-ADD-PASSPORT-001` depends on `V2-063` passport chain and `V3-SK-004` proposal/approval event normalization.
- `V2-DEVIN-001` depends on `V3-ADD-UEP-DBG-001`; `V2-DEVIN-002` depends on `V3-ADD-UEP-UI-001`; `V2-DEVIN-003` depends on `V2-066`, `V2-067`, and `V2-DEVIN-002`.

## Controlled Self-Improvement Intake (Normalized, 2026-02-27)

Objective: formalize recurring self-improvement operations around the existing two-gate model without introducing unsafe raw self-modification paths.

Primitives:
- No net-new primitive required (existing gated-loop primitive coverage is sufficient).

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

Regression coverage in `tests/client-memory-tools/self_improvement_cadence_orchestrator.test.js`. / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 10 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V3-LOOP-002 | existing-coverage-validated | [primitive-upgrade / V3] Two-Gate Evidence Pack Standard | Two-gate safety exists, but evidence quality/shape should be standardized to improve comparability and operator review speed | `client/runtime/systems/autonomy/gated_self_improvement_loop.ts` now emits canonical `self_improvement_evidence_pack` payloads (`risk`, `drift`, `yield`, `counterfactual`, `rollback_plan`, `confidence`) on run outputs and proposal history, including rollback receipt linkage; regression coverage in `tests/client-memory-tools/gated_self_improvement_loop.test.js`. / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 10 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V3-LOOP-003 | existing-coverage-validated | [hardening / V3] Human Oversight SLA + Reversion Drill for Self-Mod Lanes | Long-run safety depends on tested intervention speed and repeatable rollback drills, not just static controls | Delivered `client/runtime/systems/autonomy/self_mod_reversion_drill.ts` + `client/runtime/config/self_mod_reversion_drill_policy.json`; drill lane executes rollback tests against latest self-mod proposal, enforces SLA timing, writes deterministic drill receipts, and exposes freshness-based `promotion_blocked` status when drill age exceeds policy threshold. Regression coverage in `tests/client-memory-tools/self_mod_reversion_drill.test.js`. / 8 / 1/2/client/adapter | 10 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V3-LOOP-004 | existing-coverage-validated | [primitive-upgrade / V3] Loop-5 Trigger Bridge (Nightly + High-Success Receipt Activation) Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/backlog_runtime_anchors/v3_loop_004_anchor.ts`. | Cadence-only execution misses inflection moments; high-success signals should trigger governed self-critique/mutation passes automatically | Extended `client/runtime/systems/autonomy/self_improvement_cadence_orchestrator.ts` + `client/runtime/config/self_improvement_cadence_policy.json` with `trigger` command, confidence/strategy-score/cooldown gates (`min_confidence=0.997`), and source allowlist; trigger runs remain shadow-first by policy and emit deterministic trigger receipts with gate telemetry. / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 7 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V3-LOOP-005 | existing-coverage-validated | [extension / V3] Evolutionary Trial Cell Export from Shadow Primitives | Shadow primitives should be first-class seeds for evolutionary trial lanes instead of ad-hoc proposal artifacts | Cadence runs now emit standardized `trial_cells` metadata per cycle (`trial_cell_id`, profile, stage, apply flags, status) from shadow propose/run outputs, enabling downstream evolutionary cell schedulers without bypassing existing propose->trial->report->apply gates. / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
