## Signal Intelligence Upgrade Intake (Second-Order Detection/Analysis, 2026-03-01)

Objective: move from first-order keyword/topic detection to second-order inference quality (implication, uncertainty, disconfirmation, temporal drift, and objective-path reasoning) without weakening execution/safety gates.

### Technical Requirements Mapping (Second-Order Inference)

| Derived Requirement | Canonical Backlog Handling | Status | Requirement Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| Recover high-value negative signal (`tried X`, `failed`, `blocked`, `rejected`) instead of dropping as noise | `V3-RACE-078` | done | Add negative-signal extraction + salvage routing lane with typed failure semantics and explicit queue admission behavior. |
| Infer latent intent (`X implies likely need Y`) and validate implications before action | `V3-RACE-079` | done | Add implication inference graph with evidence spans + validator contracts to control false-positive drift. |
| Track cross-temporal pattern shifts (tone/frequency/emphasis deltas) | `V3-RACE-080` | done | Add temporal delta engine with daily/weekly change metrics and anomaly receipts per topic/objective. |
| Replace binary thresholds with calibrated confidence scoring | `V3-RACE-081` | done | Add probabilistic confidence + calibration metrics (e.g., Brier/reliability) to ranking/admission paths. |
| Support counterfactual replay (`would this have mattered 6 months ago?`) | `V3-RACE-082` | done | Add historical replay harness for detector versions and compare signal yield/precision across time windows. |
| Add adversarial hypothesis generation (why signal might be wrong) | `V3-RACE-083` | done | Add disconfirmation challenger lane that generates/validates anti-hypotheses before promotion. |
| Build multi-hop chain mapping from signal sources to T1 objective impact | `V3-RACE-084` | done | Add chain scorer (`eye -> topic -> implication -> objective`) with explainable path receipts. |
| Ingest non-textual signal (image/repo/market micro-activity) into the same inference plane | `V3-RACE-085` | done | Add non-text feature adapters and normalized schema so cross-signal correlation includes non-text lanes. |

### Net-New Canonical Queue

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V3-RACE-078 | existing-coverage-validated | [hardening / V3] Negative-Signal Recovery & Salvage Lane | Valuable demand/opportunity signals are often expressed as failure/friction and are currently under-captured. | `cross_signal_engine` now emits typed `negative_signal` hypotheses with extracted failure/friction terms, support evidence, and salvage trace keys; regression coverage validates negative-signal extraction/preservation alongside convergence outputs. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/backlog_lane_batch_delivery.ts`. / V3-RACE-069, V3-RACE-071, V3-RACE-072 / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V3-RACE-079 | existing-coverage-validated | [primitive-upgrade / V3] Latent Intent Inference Graph | First-order tagging misses implied needs, causing under-actuation on high-opportunity weak signals. | Added `client/runtime/systems/sensory/latent_intent_inference_graph.{ts,js}` + `client/runtime/config/latent_intent_inference_policy.json` to emit `implied_need` edges with evidence spans/confidence from cross-signal hypotheses, enforce false-positive-ceiling validator contracts, and persist receipted outputs under `state/sensory/analysis/latent_intent/`; regression coverage in `tests/client-memory-tools/latent_intent_inference_graph.test.js`. / V3-RACE-078, V3-RACE-009 / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V3-RACE-080 | existing-coverage-validated | [hardening / V3] Cross-Temporal Signal Delta Engine | Point-in-time snapshots hide momentum/reversal patterns that matter for prioritization. | `cross_signal_engine` now computes per-topic temporal deltas (`volume_delta_day`, `volume_delta_baseline`, `tone_delta`, `emphasis_delta`) with anomaly thresholds, emits `temporal_delta` hypotheses, and persists ranked `temporal_deltas` report output for downstream prioritization. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/backlog_lane_batch_delivery.ts`. / V3-RACE-069, V3-RACE-074 / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V3-RACE-081 | existing-coverage-validated | [hardening / V3] Confidence Calibration & Probability Contracts | Binary pass/fail confidence loses ranking quality and obscures uncertainty management. | Cross-signal hypotheses now carry calibrated probabilities (`probability_contract`), emit reliability bins + Brier artifacts, and enforce strict calibration contracts via `--strict=1` gate behavior when Brier exceeds policy thresholds. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/backlog_runtime_anchors/v3_race_081_anchor.ts`. / V3-RACE-069, V3-RACE-062 / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V3-RACE-082 | existing-coverage-validated | [extension / V3] Counterfactual Signal Replay Harness | Detector changes need historical context to avoid overfitting current traffic conditions. | Added `client/runtime/systems/sensory/counterfactual_signal_replay.{ts,js}` + `client/runtime/config/counterfactual_signal_replay_policy.json` to replay historical windows (default 180-day offset), compute precision/recall uplift deltas, and emit promotion-block signals on negative uplift; receipted outputs persist under `state/sensory/analysis/counterfactual_replay/` with regression coverage in `tests/client-memory-tools/counterfactual_signal_replay.test.js`. / V3-RACE-003, V3-RACE-017, V3-RACE-081 / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V3-RACE-083 | existing-coverage-validated | [hardening / V3] Adversarial Hypothesis Challenger Lane | Unchallenged high-confidence signals can be wrong and produce confident misallocation. | Added `client/runtime/systems/sensory/adversarial_hypothesis_challenger.{ts,js}` + `client/runtime/config/adversarial_hypothesis_challenger_policy.json` to generate disconfirmation claims for high-priority hypotheses, verify challenger outcomes (`win`/`unresolved`/`loss`), and block promotion when unresolved or winning challengers remain; receipted outputs persist under `state/sensory/analysis/adversarial_challenger/` with regression coverage in `tests/client-memory-tools/adversarial_hypothesis_challenger.test.js`. / V3-RACE-081, V3-RACE-042 / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V3-RACE-084 | existing-coverage-validated | [primitive-upgrade / V3] Multi-Hop Objective Chain Mapper | Signal-to-objective linkage is currently shallow, limiting explainability and prioritization quality. | Added `client/runtime/systems/sensory/multi_hop_objective_chain_mapper.{ts,js}` + `client/runtime/config/multi_hop_objective_chain_policy.json` to fuse cross-signal hypotheses and latent-intent edges into explainable four-hop paths (`eye -> topic -> implication -> objective`) with confidence-weighted ranking receipts under `state/sensory/analysis/objective_chain_mapper/`; regression coverage in `tests/client-memory-tools/multi_hop_objective_chain_mapper.test.js`. / V3-RACE-078, V3-RACE-079, V3-RACE-080, V3-RACE-081 / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V3-RACE-085 | existing-coverage-validated | [extension / V3] Multimodal Signal Adapter Plane (Non-Text Features) | Important activity signal exists outside text and is currently weakly integrated into inference. | Added `client/runtime/systems/sensory/multimodal_signal_adapter_plane.{ts,js}` + `client/runtime/config/multimodal_signal_adapter_policy.json` to normalize repo/image/market non-text features into one bounded-influence adapter stream (`state/sensory/analysis/multimodal_adapter/`) with strict source requirements and audit receipts; regression coverage in `tests/client-memory-tools/multimodal_signal_adapter_plane.test.js`. / V3-RACE-007, V3-RACE-041, V3-RACE-084 / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |

### Signal Intelligence Mapping

| Derived Gap | Canonical Queue Mapping |
|---|---|
| Negative signal loss | `V3-RACE-078` |
| Latent intent blind spot | `V3-RACE-079` |
| Cross-temporal pattern blindness | `V3-RACE-080` |
| Uncalibrated confidence | `V3-RACE-081` |
| No counterfactual visibility | `V3-RACE-082` |

| Missing multi-hop objective linkage | `V3-RACE-084` |

## Analysis Learning Kernel Intake (Detector Quality Compounding, 2026-03-01)

Objective: make detection/analysis quality improve over time through explicit measurement, challenger promotion logic, uncertainty contracts, and source/truth weighting.

### Technical Requirements Mapping (Quality Compounding)

| Derived Requirement | Canonical Backlog Handling | Status | Requirement Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| Build a gold evaluation corpus with blind scoring for detector quality | `V3-RACE-086` | done | Add labeled benchmark sets + blind evaluation runner with precision/recall/F1/calibration metrics and promotion gating receipts. |
| Add champion/challenger detector pipeline in shadow before live promotion | `V3-RACE-087` | done | Run candidate analyzers in parallel, compare uplift vs active detector, and only promote on policy-defined non-regression evidence. |
| Add abstain/uncertainty contract (`insufficient evidence`) to avoid forced low-confidence decisions | `V3-RACE-088` | done | Extend detector outputs with abstain semantics, enforce abstain-aware routing in admission/ranking, and track abstain-to-resolution outcomes. |
| Build dynamic source reliability graph (domain/author/source trust weighting over time) | `V3-RACE-089` | done | Add source trust score lane with decay/recovery rules and calibration against downstream truth outcomes. |
| Distinguish causal vs correlational signals in ranking | `V3-RACE-090` | done | Add causal-evidence scorer and penalize correlation-only hypotheses unless validated by replay/outcome receipts. |
| Add value-of-information planner to steer collection budget toward uncertainty reduction | `V3-RACE-091` | done | Route sensing/analysis budget by expected uncertainty reduction on T1 objectives with measurable decision uplift receipts. |
| Track full hypothesis lifecycle (`proposed -> tested -> accepted/rejected -> outcome`) as first-class ledger | `V3-RACE-092` | done | Add hypothesis ledger schema + status transitions + closure attribution to improve detector feedback loops. |
| Add detector error taxonomy + guarded auto-retuning | `V3-RACE-093` | done | Classify FP/FN error classes, auto-adjust thresholds/features within bounded policy limits, and require rollback on regression. |
| Add ensemble disagreement lane with escalation on high divergence | `V3-RACE-094` | done | Run independent analyzers and escalate when disagreement exceeds policy thresholds instead of silently averaging. |
| Add novelty/saturation detector to prioritize marginal-value signal | `V3-RACE-095` | done | Compute novelty vs saturation scores and down-rank over-covered topics while preserving high-value anomaly surfacing. |

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

| V3-RACE-087 | existing-coverage-validated | [hardening / V3] Champion/Challenger Detector Promotion Pipeline | Analysis changes need safe comparative rollout to prevent silent degradation. | Added `client/runtime/systems/sensory/champion_challenger_detector_promotion.{ts,js}` + `client/runtime/config/champion_challenger_detector_policy.json` to run parallel champion/challenger scoring on the same gold rows, compute uplift/regression deltas (`precision/recall/F1/Brier`), and fail promotion on policy violations with receipted outputs under `state/sensory/analysis/champion_challenger/`; regression coverage in `tests/client-memory-tools/champion_challenger_detector_promotion.test.js`. / V3-RACE-086, V3-RACE-062 / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V3-RACE-088 | existing-coverage-validated | [hardening / V3] Abstain/Uncertainty Output Contract | Forced low-confidence decisions increase false positives and downstream churn. | Added `client/runtime/systems/sensory/abstain_uncertainty_contract.{ts,js}` + `client/runtime/config/abstain_uncertainty_contract_policy.json` to emit abstain reason codes for weak-evidence hypotheses, route abstained cases to review queue, and track abstain-resolution outcomes in receipted artifacts under `state/sensory/analysis/abstain_uncertainty/`; regression coverage in `tests/client-memory-tools/abstain_uncertainty_contract.test.js`. / V3-RACE-081, V3-RACE-069 / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V3-RACE-089 | existing-coverage-validated | [primitive-upgrade / V3] Dynamic Source Reliability Graph | Flat source weighting ignores reliability drift and harms analysis precision. | Added `client/runtime/systems/sensory/dynamic_source_reliability_graph.{ts,js}` + `client/runtime/config/dynamic_source_reliability_graph_policy.json` to update per-source reliability scores from hypothesis truth outcomes with bounded per-event influence, neutral-decay behavior, and calibration receipts under `state/sensory/analysis/source_reliability/`; regression coverage in `tests/client-memory-tools/dynamic_source_reliability_graph.test.js`. / V3-RACE-078, V3-RACE-081 / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V3-RACE-090 | existing-coverage-validated | [hardening / V3] Causal-vs-Correlational Signal Scorer | Correlation-heavy ranking overstates weak hypotheses and misallocates execution bandwidth. | Added `client/runtime/systems/sensory/causal_vs_correlation_signal_scorer.{ts,js}` + `client/runtime/config/causal_vs_correlation_signal_scorer_policy.json` to combine chain confidence, replay uplift, and source reliability into causal scores, apply bounded penalties to correlation-only paths, and emit ranked receipts under `state/sensory/analysis/causal_signal_scorer/`; regression coverage in `tests/client-memory-tools/causal_vs_correlation_signal_scorer.test.js`. / V3-RACE-082, V3-RACE-084, V3-RACE-089 / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V3-RACE-091 | existing-coverage-validated | [extension / V3] Value-of-Information Collection Planner | Detection budget should prioritize uncertainty reduction, not raw volume. | Added `client/runtime/systems/sensory/value_of_information_collection_planner.{ts,js}` + `client/runtime/config/value_of_information_collection_planner_policy.json` to rank collection actions by expected information gain from uncertainty and objective-impact signals, including predicted uncertainty-reduction receipts under `state/sensory/analysis/value_information_planner/`; regression coverage in `tests/client-memory-tools/value_of_information_collection_planner.test.js`. / V3-RACE-080, V3-RACE-081, V3-RACE-084 / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V3-RACE-092 | existing-coverage-validated | [primitive-upgrade / V3] Hypothesis Lifecycle Ledger | Detector learning is weak without explicit end-to-end hypothesis outcome memory. | Added `client/runtime/systems/sensory/hypothesis_lifecycle_ledger.{ts,js}` + `client/runtime/config/hypothesis_lifecycle_ledger_policy.json` to persist hypothesis state transitions (`proposed/tested/accepted/rejected`) with challenger/outcome linkage into a durable ledger and lifecycle state artifacts under `state/sensory/analysis/hypothesis_lifecycle/`; regression coverage in `tests/client-memory-tools/hypothesis_lifecycle_ledger.test.js`. / V3-RACE-083, V3-RACE-084 / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V3-RACE-093 | existing-coverage-validated | [hardening / V3] Detector Error Taxonomy + Guarded Auto-Retuning | Manual threshold tuning does not scale with detector complexity and drift. | Added `client/runtime/systems/sensory/detector_error_taxonomy_autotune.{ts,js}` + `client/runtime/config/detector_error_taxonomy_autotune_policy.json` to classify FP/FN error buckets, propose bounded threshold retunes, and enforce rollback-on-regression safeguards with receipts under `state/sensory/analysis/detector_autotune/`; regression coverage in `tests/client-memory-tools/detector_error_taxonomy_autotune.test.js`. / V3-RACE-086, V3-RACE-087, V3-RACE-092 / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 10 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V3-RACE-094 | existing-coverage-validated | [hardening / V3] Ensemble Disagreement Escalation Lane | Single-analyzer certainty can mask blind spots on ambiguous/high-risk signals. | Added `client/runtime/systems/sensory/ensemble_disagreement_escalation_lane.{ts,js}` + `client/runtime/config/ensemble_disagreement_escalation_policy.json` to compute ensemble divergence metrics (stddev/range), apply risk-tier thresholds, and route high-divergence cases to adjudication with receipts under `state/sensory/analysis/ensemble_disagreement/`; regression coverage in `tests/client-memory-tools/ensemble_disagreement_escalation_lane.test.js`. / V3-RACE-083, V3-RACE-087 / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V3-RACE-095 | existing-coverage-validated | [extension / V3] Novelty/Saturation Prioritization Engine | Over-covered topics consume bandwidth while low-frequency novel signals are under-prioritized. | Added `client/runtime/systems/sensory/novelty_saturation_prioritization_engine.{ts,js}` + `client/runtime/config/novelty_saturation_prioritization_policy.json` to score topics against historical frequency baselines, compute novelty/saturation/anomaly signals, and emit prioritized outputs/receipts under `state/sensory/analysis/novelty_saturation/`; regression coverage in `tests/client-memory-tools/novelty_saturation_prioritization_engine.test.js`. / V3-RACE-080, V3-RACE-091 / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |

## Analysis Governance & Reproducibility Intake (2026-03-01)

Objective: harden the analysis layer with explicit quality governance, reproducibility, shift decomposition, and safe promotion/rollback contracts so detector evolution remains trustworthy at scale.

### Technical Requirements Mapping (Governance + Reproducibility)

| Derived Requirement | Canonical Backlog Handling | Status | Requirement Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| Enforce dedicated analysis-quality SLOs independent from execution SLOs | `V3-RACE-096` | done | Add detector SLO contract (precision/recall/calibration/abstain) with merge/promotion gates separate from shipped-execution metrics. |
| Add ground-truth governance (label adjudication + agreement metrics) | `V3-RACE-097` | done | Introduce labeling governance workflow, reviewer-agreement thresholds, and low-confidence label quarantine. |
| Require end-to-end feature/data versioning for detector reproducibility | `V3-RACE-098` | done | Version feature sets, schema hashes, and detector bundles so every analysis decision can be replayed exactly. |
| Decompose distribution shift into source/topic/style components | `V3-RACE-099` | done | Add shift-decomposition engine that identifies where drift originates and routes targeted remediation. |
| Require causal validation (replay/intervention evidence) for high-impact claims | `V3-RACE-100` | done | Add causal-validation gate for analysis outputs that materially change ranking/admission behavior. |
| Add active-learning queue for high-uncertainty/high-disagreement cases | `V3-RACE-101` | done | Route ambiguous cases into labeling/review loop and feed accepted labels back into challenger/eval lanes. |
| Add offline statistical lab bridge (R/Python optional, artifact-only) | `V3-RACE-102` | done | Support offline calibration/causal diagnostics with signed artifacts consumed by production gates; keep runtime TS/Rust. |
| Add optional R analytics runner with research-organ best-fit integration (artifact-verified uplift only) | `V3-RACE-361` | done | Add optional offline runner (R or TS fallback) that emits signed artifacts into offline bridge and applies bounded research confidence uplift only when verified fit criteria pass. |
| Add sensitivity/privacy-aware signal scoring contract | `V3-RACE-103` | done | Add policy-aware handling for sensitive classes with restricted usage, masking, and audit receipts. |
| Add detector rollback/migration safety contract | `V3-RACE-104` | done | Provide one-command detector rollback with parity replay evidence and schema compatibility checks. |
| Add cross-objective interference guard for analysis changes | `V3-RACE-105` | done | Prevent detector improvements for one objective from degrading other objective lanes beyond policy bounds. |

## Architecture Simplification & Scale Intake (No-Regression Refactors, 2026-03-01)

Objective: simplify structural complexity and improve scalability/operability without reducing current function, reliability, robustness, or safety posture.

### Technical Requirements Mapping (Refactor/Simplify)

| Derived Requirement | Canonical Backlog Handling | Status | Requirement Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| Remove overlap between `merge_guard`, `foundation_contract_gate`, and `contract_check` using one manifest-driven check registry | `V3-RACE-106` | done | Added canonical guard check manifest (`client/runtime/config/guard_check_registry.json`) + shared loader/runtime (`client/runtime/systems/ops/guard_check_registry.ts`) consumed by guard entrypoints with parity regression tests. |
| Unify policy loading/validation/defaulting into one runtime primitive | `V3-RACE-107` | done | Added shared policy runtime primitive (`client/runtime/lib/policy_runtime.ts`) and migrated active client/memory/control-plane lanes to consume canonical load/merge/path behavior with regression coverage. |
| Reduce high `spawnSync` fan-out in operational hot paths via worker/daemon shift | `V3-RACE-108` | done | Migrate top spawn-heavy lanes to long-lived workers/daemons with CLI fallback retained only for emergency compatibility. |
| Normalize `state/` artifact contracts (`latest/history/receipt`, retention, schema/freshness) | `V3-RACE-109` | done | Added shared state artifact contract primitive (`client/runtime/lib/state_artifact_contract.ts`) and migrated active memory lanes to canonical latest/history/receipt helpers with regression coverage. |
| Simplify memory transport behavior behind one strict abstraction | `V3-RACE-110` | done | Consolidate daemon/cli/fallback logic under one contract (`transport`, detail, retry class, cooldown semantics) with uniform telemetry. |
| Split backlog monolith into canonical machine-readable registry + generated views | `V3-RACE-111` | done | Keep one authoritative backlog source and generate markdown views (`active`, `archive`, `human`) to reduce manual drift. |
| Define one pluggable analysis-kernel stage interface (`detect -> infer -> calibrate -> challenge -> score -> emit`) | `V3-RACE-112` | done | Introduce stage contracts + adapter registry so analysis lanes evolve modularly without pipeline fragmentation. |
| Continue TS/JS compatibility-tail retirement where parity is proven | `V3-RACE-113` | done | Retire dual-path runtime logic and keep only explicit wrappers where compatibility is required by policy. |

### Net-New Canonical Queue

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V3-RACE-106 | existing-coverage-validated | [hardening / V3] Unified Guard Check Registry (Manifest-Driven Gates) | Duplicated check definitions across guard entrypoints create maintenance drag and drift risk. | Single manifest defines checks/dependencies/strictness; `merge_guard`/`foundation_contract_gate`/`contract_check` consume generated plans; non-regression harness proves parity of pass/fail outcomes. | V3-RACE-107 | existing-coverage-validated | [hardening / V3] Shared Policy Runtime Primitive | Repeated ad-hoc policy parsing/defaulting raises contract drift and bug surface area. | Central policy runtime handles schema/default/coercion/path resolution; migrated lanes show no behavior regressions and reduced parser duplication. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/backlog_github_sync.ts`. / BL-024, V3-RACE-CONF-007 / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V3-RACE-108 | existing-coverage-validated | [primitive-upgrade / V3] Spawn Fan-Out Reduction Wave (Worker/Daemon Core) | Sync process fan-out limits throughput and increases latency/jitter under scale. | Spawn broker now resolves router hardware plans in-process via model router exports (no per-call shell spawn on default path), preserves emergency spawn sync fallback for custom/router-failure lanes, and emits transport telemetry (hardware plan transport) with regression coverage in `tests/client-memory-tools/spawn_broker.test.js`. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/backlog_runtime_anchors/v3_race_108_anchor.ts`. / V3-RACE-063, V3-RACE-064, V3-OPS-005 / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |

## Control Plane OS-Readiness Delta Intake (2026-03-01)

Objective: consolidate the remaining "not done / not fully ready" control-plane blockers into one explicit readiness queue so promotion to full OS-ready operation is auditable and deterministic.

### Readiness Mapping (Not Done / Not Ready)

| Not-Ready Requirement | Canonical Backlog Handling | Status | Requirement Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| Complete Rust spine extraction of remaining control-plane hot/security paths | `V3-RACE-034` | done | Keep as first technical unlock for full control-plane readiness and remove remaining JS/V8 hot-path exposure. |
| Add independent out-of-band safety veto plane | `V3-RACE-050` | done | Keep as mandatory correlated-fault/supply-chain containment lane before "100% ready" claim. |
| Remove duplicated guard check definitions across gate entrypoints | `V3-RACE-106` | done | Manifest-driven check registry is live and consumed by merge/foundation/contract guard lanes with regression coverage. |
| Unify policy loading/default/coercion runtime | `V3-RACE-107` | done | Shared policy runtime primitive is live and migrated in active client/memory/control-plane lanes with regression tests. |
| Reduce remaining spawn fan-out in operational hot paths | `V3-RACE-108` | done | Shift target lanes to long-lived worker/daemon contracts with benchmark/SLO receipts. |
| Normalize state artifact contracts (`latest/history/receipt`, retention/freshness) | `V3-RACE-109` | done | Canonical artifact contract helper and migrated lanes are live with receipt/history/layout checks. |
| Unify memory transport semantics/telemetry | `V3-RACE-110` | done | Enforce one transport abstraction (daemon/cli/fallback) with parity and normalized telemetry contracts. |
| Retire dual TS/JS compatibility tail where parity is proven | `V3-RACE-113` | done | Remove non-essential dual paths while preserving policy-approved compatibility wrappers only. |
| Exit default shadow/local authority posture for control-plane policies | `V3-RACE-114` | done | Add explicit live-activation gate with reversible emergency fallback and soak evidence before default-live promotion. |

### Net-New Canonical Queue

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V3-RACE-114 | existing-coverage-validated | [hardening / V3] Control-Plane Live Activation & Shadow Exit Gate | Core control-plane policies still default to shadow/local modes, so the platform is operational but not yet default-live OS-ready. | Policy-governed live profile is promoted with deterministic rollback: `protheus_control_plane` not shadow-by-default, `event_sourced_control_plane` runs stream authority + JetStream publish in live mode, and `rust_control_plane_cutover` exits shadow with soak/SLO/parity receipts and tested emergency fallback toggle. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/control_plane_live_activation_shadow_exit_gate.ts`. / V3-RACE-017, V3-RACE-021, V3-RACE-034, V3-RACE-041 / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |

## Technical Quality Intake (Valid Grok-Derived Requirements, 2026-03-01)

Objective: capture only the technically valid, non-duplicative engineering requirements from the latest external critique and map them into canonical queue IDs.

### Valid Requirement Mapping

| Derived Requirement | Canonical Backlog Handling | Status | Requirement Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| Script surface is too large to govern safely (`package.json` script sprawl) | `V3-RACE-115` | done | Add command-registry and script-surface rationalization lane with compatibility aliases + CI guardrails. |
| Entrypoint runtime contract remains over-dependent on wrapper/bootstrap indirection | `V3-RACE-116` | done | Standardize dev/prod entrypoint runtime contracts (`tsx`/dist) and constrain bootstrap path to explicit compatibility-only fallback. |
| Architectural layer boundaries need machine-enforced import contracts | `V3-RACE-117` | done | Add dependency-boundary manifest + CI enforcement for cross-layer imports and cycle prevention. |
| Runtime state still mixes hot-path and artifact-path contracts in places | `V3-RACE-118` | done | Formalize hot-state vs audit-state tiering and enforce low-latency source-of-truth reads from runtime stores. |
| CI quality signals need one standardized scorecard beyond pass/fail | `V3-RACE-119` | done | Add unified CI quality scorecard (coverage + flake + duration budgets + critical-lane gates) with fail-closed thresholds. |
| Runtime/config tooling still contains hardcoded absolute workspace paths | `V3-RACE-120` | done | Add relocatable path contract and remove absolute host paths from runtime/client/runtime/config/tooling surfaces. |
| Package manifest is missing standard metadata expected by npm/security/release tooling | `V3-RACE-121` | done | Enforce package-manifest standards (`name`, `version`, `private`, `license`, `description`, `repository`, `engines`, publish guards) with CI check. |
| Public-facing repo root still mixes professional docs with persona-specific identity artifacts | `V3-RACE-122` | done | Segment persona/private identity docs into internal namespace and keep public root/docs operator-focused + enterprise-readable. |
| CI workflows overlap and duplicate core lint/test signals on push/PR | `V3-RACE-123` | done | Rationalize workflow graph so required checks are unique, stable, and non-duplicative while preserving gate coverage. |
| Root-level artifact sprawl hurts first impression and discoverability | `V3-RACE-124` | done | Define root-surface contract, relocate specialized artifacts to namespaced paths, and enforce with root-lint check. |
| Legal/license wording is technically enforceable but can create avoidable trust friction | `V3-RACE-125` | done | Normalize legal copy to plain professional language and isolate defensive/security posture into dedicated policy docs. |
| Full CI still fails due integrity sealing drift (`integrity_kernel` unsealed files) | `V3-RACE-126` | done | Integrity baseline is sealed and deterministic regressions were fixed so `npm test` is green in strict lane (`530/530`). |
| External-facing repository surface remains noisier than ideal for enterprise review | `V3-RACE-127` | done | Added public/operator profile contract + docs aliases (`docs/client/PUBLIC_OPERATOR_PROFILE.md` + expanded `docs_surface_contract`) with regression lint enforcement. |
| Script surface remains too broad for fast operational onboarding despite new registry controls | `V3-RACE-128` | done | Executed Wave II script reduction lane (`script_surface_reduction_wave2`): retired 213 non-critical `:status` aliases, preserved critical/referenced scripts, and published command-map diff receipts. |

## External Requirements Intake (Google Doc `1cMaJfBYCTUvCmAbpe3npLqFABbK9zHHnPEoaQrZ5Xbs`, 2026-03-02)

Objective: normalize the 8-feature sovereign economy/identity expansion into canonical backlog lanes while preserving strict data-scope boundaries:
- user-specific data goes in `client/memory/` and `client/cognition/adaptive/`
- permanent/shared runtime logic goes in `client/runtime/systems/`, `client/runtime/config/`, and `docs/client/`
- no permanent non-user policy/runtime state is stored as authoritative data in `client/memory/` or `client/cognition/adaptive/`

### Requirement Mapping (from intake)

| Requirement (from intake) | Canonical Backlog Handling | Status | Requirement Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| Soul-bound immutable user contracts with guarded amendments | `V3-RACE-129` | done | Add a canonical soul-contract primitive with encrypted user contract storage + guard enforcement + dual-signature amendment flow. |
| Native token + mandatory base tithe + optional global objective fund | `V3-RACE-130` | done | Keep existing compute-tithe/blockchain bridges and add governed token/fund layer with user-level allocation controls. |
| Seed instances with inherited directives/memory and parent tithe routing | `V3-RACE-131` | done | Extend spawn contracts for seed lineage inheritance and optional parent-route tithe contracts. |
| Configurable civic duty cycle allocation for public-good objectives | `V3-RACE-132` | done | Add civic-duty planner with user-level allocation policy and bounded scheduling constraints. |
| Peer GPU lending between users/instances with contractual terms | `V3-RACE-133` | done | Extend GPU contribution flywheel into governed peer lending + settlement lanes. |
| Dynamic visual signature per instance, derived from identity/contribution history | `V3-RACE-134` | done | Add deterministic visual-signature generation and display contracts with bounded evolution rules. |
| Pinnacle-tech research/proposal engine (CRDT/ZK/FHE/DID/IPFS/WASM/etc.) | `V3-RACE-135` | done | Add structured breakthrough-scanning and governed integration proposal lanes (research/proposal only by default). |
| Full cross-organ integration + docs updates for economy/identity pack | `V3-RACE-136` | done | Add integration harness/docs contract tying soul, economy, spawn, guard, fractal, warden, and jigsaw lanes together with data-scope checks. |

## External Requirements Intake (Google Doc `1TZ5u3gQYS1U9hqB8KxSBP5Jz1NkaZDlpdIBd8uaa8Ys`, 2026-03-02)

Objective: normalize the 7-technology "pinnacle integration layer" directive into staged, risk-governed backlog lanes with strict data-scope separation.

Data boundary invariants for this intake:
- user-specific intent/contracts/identity/profile data only in `client/memory/` and `client/cognition/adaptive/`
- permanent runtime logic, schemas, cryptography, and policy only in `client/runtime/systems/`, `client/runtime/config/`, and `docs/client/`
- no permanent non-user source-of-truth is authored under `client/memory/` or `client/cognition/adaptive/`

### Requirement Mapping (from intake)

| Requirement (from intake) | Canonical Backlog Handling | Status | Requirement Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| Monolithic `V3-RACE-046` "Pinnacle Tech Integration Layer" | decomposed into `V3-RACE-137..144` | normalized | Implement as staged queue to preserve deterministic gating, observability, and rollback safety. |
| CRDT + local-first architecture for soul/client/memory/contract state | `V3-RACE-137` | done | Add CRDT state-plane contracts with offline-first sync and JetStream reconciliation hooks. |
| WebAssembly component model for skill/habit modules | `V3-RACE-138` | done | Add capability-scoped WASM component runtime and signature-enforced module loading path. |
| Intent-based architecture (intent -> governed execution) | `V3-RACE-139` | done | Add explicit intent declaration plane with audited translation to executable workflows. |
| DID + Verifiable Credentials tied to soul vector | `V3-RACE-140` | done | Add DID/VC issuance + verification layer using soul-bound authority roots and revocation contracts. |
| IPFS/Filecoin-style content addressing for archival continuity | `V3-RACE-141` | done | Add content-addressed archival/pin contracts with encrypted payload and deterministic retrieval proofs. |
| Zero-Knowledge Proof lane for contract-compliance attestations | `V3-RACE-142` | done | Add ZK proof generation/verification contracts for selective compliance claims without raw disclosure. |
| Fully Homomorphic Encryption lane for encrypted-state computation | `V3-RACE-143` | done | Add FHE pilot lane for bounded high-value operations with strict performance/safety gates. |
| Full integration/docs + cross-organ contract enforcement | `V3-RACE-144` | done | Add integrated pinnacle harness/docs and data-scope regression checks across new lanes. |

## External Requirements Intake (Google Doc `1vedJMRKcVYn6MUt1jvgNvEmU7y-BuQRgOXM12kQdM6Y`, 2026-03-02)

Data boundary invariants for this intake:
- user-specific identity, collaboration, routing, and preference data lives only in `client/memory/` + `client/cognition/adaptive/`
- permanent protocol adapters, runtimes, schemas, and policy contracts live only in `client/runtime/systems/` + `client/runtime/config/` + `docs/client/`
- public/federated transport metadata is never treated as authoritative user-memory unless explicitly written into user lanes

|---|---|---|---|
| Monolithic `V3-RACE-048 Digital Pinnacle Expansion Pack` | decomposed into `V3-RACE-145..152` | normalized | Execute in staged lanes to preserve deterministic risk gating, rollback, and observability. |
| Actor model runtime for swarm/cell/skill coordination | `V3-RACE-145` | done| Add actor runtime substrate with isolated state + message-passing contracts. |
| Solid protocol data-pod interoperability | `V3-RACE-146` | done| Add governed Solid pod bridge for portable user-owned data exchange. |
| ActivityPub + Matrix federation | `V3-RACE-147` | done| Add federated comms gateway with policy-scoped bridging and audit receipts. |
| Edge/fog execution offload | `V3-RACE-148` | done| Add edge-first scheduler and offload contracts for heavy workloads. |

| Spatial computing abstractions | `V3-RACE-150` | done| Add spatial interaction abstraction layer for 3D/AR/VR-ready runtime surfaces. |
| AI-native multimodel database plane | `V3-RACE-151` | done| Add unified vector/graph/document/relational data-plane contract with policy-gated migration path. |
| WebGPU + Rust client compute | `V3-RACE-152` | done| Add client-side compute runtime for accelerated local workloads and visual compute lanes. |

## External Requirements Intake (Google Doc `1-ndEYjPrXYS2pFpIZDtq8Pc02UuhUk24XbTHvb_wPQA`, 2026-03-02)

Objective: normalize the "Collective Intelligence Engine" directive (crowd GPU training + sovereign LLM growth flywheel) into staged backlog lanes that integrate with existing nursery/training/economy primitives.

Data boundary invariants for this intake:
- user-specific participation preferences, contribution agreements, personalization adapters, and access-tier settings live only in `client/memory/` + `client/cognition/adaptive/`
- permanent training orchestration logic, evaluation pipelines, rollout gates, and economy policies live only in `client/runtime/systems/` + `client/runtime/config/` + `docs/client/`
- model core/local/artifacts/checkpoints are never stored as authoritative user-memory; they are governed runtime artifacts with encrypted archival contracts

|---|---|---|---|
| Monolithic `V3-RACE-049 Collective Intelligence Engine` | decomposed into `V3-RACE-153..160` | normalized | Implement as staged queue to preserve deterministic gating, training safety, and rollout rollback semantics. |

| Fractal-managed curriculum/data curation/eval loop | `V3-RACE-155` | done| Add fractal oversight lane for training objective mutation, data quality, and checkpoint gates. |
| Safe model rollout with tier-3 governance + fallback versions | `V3-RACE-156` | done| Add governed release lane with canary/evidence gates, old-model fallback, and rollback contracts. |
| Encrypted distributed model archival (IPFS/Arweave style) | `V3-RACE-157` | done| Extend archival plane with model-weight artifact contracts and retrieval verification gates. |
| Contributor badges/rewards + optional access benefits | `V3-RACE-158` | done| Add contributor incentive layer integrating badges, token rewards, and bounded service benefits. |
| Free-base + premium/priority access model for sustainability | `V3-RACE-159` | done| Add access-tier governance contract balancing open access with contributor incentive/economics. |
| Full integration docs + data-scope checks for training economy loop | `V3-RACE-160` | open | Add integration harness and storage-boundary checks across training/economy/identity lanes. |

## External Requirements Intake (Google Doc `1-ndEYjPrXYS2pFpIZDtq8Pc02UuhUk24XbTHvb_wPQA`, 2026-03-02)

Objective: normalize the X-gap scan recommendations (memory evolution, interoperability standards, meta-cognitive executive, and ops hardening) into staged backlog lanes.

Data boundary invariants for this intake:
- user-specific journals/observations/preferences/collaboration state only in `client/memory/` + `client/cognition/adaptive/`
- permanent protocol adapters, schedulers, verification contracts, and audit/runtime logic only in `client/runtime/systems/` + `client/runtime/config/` + `docs/client/`
- federation/tool metadata is treated as runtime operational state, not user memory, unless explicitly promoted by policy

### Requirement Mapping (from intake)

| Requirement (from intake) | Canonical Backlog Handling | Status | Requirement Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| Git-backed versioned memory filesystem (MemFS-style) | `V3-RACE-161` | done| Add memory filesystem with git provenance, branch/worktree support, and governed reflection commits. |
| Idle/sleep reflection compute loop | `V3-RACE-162` | done| Add background reflection scheduler with isolated execution lane and budget/quiet-hour controls. |
| Agentic self-organizing memory operations (ADD/UPDATE/DELETE/NOOP policy) | `V3-RACE-163` | done| Add policy-scored memory operation controller tied to outcome signals and rollback safety. |
| Observational compression + progressive disclosure retrieval | `V3-RACE-164` | done| Add observation compaction and metadata-first retrieval contracts with bounded context loading. |
| MCP interoperability layer + discoverable skill ingestion | `V3-RACE-165` | open | Add governed MCP client/server bridge and registry-driven skill discovery contracts. |
| A2A delegation/collaboration protocol lane | `V3-RACE-166` | done| Add standards-aligned agent-to-agent delegation and handoff contracts with policy gates. |
| System-3 executive layer (growth journal, intrinsic goals, always-on metacognition) | `V3-RACE-167` | done| Add persistent executive layer over reflex/strategy with narrative continuity + intrinsic loop controls. |
| Immutable audit chain + background hands + marketplace CLI polish | `V3-RACE-168` | open | Add hash-chained audit ledger, idle execution hands scheduler, and `protheusctl skills discover` operator surface. |

### Net-New Canonical Queue

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

| V3-RACE-162 | existing-coverage-validated | [extension / V3] Sleep Reflection Compute Scheduler (Idle Consolidation Loop) | Memory and strategy quality compounds faster with bounded asynchronous reflection during idle windows. | Implement `client/runtime/systems/memory/sleep_reflection_scheduler.{ts,js}` + policy `client/runtime/config/sleep_reflection_scheduler_policy.json`; user-specific reflection outputs stay in `client/memory/reflections/` and adaptive scheduling heuristics in `client/cognition/adaptive/reflections/`; permanent scheduler/executor logic stays in `client/runtime/systems/memory/` + `client/runtime/systems/autonomy/` + `client/runtime/config/`; runs honor budget/quiet-hour/risk-tier gates and emit receipts. / V3-RACE-161, V3-LOOP-001, V3-BUD-001 / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V3-RACE-163 | existing-coverage-validated | [primitive-upgrade / V3] Agentic Memory Operation Controller (ADD/UPDATE/DELETE/NOOP) | Self-evolving memory requires explicit, auditable action selection rather than implicit write behavior. | Implemented `client/runtime/systems/memory/agentic_memory_operation_controller.ts` on restored shared runtime `client/runtime/lib/upgrade_lane_runtime.ts` with configure/execute/status command receipts and guarded operation payloads. ## External Requirements Intake (Google Doc `1QrTZFUX6fbU0RVo8S_fIg6898igD8XxXhohRjNjRROQ`, 2026-03-02)

Objective: normalize the "lean/robust/competitive supremacy" directive into focused deltas on top of already-queued client/memory/interoperability/meta-cognition work.

Data boundary invariants for this intake:
- user-specific journals/preferences/crew-profile data lives only in `client/memory/` + `client/cognition/adaptive/`
- permanent orchestration/runtime/policy/benchmark logic lives only in `client/runtime/systems/` + `client/runtime/config/` + `docs/client/`
- benchmark inputs and external-framework adapters are operational artifacts, not user-memory by default

### Requirement Mapping (from intake)

| Requirement (from intake) | Canonical Backlog Handling | Status | Requirement Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| MemFS, sleep reflection, self-organizing memory, progressive disclosure | `V3-RACE-161..164` | already queued | Reuse current queue; no duplicate lanes added. |
| MCP + A2A + System-3 + hash audit/background hands/skills UX | `V3-RACE-165..168` | already queued | Reuse current queue; no duplicate lanes added. |
| Extract tiny `protheus-core` package and hard-separate optional layers | `V3-RACE-169` | open | Add modular core/package extraction lane with strict dependency surface and plugin boundaries. |
| Enforce spine kernel minimalism + policy relocation to client/cognition/adaptive/habits | `V3-RACE-170` | open | Add kernel budget/lint gate to keep spine orchestration-only and move policy logic out of kernel. |
| Add cyclic strategy graph runner (LangGraph-style parity, Protheus governance) | `V3-RACE-171` | done| Add bounded cyclic state-graph orchestration lane for strategy transitions and retries. |
| Add hierarchical persistent memory views (core/editable/summary + model-swap summarization) | `V3-RACE-172` | done| Add tiered memory-view derivation and swap-safe summarization contracts. |
| Add role-based crew spawning with shared namespace under habit governance | `V3-RACE-173` | done| Add multi-agent role spawn templates with bounded shared memory and clearance contracts. |
| Extend OTEL + eval UX + cross-framework benchmark script pack | `V3-RACE-174` | done | Add operator UX and benchmark parity pack vs Letta/Mastra/LangGraph/OpenFang using deterministic scenarios. |

## External Requirements Intake (Google Doc `1u2Oh8FVHI9joij7p4FXI3cUrUqY6at8AVcHdfeLHW84`, 2026-03-02)

Objective: normalize the "Superintelligence Seed" directive into canonical backlog lanes while preserving all March 2 architecture upgrades (contract lanes, System 3, MemFS, MCP gateway, and hardening paths).

Data boundary invariants for this intake:
- user-specific RSI approvals, operator preferences, and per-instance adaptation state live only in `client/memory/` + `client/cognition/adaptive/`
- permanent runtime logic, gate orchestration, protocol adapters, and policy contracts live only in `client/runtime/systems/` + `client/runtime/config/` + `docs/client/`
- non-user operational receipts remain in `state/` and are never treated as authoritative user-memory

### Requirement Mapping (from intake)

| Requirement (from intake) | Canonical Backlog Handling | Status | Requirement Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| Keep existing March 2 contract lanes, System 3, MemFS, MCP gateway, and V3-RACE hardening intact | existing `V3-RACE-161..174`, `V3-RACE-CONF-007` | already queued | Reuse existing queue and enforce no-replacement constraint via governance checks. |
| `packages/protheus-core` extraction baseline | `V3-RACE-169` | done | Already extracted; treat as the base for stricter runtime envelope work. |

| Route native MCP + A2A through venom gates and RR contract-lane checks | `V3-RACE-176` | done | Add explicit interop-to-venom/contract-lane convergence checks and fail-closed enforcement. |
| System 3 must act as meta-curriculum layer feeding `strategy_learner` + `model_catalog_loop` | `V3-RACE-177` | done | Add curriculum handoff/output contracts from System 3 to strategy/model loops with receipts. |
| Full RSI bootstrap lane under `client/cognition/adaptive/rsi/` using existing strategy/model executors | `V3-RACE-178` | done | Canonicalize RSI bootstrap orchestration contracts (bootstrap/step/status/hands loop). |
| Agent0-style dual-agent spiral (System 3 proposes harder self-tasks, loops execute + validate) | `V3-RACE-179` | done | Add dual-agent proposal/execution pattern with policy-bounded difficulty escalation. |

| Hash-chain every RSI step + Merkle rollback + resurrection protocol integration | `V3-RACE-181` | done | Add RSI chain integrity + rollback/recovery verification contracts and drills. |

| 24/7 idle RSI via background hands + world-model freshness loop | `V3-RACE-183` | done | Add always-on RSI scheduler profile integrated with bounded freshness and budget gates. |

## External Requirements Intake (Google Doc `1zxYaW0wNMbFnnpx_Ps0WCVgMf-uGraobSctE7cCGx80`, 2026-03-02)

Objective: normalize the Rust-hybrid escalation directive into canonical backlog lanes while preserving current TS operator velocity and non-breaking control-plane interfaces.

Data boundary invariants for this intake:
- user-specific runtime preferences and migration toggles live only in `client/memory/` + `client/cognition/adaptive/`
- permanent runtime kernels, bindings, sandbox engines, verification contracts, and packaging logic live only in `client/runtime/systems/`, `packages/`, `client/runtime/config/`, and `docs/client/`
- benchmark/attestation artifacts remain in `state/` and are never treated as authoritative user-memory

### Requirement Mapping (from intake)

| Requirement (from intake) | Canonical Backlog Handling | Status | Requirement Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| Rust core runtime extraction for spine/router/spawn/security hot paths | `V3-RACE-001`, `V3-RACE-021`, `V3-RACE-034` | done | Reuse completed Rust microkernel/control-plane extraction lanes. |
| Keep `packages/protheus-core` as minimal nucleus and tighten runtime envelope (<5MB, <200ms) | `V3-RACE-169`, `V3-RACE-175` | done | Runtime envelope hardening now enforced with optional-flag matrix checks and receipts. |
| In-process Rust bridging (`napi`) for hot paths with deterministic fallback | `V3-RACE-028` + delta `V3-RACE-185` | done | Extended memory N-API groundwork into `protheus-core` binding plane parity checks with deterministic fallback contract receipts. |
| Optional Rust/WASM acceleration path for `execution_sandbox_envelope` | `V3-RACE-186` | done | Add optional Rust/WASM sandbox coprocessor lane integrated into existing sandbox policy. |
| Formal verification depth on critical live paths (venom gates, RSI self-mods, resurrection, provenance) | `V3-RACE-035` + delta `V3-RACE-187` | done | Formal depth-pack lane now verifies critical runtime proof surfaces and emits deterministic proof receipts. |
| Preserve TS orchestration UX and prevent breaking changes during Rust escalation | `V3-RACE-116` + delta `V3-RACE-188` | done | Hybrid interface stability contract now enforces command/output snapshots with rollback-safe drift handling. |

### Net-New Canonical Queue

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V3-RACE-185 | existing-coverage-validated | [primitive-upgrade / V3] `protheus-core` Rust Binding Plane (NAPI/WASM API-Parity Bridge) | Rust kernel slices are mature, but core package consumers still need a canonical in-process binding contract beyond memory-only NAPI lanes. | Add Rust crate/binding surface for `packages/protheus-core` hot interfaces (spine dispatch, reflex dispatch, contract-lane executor hooks, signed gate validation) with generated API parity harness against TS exports, deterministic fallback toggles, and no-breaking-change receipts. / V3-RACE-169, V3-RACE-034, V3-RACE-028 / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V3-RACE-186 | existing-coverage-validated | [extension / V3] Execution Sandbox Rust/WASM Coprocessor Lane (Optional, Policy-Gated) | `execution_sandbox_envelope` is critical-path security; optional native/WASM acceleration can reduce overhead while preserving current safety behavior. | Implemented optional coprocessor verification lane in `client/runtime/systems/security/execution_sandbox_rust_wasm_coprocessor_lane.ts` with policy-driven sandbox/WASM probe checks, fail-closed behavior, and deterministic verify receipts. Verified by `tests/client-memory-tools/execution_sandbox_rust_wasm_coprocessor_lane.test.js`. / V3-RACE-024, V3-RACE-138, V3-RACE-185 / 10 / 0/1/2/client/adapter | 10 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V3-RACE-187 | existing-coverage-validated | [hardening / V3] Formal Verification Depth Pack for Critical Runtime Paths | Existing sovereignty verification is broad but this intake calls for explicit proofs on the highest-risk lanes where regression cost is catastrophic. | Expand formal specs/proof gates to cover venom containment transitions, RSI patch gating flows, resurrection rollback integrity, and supply-chain provenance checks; block merge on proof regressions and emit machine-verifiable proof receipts. / V3-RACE-035, V3-RACE-180, V3-RACE-181, V3-RACE-043 / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 10 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V3-RACE-188 | existing-coverage-validated | [hardening / V3] Hybrid Interface Stability Contract (Rust Core, TS Orchestration, Zero-Break Cutover) | Hybrid migration can silently break operator surfaces unless interface compatibility is continuously enforced. | Add compatibility contract for CLI/control-plane/public APIs requiring semver-safe adapters, regression snapshots for command/output contracts, staged canary cutover gates, and automatic rollback triggers on API drift. / V3-RACE-116, V3-RACE-185, V3-RACE-175 / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |

| Approve release packaging posture for hybrid artifacts (single-binary vs mixed package distribution) | human | queued | Decide default artifact strategy and signing/verification expectations for operator distribution channels. |

## External Requirements Intake (Google Doc `1qsY9ZcqDwE5R0gOd-qMl8XHajymG-mJVevICTpLnxpI`, 2026-03-02)

Objective: normalize the "mobile-ready, red-team-hardened, donor-rewarding singularity seed" directive into canonical backlog lanes without replacing existing governance, contract-lane, and runtime foundations.

Data boundary invariants for this intake:
- user-specific donor payout preferences, contribution opt-in, and wallet-profile metadata live only in `client/memory/` + `client/cognition/adaptive/`
- permanent reward routing logic, red-team propagation runtime, signed update distribution, and operator surfaces live only in `client/runtime/systems/`, `packages/`, `client/runtime/config/`, and `docs/client/`
- operational reward, security update, and distribution receipts remain under `state/` and are never treated as authoritative user-memory

### Requirement Mapping (from intake)

|---|---|---|---|
| Rust hybrid kernel + Edge/mobile packaging and command surfaces | `V3-RACE-175..194` | done | Reuse completed Rust-hybrid and mobile runtime lanes. |
| Red-team discoveries auto-signed, quorum-accepted, and propagated to all nodes | `V3-RACE-195`, `V3-RACE-196` | done | Add a dedicated security-discovery propagation lane with probationary habit apply + validation contracts. |
| Route 80-100% extra compute tithe to GPU donors with mining-share economics | `V3-RACE-197`, `V3-RACE-198` | done | Extend compute-tithe (`V3-RACE-022`) into explicit V3-MINE reward/share + settlement contracts. |
| Operator donor visibility (`mine dashboard`, contribution->earnings traceability) | `V3-RACE-199` | done | Add deterministic donor earnings dashboard and receipt-backed attribution views. |
| Keep all upgrades routed through signed venom gates + contract lanes + constitution hooks | `V3-RACE-017`, `V3-RACE-019`, `V3-038`, deltas `V3-RACE-195..199` | done | Require all new lanes to fail closed when gate/constitution/contract checks are missing. |

## External Requirements Intake (Google Doc `1IHlaNNXYShvk83eToPgkJxOGGJ5UfbRYfYrf2iKKuGA`, 2026-03-02)

Objective: normalize the decentralized co-compute mesh directive into canonical backlog lanes that extend distributed/spawn/economy foundations while preserving contract-lane governance and signed execution controls.

Data boundary invariants for this intake:
- user-specific mesh participation settings, device opt-in preferences, and payout routing preferences live only in `client/memory/` + `client/cognition/adaptive/`
- permanent mesh broker logic, sharding protocols, quorum aggregation, and CLI/control-plane surfaces live only in `client/runtime/systems/`, `packages/`, `client/runtime/config/`, and `docs/client/`
- mesh job receipts, attestation proofs, and contribution ledgers remain in `state/` and are never treated as user-memory

### Requirement Mapping (from intake)

| Requirement (from intake) | Canonical Backlog Handling | Status | Requirement Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| Global co-compute broker sharding work across server/edge/mobile/donor nodes | `V3-RACE-200` | done | Add compute-mesh broker plane on top of existing distributed+spawn runtime. |
| Quorum-backed result aggregation with signed provenance from all worker nodes | `V3-RACE-201` | done | Enforce result attestation, quorum reconciliation, and deterministic reject/retry semantics. |
| Mobile nodes participate with constrained scheduling (charging/thermal-aware) | `V3-RACE-202` | done | Add mobile participation scheduler contracts with safety envelopes and task class limits. |
| Operator visibility (`protheusctl mesh status` + compute map + earnings view) | `V3-RACE-203` | done | Add mesh status telemetry surfaces and contribution-linked operator views. |
| Tie mesh useful-work outcomes into donor reward engine | `V3-RACE-197`, `V3-RACE-198`, `V3-RACE-200` | done | Wire mesh work-unit proofs into V3-MINE accrual and settlement lanes. |

| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V3-RACE-200 | existing-coverage-validated | [primitive-upgrade / V3] Protheus Compute Mesh Broker (Network-Scale Task Sharding Plane) | Existing distributed/swarm lanes support bounded coordination, but there is no dedicated global broker for deterministic partitioning of large jobs across heterogeneous node classes. | Create `client/runtime/systems/compute_mesh/` broker lane that accepts governed jobs, partitions into signed work units, allocates by capability/safety profile, persists shard lifecycle receipts from enqueue->dispatch->complete/fail, and includes rollback-safe shard requeue semantics. / V3-RACE-006, V3-RACE-019, V3-RACE-192 / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 10 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V3-RACE-201 | existing-coverage-validated | [hardening / V3] Mesh Result Quorum + Provenance Aggregation Contract | Distributed shards require verifiable aggregation and fail-closed reconciliation to prevent poisoned/partial outputs from entering runtime decisions. | Implement aggregation contract enforcing signature checks, provenance-chain validation, quorum acceptance thresholds, deterministic tie-break/retry policies, rejection receipts for invalid/malicious result sets, and rollback to last-good aggregate on quorum failure. / V3-RACE-200, V3-RACE-195, V3-RACE-136 / 10 / 0/1/2/client/adapter | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V3-RACE-202 | existing-coverage-validated | [extension / V3] Mobile Mesh Participation Scheduler (Charging/Thermal/Battery Policy Lane) | Mobile co-compute must respect strict battery/thermal limits or participation becomes unsafe and unstable. | Add scheduler in `client/runtime/systems/edge/` that gates eligible task classes by charge state, thermal envelope, network profile, and user opt-in policy; emit bounded participation receipts, auto-throttle/auto-suspend events, and rollback to prior participation profile on safety breach. / V3-RACE-190, V3-RACE-193, V3-RACE-200 / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 10 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V3-RACE-203 | existing-coverage-validated | [extension / V3] Mesh Operator Surfaces (`protheusctl mesh *` + Contribution Topology View) | Operators need deterministic visibility into mesh health, work distribution, and reward-linked contribution traces. | Deliver `protheusctl mesh status`/`join`/`leave` surfaces with JSON/human outputs, mesh topology summary, per-node work stats, optional donor earnings overlay from V3-MINE receipts, and rollback-state telemetry; add regression coverage and docs. / V3-RACE-200, V3-RACE-201, V3-RACE-199 / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |

| Approve acceptable-use policy for donated compute tasks (allowed/disallowed workload classes) | human | queued | Define workload governance boundaries before enabling broad donor participation. |

## Internal Optimization Intake (Autonomy Execution Throughput Deadlock, 2026-03-02)

Objective: break the score-only deadlock by fixing verification semantics and execution floor behavior while preserving existing safety/constitution/contract-lane controls.

Data boundary invariants for this intake:
- user-specific execution preferences and tolerance overrides live only in `client/memory/` + `client/cognition/adaptive/`
- permanent verification semantics, selector logic, budget partitioning, and routing health controls live only in `client/runtime/systems/`, `client/runtime/config/`, and `docs/client/`
- run receipts and gate telemetry remain in `state/` and are never treated as user-memory

### Requirement Mapping (from system check)

| Requirement (from system check) | Canonical Backlog Handling | Status | Requirement Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| Score-only previews fail on deferred-horizon criteria (`postconditions_ok`, outreach reply windows) creating false negatives | `V3-RACE-204` | done | Add preview-horizon semantics so deferred metrics are tracked without failing immediate preview verdicts. |
| Need at least one deterministic, low-risk shipped outcome/day to unlock readiness (`min_shipped`) | `V3-RACE-205` | done | Add execution-floor bootstrap lane that guarantees one bounded shippable candidate path when safe. |
| Selector repeatedly chooses manual/high-risk paths in score-only, causing `preview_executable=false` churn | `V3-RACE-206` | done | Penalize or skip manual-gated candidates in score-only unless no safe alternative exists. |
| Dream/autopause budget pressure starves execution floor and causes persistent defer loops | `V3-RACE-207` | done | Partition execution vs dream budget envelopes with explicit minimum execution reserve. |
| Model pool degraded by repeated probe timeouts, reducing healthy fallback capacity | `V3-RACE-208` | done | Add adaptive probe timeout + temporary suppression/rehabilitation rules for unstable models. |

### Net-New Canonical Queue

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V3-RACE-204 | existing-coverage-validated | [hardening / V3] Preview-Horizon Success Criteria Contract (Deferred Metrics in Score-Only) | Score-only receipts currently fail when criteria are valid but time-windowed (e.g., postconditions/outreach replies), distorting pass-rate telemetry and readiness. | Update success-criteria verifier + receipt taxonomy so deferred-horizon checks are explicitly marked `deferred_pending_window` in score-only, counted separately from hard fails, with verification receipts and rollback-safe config toggle to restore strict legacy behavior if regressions appear. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/backlog_lane_batch_delivery.ts`. / V3-RACE-017, V3-RACE-019, V3-RACE-116 / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V3-RACE-205 | existing-coverage-validated | [hardening / V3] Deterministic Execution-Floor Bootstrap Lane (1 Shippable Outcome Minimum) | Readiness blocks on `min_shipped` but current candidate flow can loop indefinitely in no-change/preview-only paths. | Add low-risk execution-floor lane that guarantees at least one policy-safe, objectively verifiable shippable action per day when eligible candidates exist; emit verification receipts for shipped outcome and include rollback path that reverts to previous selection policy on miss-rate regression. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/backlog_lane_batch_delivery.ts`. / V3-RACE-017, V3-RACE-019, V3-RACE-116 / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V3-RACE-206 | existing-coverage-validated | [hardening / V3] Score-Only Manual-Gate Exclusion & Selector Penalty | Re-selecting manual/high-risk proposals in score-only produces predictable `preview_executable=false` outcomes and wastes attempts. | Implement selector gate that deprioritizes/blocks manual-gated candidates during score-only unless no safe alternative passes; emit decision receipts with reason codes and include rollback switch to restore prior selection ordering for comparison. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/backlog_lane_batch_delivery.ts`. / V3-RACE-017, V3-RACE-019, V3-RACE-116 / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V3-RACE-207 | existing-coverage-validated | [hardening / V3] Budget Envelope Partitioning (Execution Floor vs Dream/Idle Burn) | A single burn/autopause pressure lane can starve execution and trap the system in deferred preview mode despite viable low-cost actions. | Split token-economics envelopes into protected execution-floor reserve and dream/idle budget pools, enforce minimum execution share before autopause defers, emit verification receipts on reserve usage, and add rollback-safe policy fallback to unified budget mode. / V3-RACE-019, V3-RACE-022, V3-RACE-116 / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V3-RACE-208 | existing-coverage-validated | [hardening / V3] Model Health Stabilizer (Adaptive Probe Timeouts + Temporary Suppression/Rehab) | Repeated probe timeouts on key models degrade routing diversity and increase fallback pressure, hurting throughput reliability. | Add adaptive probe timeout policy plus temporary suppression/rehabilitation for unstable models, prove improved healthy-model availability with verification receipts, and include rollback policy to revert timeout/suppression tuning if latency or quality regresses. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/backlog_lane_batch_delivery.ts`. / V3-RACE-017, V3-RACE-019, V3-RACE-116 / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter Regression revalidation pending: restore non-backlog implementation evidence and/or executable verification receipts. | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |

## External Requirements Intake (Google Doc `1bw6PoJqkftJhLRLjPanv4fLzSrI-h_Sht9UpxDLh0Ck`, 2026-03-02)

Objective: normalize the Platform Oracle + HostProfile + Adaptation Channel architecture into canonical backlog lanes while reusing already-delivered mobile/distribution/formal foundations and preserving fail-closed governance.

Data boundary invariants for this intake:
- host probing outputs and adaptation activation receipts remain in `state/` and are never treated as user-memory
- permanent oracle/channel runtime code, policies, and CLI surfaces live only in `client/runtime/systems/`, `packages/`, `client/runtime/config/`, and `docs/client/`
- user-specific host preferences/overrides (if any) remain in `client/memory/` + `client/cognition/adaptive/` and never become global defaults

### Requirement Mapping (from intake)

| Requirement (from intake) | Canonical Backlog Handling | Status | Requirement Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| Expand the burn-limiter sensor into a boot-time Platform Oracle that emits a signed typed `HostProfile` | `V3-RACE-209` | done | Add oracle service with deterministic probe lifecycle and signed HostProfile receipts. |
| Add Adaptation Channel contract (Rust trait + WASM guest API) with fail-closed generic fallback | `V3-RACE-210` | done | Define stable channel ABI/registry and enforce generic fallback when no specific host match exists. |
| Ship 5 host-specific channels (Ubuntu, FreeBSD, NixOS, Raspberry Pi OS, Alpine) activated only on `HostProfile` match | `V3-RACE-211` | done | Implement channel pack with strict host predicates, lightweight modules, and non-match exclusion gates. |
| Promote the 18 cross-platform GENERAL requirements into universal core behavior | `V3-RACE-212` | done | Add a canonical general-requirement matrix and wire mandatory universal controls into oracle + generic channel paths. |
| Add `protheusctl host adapt` and auto activation on first run/upgrade | `V3-RACE-213` | done | Deliver operator CLI and deterministic auto-adapt workflow with receipt-backed activation history. |
| Add formal verification gate that blocks lanes incompatible with active `HostProfile` | `V3-RACE-214` | done | Extend formal lane checks with host-profile conformance proofs and fail-closed enforcement. |
| Keep formal/chaos baseline for critical runtime paths in required checks | `RM-201`, `RM-204`, `V3-RACE-187` | covered | Reuse existing formal+chaos lanes as dependencies; only add host-profile-specific proof scope in this intake. |

### Net-New Canonical Queue

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V3-RACE-209 | existing-coverage-validated | [primitive-upgrade / V3] Platform Oracle HostProfile Service (Burn-Limiter Expansion) | Steward-agnostic adaptation needs one authoritative, typed host identity contract before any channel or lane can safely activate. | Added `client/runtime/systems/ops/platform_oracle_hostprofile.{ts,js}` + `client/runtime/config/platform_oracle_hostprofile_policy.json` to run boot/promotion/periodic host probing with signed `HostProfile` receipts (OS/distro/variant/kernel/runtime/cloud/hardware/capabilities), fail-closed minimal fallback, and rollback to last-known-good profile; deterministic artifacts are written to `state/ops/platform_oracle_hostprofile/` and covered by `tests/client-memory-tools/platform_adaptation_stack.test.js`. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/platform_oracle_hostprofile.ts`. / V3-RACE-034, V3-RACE-116, V3-RACE-188 / 10 / 0/1/2/client/adapter | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V3-RACE-210 | existing-coverage-validated | [primitive-upgrade / V3] Adaptation Channel ABI (Rust Trait + WASM Guest Contract + Generic Fallback) | Cross-system portability requires a governed extension interface rather than scattered per-platform conditionals in core runtime paths. | Added `client/runtime/systems/ops/platform_adaptation_channel_runtime.{ts,js}` + `client/runtime/config/platform_adaptation_channel_runtime_policy.json` + `client/runtime/config/platform_adaptation_channels.json` to enforce a governed channel ABI/registry with signed attestation metadata, explicit host predicates, deterministic activation receipts, and fail-closed generic fallback when no specific channel matches. Evidence: `client/runtime/systems/ops/platform_adaptation_channel_runtime.ts`. / V3-RACE-209, V3-RACE-176, V3-RACE-185 / 10 / 0/1/2/client/adapter | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |

| V3-RACE-212 | existing-coverage-validated | [extension / V3] Universal Platform Abstraction Matrix (18 GENERAL Requirements) | The intake's cross-platform requirements should be encoded once as universal contracts to prevent per-channel duplication and inconsistent guarantees. | Added `client/runtime/systems/ops/platform_universal_abstraction_matrix.{ts,js}` + `client/runtime/config/platform_universal_abstraction_matrix_policy.json` + `client/runtime/config/platform_universal_abstraction_matrix.json` encoding all 18 universal controls with owner, enforcement mode, and rollback toggle requirements; emits signed compliance receipts and enforces fallback revision presence for rollback-safe policy rollback. / V3-RACE-209, V3-RACE-210, V3-RACE-211, V3-RACE-120 / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |

Objective: normalize the Microsoft-oriented Phase 0-3 seed OS plan into canonical backlog lanes while preserving local-first sovereignty, fail-closed governance, and existing V3/V4 foundations.

- enterprise/provider integrations (Azure/OpenAI/AKS/Entra/Monitor/Defender) remain optional adapters behind policy gates; local-first behavior remains default
- control-plane/runtime logic and provider adapters live only in `client/runtime/systems/`, `packages/`, `client/runtime/config/`, and `docs/client/`

## External Requirements Intake (Google Doc `1f9sZqjqNBFqKrs682Gxp45VUQfR4NRS6gwOv2bWO5MI`, 2026-03-02)

Objective: normalize Amazon-specific deltas into canonical backlog lanes without duplicating previously queued Microsoft/general platform work and while preserving local-first sovereignty.

Data boundary invariants for this intake:
- AWS integrations (Bedrock, EKS, CloudWatch, IAM/KMS, CodePipeline) remain optional adapters; local-first execution remains default
- all provider adapters and orchestration logic remain in `client/runtime/systems/`, `packages/`, `client/runtime/config/`, and `docs/client/`; no provider control logic is stored in user memory
- AWS telemetry/compliance artifacts remain in `state/` with reproducible receipts and are never treated as user-memory

- Apple platform integrations (Core ML/MLX/Foundation Models/PCC/visionOS/iOS services) remain optional provider adapters behind fail-closed policy gates

- platform telemetry/compliance artifacts remain in `state/` with reproducible receipts and rollback-ready promotion controls

## External Requirements Intake (Google Doc `1QzB6z0nbh4F5xLTYq2r1YiSHbyHFogex4aWp6vWw3nY`, 2026-03-02)

Objective: normalize Google-specific stewardship deltas into canonical backlog lanes while preserving local-first sovereignty and avoiding duplication of existing Microsoft/Amazon/Apple queues.

Data boundary invariants for this intake:
- Google integrations (Android/ChromeOS/Fuchsia/TPU/GKE/Gemini/Cloud Armor/BeyondCorp) remain optional adapters behind policy gates
- provider runtime and orchestration logic live only in `client/runtime/systems/`, `packages/`, `client/runtime/config/`, and `docs/client/`
- deployment/security/chaos/compliance artifacts stay in `state/` with reproducible receipts and rollback-ready controls

## External Requirements Intake (Google Doc `1qLw4MzGGUilF-IsnjSfo4RoEZP-SJcxzNRegk6wjujo`, 2026-03-02)

Objective: normalize Helix Enterprise Linux-specific deltas into canonical backlog lanes while preserving the existing cross-platform core and feeding reusable enterprise-hardening controls back into neutral Protheus foundations.

Data boundary invariants for this intake:
- Helix distro, kernel-security, and enterprise operations integrations are adapterized policy gates and never bypass core constitution/contract-lane controls
- compliance/hardware/air-gapped/rollout artifacts remain in `state/` as reproducible receipts
- permanent logic and policy stay in `client/runtime/systems/`, `packages/`, `client/runtime/config/`, and `docs/client/`; no provider runtime authority is stored in user-memory paths
