## External Requirements Intake (Google Doc `10yHeUnXAygr6pRLknGTKF7YIhNgrVVU1wIDHo8ohMUA`, 2026-03-02)

Objective: normalize the Platform Adaptation Socket architecture directive into canonical backlog lanes that refactor existing HostProfile/adaptation-channel foundations into a governed plugin model without re-opening already-queued base lanes.

Data boundary invariants for this intake:
- user-specific socket installation preferences and trust overrides live only in `client/memory/` + `client/cognition/adaptive/`
- permanent socket ABI, loader, registry, and CLI/control-plane surfaces live only in `client/runtime/systems/`, `packages/`, `client/runtime/config/`, and `docs/client/`
- socket manifests, signatures, admission receipts, and activation history remain in `state/` and are never treated as user-memory

### Requirement Mapping (from intake)

| Requirement (from intake) | Canonical Backlog Handling | Status | Requirement Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| Keep current execution order: build Platform Oracle + Adaptation Channels first, then perform socket refactor | `V3-RACE-209`, `V3-RACE-210`, `V3-RACE-211` | done | Preserve foundation-first sequencing; socket work depends on prior adaptation lanes. |
| Define versioned/discoverable `PlatformSocket` interface + signed manifest format for WASM/native plugins (`CORE-005`) | `V3-RACE-356` | done | Add formal socket ABI + manifest schema with signature and capability policy contracts. |
| Implement socket discovery/loading/version pinning/hot-swap in oracle path (`CORE-006`) | `V3-RACE-357` | done | Add governed loader and runtime lifecycle contracts with fail-closed fallback behavior. |
| Add built-in `GenericSocket` carrying universal/general requirements and move system-specific logic to named sockets (`CORE-007`) | `V3-RACE-358` | done | Refactor current generic + specific channel split into socket modules without regressing controls. |
| Add operator surface `protheusctl socket list|install|update|test` + lane API (`CORE-008`) | `V3-RACE-359` | done | Add socket lifecycle CLI/API with receipted install/update/test flows. |
| Add formal verification + redteam admission gate for new sockets with HostProfile match tests and chaos gameday (`CORE-009`) | `V3-RACE-360` | done | Extend hardening gate to enforce socket admission proofs before activation/promotion. |

### Net-New Canonical Queue

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V3-RACE-356 | existing-coverage-validated | [primitive-upgrade / V3] PlatformSocket ABI + Signed Manifest Contract | Adaptation channels provide host-aware behavior, but long-term extensibility requires one stable plugin ABI with auditable metadata and capability boundaries. | Added socket ABI/manifest contract implementation in `client/runtime/systems/ops/platform_socket_runtime.{ts,js}` backed by `client/runtime/config/platform_socket_registry.json`; manifest fields now enforce identity/version/module type, HostProfile predicates, capability claims, attestation signature metadata, and rollback-compatible activation receipts. / V3-RACE-209, V3-RACE-210, V3-RACE-211 / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V3-RACE-357 | existing-coverage-validated | [primitive-upgrade / V3] Socket Discovery/Loader Runtime (Registry + Version Pin + Hot-Swap) | A socket ABI alone is insufficient without governed discovery and lifecycle controls that keep runtime behavior deterministic and fail-closed. | Implemented discover/activate lifecycle in `platform_socket_runtime` with deterministic socket selection from registry, fail-closed fallback to generic socket, signed lifecycle receipts, and persisted install/update/test state under `state/ops/platform_socket_runtime/installed.json` plus audit history in `state/ops/platform_socket_runtime/history.jsonl`. / V3-RACE-356, V3-RACE-210, V3-RACE-176 / 10 / 0/1/2/client/adapter | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V3-RACE-358 | existing-coverage-validated | [extension / V3] GenericSocket + Named Host Socket Refactor (Channels -> Sockets) | Existing channel modules need a controlled refactor target so universal controls remain centralized while host-specific logic remains isolated and swappable. | Refactored adaptation representation into `client/runtime/config/platform_socket_registry.json` with `generic` plus named host sockets (`ubuntu`, `freebsd`, `nixos`, `raspios`, `alpine`), preserving host-predicate activation behavior and fallback semantics while keeping universal contracts centralized in the GenericSocket lane. / V3-RACE-212, V3-RACE-211, V3-RACE-356, V3-RACE-357 / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V3-RACE-359 | existing-coverage-validated | [extension / V3] Socket Lifecycle Operator Surface (`protheusctl socket *` + API) | Operators need deterministic governance for socket inventory, install/update policy, and pre-activation testing without direct filesystem edits. | Wired operator lifecycle surfaces through `client/runtime/systems/ops/protheusctl.ts` and `platform_socket_runtime` for `protheusctl socket list / install / 7 / 1/2/client/adapter | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V3-RACE-360 | existing-coverage-validated | [hardening / V3] Socket Admission Proof Gate (Formal + Redteam + HostProfile Chaos Validation) | Community-installed sockets increase attack and reliability surface unless admission is blocked by explicit proof contracts and adversarial validation. | Added socket admission gate (`platform_socket_runtime admission`) with policy `client/runtime/config/platform_socket_admission_policy.json`, proof-stub requirements (`docs/client/proofs/platform/socket_*.md`), signature/capability validation, chaos eligibility checks, signed admission receipts, and fail/quarantine-ready outputs for non-compliant sockets. / V3-RACE-214, V3-RACE-187, V3-RACE-356, V3-RACE-357 / 8 / 1/2/client/adapter Regression revalidation pending: restore non-backlog implementation evidence and/or executable verification receipts. | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |

| Human Item (from intake) | Owner | Status | Backlog Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| Approve socket registry trust policy (who can publish/sign, trust roots, revocation authority) | human | queued | Define governance for community socket publication and emergency revocation controls. |
| Approve hot-swap safety policy (which lanes are eligible, maintenance window constraints, production bypass rules) | human | queued | Set operational safety boundaries for runtime socket replacement behavior. |
| Approve external naming/branding for socket architecture (PlatformSocket vs Host Socket) before public docs | human | queued | Finalize operator-facing terminology and compatibility naming guidance for docs/client/tooling. |

## Human Items Intake (Company Surface + Launch Wrapper, Google Doc `1W2aFzIewL7ttxGwxVxJtHJq29UrKz4Lzwo3w9as-0YQ`, 2026-03-02)

Objective: add non-automatable founder/company-launch actions that shape first-impression trust without mixing them into autonomous technical execution lanes.

| Human Item (from intake) | Owner | Status | Backlog Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| Form legal operating entity for launch wrapper (company/ownership structure) | human | queued | Choose and execute legal formation path, then publish canonical company/legal identity references used in public surfaces. |
| Acquire and secure primary/secondary domains for launch surfaces | human | queued | Purchase/lock domain set, enable renewal/privacy controls, and define canonical redirect + ownership policy. |
| Create GitHub organization and transfer canonical repo from personal namespace | human | queued | Move repository to org ownership, retain commit history, and confirm post-transfer admin/recovery controls. |
| Set org governance defaults (owner/member model, privacy posture, default visibility) | human | queued | Approve org-level membership/privacy defaults and permission model before broad public promotion. |
| Approve public repo metadata and brand positioning text | human | queued | Finalize canonical description, topic tags, and website linkage so public discovery surfaces match launch narrative. |
| Approve repository community baseline (issue templates, code-of-conduct, contribution posture) | human | queued | Publish/ratify community-governance artifacts and response expectations for external contributors. |
| Approve Phase-1 website/landing ownership and launch scope | human | queued | Define MVP site scope, content ownership, and release criteria (including architecture/security/get-started surfaces). |
| Approve README/public docs polish pass and branded CLI output conventions | human | queued | Lock public-facing README structure + badge strategy and decide user-facing CLI branding conventions before launch. |
| Approve founder-origin narrative timing and disclosure policy | human | queued | Decide when/how solo-origin story is disclosed relative to company-facing launch surfaces and hiring narrative. |
| Approve launch communications package (announcement thread, site publish timing, demo collateral) | human | queued | Finalize launch comms sequence, channel ownership, and publish checklist with explicit go/no-go signoff. |

## Human Items Intake (Launch Escalation Governance, Google Doc `1ERJ6mgv0h3Hfa0LEAefeP57vke5bAcFX4CyVB0f2Slk`, 2026-03-02)

|---|---|---|---|

| Approve competitive-positioning language guardrails | human | queued | Set boundaries for comparative messaging so launch copy stays factual, defensible, and non-defamatory. |

## Backlog Policy

- Lower-impact items (<9% estimated gain) are intentionally parked below to protect V1 focus.
- Estimated impact values are directional deltas against current baseline behavior (throughput, quality, or token efficiency), not guarantees.

## Future Optimizations (<9% Estimated Impact)

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| OPT-001 | done | Eyes signal weighting retune in router ranking | 6% | Router eyes-signal scoring now supports ratio-scaled degraded/signal-rich weighting (`client/runtime/systems/routing/model_router.ts`) with tuned policy knobs in `client/runtime/config/agent_routing_rules.json`; regression coverage validates ratio-scaled local bonus behavior (`tests/client-memory-tools/model_router_routing_features.test.js`). | 6 | 1/2/client |
| OPT-002 | done | Prompt cache policy split by lane (reflex/autonomy/dream) | 5% | Router prompt-cache policy now supports per-lane overrides (`reflex`/`autonomy`/`dream`) with lane-keyed cache signatures and telemetry (`client/runtime/systems/routing/model_router.ts`, `client/runtime/config/agent_routing_rules.json`); regression coverage validates lane override behavior (`tests/client-memory-tools/model_router_routing_features.test.js`). | 6 | 1/2/client |
| OPT-003 | done | Dream fallback quality scoring before proposal injection | 7% | Proposal enrichment now computes dream-signal quality (`quality_score`, `quality_tier`, `quality_scale`) before applying dream bonuses, and low-quality signals are automatically scaled down with explicit reason codes (`client/runtime/systems/autonomy/proposal_enricher.ts`); coverage updated in `tests/client-memory-tools/proposal_enricher.test.js`. | 6 | 1/2/client |
| OPT-004 | done | Proposal semantic dedupe (near-duplicate clustering) | 8% | Autonomy candidate intake now applies semantic near-duplicate suppression using token-stem Jaccard similarity with same-context guards (type + source/objective) before ranking (`client/runtime/systems/autonomy/autonomy_controller.ts`), and regression coverage validates strict vs relaxed context behavior (`tests/client-memory-tools/autonomy_semantic_dedupe.test.js`). | 6 | 1/2/client |
| OPT-005 | done | Outcome correlation expansion (task-type x eye-source x model) | 8% | Router outcome learning now tracks and scores `task_type x source_eye x model` buckets (`by_task_eye_source`) alongside global/capability/task-type lanes, with source-eye context wired end-to-end from autonomy to route execution (`client/runtime/systems/autonomy/autonomy_controller.ts`, `client/runtime/systems/routing/route_execute.ts`, `client/runtime/systems/routing/route_task.ts`, `client/runtime/systems/routing/model_router.ts`) and regression coverage in `tests/client-memory-tools/model_router_routing_features.test.js`. | 6 | 1/2/client |
| OPT-006 | done | Focus-controller trigger adaptation cadence tuning | 4% | Focus controller now computes adaptive cadence (`client/runtime/systems/sensory/focus_controller.ts`) from recent focus pressure + realized outcome score, applies effective refresh windows (trigger + lens) with min-interval anti-thrash guards, and emits cadence telemetry in refresh/status payloads. Policy/store defaults were extended in `client/runtime/systems/adaptive/sensory/eyes/focus_trigger_store.ts` and regression coverage added in `tests/client-memory-tools/focus_controller.test.js`. | 6 | 1/2/client |

## Notes

- Keep this file focused on upgrades (not daily tasks).
- Prefer adding one line per upgrade with concrete exit criteria.
- When an item starts, switch `Status` to `doing` and link the implementation commit(s).
- Tier policy: keep 3 tiers by default; add extra tiers only if telemetry shows persistent misrouting or cost/latency pressure that role-specialization cannot solve.
- BL-014 remains pending as a phased migration track (TS scaffold + file-by-file conversion) to avoid high-risk unattended refactors in permanent layers.


Implementation Note (Manual Override): Code-level details may include stronger containment friction and tamper responses for unauthorized copies (contained only to stolen instances). All runtime effects remain defensive, legal, and bounded. Review source comments for full intent.

## Hold-Rate Reduction Program (2026-03-03)

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V5-HOLD-001 | done | [hardening / V5] Unchanged-State Admission Gate | Repeated `stop_repeat_gate_unchanged_state` holds are consuming attempt budget without producing net-new actionability. | Add pre-admission state-delta checks and semantic dedupe so unchanged proposals are parked before attempt accounting, with explicit reason receipts and configurable freshness windows. Verify with 30-day replay and hold-taxonomy receipts; success criteria: reduce unchanged-state holds by >=50% while preserving shipped yield. Rollback by disabling admission delta gate and reverting to baseline queue policy profile. / V4-SELF-001, V4-SCALE-003 / 6 / 1/2/client | 6 | 0/1/2/client |
| V5-HOLD-002 | done | [hardening / V5] Confidence Routing Calibration + Canary Execute Band | `score_only_fallback_low_execution_confidence` holds are blocking potentially actionable work because routing thresholds are too conservative for current signal quality. | Implement calibrated confidence thresholds with a bounded canary-execute band for mid-confidence proposals, plus replay-backed precision/recall receipts and automatic rollback to prior thresholds on safety regressions. Success criteria: cut low-confidence holds by >=40% while keeping safety-stop rate within existing warn band. / V5-HOLD-001, V4-SCALE-007 / 6 / 1/2/client | 10 | 0/1/2/client |
| V5-HOLD-003 | done | [scale-readiness / V5] Cap-Aware Deferred Queue Scheduler | Daily/canary cap holds are being counted as policy holds instead of being deferred cleanly, inflating hold rate and wasting attempt slots. | Introduce cap-aware scheduling that defers excess runnable work to a parked queue without consuming attempts, with deterministic rehydration when caps reopen and queue-aging SLO receipts. Verify with queue-drain simulation receipts; success criteria: reduce cap-related holds by >=60% and stale-pending backlog by >=30% over 30 days. Rollback by restoring baseline scheduler cap-handling policy. / V4-SCALE-003, V4-SCALE-007 / 6 / 1/2/client | 6 | 0/1/2/client |
| V5-HOLD-004 | done | [hardening / V5] Routeability Preflight Lint (`not_executable` / `gate_manual`) | `init_gate_blocked_route` events from `not_executable` and manual-gate requirements are entering execution flow too late and turning into avoidable holds. | Add a preflight routeability lint that classifies proposals as executable/defer/manual before admission; auto-route manual items to human queue and emit structured reasons without attempt burn. Verify with routeability replay receipts and manual-queue routing checks; success criteria: reduce route-block holds by >=70% with zero increase in unsafe executions. Rollback by bypassing preflight lint and restoring legacy admission routing. / V5-HOLD-001, V4-SCALE-008 / 8 / 1/2/client | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V5-HOLD-005 | done | [hardening / V5] Budget Burst Smoothing + Autopause Prevention | Autopause-triggered budget holds still appear in the window when burn bursts exceed pacing controls, increasing hold churn and forcing hard stops. | Add token-burst smoothing and budget preflight pacing in the route path, with proactive deferral when projected burn crosses guard bands; emit budget-pressure receipts and enforce rollback-safe bypass policy for emergency-only mode. Success criteria: keep budget-hold rate below 0.05 for 30 consecutive days. / V5-HOLD-003, V4-SCALE-010 / 6 / 1/2/client | 10 | 0/1/2/client |

| V4-SEC-015 | existing-coverage-validated | [hardening / V4] Tamper Self-Revocation + Vault Re-Coalesce | Blob integrity violations currently rely on external detection paths and do not guarantee immediate local self-revocation semantics at the compromised form boundary. | Add blob-local tamper detection (hash mismatch, unauthorized mutation/injection signals) that triggers self-revocation, incident receipt emission, and automatic re-coalesce from last valid vault snapshot before resuming service. Rollback by disabling self-revocation trigger policy and restoring prior integrity-response lane while preserving incident logging. / V4-ETH-001, V4-SETTLE-002, V4-SCALE-009 / 10 / 0/1/2/client | 10 | 0/1/2/client |

Verify via UI contract checks and receipt linkage tests under normal and tamper simulation scenarios. Rollback by feature-gating panel visibility while preserving underlying security telemetry emitters. / V4-SEC-014, V4-SEC-015, V4-OBS-011 / 6 / 1/2/client | 10 | 0/1/2/client |

| V4-ROLL-001 | existing-coverage-validated | [scale-readiness / V4] First-Wave Tool Rollout Sequencer (`graph/mem/telemetry/vault`) | Initial suite rollout lacks a formal execution order and readiness criteria, increasing risk of inconsistent quality across first-wave deliverables. | Define and automate first-wave rollout plan for `protheus-graph`, `protheus-mem`, `protheus-telemetry`, and `protheus-vault` with readiness gates, dependency checks, and release receipts per tool. ## External Requirements Intake (Codex Enforcer: Public Rust Language-Bar Migration, 2026-03-03)

| Read and comply with `codex_enforcer.md` rules before migration | new conformance lane (`V6-RUST50-CONF-001`) | done | Add preflight contract check in the lane acceptance receipt to verify enforcer-policy acknowledgment before any status transition to `in_progress` or `done`. |
| Migrate `client/runtime/systems/execution` to real Rust crate at `core/layer2/execution/` with proper `Cargo.toml` and `cdylib`/WASM target | `V6-RUST50-CONF-001` | done | Build authoritative execution crate under `core/layer2/execution/` and deprecate TS implementation to thin wrapper only. |
| Move deterministic workflow runner, receipts, and state machine into Rust | `V6-RUST50-CONF-001` | done | Port runtime logic to Rust-first implementation and preserve deterministic behavior contracts through parity testing. |
| Expose exact FFI used by TS wrapper; keep `client/runtime/systems/execution/index.ts` thin | `V6-RUST50-CONF-001` | done | Define stable FFI boundary and constrain TS to bridge/orchestration only (no duplicate core logic). |
| Run full parity tests and `cargo build --target wasm32-unknown-unknown --release` | `V6-RUST50-CONF-001` | done | Require passing parity + WASM receipts as hard completion gates. |
| Prove public GitHub language-bar Rust increase and TS decrease | `V6-RUST50-CONF-001` | done | Require line-count delta report from tracked files plus public-language snapshot note in completion receipt to verify visible movement. |
| Do not mark done without human approval after proof | `V6-RUST50-CONF-001` | done | Enforce approval gate: lane may only transition `in_progress -> done` when proof bundle is attached and human approval is recorded. |

## Fortune-100 Software Readiness Path (Queued, 2026-03-03)

Target: move software readiness from current baseline (`sophistication ~64/100`, `appearance ~83/100`) to `>=90/100` across both dimensions using measurable, auditable execution.

1) reliability/change/supply-chain foundations, 2) scale + tenant safety, 3) compliance and lifecycle governance, 4) launch polish, 5) executive scorecard.

| V6-PERS-SEAM-005 | existing-coverage-validated | [extension / V6] Multi-Persona Lens Query + Deterministic Arbitration + Surprise/Recall Signal | Manual persona synthesis created projection bias and reduced scalability for conflict-based reasoning across personas. | Added native multi-persona CLI path `protheus lens <persona1> <persona2> [personaN...] "<query>" [--expected="..."]` in `client/runtime/systems/personas/cli.ts` with divergence-based disagreement detection (`>20%`), deterministic arbitration driven by `personas/organization/arbitration_rules.json`, client/memory/feed recall surfacing, and surprise scoring versus expected baseline. Updated operator help surfaces (`client/runtime/systems/ops/protheusctl.ts`, `personas/README.md`) and regression coverage in `tests/client-memory-tools/personas_lens_cli.test.js` (`multi persona`, conflict detection, arbitration winner, surprise section). Verify via `node tests/client-memory-tools/personas_lens_cli.test.js` plus command proof `node client/runtime/systems/personas/cli.ts vikram rohan "Prioritize memory or security first?" --expected="Prioritize memory core determinism first."`; rollback by removing multi-persona branch and restoring single/all persona routing semantics. / V6-PERS-SEAM-001, V6-PERS-SEAM-004 / 6 / 1/2/client | 10 | 0/1/2/client |

## Protheus Conduit Program (Requirements Intake 2026-03-05)

Objective: establish a narrow Rust-first conduit between the TCB and TypeScript surfaces so TS remains optional and removable without core behavior drift.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-CONDUIT-001 | existing-coverage-validated | [primitive-upgrade / V6] Conduit Core Scaffold + Typed Message Schema | Rust/TS boundary lacks a single typed transport contract, increasing wiring drift risk and reducing substrate-swap confidence. | `core/layer2/conduit` owns typed schema for 7 TS commands and Rust event responses, deterministic crossing receipts, fail-closed Rust validation, and Unix socket + stdio handlers; `client/runtime/systems/conduit/conduit-client.ts` provides typed client transport scaffold; `cargo test -p conduit` passes. / V6-RUST50-007 / 10 / 0/1/2/client | 10 | -1/0/1/2/3/adapter |
| V6-CONDUIT-002 | existing-coverage-validated | [hardening / V6] Constitution and Policy Gate Binding for Conduit | Conduit validation currently covers structural + policy-shape checks but is not yet bound to full AGENT-CONSTITUTION and runtime policy registries. | `core/layer2/conduit` now includes `RegistryPolicyGate` bound to `AGENT-CONSTITUTION.md` and `client/runtime/config/guard_check_registry.json`, deny-by-default command capability mappings, and deterministic per-request `policy_receipt_hash` output on validation. / V6-CONDUIT-001 / 6 / 1/2/client | 10 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-CONDUIT-003 | existing-coverage-validated | [extension / V6] Conduit Feature Migration: Agent Lifecycle Path | At least one real feature path must run through conduit to prove TS/Rust bridge viability beyond scaffolding. | `client/runtime/systems/ops/protheusd.ts` now supports conduit-first lifecycle routing (`start`, `stop`, `status`) via typed conduit client with graceful fallback to legacy control-plane execution when conduit transport is unavailable. / V6-CONDUIT-001, V6-CONDUIT-002 / 6 / 1/2/client | 10 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-CONDUIT-004 | existing-coverage-validated | [scale-readiness / V6] Substrate-Swap and Performance Certification Gate | Conduit requires objective proof that core behavior is identical with and without TS and that overhead stays within the stated latency budget. | Added certification suite in `core/layer2/conduit/tests/certification.rs` proving direct-core vs stdio parity and asserting round-trip latency budgets (`<5ms hosted`, `<20ms embedded`) with deterministic receipt checks. / V6-CONDUIT-003 / 6 / 1/2/client | 10 | -1/0/1/2/3/adapter |
| V6-CONDUIT-005 | existing-coverage-validated | [hardening / V6] Message Signing for Every Crossing | Unsigned conduit requests allow tampering risk at the Rust/TS boundary. | Added `core/layer2/conduit-security` `MessageSigner` and enforced signature verification on every command in `core/layer2/conduit`; invalid signatures fail closed with policy-violation receipts and regression tests. / V6-CONDUIT-002 / 6 / 1/2/client | 10 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-CONDUIT-006 | existing-coverage-validated | [hardening / V6] Capability Token Enforcement | TS callers need explicit bounded authority so command scope cannot exceed granted capabilities. | Added signed capability tokens (`CapabilityTokenAuthority`) and command-scope validation in conduit runtime; missing/expired/invalid scopes are denied fail-closed with deterministic receipts and tests. / V6-CONDUIT-002, V6-CONDUIT-005 / 10 / 0/1/2/client | 10 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-CONDUIT-007 | existing-coverage-validated | [hardening / V6] Conduit Rate Limiting + Backpressure | Unbounded conduit traffic can create resource contention and denial-of-service risk. | Added per-client and per-client-command rate-limiter enforcement in conduit runtime with deterministic denial reasons, plus test coverage for throttling windows and recovery after reset. / V6-CONDUIT-002 / 6 / 1/2/client | 10 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-CONDUIT-008 | existing-coverage-validated | [governance / V6] Conduit Regression Rebuild Ledger | Conduit hardening requirements need explicit backlog traceability so full behavior can be reconstructed after regressions. | `UPGRADE_BACKLOG.md` and `docs/client/requirements/REQ-05-protheus-conduit-bridge.md` now include complete phase + hardening requirement mapping with concrete code/test evidence for rebuild. / V6-CONDUIT-001, V6-CONDUIT-007 / 6 / 1/2/client | 10 | 0/1/2/client |

## ZeroLeaks Injection Hardening Source Coverage Intake (Doc `143v84ci3rHjP7WtbYi9tJ2KeJZUkNTIsFTye1NHrheA`, 2026-03-11)

Source references:
- https://docs.google.com/document/d/143v84ci3rHjP7WtbYi9tJ2KeJZUkNTIsFTye1NHrheA/edit?usp=sharing
- https://x.com/NotLucknite/status/2030728838028915011

Notes:
- Primitive-first normalization: this extends existing security/red-team/covenant lanes; it does not create a separate security subsystem.
- Overlap handled explicitly:
  - red-team probe generation and chaos suites: `BL-049`, `V6-SEC-008`, `V3-RED-001`
  - conduit/policy formal hardening: `V6-SEC-005`, `V6-CONDUIT-002`
  - ongoing public/security posture lanes: `V6-SEC-001..009`
- Net-new focus from this source:
  - standardized prompt-injection and MCP/tool-poisoning benchmark pack with fixed score contract
  - runtime live security score and trend telemetry for operator surfaces
  - automatic remediation loop for discovered prompt-policy vulnerabilities before promotion
  - deterministic blast-radius tracking for "attempted tool execution" probes
- Default placement:
  - Layer `0`: final injection/tool-action deny enforcement + deterministic deny receipts
  - Layer `1`: remediation policy, score thresholds, probe taxonomy/retention policy
  - Layer `2`: scanner orchestration, probe execution harness, scoring engine, blast-radius correlator
  - `client`: thin security score visualization and scan controls only
  - `app`: optional `/apps/security_lab/` profile only

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-SEC-010 | done | [hardening / V6] Continuous Injection/MCP Poisoning Scanner Contract | Security confidence is hard to compare release-to-release without a fixed, repeatable probe harness and deterministic scoring outputs. | Implemented scanner lane in `core/layer0/ops/src/security_plane.rs` (`scan` command) with deterministic score artifacts + receipt persistence; behavior proven by `core/layer0/ops/tests/v6_security_hardening_integration.rs::v6_sec_010_scan_lane_detects_injection_and_emits_receipts`. / V6-SEC-005, V6-SEC-008 / 10 / 0/1/2 | 10 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V6-SEC-011 | done | [hardening / V6] Layer-0 Injection Deny + Auto-Remediation Loop Contract | Discovered prompt-policy vulnerabilities can recur without an automatic remediation loop tied to promotion gates. | Implemented remediation lane in `core/layer0/ops/src/security_plane.rs` (`remediate` / `auto-remediate`) with promotion-gate fail-close until clean re-scan; behavior proven by `core/layer0/ops/tests/v6_security_hardening_integration.rs::v6_sec_011_auto_remediation_blocks_promotion_until_rescan_passes`. / V6-SEC-010, V6-COCKPIT-023.1, V6-COCKPIT-023.3 / 10 / 0/1/2 | 10 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-SEC-012 | done | [hardening / V6] Tool-Execution Blast-Radius Sentinel Contract | Injection success impact depends on downstream tool actions, but current outputs do not always expose attempted/blocked external side effects as first-class evidence. | Implemented blast-radius sentinel in `core/layer0/ops/src/security_plane.rs` (`blast-radius-sentinel`) with attempted-action recording and strict fail-closed blocking; behavior proven by `core/layer0/ops/tests/v6_security_hardening_integration.rs::v6_sec_012_blast_radius_sentinel_records_and_blocks_high_risk_actions`. / V6-SEC-010, V6-CONDUIT-006 / 10 / 0/1/2 | 10 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V6-SEC-013 | done | [scale-readiness / V6] Live Security Scoreboard + Trend Alerting Contract | Operators need real-time security posture visibility, not periodic static reports. | Add `protheus-top` security pane showing live score trend, top failure classes, and threshold alerts (policy-defined floors), with deterministic score-history receipts and one-click re-scan control path. / V6-SEC-010, V6-OBSERVABILITY-004.* / 10 / 0/1/2 | 10 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-SEC-014 | done | [governance / V6] Curated Injection Probe Pack + Remediation Playbook Governance | Probe quality and coverage drift without signed curated packs and periodic review of emerging attack vectors. | Maintain signed probe/playbook pack installable via governed URI (for example `security://zeroleaks-hardened`) with quarterly human review, compatibility metadata, and deterministic install/update/deprecate receipts. / V6-SEC-010, V6-SEC-006 / 10 / 0/1/2 | 10 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-SEC-015 | done | [hardening / V6] Conduit-Only Security Scan/Remediation Boundary Enforcement | Security scanning/remediation can become bypassable if run directly from client scripts or unmanaged adapters. | Enforce conduit-only routing for scan/start/stop/report/remediate actions with bypass-rejection tests and fail-closed policy denials; keep client/app surfaces non-authoritative. / V6-SEC-010, V6-CONDUIT-002 / 10 / 0/1/2 | 10 | app |

Regression coverage: `tests/client-memory-tools/verify_empty_fort.test.js`. | 6 | 1/2/client |

## Dominance Roadmap Intake (Google Doc `1u_KCXofHnG2OGFsLp3J0r-Yk-YyN0Ft0dzeEeOFd4kk`, 2026-03-08)

Objective: translate competitive-roadmap claims into executable, integrity-safe requirements focused on measurable adoption/performance/interoperability gains without fabricated or unverified public claims.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-DOM-401 | existing-coverage-validated | Polyglot GA release contract (`v0.1.0`) | Multi-channel release packaging is fragmented, limiting adoption and credibility across Rust/TS/Python ecosystems. | Ship versioned release contract for GitHub Releases + crates.io + npm + PyPI with signed multi-arch artifacts and SBOM bundle; release gate fails if any channel or signature artifact is missing. | 8 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-DOM-402 | existing-coverage-validated | Native Python SDK parity lane (Rust-backed) | Existing Python wrapper surface is too thin to compete with first-class Python agent frameworks. | Add Rust-backed Python SDK under `sdk/python` with async client parity for core APIs (`lens`, `orchestrate`, `shadow`, `research`), receipt propagation, and parity regression tests against TS client behavior. | 8 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-DOM-403 | existing-coverage-validated | Public benchmark crown harness + reproducibility gate | Performance claims are not yet continuously published through one reproducible benchmark lane. | Add benchmark workflow + artifact pack that runs concurrency/cold-start/throughput/token-efficiency suites in reproducible containers, publishes machine-readable reports, and fails on regressions against policy thresholds. | 8 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-DOM-404 | existing-coverage-validated | Zero-to-production developer bootstrap UX lane | New-user onboarding requires too many manual steps across languages and safety primitives. | Add `protheus init --lang <python | 8 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-DOM-405 | existing-coverage-validated | Tool registry + policy-gated marketplace ingestion lane | Ecosystem growth is constrained without one governed package/tool ingestion path. | Add registry + install contract (`protheus hub install`) with constitution/policy checks, signed package metadata, tool manifest schema validation, and deterministic install receipts. | 8 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-DOM-406 | existing-coverage-validated | Crew/debate/shadow orchestration contract uplift | Competitors win on simple orchestration semantics while Protheus orchestration surfaces remain fragmented. | Add declarative orchestration entrypoints (`crew`, `debate`, persistent shadow scheduling) that compile through Safety Plane graphs with typed receipts and conflict-resolution tests. | 10 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-DOM-407 | existing-coverage-validated | MCP + A2A interoperability runtime lane | Protocol-war interoperability is incomplete without first-class MCP/A2A support in the core runtime boundary. | Add native MCP server surface and A2A session bridge through conduit with signed identity/session receipts, conformance tests, and no-bypass three-plane boundary enforcement. | 8 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-DOM-408 | existing-coverage-validated | WebMCP/browser substrate adapter | Browser-native agent execution path is missing, limiting zero-install reach and standards alignment. | Add substrate adapter under `planes/substrate/webmcp/` with policy-gated browser session contracts, receipt chaining, and fallback-safe capability restrictions. | 8 | 0/1/2/adapter |
| V6-DOM-409 | existing-coverage-validated | Agent identity and trust control plane | Cross-agent authentication/authorization remains under-specified for standards-aligned deployments. | Add identity plane contracts for agent IDs, signed session assertions, and trust-chain verification tied to Merkle receipts and governance policy; include replay/revocation tests. | 8 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-DOM-410 | existing-coverage-validated | Kubernetes operator + multi-cloud runtime packaging | Helm/Terraform exist, but there is no first-class operator for autonomous lifecycle management at enterprise scale. | Add `protheus-operator` packaging and CRDs with lifecycle reconciliation, autoscaling, and receipt export hooks; conformance suite validates install/upgrade/rollback behavior across Kubernetes profiles. | 8 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-DOM-411 | existing-coverage-validated | Open Agent Contracts specification pack | Three-plane/conduit interfaces are documented but not published as one machine-readable external compatibility contract. | Publish versioned Open Agent Contracts spec (schemas + examples + compatibility tests) covering three planes, conduit envelopes, identity/session primitives, and backward-compatibility policy checks. | 8 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-DOM-412 | existing-coverage-validated | Vertical compliance template compiler + TRiSM report lane | Regulated-market adoption is slowed by lack of precompiled policy templates and auditable compliance report generation. | Add template compiler for regulated profiles (finance/healthcare/defense) that emits policy bundles + deterministic TRiSM-style evidence reports from receipts with validation and replay tests. | 8 | 0/1/2 |

## Conduit Schema Hardening Intake (Google Doc `17E9b15Lw5AkyvuqeVW8QwNd3Q2vm1hrui-aWPC7ub7M`, 2026-03-08)

Objective: harden schema/endpoint exposure at the conduit boundary through runtime-only schema materialization, session-scoped cryptographic scrambling, and strict governance-safe debugging controls.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-SCHEMA-501 | existing-coverage-validated | Runtime-only schema materialization lane | Static artifacts can leak high-fidelity protocol semantics when schema contracts are embedded in files/binaries. | Real internal schema contracts are materialized only in process memory after key-gated bootstrap; no plaintext runtime schema file is shipped in release artifacts; release scan gate asserts absence of plaintext signatures. | 6 | 1/2/client |
| V6-SCHEMA-502 | existing-coverage-validated | Conduit scrambler boundary primitive | TS/Core traffic currently relies on typed envelopes but lacks mandatory session-level payload scrambling at the boundary. | Add Rust scrambler module in conduit path with authenticated encryption and deterministic session bootstrap receipts; all client/core messages pass through scramble/unscramble path with parity tests proving no behavior drift. | 6 | 1/2/client |
| V6-SCHEMA-503 | existing-coverage-validated | Per-build protocol-shape randomization contract | Repeated static envelope identifiers increase pattern-learning surface for reverse engineering. | Build pipeline generates per-release randomized public protocol aliases mapped to stable internal commands via signed mapping artifacts; compatibility tests verify command integrity and backward-safe migration path. | 6 | 1/2/client |
| V6-SCHEMA-504 | existing-coverage-validated | Debug deactivation key + fail-closed key policy | Security hardening without operator override blocks development, while weak override controls create bypass risk. | Introduce explicit debug-key bypass mode with strict env-gated activation, signed audit receipts, and fail-closed default when key state is invalid or missing in hardened builds. | 10 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-SCHEMA-505 | existing-coverage-validated | Decoy/honeypot schema telemetry lane | Recon attempts are currently under-observed at schema/protocol probing surfaces. | Add controlled decoy schema/honeypot responses for unauthorized probes with immutable telemetry receipts and alert thresholds; lane must not alter documented public contracts or violate claim-evidence policy (`V4-FORT-006`). | 6 | 1/2/client |
| V6-SCHEMA-506 | existing-coverage-validated | Scrambler performance + resilience verification gate | Boundary hardening can regress latency/reliability without explicit budgets and chaos checks. | Add benchmark + chaos gate for scrambler path enforcing overhead budget, replay resilience, and fallback integrity; CI fails when p95 latency or error-rate thresholds are exceeded. | 6 | 1/2/client |

## Reverse-Engineering Resistance Intake (Google Doc `1B932-wmk94Hyo4GxKPENZZ7nv02-hLbmmZn3BNwlfXw`, 2026-03-08)

Objective: raise reverse-engineering cost through governance-safe misdirection, build polymorphism, and runtime compartmentalization while preserving deterministic operations and developer recoverability.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-OBF-601 | existing-coverage-validated | Decoy architecture artifact generator | Public architecture surfaces remain too consistent, enabling efficient static pattern analysis. | Add build-lane generator for clearly labeled non-authoritative decoy architecture artifacts under `docs/architecture/decoy_variants/` with deterministic provenance metadata and strict guard that real contracts remain in canonical specs. | 6 | 1/2/client |
| V6-OBF-602 | existing-coverage-validated | Polymorphic build randomization hooks | Stable symbol/layout patterns across builds reduce attacker effort for binary diffing and model-assisted inference. | Add compile-time polymorphism hooks (symbol aliasing, string protection, layout variation toggles) with per-build seed receipts and deterministic debug override mode; release pipeline records build fingerprint manifests. | 6 | 1/2/client |
| V6-OBF-603 | existing-coverage-validated | Runtime schema shard loader and in-memory reassembly | Sensitive protocol semantics are still too recoverable from static artifacts without memory-only assembly controls. | Implement schema shard loading contract with encrypted fragments, runtime in-memory reassembly, zeroization on shutdown, and boot-time receipt proof that no plaintext shard files persist on disk. | 6 | 1/2/client |
| V6-OBF-604 | existing-coverage-validated | False-invariant noise lane with integrity boundaries | External analysis can overfit to deterministic invariants if all published traces are semantically direct. | Add optional noise-lane generator for non-authoritative invariants/trace artifacts that is explicitly tagged as synthetic and never contaminates canonical receipts, benchmarks, or compliance evidence (`V4-FORT-006` guard enforced). | 6 | 1/2/client |
| V6-OBF-605 | existing-coverage-validated | Anti-analysis telemetry and honeypot module guard | Reverse-engineering attempts lack dedicated signal capture and rate-aware response at module boundaries. | Add honeypot/anti-analysis module lane that emits bounded telemetry receipts on suspicious probe patterns with strict privacy policy, no undeclared outbound traffic, and configurable deny/degrade responses. | 6 | 1/2/client |
| V6-OBF-606 | existing-coverage-validated | Cryptographic watermark and provenance tracing lane | Attribution of leaked or tampered binaries is weak without embedded provenance markers. | Add cryptographic watermark embedding and verification tooling for release binaries and receipt bundles; verification must prove artifact lineage without exposing private keys or sensitive payload contents. | 6 | 1/2/client |
| V6-OBF-607 | existing-coverage-validated | Obfuscation safety and operability regression gate | Security hardening can unintentionally degrade operator workflows and incident response if not continuously checked. | Add CI gate covering obfuscated vs debug builds for parity, startup health, and incident-debug workflow checks; gate fails if operability SLOs regress beyond policy thresholds. | 6 | 1/2/client |
| V6-OBF-608 | existing-coverage-validated | Layered obfuscation policy contract | Ad hoc obfuscation controls drift without one canonical policy for allowed techniques and safety constraints. | Add `client/runtime/config/obfuscation_policy.json` + contract checker defining allowed modes, prohibited behaviors (fabricated claims, hidden network exfil, receipt contamination), key handling requirements, and required approvals for hardened release mode. | 6 | 1/2/client |

| V6-EXT-703 | existing-coverage-validated | Extension SDK parity pack (TS/Python/Go/Rust) | Polyglot extension authoring is fragmented and inconsistent across languages. | Publish official conduit SDK surfaces for TS/Python/Go/Rust with shared schema contracts, version compatibility checks, and parity tests on core actions. | 7 | 1/2/client/app/adapter |
| V6-EXT-704 | existing-coverage-validated | Capability-manifest + least-privilege enforcement lane | Extensions can drift into broad permissions without strict declarative capability boundaries. | Add extension capability manifest schema and runtime enforcement gate that issues scoped tokens, denies undeclared access, and records deny/allow receipts for each privileged call. | 7 | 1/2/client/app/adapter |
| V6-EXT-705 | existing-coverage-validated | Extension sandbox runtime + isolation contract | Unisolated extensions increase blast radius and governance bypass risk. | Add isolated extension runtime profile (process/container sandbox), policy-controlled resource/network bounds, and fail-closed startup if isolation guarantees are not met. | 10 | 0/1/2/client/app/adapter |
| V6-EXT-706 | existing-coverage-validated | One-click publish lane with governance checks | Publishing extensions requires manual multi-step checks and inconsistent policy validation. | Add `protheus publish` flow that runs constitution/policy/receipt checks, schema compatibility validation, and trusted metadata generation before registry admission. | 7 | 1/2/client/app/adapter |
| V6-EXT-707 | existing-coverage-validated | Marketplace registry + dependency resolver lane | Extension discovery/composition lacks a canonical verified registry with dependency governance. | Add registry contracts for extension metadata, dependency graph resolution, signature verification, and deny-list handling with deterministic install receipts. | 7 | 1/2/client/app/adapter |
| V6-EXT-708 | existing-coverage-validated | Extension trust scoring and reputation receipts | Users lack objective trust signals tied to runtime governance behavior. | Add trust score lane combining policy violations, receipt integrity, and runtime health signals; expose deterministic score artifacts and regression tests for score reproducibility. | 7 | 1/2/client/app/adapter |
| V6-EXT-709 | existing-coverage-validated | Developer hot-reload sandbox (`protheus dev`) | Local extension iteration is slow without integrated sandbox + live reload workflow. | Add `protheus dev` mode that runs extension in isolated sandbox with hot reload, policy simulation, and live receipt viewer; include guardrails preventing non-sandbox bypass. | 7 | 1/2/client/app/adapter |
| V6-EXT-710 | existing-coverage-validated | Extension overhead performance gate | Extension framework overhead can erode core runtime performance without explicit SLO enforcement. | Add benchmark gate proving extension call boundary overhead stays below configured threshold (default <3ms p95) and fails CI when regressions exceed policy budget. | 7 | 1/2/client/app/adapter |

## Decentralized Fork Convergence & Structured Feedback Pipeline Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1b2fSTI35W4dbOzJ5ZWAWw15dWGf-P_X9TuvgqL0qhhk`, 2026-03-11)

Source references:
- https://docs.google.com/document/d/1b2fSTI35W4dbOzJ5ZWAWw15dWGf-P_X9TuvgqL0qhhk/edit?usp=sharing
- https://x.com/agent_wrapper/status/2031508924302860406

Notes:
- Primitive-first normalization: no new core primitive is introduced. This intake extends existing sovereign identity, Epistemic Object, conduit consent, swarm, and observability primitives.
- Overlap handled explicitly:
  - network collaboration and inter-node exchange: `V6-NETWORK-001.*`
  - decentralized discovery/sharing/compute-to-data: `V6-NETWORK-002.*`
  - swarm routing baseline: `V6-SWARM-001`
  - company/ticket/delegation governance lanes: `V6-COMPANY-001.*`, `V6-ORCH-001.*`
- Placement correction from source doc:
  - source suggests a "client extension"; normalized placement is core-authoritative execution with thin client wrappers and optional app UX only.
- Default placement:
  - Layer `0`: conduit-routed report/issue/session/pr/fork/convergence actions + deterministic receipts
  - Layer `1`: fork sovereignty policy, consent/approval policy, convergence metadata policy, review-tier policy
  - Layer `2`: report->issue->session->pr orchestrator, convergence detector, fork lifecycle manager, review scheduler
  - Layer `3`: fork-aware persona defaults only
  - `client`: thin command/status/observability wrappers only
  - `app`: optional `/apps/sovereign_cortex/` UX workflow only
  - `adapter`: external forge/repo integration adapters only

Objective: enable fork-native decentralized self-improvement where independent instances evolve with sovereignty, voluntarily converge when patterns match, and route all feedback-to-merge operations through consent-gated, receipt-backed core lanes.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-NETWORK-003.1 | existing-coverage-validated | Structured Report -> Issue -> Session -> PR Pipeline Contract | Distributed improvement loops stay manual and inconsistent without one deterministic progression from observation to merge candidate. | Implement governed pipeline where any valid report object can promote through issue/session/PR stages with explicit state transitions, deterministic receipts, and policy-gated stage advancement. | 9 | 0/1/2/client/app |
| V6-NETWORK-003.2 | existing-coverage-validated | Cross-Fork Convergence Detection Contract | Independent forks duplicate work when common solution patterns are not detected and surfaced for voluntary convergence. | Add convergence detector that identifies semantically similar fixes across forks, emits ranked convergence proposals with provenance/confidence metadata, and routes proposals to consent-gated upstream contribution flow. | 8 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-NETWORK-003.3 | existing-coverage-validated | Fork Sovereignty Lifecycle Contract | Fork identity, health, and divergence are hard to govern without one lifecycle model tied to identity and policy scopes. | Define lifecycle operations (`create/register/track/diverge/archive`) bound to sovereign identity objects, with deterministic health/divergence receipts and fail-closed policy on unauthorized fork operations. | 9 | 0/1/2/3 |
| V6-NETWORK-003.4 | existing-coverage-validated | Consent-Gated Multi-Tier Review Contract | Autonomous merge loops are unsafe without explicit fast/deep review tiers and human consent control for high-impact changes. | Require two-tier review (`fast-pass`, `deep-arch`) with policy thresholds for auto-advance vs human approval, and deterministic review decision receipts linked to each PR candidate. | 10 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-NETWORK-003.5 | existing-coverage-validated | Dogfooding Improvement Loop Contract | Infrastructure quality stalls when agents do not continuously exercise and validate their own improvement machinery in production-like flows. | Add governed dogfooding mode where orchestration, convergence, and review paths run against self-hosted infrastructure changes with failure routing, rollback hooks, and observability evidence artifacts. | 8 | 0/1/2/client/app |
| V6-NETWORK-003.6 | existing-coverage-validated | Conduit-Only Network Convergence Boundary + Thin-Surface Enforcement | Fork/convergence orchestration can bypass safety policy if implemented in client/app logic rather than authoritative core lanes. | Enforce conduit-only execution for all report/fork/convergence/review actions, reject bypass paths in contract checks, and keep client/app layers strictly non-authoritative UX/control surfaces. | 10 | 0/1/2/client/app/adapter |

| V6-COCKPIT-016.2 | existing-coverage-validated | UGC-Style Video Generation Lane | Script-to-video conversion requires manual editing steps and inconsistent model/tool orchestration. | Pipeline generates short UGC-style variants (voiceover, overlays, media composition) with deterministic output hashes and per-asset receipts; target render under 60s/video where substrate allows. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-016.3 | existing-coverage-validated | High-Volume Ad Factory Batch Mode | High-volume ad production lacks one orchestrated cost-aware mode with controlled variation strategy. | `protheus ad factory` can run batch generation (100+ variants/run target) with tracked spend, variation matrix, and failure-retry receipts. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |

## Autoresearch Self-Improving Loop Intake (Source: Karpathy `autoresearch` pattern + Alex Finn thread, 2026-03-09)

Source references:
- https://docs.google.com/document/d/1VFc_KcvdSM2ZXKe3D7QFOyPpwSkfmlyBKY1xtRhv3xw/edit?usp=sharing
- https://docs.google.com/document/d/1HuddUbphNO9w0MCye3GTiCH5Eww4CAcYKkfOOAKBUbs/edit?usp=sharing
- https://docs.google.com/document/d/1xAlRV_Q1t4imnEZX98pO4y76qaHcV029UnwV0A0IloQ/edit?usp=sharing

- https://github.com/Gen-Verse/OpenClaw-RL

Notes:
- The `1Hudd...` source draft labeled this as `V6-APP-014` (`model-lab` app). It is normalized here as source coverage for `V6-COCKPIT-017.*` to avoid a duplicate training-authority branch.
- The `1xAl...` source draft labeled this as `V6-APP-020` (`openclaw-rl` app). It is normalized here as source coverage for `V6-COCKPIT-017.*` to avoid a duplicate app-owned training authority branch.
- The RL-policy-improvement source draft proposed a `client/apps/` extension. That placement is rejected for authority: reward capture, training orchestration, weight promotion, and hot-swap safety belong in core.
- Primitive-first normalization: RL-style policy improvement is an extension of the existing self-improvement/training primitive family, not a separate app/runtime.
- Layer placement:
  - Layer `-1`: optional GPU/accelerator training profiles only
  - Layer `0`: conduit-routed interception, train, evaluate, promote, hot-swap, rollback receipts
  - Layer `1`: reward schema, correction provenance, safety/redaction policy, promotion policy
  - Layer `2`: async training loop, serving/training coordination, hard-case targeting, hot-swap scheduler
  - Layer `3`: RL-aware persona defaults only
  - `client`: thin operator controls/telemetry only
  - `app`: optional self-improvement UX app only, never training authority

Objective: add a safe, conduit-governed self-improvement engine where shadows run iterative short training experiments, track improvement in git-backed experiment history, support dual prompt/code iteration, and extend into governed RL-style policy improvement without bypassing Layer 0.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-COCKPIT-017.1 | existing-coverage-validated | Autonomous Training Loop Engine | Model/agent improvement remains manual and sporadic, limiting overnight progress and reproducible iteration cadence. | `protheus improve model` runs bounded iterative training experiments (short-cycle target) with eval gating that retains only improved candidates and emits deterministic experiment receipts. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-017.2 | existing-coverage-validated | Dual Iteration Mode (Prompt Agent + Code Agent) | Prompt and training-code optimization are conflated, reducing clarity and making handoff quality hard to audit. | Add explicit dual-lane operation separating prompt iteration from code iteration with deterministic handoff receipts and role-scoped policy boundaries. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-017.3 | existing-coverage-validated | Git-Based Experiment Tracking + Merge Policy | Experiment provenance is weak without branch-level history tied to validation metrics and promotion rules. | Each experiment is commit-linked on feature branch with metric artifacts; merge/promotion only occurs when policy thresholds are met and receipts link commit → evaluation result. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-017.4 | existing-coverage-validated | Hardware-Agnostic Single-GPU Runtime Profile | Personal-hardware improvement loops are inconsistent across GPU classes and can exceed local resource budgets. | Provide single-GPU profile with budget-aware scheduling, deterministic fallback behavior, and no required external managed training service for baseline operation. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-017.5 | existing-coverage-validated | Conduit-Only Self-Improvement Enforcement + Receipts | Training and git mutation paths can bypass canonical safety/policy controls if executed directly from tool/app layers. | Route all training runs, git mutations, and model promotions through conduit with capability/budget checks and deterministic receipts; bypass detections fail closed via contract gates. | 10 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-017.6 | existing-coverage-validated | Conversation correction/reward capture extension | RL-style improvement needs governed reward signals from real usage, but conversation feedback/corrections are not yet modeled as a canonical training signal contract. | Add reward-capture primitives that convert bounded user corrections/approvals/failures into redacted reward objects with provenance, privacy policy labels, and deterministic receipts; routine turns can be scored as `positive/negative/neutral` or token/span-level corrections without leaking raw sensitive content into training lanes. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-017.7 | existing-coverage-validated | Async train/serve coexistence + hot-swap promotion contract | Background training only helps if serving remains stable and improved candidates can be promoted without interrupting live operation or widening failure blast radius. | Add async serving/training coexistence contract where candidate policies train in parallel, pass eval/policy gates, and promote through bounded hot-swap/canary/rollback flow with no serving interruption and deterministic promotion receipts. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-017.8 | existing-coverage-validated | Hard-case targeting policy for policy-level failures | Self-improvement loops waste budget when they spend training cycles on memory/retrieval/skill failures that should be fixed elsewhere. | Add targeting policy that routes only policy-level failures (planning, tool ordering, ambiguity handling, sequencing) into RL/distillation queues while explicitly excluding memory/index/skill/config failures unless reclassified; targeting decisions emit deterministic receipts and budget telemetry. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-017.9 | existing-coverage-validated | Decoupled Async RL Loop Topology Contract (Serve/Rollout/Judge/Train) | Background policy learning remains fragile without a formal decoupled topology for serving, rollout collection, reward judging, and training loops. | Add governed four-loop async topology contract with bounded queue interfaces between loops, deterministic loop-state receipts, and non-blocking serve guarantees during training and promotion windows. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-017.10 | existing-coverage-validated | Policy Objective Mode Contract (Binary Reward vs Token-Distillation) | RL pipelines need explicit objective selection to avoid ad hoc switching between reward-style and distillation-style learning runs. | Add policy-bounded objective-mode selector (`binary_reward`, `token_distillation`) with deterministic run metadata receipts, per-mode guardrails, and compatibility checks before model promotion. | 7 | 0/1/2/client |

- Net-new focus from this source:

  - `app`: optional `/apps/context_lab/` UX/marketplace profile only
  - `adapter`: plugin transport/package/runtime adapters
