| V6-CONTEXT-001.1 | existing-coverage-validated | Full Context Pipeline Plugin Interface Contract | Current plugin coverage is narrow; teams cannot safely replace the whole context lifecycle without ad hoc runtime modifications. | Add versioned plugin interface spanning context assembly, compaction, expansion, and summarization stages; plugin stage calls emit deterministic receipts and fail closed on policy/schema violations. | 8 | 1/2/client |
| V6-CONTEXT-001.2 | existing-coverage-validated | Standardized On-Demand Summary Expansion API Contract | Lossless strategies need a canonical expansion hook so high-fidelity context can be reconstructed without bespoke code paths. | Add `expand(summary_id, budget)` contract with provenance/latency/budget receipts and policy-bounded expansion behavior; default lossless strategy is implemented as a first-party plugin over this API. | 8 | 1/2/client |
| V6-CONTEXT-001.3 | existing-coverage-validated | Per-Topic/Per-Channel Context Strategy Routing Contract | Multi-agent/channel deployments need different context behaviors by scope, but current strategy selection is not fully scope-aware. | Add scope binding manifests (`topic/channel/agent -> strategy`) evaluated by router at runtime, with deterministic scope-resolution receipts and fail-closed fallback when plugin or scope policy is invalid. | 10 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-CONTEXT-001.4 | existing-coverage-validated | Context Strategy Hot-Swap + Migration Safety Contract | Mid-session strategy changes risk context loss/corruption without a governed state-migration and rollback mechanism. | Add `context plugin swap` flow with preflight compatibility checks, state migration plan, atomic cutover, and automatic rollback on failure; every migration/cutover/rollback step emits deterministic receipts. | 8 | 1/2/client |
| V6-CONTEXT-001.5 | existing-coverage-validated | Curated Context Plugin Pack Governance Lane | Community strategy innovation is hard to adopt safely without signed curated packs, benchmark metadata, and review cadence. | Maintain signed context plugin pack installable via governed URI (for example `context://lossless-claw`) with benchmark/provenance metadata, quarterly human review, and deterministic install/update/deprecate receipts. | 8 | 1/2/client |
| V6-CONTEXT-001.6 | existing-coverage-validated | Conduit-Only Context Plugin Boundary + Thin Client Enforcement | Plugin loading/routing/swapping can bypass policy controls if implemented directly in client/app code paths. | Enforce conduit-only routing for plugin install/activate/route/swap/remove actions with bypass-rejection tests and fail-closed policy checks; client/app surfaces remain non-authoritative controls. | 10 | 0/1/2/client |

## Default Eyes & Hands Framework Intake (Internal design doc, 2026-03-11)

Source references:
- https://docs.google.com/document/d/10nbmhL4eItx8qHFZl4JFDPPE6H4b69x3Y0W0C0CAX5Q/edit?usp=sharing

Notes:
- Source draft proposed a `client/apps/` extension. That placement is rejected for authority: default eye/hand registry, activation policy, scheduling, and I/O governance belong in core.
- Primitive-first normalization: this intake extends the existing Eye/sensor primitive, tool/hand capability packs, connector/API lanes, persona bindings, and observability registry. It should not create a separate app-owned device/tool system.
- MarketEye is treated as a packaged default Eye profile built on existing research/API/connector primitives, not as a new primitive family.
- Layer placement:
  - Layer `0`: conduit-routed eye/hand activation, I/O, receipts, fail-closed policy gates
  - Layer `1`: registry metadata, trigger policy, capability scope, provider/privacy/budget policy
  - Layer `2`: eye polling/refresh, hand invocation orchestration, packaged default profile loading
  - Layer `3`: persona bindings and opt-in defaults only
  - `client`: thin `enable/status/view` surfaces only
  - `app`: optional showcase/default-app UX only
  - `adapter`: provider-specific feeds/APIs/device drivers

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-EYES-001.1 | existing-coverage-validated | Default Eye/Hand registry primitive extension | Out-of-the-box utility requires a canonical registry of packaged sensors and action packs, but that registry must remain core-owned rather than app-owned metadata. | Add a core-owned registry for packaged default Eyes and Hands with typed metadata (capabilities, triggers, policy class, status, provider/adapters, budget profile) and deterministic activation/deactivation/status receipts. | 6 | 1/2/client |
| V6-EYES-001.2 | existing-coverage-validated | Packaged default Eye profiles over research/API/connector primitives | Useful defaults should be shipped as governed profiles over existing primitives, not as one-off feature silos. | Add packaged default Eye profiles (including MarketEye/market-data profile) implemented through existing research/API/connector lanes so `protheus eye enable <name>` or persona defaults can activate them without introducing new authority outside core; provider-specific transport stays in adapters. | 6 | 1/2/client |
| V6-EYES-001.3 | existing-coverage-validated | Packaged default Hand capability packs | Default "Hands" should be capability-scoped action packs over existing tool/sub-agent/runtime primitives, not a separate execution framework. | Add packaged default Hand profiles that bind existing tool/runtime/sub-agent capabilities into reusable action packs with explicit capability scopes, trigger rules, and deterministic invocation receipts. | 6 | 1/2/client |
| V6-EYES-001.4 | existing-coverage-validated | Persona-aware opt-in activation + observability surfacing | Default Eyes/Hands must remain opt-in and visible, otherwise packaged capability silently widens the runtime surface. | Extend persona bindings and observability so relevant packaged Eyes/Hands can be suggested or enabled per persona with explicit opt-in, clear status in `protheus-top`, and deterministic activation/telemetry receipts. | 6 | 1/2/client |
| V6-EYES-001.5 | existing-coverage-validated | Conduit-only Eye/Hand guardrails + adapter boundaries | Packaged defaults increase attack surface unless all sensing/action I/O remains policy-gated and adapter-specific logic is kept outside client/app authority. | Enforce Layer-0 conduit checks for all Eye/Hand I/O, keep provider/device specifics in adapters, and emit deterministic receipts for registry loads, activations, polls, tool invocations, and policy denials. | 10 | 0/1/2/client/adapter |

## Autonomous Economic Specialization App Intake (CashClaw pattern, 2026-03-11)

Source references:
- https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Nwdn4QIZ_epxYuvNLb4ikVadUSN7HIN0DpxJd78nCYg/edit?usp=sharing
- https://x.com/moltlaunch/status/2031128159794397578

Notes:
- This is not a new primitive family. It is an application/workflow layer over existing research/discovery, capability-pack, payment/reputation, and self-improvement primitives.
- Correct placement is `/apps`, not `client`, with thin client exposure only if needed for UX. Authority for discovery, execution, payment logging, reputation, and learning remains in core primitives and existing policy lanes.
- Primitive composition:
  - task discovery builds on research/API/marketplace primitives
  - delivery builds on existing Hands/tool/capability-pack/runtime primitives
  - payment/reputation builds on economy and ledger primitives
  - feedback-driven improvement builds on memory + self-improvement primitives
- Layer placement:
  - Layer `0`: conduit-routed discovery/delivery/payment/reputation/learning receipts
  - Layer `1`: specialization profile, payment/reputation policy, budget/risk/privacy controls
  - Layer `2`: task matching, workflow execution, escrow/payment routing, feedback ingestion
  - Layer `3`: economic-agent persona defaults only
  - `app`: primary home (`/apps/cashclaw` or equivalent)
  - `client`: optional thin launch/status/UX surfaces only
  - `adapter`: marketplace/onchain/provider-specific connectors

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-APP-016.1 | existing-coverage-validated | Specialization + paid-task discovery app workflow | Autonomous economic agents need a reusable workflow for specialization-constrained task discovery, but this should compose existing research/marketplace primitives rather than create a bespoke core subsystem. | Ship `/apps` workflow that lets an agent adopt a specialization profile and discover paid tasks from approved marketplaces/providers using existing discovery primitives, with deterministic discovery and match receipts. | 6 | app |
| V6-APP-016.2 | existing-coverage-validated | Feedback/reputation/payment compounding loop over existing primitives | Economic specialization only becomes self-improving when payment outcomes, feedback, and learned rules are retained and routed back into memory and policy-improvement lanes. | Store payments, reputation changes, feedback, and learned specialization rules through existing Epistemic Object/economy primitives, and wire them into governed improvement loops so performance compounds without introducing a separate learning subsystem. | 6 | app |
| V6-APP-016.3 | existing-coverage-validated | Escrow/payment/reputation handling over economy/ledger primitives | Money-making workflows need payment and reputation state, but that state should extend existing economy/ledger primitives instead of inventing a second commerce backend. | Add app-level payment/reputation workflow that routes escrow simulation, payment receipts, payout confirmation, and reputation updates through existing economy/ledger policies and emits deterministic lifecycle receipts. | 6 | app |
| V6-APP-016.4 | existing-coverage-validated | Conduit-only economic workflow enforcement + `/apps` placement contract | Economic automation increases risk and should remain explicitly outside the client boundary while still using canonical safety and audit paths. | Ship the specialization workflow under `/apps`, keep any client wrapper thin, and enforce conduit-only routing for discovery, delivery, payment, reputation, and improvement actions with deterministic receipts and fail-closed policy checks. | 10 | 0/app |

## CLAUDE.md Workflow Config Loader Intake (Doc `1bR1dCvFmjqDnWqDS3i6R5pQ4qDHcytY0yesKtQ_yy4U`, 2026-03-11)

Source references:
- https://docs.google.com/document/d/1bR1dCvFmjqDnWqDS3i6R5pQ4qDHcytY0yesKtQ_yy4U/edit?usp=sharing
- https://x.com/DAIEvolutionHub/status/2030979189906534800

Notes:
- Primitive-first normalization: this is an app/workflow composition over existing primitives (persona constitution loading, Epistemic Objects, orchestrator delegation, verification lanes, receipts), not a new core subsystem.
- Default placement:
  - Layer `0`: existing conduit + receipt enforcement only
  - Layer `1`: existing policy/memory/rule-object primitives
  - Layer `2`: existing planning/delegation/research/verification primitives
  - Layer `3`: constitution/persona overlays (loader hooks only)
  - `client`: thin command + observability surface only
  - `app`: primary implementation at `/apps/claude_workflow/`

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-APP-013.1 | existing-coverage-validated | `CLAUDE.md` Config Parser over Constitution + Rule Objects | Coding workflow rules are scattered across prompts and ad hoc notes, so behavior is not consistently reproducible across projects. | Dropping `CLAUDE.md` in a project root is detected by the app and parsed into governed rule objects (Epistemic Objects) using existing constitution/persona contracts, with deterministic parse/apply receipts and no duplicate rule engine outside core primitives. | 6 | app |
| V6-APP-013.2 | existing-coverage-validated | Planning-Mode + Sub-Agent Delegation Profile | Complex tasks still rely on manual decomposition, causing context bloat and inconsistent delegation behavior. | The app activates an existing planning/delegation profile for complex tasks, spawning bounded sub-agents via current Layer-2 orchestrator lanes, preserving parent-child lineage and deterministic handoff receipts. | 6 | app |
| V6-APP-013.3 | existing-coverage-validated | Correction-to-Rule Capture Loop | User corrections are often lost after a session, so coding behavior does not compound reliably. | User corrections are converted into policy-scoped rule objects through existing memory/learning primitives with confidence, provenance, and retention metadata, and subsequent runs show rule reuse with deterministic capture/apply receipts. | 6 | app |
| V6-APP-013.4 | existing-coverage-validated | Verification + Autonomous Bug-Fix Execution Profile | Code changes can still be committed without a full trace-debug-fix verification loop, increasing regression risk. | The app binds existing research/verify/debug/fix primitives into a required pre-commit profile that executes tests/checks, performs bounded repair attempts, and blocks blind commits unless policy exceptions are explicitly granted and receipted. | 6 | app |
| V6-APP-013.5 | existing-coverage-validated | Conduit-Only Workflow Enforcement + `/apps` Placement Contract | Workflow loaders can drift into client-owned authority paths if placement and guardrails are not explicit. | Ship implementation under `/apps/claude_workflow/`, keep client wrappers thin (`run/status/explain`), and enforce conduit-only routing for parsing, planning, delegation, verification, and fix actions with deterministic receipts and fail-closed policy denials. | 10 | 0/app |

## Claude Code System Prompt Architecture Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1kEEEjVq_1oAVsJt87IveSu-Y7B97JlBowl_P9E3iQ04`, 2026-03-11)

Source references:
- https://docs.google.com/document/d/1kEEEjVq_1oAVsJt87IveSu-Y7B97JlBowl_P9E3iQ04/edit?usp=sharing
- https://github.com/Piebald-AI/claude-code-system-prompts

Notes:
- Primitive-first normalization: this intake extends prompt-governance, verification, and security-monitoring primitives; it does not create a separate agent runtime.
- Overlap handled explicitly:
  - CLAUDE.md workflow loader/app profile: `V6-APP-013.*`
  - Planning + execute + offload harness contracts: `V6-COCKPIT-022.*`
  - Drift/prompt hardening management lane: `V6-COCKPIT-019.3`
  - Existing skill composition and reasoning profile registry: `V6-SKILLS-001.*`
- Net-new focus from this source:
  - high-cardinality modular micro-prompt registry and conditional composition policy
  - first-class verification-specialist + security-monitor persona contracts as governed sub-agents
  - strict tool-usage hierarchy contract (prefer dedicated edit/read/write tools, constrained shell usage)
  - engineering-discipline prompt guardrails (`minimize file creation`, `no premature abstractions`) as enforceable policy objects
- Default placement:
  - Layer `0`: final deny/allow enforcement on risky tool actions + deterministic receipts
  - Layer `1`: micro-prompt registry, signature/versioning, rule-object policy storage
  - Layer `2`: dynamic prompt composer, verifier/security monitor orchestration, tool-policy router
  - Layer `3`: persona overlays (verification specialist/security monitor styles) only
  - `client`: thin inspect/status/explain surfaces only
  - `app`: optional `/apps/prompt_studio/` authoring UX only
  - `adapter`: model-provider prompt compilation/render adapters only

Objective: absorb modular Claude-style system-prompt patterns into governed core primitives so prompt behavior is composable, auditable, and enforceable without expanding client authority.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-COCKPIT-023.1 | existing-coverage-validated | Modular Micro-Prompt Registry + Conditional Composition Contract | Monolithic persona prompts are hard to evolve safely and can cause drift when small rule changes require full prompt rewrites. | Add versioned micro-prompt registry with signature/provenance metadata and dynamic composition rules (task/env/sub-agent class), producing deterministic composition receipts and reproducible prompt manifests per run. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-023.2 | existing-coverage-validated | Verification-Specialist Sub-Agent Contract | Code/task execution quality varies without a mandatory adversarial verification pass that can independently assert PASS/FAIL/PARTIAL outcomes. | Add governed verification-specialist profile that runs post-execution checks (build/lint/tests/edge probes), emits verdict objects with evidence references, and can gate promotion/commit workflows through policy-defined thresholds. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-023.3 | existing-coverage-validated | Persistent Security-Monitor Sub-Agent Contract | Tool calls and edits can bypass intent-safety expectations unless a dedicated monitor continuously evaluates action intent/risk against block/allow policy. | Add always-on security-monitor profile that inspects proposed actions in parallel, issues allow/deny recommendations with deterministic reason codes, and routes final high-risk decisions through Layer-0 deny/approve enforcement receipts. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-023.4 | existing-coverage-validated | Project Context File Synthesizer Contract (`CLAUDE.md`/`AGENTS.md` Delta Sync) | Project conventions and decisions decay when context files are manually maintained and diverge from actual execution history. | Add governed context-file synthesis/update contract that proposes or applies bounded diffs for project context docs from receipt-backed history, with policy-scoped write permissions and deterministic change receipts linked to source evidence. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-023.5 | existing-coverage-validated | Tool-Usage Hierarchy + Anti-Abstraction Guardrail Contract | Agents overuse shell/quick hacks and create unnecessary files/abstractions when tool-selection and engineering-discipline rules are not enforceable runtime objects. | Add policy objects enforcing preferred tool order (edit/read/write before shell where applicable), bounded shell use tiers, and guardrails for unnecessary file creation/abstraction churn; violations fail closed or require explicit override receipts. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-023.6 | existing-coverage-validated | Conduit-Only Prompt Governance Boundary + Thin Client Enforcement | Prompt assembly and specialist-agent routing can drift into client-owned code paths, weakening auditability and policy enforcement. | Enforce conduit-only routing for prompt compose/select/update and specialist-agent invocation/gating actions, with bypass-rejection tests and client/app surfaces limited to non-authoritative UX wrappers. | 10 | 0/1/2/client |

## OpenCode Coding-Agent UX Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1e1cAKPgdtYJNntQX2abA6_fNGvoUNQ4V1fKMrXUWHIs`, 2026-03-11)

Source references:
- https://docs.google.com/document/d/1e1cAKPgdtYJNntQX2abA6_fNGvoUNQ4V1fKMrXUWHIs/edit?usp=sharing
- https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode

Notes:
- Primitive-first normalization: this intake extends existing coding-agent orchestration, shell safety, routing, and gateway primitives; it does not introduce a parallel runtime stack.
- Overlap handled explicitly:
  - Planner/executor split and handoff receipts: `V6-COCKPIT-009.1`
  - `write_todos` + sandboxed `execute` contracts: `V6-COCKPIT-022.1`, `V6-COCKPIT-022.2`
  - Provider/model routing and fallback scoreboards: `V6-LLMN-001..004`, `V3-RACE-004`
  - Gateway + remote/mobile attach contracts: `V6-COCKPIT-012.2`, `V6-PERSIST-001.2`
- Net-new focus from this source:
  - explicit operator-facing `build` vs `plan` mode UX contract with instant mode-switch semantics
  - terminal-native coding cockpit contract with embedded LSP intelligence and action traceability
  - unified multi-client coding-surface parity contract (TUI/desktop/mobile/IDE) over one gateway authority path
  - curated OpenCode-compatible profile pack as governed templates, not authority code forks
- Default placement:
  - Layer `0`: final permission/deny enforcement for mode-restricted operations + deterministic receipts
  - Layer `1`: mode policy objects (`plan` read-only, command risk tiers), LSP/privacy policy controls
  - Layer `2`: mode switch coordinator, coding-session orchestrator, gateway attach broker
  - Layer `3`: coding persona presets (`build`, `plan`) only
  - `client`: TUI rendering, keybinding UX, and status surfaces only (no authority logic)
  - `app`: optional `/apps/code_cockpit/` UX profile only
  - `adapter`: LSP servers, IDE connectors, and transport adapters

Objective: absorb OpenCode-style coding-agent UX strengths (safe mode switching, terminal-native flow, and multi-client control) while preserving core authority and conduit-only enforcement.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-COCKPIT-024.1 | existing-coverage-validated | Explicit Build/Plan Persona Pair + Instant Mode-Switch Contract | Coding sessions need clear, operator-visible safety modes; without a first-class switch contract, read-only planning can drift into unintended mutation paths. | Add governed `build`/`plan` persona pair with instant switch action (`protheus mode build | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-024.2 | existing-coverage-validated | Terminal-Native Coding Cockpit + Embedded LSP Contract | Coding-agent usability degrades when terminal workflows and code intelligence (hover/diagnostics/completions) are split across disconnected tools. | Add `protheus tui` coding cockpit contract with embedded LSP-backed code-intelligence events routed through gateway authority and deterministically traced; client remains render-only and cannot bypass policy gates for file/tool actions. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-024.3 | existing-coverage-validated | Unified Multi-Client Coding Surface Parity Contract | Remote coding control becomes brittle when TUI/desktop/mobile/IDE clients implement divergent command semantics over separate execution paths. | Define one gateway command/session contract for coding surfaces so TUI, desktop, mobile, and IDE connectors share identical attach/auth/action semantics with deterministic parity receipts and fail-closed auth scope checks. | 10 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-024.4 | existing-coverage-validated | Per-Agent Routing Override Contract over Existing Zen/Router Primitives | Teams need per-agent model routing overrides in coding workflows without creating a second provider-routing subsystem. | Add per-agent routing profile overlays (cost/latency/quality constraints, provider allowlists) that compile into existing router primitives and emit deterministic route-decision receipts with fallback evidence. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-024.5 | existing-coverage-validated | OpenCode-Compatible Profile Template Governance Lane | Teams migrating from OpenCode need familiar build/plan/TUI presets, but unmanaged imports can bypass policy and drift from core contracts. | Maintain signed template pack installable via governed URI (for example `opencode://build-plan`) that maps to existing core primitives, with quarterly human review, compatibility metadata, and deterministic install/activate/deprecate receipts. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-024.6 | existing-coverage-validated | Conduit-Only Coding Cockpit Boundary + Thin Client Enforcement | Rich coding UX can accidentally centralize authority in client-side logic if boundary rules are not explicit and tested. | Enforce conduit-only routing for mode switching, tool/file actions, LSP-triggered operations, and remote client commands; add bypass-rejection tests proving client/app surfaces remain non-authoritative wrappers. | 10 | 0/1/2/client |

## AlphaClaw-Style GUI Management Intake (Source: `alphaclaw`, 2026-03-09)

Objective: add an elegant self-hosted management surface for daemon operations (setup, drift controls, backups, file operations, connectors, self-healing) while preserving conduit-only authority and deterministic receipts.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-COCKPIT-019.1 | existing-coverage-validated | Elegant GUI Management Layer | Setup and day-2 operations remain CLI-heavy, increasing operator friction for routine maintenance and onboarding. | `protheus gui` exposes a self-hosted management UI (deploy/bootstrap/status/settings) including env/model-key controls and management receipts for all actions. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-019.2 | existing-coverage-validated | Auto-Backup + GitHub Sync Lane | Workspace/config/state recovery depends on manual backup discipline, risking drift and lost operational context. | Add policy-gated auto-backup lane with one-click import/sync and deterministic commit/push receipts for backup actions. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-019.3 | existing-coverage-validated | Prompt Hardening + Drift Doctor Engine | Prompt/config drift can silently degrade quality and policy compliance without continuous diagnostic tooling. | `protheus doctor drift` scans prompts/workspace state, emits hardening recommendations, and writes deterministic drift-analysis receipts. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-019.4 | existing-coverage-validated | Telegram Multi-Topic Setup Wizard | Multi-topic Telegram orchestration is operationally complex and error-prone when configured manually. | `protheus connector add telegram` runs guided multi-topic setup with concurrency-safe defaults and receipt-linked provisioning state. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-019.5 | existing-coverage-validated | In-Browser File Browser/Editor + Git View | File-level operations still require shell workflows, limiting accessibility for non-terminal operators. | GUI supports browse/edit/commit workflows with explicit git state visibility and deterministic file-operation receipts. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-019.6 | existing-coverage-validated | Watchdog Self-Healing + Webhook Replay/Debug + Token Analytics | Runtime failures and webhook/debug telemetry handling are fragmented, making incident response slower and less predictable. | Add always-on watchdog restart policies, webhook replay/debug tooling, token analytics view, and one-click update controls with deterministic operation receipts. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-019.7 | existing-coverage-validated | Conduit-Only Management Enforcement + Receipts | GUI/ops actions can bypass canonical policy and audit paths when executed via direct management surfaces. | Route all GUI management, backup, drift, webhook, key-management, and watchdog actions through conduit with capability/policy checks and deterministic receipts; bypass paths fail closed by contract checks. | 10 | 0/1/2/client |

## Creator Outreach Automation App Intake (Source: OpenClaw creator-outreach demo pattern, 2026-03-09)

Objective: add a default top-level app for autonomous creator discovery and outreach that sits in `/apps` (not `client/`) and runs on top of client/runtime surfaces with conduit-only execution.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-APP-001.1 | existing-coverage-validated | Creator Discovery Engine (YouTube/Twitch) | Creator lead generation is manual and inconsistent, limiting campaign scale and response speed. | `protheus app run creator-outreach` discovers and scores creators by niche/engagement/fit (100+ target per run) with deterministic discovery receipts. | 6 | app |
| V6-APP-001.2 | existing-coverage-validated | Personalized Outreach + Follow-Up Sequences | Outreach messaging lacks individualized personalization and repeatable follow-up automation. | Generate unique per-creator messages and follow-up sequences via approved connectors with receipt-linked prompt/template metadata and send outcomes. | 6 | app |
| V6-APP-001.3 | existing-coverage-validated | Response Handling + Call Booking Workflow | Reply handling and meeting booking are fragmented and can miss qualified responses in high-volume campaigns. | Run 24/7 response triage + booking flow with human-review gate options, deterministic response receipts, and booking event linkage. | 6 | app |
| V6-APP-001.4 | existing-coverage-validated | 24/7 Autonomous Campaign Operation Profile | Campaign execution stalls without continuous schedule/heartbeat operation when users are offline. | Support persistent scheduled/background operation with campaign state checkpoints, health markers, and resumable execution receipts. | 6 | app |
| V6-APP-001.5 | existing-coverage-validated | Conduit-Only Outreach Enforcement + Default `/apps` Placement | Outreach apps can bypass policy controls or drift into client-owned authority paths without explicit placement and boundary contracts. | Ship scaffold at `/apps/creator_outreach/`, keep client wrappers thin, and enforce conduit-only routing for discovery/sending/response/booking with deterministic receipts and policy checks. | 10 | 0/app |

## Protheus 2.0 "Instrumented Aliveness" Intake (Source: `local/workspace/proposals/protheus_optimization_v2.md`, AGE-10, 2026-03-10)

Objective: restore subjective aliveness and operational velocity without weakening safety-plane governance. Reward loops, proposal approval policy, task-class routing, health instrumentation, and operator reporting remain core-owned and conduit-governed; client stays limited to thin reporting/control surfaces.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-ALIVE-001.1 | existing-coverage-validated | Micro-Dopamine Event Engine + Objective Auto-Verification | Motivation/reward feedback flatlines when only manually verified achievements count, leaving obvious maintenance/progress work unrewarded. | Add deterministic micro-event registry (issue creation, health checks, anomaly logging, docs, community maintenance, etc.) with capped point schedules and auto-verification for objective work classes; all accrual writes receipt-linked dopamine ledger entries and exposes weekly roll-up state without requiring human confirmation for routine objective work. | 6 | 1/2/client |
| V6-ALIVE-001.2 | existing-coverage-validated | Confidence-Gated Autophagy Auto-Approval + Rollback Window | Self-optimization proposals stall for days because every proposal waits on manual approval even when confidence and rollback posture are strong. | Add auto-approval policy for bounded proposal classes with configurable thresholds (`min_confidence`, historical success floor, max impact score, exclusions), supervised delayed-commit flow, and automatic rollback/regret-issue creation on degradation; high-confidence proposals execute within a bounded rollback window and emit approval/rollback receipts. | 6 | 1/2/client |
| V6-ALIVE-001.3 | existing-coverage-validated | Progressive Right-Hemisphere Task Classifier + Synthesis Windows | Dual-brain remains stuck in left-only mode because no task classifier safely distinguishes creative/synthetic work from execution-critical work. | Add task-class policy for right-brain eligibility (`synthesis`, `creative_writing`, `pattern_recognition`, `strategy`, `design`, `meta_analysis`) with safe zones, rollback triggers, and bounded scheduled synthesis sessions; right-brain usage becomes measurable and fail-closed for execution/health/alert tasks. | 10 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-ALIVE-001.4 | existing-coverage-validated | Async Health Monitoring Envelope | Blocking health checks and immediate failure escalation increase cognitive overhead and make the system feel heavier than necessary. | Move health polling into background async envelopes with non-blocking execution, configurable escalation after consecutive failures, and aggregated hourly health receipts; normal operation is not blocked on routine health checks unless explicit critical policy says so. | 8 | 1/2/client |
| V6-ALIVE-001.5 | existing-coverage-validated | Tiered Verbosity + Deep-Dive Instrumentation Mode | Every action currently risks producing full-strength monitoring artifacts, creating noise and operator fatigue. | Add severity-aware instrumentation policy (`critical/full`, `normal/lightweight`, `routine/sampled`, `noise/suppressed`) plus time-bounded deep-dive mode that temporarily restores full capture for debugging/anomaly investigation; routine instrumentation noise drops while preserving critical auditability. | 6 | 1/2/client |
| V6-ALIVE-001.6 | existing-coverage-validated | Unified Daily Standup + Critical Update Bridge | The operator experience is fragmented across health checks, proposals, blockers, and activity surfaces with no single daily briefing. | Auto-generate a daily standup summary (`yesterday`, `today`, `blockers`, `proposed actions`) from receipts/state and publish it to the governed operator surfaces (Linear/Slack/internal dashboard as policy allows); critical escalations can mirror into the same channel family with deterministic delivery receipts. | 6 | 1/2/client |
| V6-ALIVE-001.7 | existing-coverage-validated | Weekly Cohort Dopamine Scoring + Trend View | Daily scoring creates pressure and poor trend visibility, so dopamine feedback is unstable and hard to interpret. | Add weekly cohort scoring model with targets/bonuses, trend summaries, and roll-up reporting tied to the dopamine ledger so operators can review directional progress instead of noisy daily fluctuations. | 6 | 1/2/client |

## Long-Form Creative Writing App Intake (Doc `1eeV_xJF9UB0qXoROXjHU_tof0kgMqb8mYS8CkYg_6Uo`, 2026-03-11)

Source references:
- https://docs.google.com/document/d/1eeV_xJF9UB0qXoROXjHU_tof0kgMqb8mYS8CkYg_6Uo/edit?usp=sharing
- https://github.com/steven-tey/novel

Notes:
- Primitive-first normalization: this is an `/apps` workflow built on existing memory, persona, routing, streaming, and receipt primitives. It is not a new core primitive family.
- Default placement:
  - Layer `0`: conduit-routed generation/edit operations and deterministic receipts
  - Layer `1`: existing Epistemic Object, memory, and policy contracts for character/world state
  - Layer `2`: existing model routing and orchestration contracts for plot/prose task splits
  - Layer `3`: creative persona overlays and tone-control profiles only
  - `client`: thin launch/status/editor wrappers only
  - `app`: primary implementation at `/apps/novel/`

Objective: ship a long-form storytelling app that can generate, revise, and navigate multi-chapter narratives with continuity guarantees while keeping all authority in core and all app logic in `/apps`.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-APP-002.1 | existing-coverage-validated | Chapter-by-Chapter Generation Profile | Long-form writing sessions drift in tone and plot without a governed chapter generation loop tied to persistent state. | `protheus app run novel "<prompt>"` generates structured chapter outputs that bind to existing persona + memory primitives and emit deterministic generation receipts per chapter. | 6 | app |
| V6-APP-002.2 | existing-coverage-validated | Character/World Consistency Contract over Epistemic Objects | Character sheets, lore, and relationship state become inconsistent when not enforced through one persistent object contract. | Chapter generation reads/writes character/world/plot objects through existing memory primitives; consistency checks run per chapter and emit deterministic validation receipts with any detected drift markers. | 6 | app |
| V6-APP-002.3 | existing-coverage-validated | Streaming Editor + Chapter Navigation Reference App (`/apps/novel`) | Creative workflows are slow without live streaming output and chapter-level edit/navigation controls in one focused app surface. | Ship `/apps/novel/` with streaming markdown editor, chapter navigation, and revision actions as thin app UX over existing streaming/edit primitives; no duplicate orchestration authority in client code. | 6 | app |
| V6-APP-002.4 | existing-coverage-validated | Multi-Model Plot/Prose Routing Profile | Plot planning and prose drafting need different model characteristics, but current usage is manually selected and inconsistent. | Use existing Layer-2 routing primitives to split planning/prose tasks by policy-scoped profiles with deterministic routing decision receipts and budget telemetry. | 6 | app |
| V6-APP-002.5 | existing-coverage-validated | Conduit-Only Creative Workflow Enforcement + `/apps` Placement Contract | Story generation/edit flows can bypass policy and boundary rules when embedded directly into client/runtime authority lanes. | Keep implementation in `/apps/novel/`, keep client wrappers thin (`run/status/edit/view`), and route generate/revise/export actions through conduit with fail-closed policy checks and deterministic receipts. | 10 | 0/app |

## Local LLM Comparison & Multi-Model Streaming Intake (Doc `1aJJ7LhHU1zJWlngBR76qEdQ02Ey1zrPtxBu6TZ_Pioc`, 2026-03-11)

Source references:
- https://docs.google.com/document/d/1aJJ7LhHU1zJWlngBR76qEdQ02Ey1zrPtxBu6TZ_Pioc/edit?usp=sharing
- https://github.com/Troyanovsky/Local-LLM-Comparison-Colab-UI

Notes:
- Primitive-first normalization: this is a core cognition/runtime extension over existing inference-routing + streaming + observability primitives, with optional thin app/client UX.
- The source draft suggested a distinct comparison folder. Normalize instead to existing model router/stream telemetry lanes plus one compare-session contract to avoid a parallel inference subsystem.
- Default placement:
  - Layer `-1`: existing local GPU/runtime substrates only
  - Layer `0`: conduit-routed per-model generation + deterministic token/session receipts
  - Layer `1`: compare-session policy (budget, model-count cap, metric retention, privacy labels)
  - Layer `2`: multi-model fanout orchestration + synchronized stream aggregation + metrics pipeline
  - Layer `3`: optional research/testing persona defaults only
  - `client`: thin `compare run/status/view` CLI + observability surfaces only
  - `app`: optional `/apps/model_compare/` reference UI profile only

Objective: add a governed side-by-side local-model comparison capability for synchronized streaming outputs and live performance telemetry while keeping inference authority in core and client/app surfaces thin.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-COGNITION-004.1 | existing-coverage-validated | Side-by-Side Multi-Model Streaming Compare Session Contract | Model selection quality is weak without synchronized, same-prompt comparisons across multiple local models under one deterministic session contract. | `protheus compare models <a> <b> [c] [d]` runs a bounded compare session (2-4 models policy-scoped) with synchronized streaming output lanes and deterministic per-model/per-step receipts linked to one session id. | 8 | 1/2/3/client/app |
| V6-COGNITION-004.2 | existing-coverage-validated | Live Per-Model Performance Metrics Pipeline | Developers cannot reliably evaluate throughput/latency tradeoffs when metrics are sampled inconsistently or detached from generation sessions. | Compare sessions surface real-time per-model metrics (tokens/sec, first-token latency, end-to-end latency, memory footprint) with deterministic metric receipts and session-linked observability output. | 8 | 1/2/3/client/app |
| V6-COGNITION-004.3 | existing-coverage-validated | Governed Local Model Discovery + Hot-Swap Contract | Local-model workflows are brittle when model inventory, quantization fit, and hot-swap behavior are not standardized under one contract. | Provide governed local-model discovery and policy-bounded swap/load flows (including quantization/memory-fit checks) so compare sessions can switch models without losing audit lineage; every load/swap decision emits deterministic receipts. | 8 | 1/2/3/client/app |
| V6-COGNITION-004.4 | existing-coverage-validated | Conduit-Only Compare Execution + Receipts | Parallel local inference calls can bypass policy/budget controls when driven directly from UI/runtime adapters. | Route all compare-session actions (fanout, stream, metrics, load/swap, stop/export) through conduit with fail-closed policy checks, deterministic receipts, and contract tests that reject bypass paths. | 10 | 0/1/2/3/client/app |
| V6-COGNITION-004.5 | existing-coverage-validated | Persona/Shadow Compare Profile + Thin UX Surface | Research/testing personas need reusable comparison mode without embedding inference authority into client interfaces. | Add optional research/testing persona profile plus thin CLI/observability surface (`protheus compare`) over core compare-session contracts; no duplicate orchestration logic in client code. | 8 | 1/2/3/client/app |

## LLM Output Evaluation Engine Intake (Doc `1WI1rnoUw5SObFRbDiJnZZsA2g7ViWGLqML0OBfbfwag`, 2026-03-11)

- https://github.com/TonicAI/tonic_validate

Notes:
- Primitive-first normalization: this is a core observability/reliability extension over existing run traces, receipts, Epistemic Objects, and report surfaces; not a separate app-owned evaluator silo.
- Default placement:
  - Layer `0`: conduit-routed evaluation runs + deterministic score/report receipts
  - Layer `1`: evaluation policy templates, score schema, retention/privacy labels, metric registration rules
  - Layer `2`: evaluation orchestration (hallucination/faithfulness/relevance), RAG-specific checks, and reporting pipeline
  - Layer `3`: optional persona-level quality gates only
  - `client`: thin CLI/observability surfaces (`eval run/report/status`) only
  - `app`: optional `/apps/eval_lab/` UX profile only

Objective: add automated quality evaluation for model/agent outputs (hallucination, faithfulness, relevance, retrieval quality, custom metrics) with deterministic receipts and trend reporting.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-OBSERVABILITY-003.1 | existing-coverage-validated | Automated Hallucination/Faithfulness/Answer-Relevance Scoring | Agent quality issues are discovered late because core runs lack consistent post-response evaluation signals. | Add bounded automated scoring lanes for hallucination risk, faithfulness to supplied context, and answer relevance on governed run classes; each evaluated response emits deterministic score receipts linked to run/session ids. | 7 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-OBSERVABILITY-003.2 | existing-coverage-validated | RAG Retrieval/Context/Citation Relevance Evaluators | Retrieval workflows degrade silently when retrieval quality and citation grounding are not continuously measured. | Add RAG-oriented evaluators (retrieval quality, context relevance, citation grounding/accuracy) that store results as Epistemic Objects with provenance/confidence labels and deterministic evaluation receipts. | 7 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-OBSERVABILITY-003.3 | existing-coverage-validated | Custom Metric Registration Contract (Template + Policy) | Teams need domain-specific quality metrics, but ad hoc evaluators create inconsistent execution and safety posture. | Provide governed custom metric registration (schema/template-driven) with policy-scoped execution limits and deterministic registration/invocation receipts; registered metrics can be attached to selected workflows without bypassing conduit checks. | 7 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-OBSERVABILITY-003.4 | existing-coverage-validated | Structured Reporting + Trend Dashboard Contract | Raw point-in-time scores are hard to operationalize without longitudinal views, pass/fail summaries, and failure exemplars. | Generate structured evaluation reports (pass/fail, trend slices, representative failures, metric drift) available via CLI/dashboard surfaces, all linked to deterministic report-generation receipts. | 7 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-OBSERVABILITY-003.5 | existing-coverage-validated | Conduit-Only Evaluation Enforcement + Thin Client Boundary | Evaluation systems can become unaudited side channels if scoring/reporting paths run outside canonical policy and receipt flow. | Enforce conduit-only routing for evaluation, metric registration, and report generation with fail-closed policy checks; keep client as thin display/control surfaces with no evaluation authority logic embedded. | 10 | 0/1/2/client |

## Opik Native Observability Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1kl5o9PRvp98-6Z6oYnp1feviefzJSGrELDf0sq8fIR4`, 2026-03-11)

Source references:
- https://docs.google.com/document/d/1kl5o9PRvp98-6Z6oYnp1feviefzJSGrELDf0sq8fIR4/edit?usp=sharing
- https://github.com/comet-ml/opik-openclaw
- https://x.com/techNmak/status/2030992194119864499

Notes:
- Primitive-first normalization: this intake extends existing observability/evaluation/extension primitives; it does not introduce a separate tracing platform.
- Overlap handled explicitly:
  - Baseline monitoring/alert/workflow lanes: `V6-OBSERVABILITY-001.*`
  - Evaluation/report scoring lanes: `V6-OBSERVABILITY-003.*`
  - Tool-event chat schema surfaces: `V6-COCKPIT-021.1`
  - Extension/plugin governance and sandbox lanes: `V6-EXT-704..709`
- Net-new focus from this source:
  - deterministic pre-LLM context assembly tracing contract (prompt/history/tools/skills/memory breakdown)
  - gateway-native observability plugin contract (in-process capture with isolation and capability policy)
  - unified per-request lineage linking tool I/O, errors, and sub-agent delegation trees
  - per-request model/provider/cost/session-thread ledger views with export and trend continuity
- Default placement:
  - Layer `0`: conduit-routed trace events + immutable receipt chain + final deny/allow enforcement
  - Layer `1`: trace-retention/privacy policy, plugin capability policy, cost-ledger schema contracts
  - Layer `2`: trace collector, context-assembly tracer, tool/delegation correlator, plugin runtime host
  - Layer `3`: optional operator persona display preferences only
  - `client`: thin `trace`/dashboard rendering and export controls only
  - `app`: optional `/apps/observability_studio/` UX profile only
  - `adapter`: provider-specific telemetry sinks/storage adapters

Objective: add native gateway-level deep tracing for context assembly, tool/delegation lineage, and request-level cost/model accounting while preserving conduit-governed authority and thin client boundaries.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-OBSERVABILITY-004.1 | existing-coverage-validated | Pre-LLM Context Assembly Trace Contract | Operators cannot reliably audit model behavior when the exact assembled context payload is opaque before invocation. | Add deterministic pre-call context trace artifact (`system`, `history`, `tool schemas`, `skills`, `memory`) attached to each LLM call receipt; support `protheus trace context` for on-demand replay without exposing disallowed sensitive fields. | 7 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-OBSERVABILITY-004.2 | existing-coverage-validated | Native Gateway Observability Plugin Contract | External proxy tracing misses runtime-internal events and creates governance bypass risk for sensitive traces. | Add in-gateway plugin host contract for observability plugins with signed manifests, capability-scoped access, isolation policy, and deterministic plugin lifecycle receipts (`install`, `activate`, `suspend`, `remove`). | 7 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-OBSERVABILITY-004.3 | existing-coverage-validated | Tool/Delegation End-to-End Lineage Correlation Contract | Tool inputs/outputs/errors and sub-agent handoffs are hard to debug when events are not causally stitched to parent requests. | Correlate every tool call and delegation hop into one lineage graph linked to parent request/session ids, with deterministic timing/error/state receipts and drill-down queries in observability surfaces. | 7 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-OBSERVABILITY-004.4 | existing-coverage-validated | Per-Request Model + Cost + Thread Ledger Contract | Cost visibility is fragmented when usage is aggregated only at coarse time windows without request-level model/provider attribution. | Emit request-level cost ledger entries (model, provider, tokens, unit costs, totals, thread/session linkage) with exportable historical trend views and deterministic accounting receipts. | 7 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-OBSERVABILITY-004.5 | existing-coverage-validated | Curated Native Observability Plugin Template Governance Lane | Plugin quality and safety drift without signed curated templates and periodic review of tracing patterns/privacy controls. | Maintain signed plugin template pack installable via governed URI (for example `observability://opik-native`) with quarterly human review, compatibility metadata, and deterministic install/update/deprecate receipts. | 7 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-OBSERVABILITY-004.6 | existing-coverage-validated | Conduit-Only Deep-Trace Boundary + Thin Client Enforcement | Deep-tracing features can leak authority into client code paths unless plugin/tracing control is centrally enforced. | Enforce conduit-only routing for trace capture, plugin control, lineage queries, and ledger exports with fail-closed policy checks and bypass-rejection tests; client/app remain non-authoritative render/control surfaces. | 10 | 0/1/2/client |

## Local Policy Enforcement & Hash-Chained Audit Trail Source Coverage Addendum (Doc `1jXTbAvxCT7NgxuGi4620HJT0zrfqW8mfYJE4jjDzP5s`, 2026-03-11)

Source references:
- https://docs.google.com/document/d/1jXTbAvxCT7NgxuGi4620HJT0zrfqW8mfYJE4jjDzP5s/edit?usp=sharing
- https://x.com/luccasveg/status/2031410745959756246

Notes:
- Primitive-first normalization: this intake extends existing observability/policy/receipt primitives; it does not create a separate client-authority guardrail subsystem.
- Overlap handled explicitly:
  - Existing deep-trace observability lane: `V6-OBSERVABILITY-004.1..004.6`
  - Existing safety/policy execution boundaries: `V6-PERSIST-001.6`, `V6-SEC-010..017`, `V6-CONDUIT-002`, `V6-CONDUIT-006`
  - Existing context/lineage/event tracing lanes: `V6-OBSERVABILITY-001.*`, `V6-OBSERVABILITY-003.*`, `V6-CONTEXT-001.*`
- Net-new focus from this source:
  - user-authored local "commandment" rules as governed policy objects with bounded block/flag actions
  - explicit violation-event linkage into the existing hash-chained receipt lineage
  - operator-first live monitoring views focused on enforcement events and policy outcomes
- Default placement:
  - Layer `0`: final conduit-routed allow/deny enforcement + immutable receipt hash chain
  - Layer `1`: commandment-rule schema, priority/conflict policy, retention/privacy policy for violation artifacts
  - Layer `2`: policy evaluator and violation monitor/notification orchestrator
  - Layer `3`: optional policy-aware persona defaults only
  - `client`: thin status/watch/audit UX only
  - `app`: optional `/apps/crabwise/` policy cockpit profile

Objective: extend current observability and policy primitives with local commandment enforcement and violation-linked hash-chained audit review, while keeping authority in core and client/app layers thin.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-OBSERVABILITY-004.7 | existing-coverage-validated | Local Commandment Rule Objects + Priority/Conflict Contract | Users need plain-language local rules ("never do X without confirmation"), but ad hoc prompt-only rules are brittle and non-auditable across long-running sessions. | Add governed commandment-rule objects (scope, action class, confirm/block mode, priority, conflict policy) evaluated in conduit policy flow; all rule evaluations emit deterministic allow/deny/override receipts. | 8 | 0/1/2/client/app |
| V6-OBSERVABILITY-004.8 | existing-coverage-validated | Violation-Linked Hash-Chain Audit Continuity Contract | Existing receipt chains lose policy intent visibility when violation events are not first-class linked artifacts. | Extend receipt lineage so commandment checks and violation decisions are appended as hash-linked events with actor/tool/action context and immutable replay continuity in `protheus audit`/`protheus-top`. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-OBSERVABILITY-004.9 | existing-coverage-validated | Enforcement-Focused Live Monitor + Notification Contract | Operators cannot react quickly when policy blocks/flags are buried inside generic telemetry streams. | Add live monitor views for rule hits, block/allow rates, top violated commandments, and escalation notifications (terminal/connector routes) with deterministic event receipts and filter/query controls. | 7 | 1/2/client/app |
| V6-OBSERVABILITY-004.10 | existing-coverage-validated | Conduit-Only Commandment Enforcement + Thin Client/App Boundary Contract | Commandment UX extensions can accidentally move authority into client/app code unless execution boundaries are explicit and test-enforced. | Enforce that all rule parsing, evaluation, block/allow decisions, and audit writes run through conduit-governed core lanes; client/app remain render/control surfaces only, with bypass-rejection tests required. | 10 | 0/1/2/client/app |

## Local MoE Chat & Quantized Inference Intake (Doc `1fZdbo3uVCccuLDdWdWYCdbkasubqMSC2eJI0sR8RUXk`, 2026-03-11)

  - Browser-local inference substrate: `V6-SUBSTRATE-003.*`
  - Multi-model side-by-side compare sessions: `V6-COGNITION-004.*`
  - This intake focuses on production local MoE chat runtime profiles (quantized MoE load/run/stream/swap) over those shared primitives.

  - Layer `0`: conduit-routed token generation and session actions with deterministic receipts
  - Layer `1`: local model policy/budget/privacy templates + quantized-load guardrails

  - `app`: optional `/apps/local_chat/` reference UX only

Objective: provide a fast, private local MoE chat path (Mixtral-class quantized models) with governed loading, streaming, and model switching, while keeping all authority and truth in core lanes.

| V6-COGNITION-003.2 | existing-coverage-validated | Local Streaming Chat Session Contract | Local inference UX is brittle without one canonical session contract for streaming tokens, message history, and tool output display. | `protheus chat --local --model <profile>` launches a governed streaming session with persisted session state, deterministic token/message receipts, and policy-bounded context management. | 8 | 1/2/3/client/app |
| V6-COGNITION-003.3 | existing-coverage-validated | One-Command Model Provisioning + Swap Contract | Local model onboarding is too manual when download, quantization selection, and cache registration are not unified. | Add one-command provisioning that resolves model profile, verifies artifacts, applies policy-bounded quantization/memory-fit selection, registers local cache metadata, and emits deterministic provision/swap receipts. | 8 | 1/2/3/client/app |
| V6-COGNITION-003.4 | existing-coverage-validated | Conduit-Only Local Inference Enforcement | Local model execution can bypass policy/audit paths when runtimes are invoked directly from client/UI code. | Route all local model provision/load/run/swap/stop actions through conduit with fail-closed policy checks and deterministic receipts; contract tests reject bypass paths. | 10 | 0/1/2/3/client/app |

## Modular LLM Fine-Tuning Pipeline Intake (Doc `1KOqHIyIef45T0cIMNcDj49kfQQEiKgTYbfwzYfWoibY`, 2026-03-11)

Source references:
- https://docs.google.com/document/d/1KOqHIyIef45T0cIMNcDj49kfQQEiKgTYbfwzYfWoibY/edit?usp=sharing
- https://github.com/hiyouga/LlamaFactory

Notes:
- Primitive-first normalization: this is a core training/self-improvement extension and must not become a client-owned fine-tune subsystem.
- Overlap handled explicitly:
  - Autonomous improvement loop and promotion gates: `V6-COCKPIT-017.*`
  - Existing lower-level training/adapter lanes already present under V3 (`V3-ASSIM-018`, `V3-ASSIM-019`, `V3-RACE-011`) are treated as historical substrate and implementation anchors.
  - This intake defines the V6 unified multi-method fine-tune contract on top of those primitives.
- Default placement:
  - Layer `-1`: GPU/accelerator runtime profiles and distributed training adapters
  - Layer `0`: conduit-routed train/eval/merge/export actions + deterministic receipts
  - Layer `1`: fine-tune policy templates (budget/privacy/data/governance), checkpoint retention, promotion constraints
  - Layer `2`: unified fine-tune orchestrator (LoRA/QLoRA/DoRA/full), experiment tracker, merge/export pipeline
  - Layer `3`: optional self-improvement persona defaults only
  - `client`: thin `improve/finetune/eval/export` control + observability surfaces only
  - `app`: optional `/apps/model_lab/` UX profile only

Objective: add a unified, governed fine-tuning pipeline for local/self-hosted model improvement across common adapter/full-tune methods, with built-in evaluation, experiment tracking, and deployable model export.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-COGNITION-002.1 | existing-coverage-validated | Unified Multi-Method Fine-Tuning Orchestrator Contract | Fine-tuning remains fragmented across scripts/backends, making reproducibility and policy enforcement inconsistent across model families. | Add one orchestrator contract supporting LoRA/QLoRA/DoRA/full fine-tune profiles with single/multi-GPU execution, deterministic epoch/checkpoint receipts, and policy-bounded resource scheduling. | 8 | 1/2/3/client/app |
| V6-COGNITION-002.2 | existing-coverage-validated | Training Observability + Experiment Tracking Contract | Training quality and regression analysis are weak when run metadata, curves, and hyperparameters are not tracked under one canonical schema. | Provide unified experiment tracking (run config, loss/metric curves, checkpoints, comparisons) surfaced in governed observability views with deterministic per-epoch/per-run receipts. | 8 | 1/2/3/client/app |
| V6-COGNITION-002.3 | existing-coverage-validated | Adapter Merge + Export Contract | Fine-tuned artifacts are hard to deploy when merge/export paths are ad hoc and format conversion lacks provenance guarantees. | Add governed merge/export pipeline (adapter merge and format export profiles such as GGUF/ONNX/SafeTensors where supported) with deterministic artifact, hash, and provenance receipts. | 8 | 1/2/3/client/app/adapter |
| V6-COGNITION-002.4 | existing-coverage-validated | Eval-Triggered Self-Improvement Training Loop Integration | Fine-tuning effort is wasted when evaluation outcomes are not fed back into autonomous retraining decisions with bounded policy controls. | Integrate fine-tune pipeline with evaluation/promotion loops so failed thresholds can trigger bounded retraining plans linked to `V6-COCKPIT-017.*` policies and deterministic trigger/promotion receipts. | 8 | 1/2/3/client/app |
| V6-COGNITION-002.5 | existing-coverage-validated | Conduit-Only Fine-Tune/Eval/Merge/Export Enforcement | Training and artifact mutation paths can bypass safety/audit policy if backend tools are called directly from client or ad hoc scripts. | Route all fine-tune, eval, merge, and export actions through conduit with fail-closed policy checks, deterministic receipts, and contract tests that reject bypass execution paths. | 10 | 0/1/2/3/client/app |

## Reservation & Booking Automation Intake (Doc `19eiVbtG0CAKHOTGW2aDaG9lG7pWF0PKkBVFt27PoHJQ`, 2026-03-11)

Source references:
- https://docs.google.com/document/d/19eiVbtG0CAKHOTGW2aDaG9lG7pWF0PKkBVFt27PoHJQ/edit?usp=sharing
- https://github.com/Alkaar/resy-booking-bot

Notes:
- Source draft labeled this as `V6-TOOLS-003`, but that ID is already consumed by an existing completed notification lane; normalized here to `V6-TOOLS-004.*`.
- Primitive-first normalization: booking automation is a core tools/scheduling/policy extension with optional app UX, not client-owned authority logic.
- Compliance normalization: source "anti-detection/stealth" requirements are translated into policy-safe execution shaping (rate/tempo/jitter, provider constraints, consent gates). Explicit bypass/evasion primitives are out of scope and must fail closed.
- Default placement:
  - Layer `0`: conduit-routed monitor/attempt/confirm/cancel actions + deterministic booking receipts
  - Layer `1`: consent, budget, rate-limit, provider policy contracts; account scope and audit retention rules
  - Layer `2`: availability watcher, timing scheduler, booking executor, retry/backoff and notification orchestration
  - Layer `3`: optional concierge/dining persona defaults only
  - `client`: thin `book run/status/history` wrappers only
  - `app`: optional `/apps/concierge_booking/` workflow UI only
  - `adapter`: provider-specific booking/connectivity adapters

Objective: add governed reservation monitoring and booking automation (restaurants/events/tickets) with deterministic receipts, retry logic, and operator notifications, while preserving strict policy controls.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-TOOLS-004.1 | existing-coverage-validated | Real-Time Availability Watch Contract | Reservation opportunities are missed because slot monitoring is manual, slow, and inconsistent across providers/accounts. | Add bounded availability watchers (poll/stream by provider capability) that monitor multiple targets/accounts under policy-scoped limits and emit deterministic slot-detected receipts within configured latency targets. | 7 | 0/1/2/adapter/client/app |
| V6-TOOLS-004.2 | existing-coverage-validated | Precision Booking Attempt Orchestrator | High-demand booking windows require deterministic timing, form fill sequencing, and robust stepwise execution under transient failures. | `protheus book "<target>"` runs governed booking attempts with scheduled timing controls, deterministic step receipts (select/fill/submit/confirm), and explicit outcome states (`success`, `failed`, `expired`, `policy_denied`). | 7 | 0/1/2/adapter/client/app |
| V6-TOOLS-004.3 | existing-coverage-validated | Provider-Compliance Execution Shaping (No Evasion) | Booking workflows fail or violate policy when automation behavior is unmanaged or attempts to bypass provider protections. | Add compliance-first execution shaping (human-like pacing envelopes, bounded jitter, provider rate guardrails, captcha/manual handoff hooks) and hard-deny prohibited bypass patterns; all deny/allow decisions produce deterministic policy receipts. | 7 | 0/1/2/adapter/client/app |
| V6-TOOLS-004.4 | existing-coverage-validated | Notification + Smart Retry/Backoff Contract | Users lose booking context when failures/successes are not surfaced immediately with structured retry behavior. | Add receipt-linked notifications (success/failure/next-attempt) and bounded retry policies with exponential backoff and stop conditions; every retry decision and notification delivery emits deterministic receipts. | 7 | 0/1/2/adapter/client/app |
| V6-TOOLS-004.5 | existing-coverage-validated | Conduit-Only Booking Enforcement + Thin Client Boundary | Booking automation can become unsafe/audit-incomplete if monitor/attempt flows execute directly from client scripts or adapters. | Route all booking operations through conduit with fail-closed consent/budget/rate-policy checks, deterministic receipts, and contract tests that reject bypass paths; keep client and optional app layers thin and non-authoritative. | 10 | 0/1/2/adapter/client/app |

## Vercel Chatbot Template Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1bxEAbOUGJuY7mfonZjEx15pmSEFVssr3CCjfiYcPIDQ`, 2026-03-11)

- https://docs.google.com/document/d/1bxEAbOUGJuY7mfonZjEx15pmSEFVssr3CCjfiYcPIDQ/edit?usp=sharing
- https://github.com/vercel/chatbot

Notes:
- This source heavily overlaps existing chat lanes and is normalized as source coverage + net-new deltas, not a duplicate chat subsystem.
- Already covered by existing items:
  - Streaming chat/session UX baseline: `V6-APP-008.1`, `V6-APP-007.1`
  - Gateway + persistent connector/session runtime: `V6-COCKPIT-012.2`
  - Attach/disconnect memory continuity: `V6-PERSIST-001.3`, `V6-HERMES-001.3`
  - Tool-call roundtrip behavior in chat starter profile: `V6-APP-008.1`
- Net-new focus from this intake is chat-surface hardening contracts around multi-user auth/session boundaries, attachment object lifecycle, and structured tool-event schemas for web UIs.
- Default placement:
  - Layer `0`: conduit-routed message/tool/attachment actions + deterministic receipts
  - Layer `1`: auth/session policy, attachment policy (type/size/scan/retention), structured tool-event schema policy
  - Layer `2`: chat session runtime, structured tool-call/event broker, attachment ingest/store handlers
  - Layer `3`: optional chat persona presentation defaults only
  - `client`: thin CLI/web UI wrappers and observability views only
  - `app`: optional `/apps/chat_console/` reference UI profile only
  - `adapter`: provider-specific auth/blob storage adapters only

Objective: harden the production chat surface with governed structured tool-calling, durable history/session contracts, safe attachment handling, and multi-user auth/session boundaries while keeping core authority intact and client thin.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-COCKPIT-021.1 | existing-coverage-validated | Structured Tool-Event Chat Contract (Schema + Replay) | Tool-calling chats become brittle when tool requests/results/events lack one canonical schema across streaming UI and runtime. | Add a governed structured tool-event schema for chat sessions (request, progress, result, error) with deterministic receipts and replay-safe rendering contracts for CLI/web surfaces. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-021.2 | existing-coverage-validated | Multi-User Auth + Session-Tenancy Boundary Contract | Shared chat surfaces risk cross-user leakage without explicit auth/session partitioning and scope enforcement. | Add auth/session tenancy contract for chat surfaces (identity-bound sessions, scope checks, revocation/expiry, dmScope alignment) with deterministic session lifecycle receipts and fail-closed denial behavior. | 10 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-021.3 | existing-coverage-validated | Chat Attachment Ingest + Blob Lifecycle Contract | File uploads in chat can bypass retention/privacy controls when attachment handling is not governed as first-class runtime objects. | Add governed attachment ingest/store/reference contracts (type/size validation, malware/content policy hooks, retention labels, signed blob handles) with deterministic upload/access/delete receipts linked to chat threads. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-021.4 | existing-coverage-validated | Conduit-Only Chat Surface Enforcement + Thin Client Boundary | Polished chat UIs can accidentally embed authority logic (tool routing/session mutation/storage actions) in client code paths. | Enforce conduit-only routing for message stream, tool invocation, history mutation, and attachment actions; keep client/app surfaces thin and non-authoritative, with contract tests that reject bypass paths. | 10 | 0/1/2/client |

## DeepAgents Harness Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1t1O_xrDE9sLz3iA9-D2PhSEsfvY7B1_ckv1gnvCiZEs`, 2026-03-11)

Source references:
- https://docs.google.com/document/d/1t1O_xrDE9sLz3iA9-D2PhSEsfvY7B1_ckv1gnvCiZEs/edit?usp=sharing
- https://github.com/langchain-ai/deepagents

Notes:
- Primitive-first normalization: this intake extends existing planning/tooling/context primitives; it does not introduce a parallel agent runtime.
- Overlap handled explicitly:
  - Adaptive context compaction/reminders/safety controls: `V6-COCKPIT-009.*`
  - Gateway/workspace memory + tool-mode controls: `V6-COCKPIT-012.*`
  - MCP/workflow orchestration and graph compile lanes: `V6-MCP-001.*`, `V6-FLOW-001.*`
  - Existing shell/tool safety gates: `V2-HLX-003`, `V6-COCKPIT-009.6`
- Net-new focus from this source:
  - first-class, runtime-native `write_todos` planning primitive with progress/replan semantics
  - governed sandboxed `execute` tool with explicit human approval contracts for dangerous commands
  - automatic large-output/context offload to governed filesystem/object lanes with resumable summaries
  - first-class compiled agent graph return contract for streaming/checkpoint/studio interoperability
  - curated batteries-included deep-harness template lane
- Default placement:
  - Layer `0`: conduit-routed plan/execute/offload/compile actions + deterministic receipts + approval enforcement
  - Layer `1`: policy gates for command risk tiers, context offload thresholds, graph exposure scopes
  - Layer `2`: todo planner runtime, shell executor broker, offload controller, graph compiler
  - Layer `3`: optional operator persona presets only
  - `client`: thin task/status/approval/streaming UX wrappers only
  - `app`: optional `/apps/deep_harness_workbench/` profile only
  - `adapter`: shell sandbox providers, filesystem/object backends, graph/studio adapter bridges

Objective: add a governed, batteries-included agent harness contract (plan -> execute -> offload -> checkpoint/stream) with explicit safety approvals and deterministic receipts, while keeping all authority core-side.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-COCKPIT-022.1 | existing-coverage-validated | First-Class `write_todos` Planning Primitive Contract | Agents drift without a canonical plan tracker that can decompose goals, track progress, and trigger re-planning under blockage. | Add governed `write_todos` primitive with typed task states (`queued`, `in_progress`, `blocked`, `done`), dependency edges, and deterministic update/replan receipts; expose via thin `protheus plan`/dashboard surfaces only. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-022.2 | existing-coverage-validated | Sandboxed `execute` Tool Contract with Tiered Human Approval Gates | Shell execution paths remain high-risk unless every command runs in sandboxed runtime with explicit risk-tier approvals and policy receipts. | Add governed `execute` tool contract routed through conduit with sandbox isolation, command risk classification, required human approval for dangerous tiers, and deterministic execute/deny/audit receipts. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-022.3 | existing-coverage-validated | Automatic Context Spillover Offload Contract | Long tasks degrade reliability when large outputs remain in prompt context instead of moving to persistent governed artifacts with compact references. | Implement automatic spillover/offload thresholds that write large artifacts to governed storage, keep receipt-linked summaries in active context, and allow deterministic lazy rehydrate by pointer. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-022.4 | existing-coverage-validated | Compiled Agent Graph Return + Runtime Interop Contract | Advanced runtime features (streaming, checkpointing, external studio inspection) are harder to use without a canonical compiled graph artifact contract. | Add `protheus agent compile` contract that returns a governed compiled graph artifact with stream/checkpoint metadata and deterministic compile/version receipts; no client-owned execution authority is introduced. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-022.5 | existing-coverage-validated | Curated Deep Harness Template Governance Lane | "Batteries-included" harness quality drifts without signed, reviewed templates that encode safe defaults for planning/filesystem/shell/sub-agent flows. | Maintain signed template pack installable via governed URI (for example `harness://deep-agent`) with quarterly human review, compatibility metadata, and deterministic install/activate/deprecate receipts. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-022.6 | existing-coverage-validated | Conduit-Only Harness Boundary + Thin Client Enforcement | Harness features can regress into client-owned authority logic unless boundary enforcement is explicit and tested. | Enforce conduit-only routing for todo, execute, offload, and compile flows with fail-closed bypass-rejection tests; keep client/app surfaces non-authoritative and UX-only. | 10 | 0/1/2/client |

Notes:

  - Layer `1`: experiment policy (budget/safety/data-governance), reference-artifact provenance, retention limits

  - Layer `3`: optional educational/self-improvement persona defaults only
  - `client`: thin CLI/observability controls only (`llm-lab`, `improve model`, experiment views)
  - `app`: optional `/apps/llm_lab/` reference UX only

| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-COGNITION-001.1 | existing-coverage-validated | Reference Transformer Component Contract | Model internals are difficult to inspect/teach/debug when only opaque production kernels are available. | Add reference implementations for tokenizer/embedding/attention/FFN/norm/decoder components under governed experimental contracts, with deterministic component-test receipts and parity checks against known baselines. | 8 | 1/2/3/client/app |
| V6-COGNITION-001.2 | existing-coverage-validated | Reference Pretrain/Fine-Tune Loop Contract | Research and pedagogy suffer when there is no canonical minimal loop for pretrain/finetune experiments with reproducible artifacts. | Provide bounded reference loops (pretrain + supervised finetune + adapter-style variants) with deterministic epoch/checkpoint/eval receipts and integration hooks into `V6-COGNITION-002.*` promotion policies. | 8 | 1/2/3/client/app/adapter |
| V6-COGNITION-001.3 | existing-coverage-validated | KV Cache + Memory-Efficient Inference Reference Path | Edge/local experimentation regresses without a standardized low-memory inference path and cache correctness validation. | Add reference KV-cache inference path with deterministic cache-correctness tests, memory telemetry receipts, and policy-bounded low-memory execution profiles. | 8 | 1/2/3/client/app |
| V6-COGNITION-001.4 | existing-coverage-validated | Unified Architecture Adapter Interface (Llama/Qwen/Gemma-Class) | Multi-architecture experimentation is fragmented when each model family uses ad hoc loader/runtime interfaces. | Define a unified adapter interface for major architecture classes with deterministic load/config receipts and conformance tests proving compatible run/eval behavior under one contract. | 8 | 1/2/3/client/app/adapter |
| V6-COGNITION-001.5 | existing-coverage-validated | Conduit-Only Experiment Enforcement + Non-Production Boundary | Educational/reference paths can accidentally become ungoverned production paths if boundaries are not explicit. | Enforce conduit-only execution and mark reference lanes as non-production by default; promotions into production lanes require explicit policy gates, deterministic receipts, and fail-closed boundary checks. | 10 | 0/1/2/3/client/app |

| V6-DEPLOY-001.2 | existing-coverage-validated | Persistent Volume + SQLite State Continuity Contract | Restart-safe state continuity is fragile when storage mounts and persistence checks are manual per platform. | Enforce deployment-time persistent storage contract (volumes + SQLite/state paths + permission checks), with deterministic boot/restart continuity receipts and fail-closed deployment status when persistence validation fails. | 10 | 0/1/2/client/adapter |
| V6-DEPLOY-001.3 | existing-coverage-validated | Password-Bootstrap Web Setup Contract | Non-CLI operators face setup friction and unsafe defaults when first-run auth/bootstrap is not standardized. | Add first-run `/setup` bootstrap contract requiring strong password/credential initialization before dashboard access, issuing deterministic setup/auth receipts and denying dashboard routes until bootstrap completion. | 8 | 0/1/2/client/adapter |
| V6-DEPLOY-001.4 | existing-coverage-validated | Zero-Config Multi-Provider Bootstrap Routing Contract | Early deployments stall when model-provider configuration/routing requires manual deep configuration across environments. | Add deployment bootstrap profile that maps env/config provider declarations into existing routing primitives with deterministic provider validation receipts, bounded defaults, and cost-cap policy enforcement from first boot. | 8 | 0/1/2/client/adapter |
| V6-DEPLOY-001.5 | existing-coverage-validated | Curated Deployment Template Governance Lane (`deploy://`) | Community templates drift in security/quality without signed curation and periodic review. | Maintain signed deployment template pack installable via governed URI (for example `deploy://railway`) with quarterly human review, security checklist metadata, compatibility tests, and deterministic install/update/deprecate receipts. | 8 | 0/1/2/client/adapter |
| V6-DEPLOY-001.6 | existing-coverage-validated | Conduit-Only Deployment Boundary + Thin Client Enforcement | Deployment/setup logic can become bypassable if implemented directly in client scripts or unmanaged app adapters. | Enforce conduit-only routing for deploy/bootstrap/storage/auth/provider-config operations with bypass-rejection tests and fail-closed policy denials; keep client/app layers non-authoritative. | 10 | 0/1/2/client/adapter |

  - conduit/policy enforcement and capability gating: `V6-CONDUIT-002`, `V6-CONDUIT-006`, `V6-CONDUIT-007`

  - one-command desktop workflow assimilation profile and curated CUA template governance lane

  - `adapter`: OS accessibility/desktop I/O providers (macOS/Windows/Linux) and vision fallback providers

Objective: extend existing desktop-control lanes with explicit cross-platform sandbox guarantees, modular CUA team-role orchestration, and one-command desktop workflow assimilation, while preserving conduit-only authority and thin client/app boundaries.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
