| V6-APP-011.7 | existing-coverage-validated | Redacted Visual Evidence Receipt Contract (Before/After Frames) | Desktop action audits are hard to trust without standardized visual evidence while preserving privacy-sensitive redaction. | Emit policy-scoped pre/post action visual evidence receipts (hashed frame references + redacted overlays) for desktop actions; privacy policy governs which pixels/regions may be retained or must be masked/omitted. | 6 | app |
| V6-APP-011.8 | existing-coverage-validated | Modular CUA Role-Pack Contract (Brain/Planner/Actor/Memory) | Desktop-agent behavior degrades when planning, execution, and state management are fused without explicit handoff contracts. | Add installable role-pack profile (`brain/planner/actor/memory`) that orchestrates role handoffs via deterministic task/context receipts and bounded delegation policies under existing orchestrator primitives. | 6 | app |
| V6-APP-011.9 | existing-coverage-validated | One-Command Desktop Workflow Assimilation Contract | Reusable desktop workflows are still expensive to codify manually from raw interaction traces. | Add `protheus assimilate desktop <workflow>` profile that compiles traces/intents into governed desktop recipe objects, binds role-pack defaults, and emits deterministic assimilation/replay-verification receipts. | 6 | app |
| V6-APP-011.10 | existing-coverage-validated | Curated CUA Template Governance Lane (`cua://`) | Desktop workflow templates drift in safety and quality without signed curation and recurring OS-compatibility review. | Maintain signed CUA template pack installable via governed URI (for example `cua://desktop-agent`) with quarterly human review, OS compatibility metadata, and deterministic install/update/deprecate receipts. | 6 | app |

## Driftwatch Fleet Observability Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1Vl8VjbRWTsAH4vz5jp4ydYGC9Xum4QGhIxix89WRThg`, 2026-03-11)

- https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Vl8VjbRWTsAH4vz5jp4ydYGC9Xum4QGhIxix89WRThg/edit?usp=sharing
- https://x.com/GitHub_Daily/status/2030546613513646452

Notes:
- Primitive-first normalization: this intake extends existing observability, context-health, and governance-report lanes; it does not create a parallel monitoring subsystem.
- Overlap handled explicitly:
  - baseline monitoring/alerts/workflows: `V6-OBSERVABILITY-001.*`
  - evaluation/quality-loop contracts: `V6-OBSERVABILITY-003.*`
  - deep-trace/plugin contracts: `V6-OBSERVABILITY-004.*`
  - context integrity + drift checks: `V6-COCKPIT-014.*`, `V3-RACE-CONF-007`
  - architecture publication baseline: `V3-RACE-215`
- Net-new focus from this source:
  - automatic fleet architecture scanner with interactive tree/relationship map
  - governance health scoring + prioritized gap detection/remediation artifacts
  - local-only privacy-preserving visualization with redaction contract
  - curated driftwatch ruleset/template pack lifecycle
  - enterprise export/integration contract for diagram and score artifacts
- Default placement:
  - Layer `0`: conduit-routed scan/score/export operations with deterministic receipts and fail-closed policy checks
  - Layer `1`: governance scoring policy, redaction/privacy policy, report retention/export policy
  - Layer `2`: scanner/indexer, architecture graph compiler, health scorer, remediation suggester, export orchestrator
  - Layer `3`: optional operator persona defaults only
  - `client`: thin `protheus observe`/`protheus-top` rendering and controls only
  - `app`: optional `/apps/driftwatch_console/` workflow surface
  - `adapter`: enterprise export/integration adapters (Mermaid/Draw.io/PDF/Notion/Confluence/Slack)

Objective: provide always-on, receipt-linked, privacy-safe fleet introspection that converts governance files and runtime signals into actionable architecture maps, health scores, and integration-ready reports without moving authority into client/app layers.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-OBSERVABILITY-005.1 | existing-coverage-validated | Automated Fleet Architecture Scanner + Interactive Tree Contract | Large shadow fleets are hard to reason about when role/delegation/memory topology remains implicit in scattered files and configs. | Add `protheus observe architecture` contract that scans governed workspace/runtime artifacts and emits interactive graph artifacts (CLI JSON/SVG + dashboard view) in under policy-bounded latency, with deterministic scan/render receipts and live watcher-triggered refresh support. | 7 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-OBSERVABILITY-005.2 | existing-coverage-validated | Governance Health Score + Gap Detection Contract | Operators lack one consistent fleet-level confidence metric and prioritized remediation list for governance drift and missing controls. | Compute per-fleet and per-persona health scores (0-100) from policy-defined factors (required files, heartbeat freshness, delegation integrity, covenant coverage), emit deterministic score receipts, and generate ranked gaps with machine-actionable remediation proposals (`command`, `patch`, or PR artifact). | 7 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-OBSERVABILITY-005.3 | existing-coverage-validated | Privacy-First Local Visualization + Redaction Contract | Architecture observability can leak sensitive tokens/keys/identifiers when visualization and export paths are not explicitly local-first and redaction-scoped. | Enforce local-only rendering by default, apply Layer-1 redaction policies before any visualization/export artifact is materialized, and reject non-compliant output attempts with deterministic denial receipts and masked-field evidence logs. | 7 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-OBSERVABILITY-005.4 | existing-coverage-validated | Automated Remediation Suggestion + Apply/Revert Contract | Score/gap dashboards stall if remediation remains manual and detached from governed execution/revert semantics. | Add remediation pipeline that converts high-confidence gap findings into policy-gated apply plans (command bundles or PR drafts), with deterministic propose/apply/verify/revert receipts and explicit rollback pointers for every automated change. | 7 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-OBSERVABILITY-005.5 | existing-coverage-validated | Curated Driftwatch Ruleset + Template Governance Lane (`driftwatch://`) | Fleet-scanning rules and dashboard templates drift in quality/safety without signed curation and recurring human review. | Maintain signed ruleset/template packs installable via governed URI (for example `driftwatch://core-fleet-observe`) with quarterly human review, compatibility metadata, and deterministic install/update/deprecate receipts. | 7 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-OBSERVABILITY-005.6 | existing-coverage-validated | Enterprise Export + Integration Contract (Mermaid/Draw.io/PDF/JSON + Alert Hooks) | Observability output has low operational value when architecture/health artifacts cannot flow directly into enterprise documentation and alerting surfaces. | Provide governed export adapters for Mermaid/Draw.io/PDF/JSON plus integration hooks (Notion/Confluence/Slack/webhook alerts), with deterministic export/delivery receipts and policy-scoped outbound controls. | 7 | 0/1/2/client |

## CLI-Anything Tool Generation Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1YdExoPszYGqAn6g0zSiEw9yc6_UpqcnVoIMR7KteP-w`, 2026-03-11)

Source references:
- https://docs.google.com/document/d/1YdExoPszYGqAn6g0zSiEw9yc6_UpqcnVoIMR7KteP-w/edit?usp=sharing
- https://github.com/HKUDS/CLI-Anything

Notes:
- Primitive-first normalization: this intake extends existing extension/tooling lanes; it does not create a parallel tool runtime.
- Overlap handled explicitly:
  - baseline tool/app scaffold lanes: `V6-EXT-701`, `V6-EXT-702`
  - capability/sandbox/publish/registry controls: `V6-EXT-704..709`
  - extension overhead/perf gate: `V6-EXT-710`
  - API/CLI lifecycle and compatibility contracts: `V6-F100-007`
  - existing standalone CLI packaging surfaces: `V4-SUITE-001..011`
- Net-new focus from this source:
  - one-command 7-phase codebase-to-agent-CLI generation pipeline
  - universal "agent CLI skin" conformance contract (stateful REPL + `--json` + self-describing help)
  - strict multi-layer generated-tool verification gate before activation/publish
  - curated professional backend harness library lane (`tools://`)
  - fail-closed real-backend safety-wrapper contract for high-value software integrations
- Default placement:
  - Layer `0`: conduit-routed generate/test/publish/activate operations + deterministic receipts
  - Layer `1`: CLI-skin specification policy, activation quality thresholds, backend wrapper risk policy
  - Layer `2`: generation pipeline orchestrator, harness compiler, verification gate, publish/admission broker
  - Layer `3`: optional Toolsmith persona defaults only
  - `client`: thin `protheus tools generate/test/publish` wrappers + CLI UX surfaces only
  - `app`: optional `/apps/toolsmith_console/` workflow UI
  - `adapter`: backend adapters/wrappers for target software runtimes (e.g., Blender, LibreOffice, media/edit pipelines)

Objective: make arbitrary software/codebases agent-native through one governed generation pipeline that emits standardized CLIs, enforces strict test/activation gates, and uses fail-closed backend wrappers, while keeping all authority in core layers.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-EXT-711 | existing-coverage-validated | Seven-Phase Codebase-to-Agent-CLI Generation Pipeline Contract | Converting existing software into agent-usable CLIs is still manual and inconsistent, limiting integration velocity and reliability. | Add `protheus tools generate <path-or-repo>` pipeline executing deterministic phases (`analyze`, `design`, `implement`, `test-plan`, `test`, `document`, `publish-candidate`) and emitting signed phase core/local/artifacts/receipts; output must pass policy checks before activation. | 7 | 1/2/client/app/adapter |
| V6-EXT-712 | existing-coverage-validated | Universal Agent CLI Skin Specification Contract (REPL + JSON + Help) | Tool UX/interoperability degrades when generated and native CLIs expose inconsistent flags, output schemas, and interaction models. | Define/enforce canonical CLI skin requiring stateful REPL support, `--json` machine output, normalized `--help` schema, and consistent progress/error envelopes; conformance is validated in CI with deterministic receipts. | 7 | 1/2/client/app/adapter |
| V6-EXT-713 | existing-coverage-validated | Multi-Layer Generated Tool Verification Gate Contract | Generated tools can appear functional while failing under real backend or subprocess conditions without one mandatory quality gate. | Require generated tools to pass unit, CLI subprocess, project-file E2E, and real-backend invocation tests with deterministic result artifacts; activation/publish is denied if any required suite fails policy thresholds. | 7 | 1/2/client/app/adapter |
| V6-EXT-714 | existing-coverage-validated | Curated Professional Harness Library Governance Lane (`tools://`) | High-value software harnesses drift in safety/quality without signed curation and recurring human review. | Maintain signed harness packs for priority software categories installable via governed URI (for example `tools://blender-core`) with quarterly human review, compatibility metadata, and deterministic install/update/deprecate receipts. | 7 | 1/2/client/app/adapter |
| V6-EXT-715 | existing-coverage-validated | Real-Backend Safety Wrapper Contract (Fail-Closed Delegation) | Deep integrations become brittle or unsafe when backend calls are ad hoc and lack one policy-governed wrapper model. | Provide standardized safety wrappers for backend delegation (auth, resource bounds, timeout/retry, input/output schema guards, deny-on-policy-breach) with deterministic call receipts and explicit fallback/error semantics. | 10 | 0/1/2/client/app/adapter |
| V6-EXT-716 | existing-coverage-validated | Conduit-Only Tool-Generation Boundary + Thin Client Enforcement | Tool generation/testing/publish flows can bypass governance if implemented directly in client scripts or app code paths. | Enforce conduit-only routing for generation, verification, publish, and activation operations with bypass-rejection tests and fail-closed policy denials; client/app surfaces remain non-authoritative UX layers only. | 10 | 0/1/2/client/app/adapter |

- https://x.com/s_mohinii/status/2030629797878268095

Notes:
- Primitive-first normalization: this intake extends existing `V6-SKILLS-001.*` lanes and does not create a parallel skills subsystem.
- Overlap handled explicitly:
  - base skill package scaffold and execution contracts: `V6-SKILLS-001.1..001.5`
  - curated external skill ingest/import baseline: `V6-SKILLS-001.6`
  - first-party reference profiles and evolution loop: `V6-SKILLS-001.7..001.9`
  - extension registry/capability/runtime governance: `V6-EXT-704..709`
- Net-new focus from this source:
  - official signed first-party skills library lifecycle (`protheus/skills`) with heartbeat-driven sync contract
  - explicit interoperable skill package specification + OpenAI-skill conversion contract
  - persona-as-skill packaged install contract for zero-prompt-repeat adoption
  - formal ecosystem compatibility matrix (local daemon, cloud, third-party runners)
- Default placement:
  - Layer `0`: conduit-routed install/import/run/update actions + deterministic receipts
  - Layer `1`: manifest-signing policy, compatibility policy, curation/update governance policy
  - Layer `2`: library sync service, conversion/import compiler, persona-skill binder, compatibility validator
  - Layer `3`: expert persona profiles exposed as installable skill bundles
  - `client`: thin `protheus skill|persona install|sync|status` wrappers + observability only
  - `app`: optional `/apps/skills_console/` UX only
  - `adapter`: external skill-source adapters (GitHub/registry mirrors/third-party runner adapters)

Objective: harden the skills surface into an official, signed, interoperable ecosystem where curated skill packs and persona bundles install in one command and run under conduit-only authority with compatibility guarantees.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-SKILLS-001.10 | existing-coverage-validated | Official Signed Skills Library Sync Contract (`protheus/skills`) | Skill quality and trust drift when first-party skills are distributed ad hoc instead of through one signed, centrally governed library channel. | Add official skills-library sync contract that installs/updates signed skill packs via governed URI (`skills://<name>`), performs signature/provenance checks, binds to permitted personas, and emits deterministic install/update/sync receipts (including heartbeat-triggered refresh receipts). | 7 | 1/2/client/app |
| V6-SKILLS-001.11 | existing-coverage-validated | Canonical Interop Skill Spec + OpenAI Skill Conversion Contract | External skill ecosystems remain costly to adopt when folder formats and manifests are not normalized into one canonical compatible specification. | Publish canonical skill spec (`docs/skills-spec.md`) and add conversion pipeline that imports OpenAI-style skill folders into governed Protheus skill objects with schema validation, missing-field synthesis hooks, and deterministic conversion receipts. | 7 | 1/2/client/app |
| V6-SKILLS-001.12 | existing-coverage-validated | Persona-as-Skill Bundle Contract (One-Command Expert Install) | Users repeatedly rebuild persona prompts/tool bindings because expert patterns are not packaged as reusable installable skill bundles. | Add one-command persona-skill install contract (for example `protheus persona install research-expert`) that binds required tools/policies/memory defaults and emits deterministic bind/activate receipts with startup latency bounded by policy targets. | 7 | 1/2/client/app |
| V6-SKILLS-001.13 | existing-coverage-validated | Human-Curated Skill Governance + Signing Cadence Contract | Without explicit human stewardship cadence, curated skill libraries accumulate stale or unsafe artifacts despite technical validation. | Establish signed curation lifecycle (quarterly review, changelog provenance, deprecation policy) with deterministic governance receipts for approve/reject/deprecate actions and policy gate that blocks unsigned first-party skill publication. | 7 | 1/2/client/app |
| V6-SKILLS-001.14 | existing-coverage-validated | Multi-Runner Compatibility Matrix + Migration Contract | Skill portability breaks when behavior diverges across local daemon, cloud runtimes, and third-party agent runners without tested compatibility profiles. | Publish and enforce compatibility matrix with conformance tests per runner profile, generate migration guides/adapters for incompatible surfaces, and emit deterministic compatibility-check receipts before cross-runner activation. | 7 | 1/2/client/app |

## OpenClaw Managed Hosting Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1mn1_m4yp5c1bx-Sh94mKLo1kNk4luWytB-2hVgMf93A`, 2026-03-11)

- https://docs.google.com/document/d/1mn1_m4yp5c1bx-Sh94mKLo1kNk4luWytB-2hVgMf93A/edit?usp=sharing
- https://x.com/Ronycoder/status/2030576789995815296

Notes:
- Primitive-first normalization: this intake extends existing deployment, persistence, transport, and platform-adaptation lanes; it does not create a parallel runtime.
- Overlap handled explicitly:
  - native push transport baseline + websocket stream contract: `V6-COCKPIT-006` (done), `V6-COCKPIT-007` (queued)
  - sub-agent/background routing contracts: `V6-PERSIST-001.5`, `V6-AGENCY-001.*`, `V6-COCKPIT-022.*`
  - existing backup/ops GUI and recovery baselines: `V6-COCKPIT-019.2`, `V4-SCALE-006`, `V3-FCH-003`
  - existing enterprise support/SLA governance anchors: `V6-F100-044`, `V6-F100-A-011` (human gated)
- Net-new focus from this source:
  - true zero-downtime rolling updates with in-flight shadow continuity and deterministic config/state migration
  - full-state snapshot backup/restore with forked experiment sandboxes
  - first-class external secrets federation lane at V6 policy/authority layers
  - managed-cloud control-plane contracts (tenant/workspace isolation, health/SLA evidence, managed auto-ops)
  - explicit Windows + Android first-class platform adapter wave
- Default placement:
  - Layer `0`: conduit-routed update/backup/restore/secrets/cloud-control actions + deterministic receipts
  - Layer `1`: migration safety policy, secrets federation policy, tenant isolation policy, backup retention policy
  - Layer `2`: rolling update orchestrator, state migration verifier, snapshot/fork manager, cloud control plane, host-adapter broker
  - Layer `3`: optional operator persona defaults only
  - `client`: thin `deploy/update/backup/cloud/status` wrappers and dashboards only
  - `app`: optional `/apps/cloud_console/` for managed operations UX
  - `adapter`: secrets-provider adapters (Vault/AWS/1Password), Windows/Android platform adapters

Objective: remove self-hosting fragility by making upgrades, backup/restore, secrets federation, and managed cloud operations deterministic and safe-by-default while preserving core authority and thin client/app boundaries.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-DEPLOY-001.7 | existing-coverage-validated | Zero-Downtime Rolling Update + Stateful Migration Contract | Upgrades still risk runtime interruption and config/state breakage when migration and rollout are not one atomic governed path. | Add rolling update orchestration that stages/canaries new runtime while old workers continue serving, performs covenant-checked config/state migrations, and only cuts traffic after parity/health gates pass; automatic rollback triggers on migration or SLO failure with deterministic rollout/migrate/rollback receipts. | 8 | 0/1/2/client/adapter |
| V6-PERSIST-001.13 | existing-coverage-validated | Full-State Snapshot Backup/Restore + Forked Experiment Contract | Operators cannot safely iterate when backups are partial and restore paths do not support instant branch/fork testing. | Add one-command full-state snapshot contract (`memory`, `receipts`, `policies`, `persona/agent state`, `scheduler`, `connectors`) with deterministic restore and optional forked-namespace restore mode for experiment sandboxes; every snapshot/restore/fork operation emits deterministic receipts and integrity hashes. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-SEC-016 | done | External Secrets Federation Contract (Vault/AWS/1Password) | Enterprise adoption slows when secrets handling is not uniformly mapped to first-class external secret managers under one governed contract. | Implemented `secrets-federation` lane in `core/layer0/ops/src/security_plane.rs` with provider-scoped handles, fail-closed fetch, rotation/revocation semantics, and deterministic receipts; behavior proven by `core/layer0/ops/tests/v6_security_hardening_integration.rs::v6_sec_016_secrets_federation_issues_scoped_handles_and_revokes_them`. | 10 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-CLOUD-001.1 | existing-coverage-validated | Managed Cloud Control Plane Contract (Private Instance Lifecycle) | Non-technical users face high friction without a one-command managed instance lifecycle for deployment, updates, and runtime ops. | Add managed instance control-plane contract (`create`, `upgrade`, `suspend`, `resume`, `destroy`, `clone`) with deterministic lifecycle receipts, policy-scoped defaults, and guarded workspace bootstrap flow that requires secure setup completion before exposure. | 8 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V6-CLOUD-001.2 | existing-coverage-validated | Tenant/Workspace Isolation + Managed Ops Health/SLA Evidence Contract | Managed multi-tenant offerings are untrustworthy without explicit workspace isolation boundaries and continuously measured ops health evidence. | Enforce workspace-isolation policy (identity/session/storage/runtime boundaries), emit periodic managed-ops health artifacts (`uptime`, `update success`, `restore drill freshness`, `incident MTTR`), and block high-risk operations when isolation/health checks fail; all checks produce deterministic receipts. | 8 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V6-SEED-812 | existing-coverage-validated | First-Class Windows Socket/Adapter Profile | Platform support remains uneven when Windows runtime behavior relies on generic fallback paths rather than explicit governed adapters. | Deliver Windows-first socket/adapter profile with install/update/service contracts, policy-bound capability matrix, and deterministic conformance receipts proving parity with core runtime contracts. | 6 | 1/2/client/adapter |
| V6-SEED-813 | existing-coverage-validated | First-Class Android Edge Runtime/Adapter Profile | Mobile/edge operations are constrained without a dedicated Android runtime adapter contract and lifecycle governance. | Deliver Android adapter profile covering background service lifecycle, constrained-resource scheduling, secure local storage, and attach/reconnect continuity with deterministic conformance and rollback receipts. | 6 | 1/2/client/adapter |

## Personal #1 Ranking Criteria Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1giphwvzmz8cPaw3Ltu1A2s9x8UE-WclPPkwhz_Q12ug`, 2026-03-11)

Source references:
- https://docs.google.com/document/d/1giphwvzmz8cPaw3Ltu1A2s9x8UE-WclPPkwhz_Q12ug/edit?usp=sharing

Notes:
- Primitive-first normalization: this intake is mostly overlap with existing proof/scale/release lanes and is therefore mapped as source coverage deltas, not a new subsystem.
- Overlap handled explicitly:
  - formal proof expansion and proof-runtime CI lanes: `V6-SEC-005`, `V6-TECH-301..305`, `V7-TOP1-001..004`
  - self-host/bootstrap maturity lanes: `V3-062`, `V6-MPLAN-910`
  - scale/load/fleet evidence lanes: `V4-SCALE-001`, `V6-F100-024` (blocked), `V6-F100-025` (blocked)
  - benchmark and release evidence lanes: `V6-COMP-001`, `V6-DOM-401`, `V6-F100-003`, `V6-F100-A-012`
  - backlog/SRS conformance baselines: `V3-RACE-CONF-003`, `V6-F100-038`
- Net-new focus from this source:
  - explicit sacred-core nightly proof-coverage floor with fail-closed gate on regression
  - autonomous "runtime builds itself" receipt contract via shadow-operator orchestration
  - nightly Dream Sequencer ingest of SRS/TODO/issues with deterministic PR-draft output queue
  - per-release traceability matrix contract (`SRS -> code anchors -> receipts -> artifacts`)
  - explicit 1,000-node/72h autonomous fleet soak evidence lane
- Default placement:
  - Layer `0`: conduit-routed prove/bootstrap/ingest/release-evidence operations + deterministic receipts
  - Layer `1`: proof-coverage policy, release-traceability policy, backlog-ingest policy
  - Layer `2`: proof orchestrator, self-host bootstrap pipeline, Dream Sequencer backlog compiler, fleet soak harness
  - `client`: thin status/report wrappers only
  - `app`: optional `/apps/evidence_console/` for human review and publication workflows

Objective: close residual proof-maturity and evidence-publication gaps by binding nightly proof coverage, autonomous self-hosting, backlog-to-PR loops, release traceability, and 1k-node soak evidence into deterministic core-owned contracts.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V7-TOP1-011 | existing-coverage-validated | Sacred-Core Nightly Proof Coverage Floor Contract | Proof suites run, but there is no explicit nightly coverage floor proving 100% covenant/fail-closed path inclusion for sacred core invariants. | Add nightly model-check + symbolic/proof coverage gate for Layer-0/1 critical paths; CI fails closed if coverage drops below policy floor or any sacred invariant proof artifact is missing, with deterministic coverage receipts attached per run. | 10 | 0/1/2 |
| V7-TOP1-012 | existing-coverage-validated | Autonomous Self-Hosting Build Receipt Contract | Self-hosting capability exists, but there is no recurring proof that Protheus can rebuild and validate its own runtime end-to-end through governed agent orchestration. | Add `protheus self-host verify` flow where shadow operators execute full source bootstrap/build/verify pipeline (Rust core + client runtime), emitting deterministic stage receipts and fail-closed rollback when bootstrap parity gates fail. | 10 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-MPLAN-911 | existing-coverage-validated | Nightly Backlog/SRS/Issue Ingest -> Prioritized PR Draft Contract | Governance artifacts are present but not yet unified into one nightly autonomous planning loop that converts state into reviewable implementation drafts. | Nightly Dream Sequencer run ingests `docs/workspace/SRS.md`, `TODO`, and repo issues, emits prioritized covenant-checked proposal/PR draft bundles with deterministic lineage receipts, and updates conformance status without bypassing human approval gates. | 9 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-DOM-413 | existing-coverage-validated | Release Evidence Traceability Matrix Contract (`SRS -> code -> receipt -> artifact`) | Release bundles include proofs/artifacts, but traceability from requirement to code anchors and runtime receipts is not yet guaranteed as one machine-verifiable matrix on every tag. | Each tagged release ships a deterministic traceability matrix linking active SRS IDs to code anchors, verification receipts, and attached release artifacts (SBOM/signatures/bench/proofs); release gate fails if any required link is unresolved. | 8 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-F100-046 | existing-coverage-validated | 1,000-Node 72h Autonomous Fleet Soak Evidence Contract | Horizontal-scale credibility remains blocked without a recurring large-fleet soak lane that proves stability under autonomous load for sustained windows. | Run deterministic 1,000-node equivalent shadow-operator soak scenario for 72h in governed profiles, publish uptime/error-budget/failover receipts and operator dashboard snapshots, and block promotion when soak thresholds are not met. | 9 | 0/1/2/client |

## Dominance Gap-Closing Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1Uu0M862I5n6soIbZuB2j3MXQN4VjdtY2ieH-XTspWe0`, 2026-03-11)

Source references:
- https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Uu0M862I5n6soIbZuB2j3MXQN4VjdtY2ieH-XTspWe0/edit?usp=sharing

Notes:
- Primitive-first normalization: this intake mostly overlaps existing release/proof/benchmark/compliance lanes and is added as net-new deltas only.
- Overlap handled explicitly:
  - release signing/SBOM/provenance baseline: `V6-F100-003`, `V6-F100-A-012`, `V6-DOM-401`
  - proof and nightly verification baseline: `V6-SEC-005`, `V6-TECH-301..305`, `V7-TOP1-011`
  - benchmark matrix baseline: `V6-COMP-001`, `V6-DOM-403`
  - migration importer baseline: `V4-MIGR-003`
  - community starter issue baseline: `V6-GAP-004`
- Net-new focus from this source:
  - explicit test-coverage release/merge floor contract for core matrix runs
  - CVE/SBOM alert publication bridge into GitHub Security/advisory surfaces
  - machine-generated compliance export command for traceability bundles
  - release-time public documentation pack generation (README + whitepaper PDF)
  - public anonymized telemetry benchmark publication contract

Default placement:
- Layer `0`: conduit-routed publish/export/report actions + deterministic receipts
- Layer `1`: redaction/privacy policy, publication policy, compliance export policy
- Layer `2`: release-doc generator, advisory publisher, compliance exporter, telemetry publication pipeline
- `client`: thin `status/export/publish` wrappers only
- `app`: optional `/apps/public_evidence_console/` for operator-facing review

Objective: convert internal excellence into reproducible public proof surfaces (coverage, advisories, compliance exports, docs, telemetry) without introducing duplicate authority paths.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-F100-047 | existing-coverage-validated | Core Matrix Test-Coverage Floor Gate (>=95%) | Build/test gates can pass while leaving critical runtime surfaces under-tested when no explicit minimum coverage floor is enforced at release/merge boundaries. | Enforce deterministic coverage threshold policy for core matrix suites (`Rust + TS + verify`) with fail-closed merge/release behavior below floor, coverage artifacts attached to receipts, and class-based allowlist only for approved generated/fixture paths. | 10 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-SEC-017 | done | CVE + SBOM Advisory Publication Bridge Contract | Security disclosures and SBOM changes are documented, but automated publication into security advisory channels is not one canonical receipt-linked path. | Add policy-gated advisory bridge that emits CVE/SBOM delta alerts to GitHub Security/advisory surfaces with deterministic publication receipts, severity mapping, and retry/escalation behavior for failed publication attempts. | 10 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-DOM-414 | existing-coverage-validated | `protheusctl compliance export` Traceability Bundle Contract | Compliance evidence exists across lanes but operators lack one deterministic command that packages SRS-to-code-to-receipt traceability for audits. | Implement `protheusctl compliance export` generating signed compliance bundles (traceability matrix, policy manifests, proof receipts, SBOM/signature links) with deterministic schema and redaction-safe profiles (`internal`, `customer`, `auditor`). | 8 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-DOM-415 | existing-coverage-validated | Release Documentation Autogenesis Pack (README + Whitepaper PDF) | Public trust and onboarding lag when release-aligned documentation remains manually curated and drifts from current SRS/evidence state. | On tagged releases, generate/update public docs pack from canonical sources (`SRS`, `ARCHITECTURE`, release receipts) including machine-generated whitepaper PDF + README evidence sections; gate fails on unresolved references or stale source hashes. | 8 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-DOM-416 | existing-coverage-validated | Public Migration Guide Autogenesis + Importer Mapping Contract | Importers exist, but migration guidance is not continuously regenerated from current importer capabilities and compatibility constraints. | Auto-generate migration guides/matrices (e.g., LangGraph/CrewAI/OpenFang -> Protheus) from importer contracts and conformance tests, publish deterministic guide artifacts on release, and fail publication when mapping coverage drops below policy threshold. | 8 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-DOM-417 | existing-coverage-validated | Anonymized Public Telemetry Benchmark Publication Contract | Community confidence is limited when benchmark telemetry remains internal and lacks a governed public publication path. | Publish anonymized benchmark/health telemetry snapshots (no sensitive data) from `protheus-top` evidence lanes on scheduled cadence with deterministic publication receipts, redaction verification, and policy-controlled opt-out/airgap behavior. | 8 | 0/1/2 |

## Agentix Production Run Observability Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1Hh2SidqH1vZdO5iYdmHA9-j4nig97km8VH-oUbMIud0`, 2026-03-11)

Source references:
- https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Hh2SidqH1vZdO5iYdmHA9-j4nig97km8VH-oUbMIud0/edit?usp=sharing
- https://www.agentixlabs.com/blog/general/from-logs-to-run-reviews-agent-observability-for-production-agents/

Notes:
- Primitive-first normalization: this is a net-new `V6-OBSERVABILITY-002.*` family that bridges legacy tracing and newer eval/deep-trace lanes into one production run-review contract.
- Overlap handled explicitly:
  - legacy end-to-end trace propagation baseline: `V3-OBS-002`, `V3-RACE-005`
  - evaluation/reporting lanes: `V6-OBSERVABILITY-003.*`
  - deep trace lineage and cost ledger lanes: `V6-OBSERVABILITY-004.1..004.4`
  - fleet-level observability and diagnostics lanes: `V6-OBSERVABILITY-001.*`, `V6-OBSERVABILITY-005.*`
- Net-new focus from this source:
  - unified run-level trace bundle contract with deterministic replay hooks
  - hybrid human + automated run-review queue and scoring loop
  - mandatory prompt/tool/schema version pinning per run
  - explicit cost-per-success outcome accounting
  - standardized failure taxonomy bound to replay and triage workflow
  - conduit-only run review/replay operations with privacy-safe storage boundaries
- Default placement:
  - Layer `0`: conduit-routed trace/write/read/replay/review actions + deterministic receipts
  - Layer `1`: redaction/privacy policy, review sampling policy, failure taxonomy policy, outcome-success policy
  - Layer `2`: run trace capture engine, review queue orchestrator, replay executor, cost/outcome correlator
  - Layer `3`: optional run-review persona templates only
  - `client`: thin `protheus trace`/`protheus review` wrappers + `protheus-top` visualization only
  - `app`: optional `/apps/run_review_console/` for analyst workflows

Objective: deliver production-grade run observability where every agent run is traceable, reviewable, replayable, and economically scored without leaking authority or private chain-of-thought data.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-OBSERVABILITY-002.1 | existing-coverage-validated | End-to-End Run Trace Bundle Contract (`run_id`) | Existing telemetry is spread across multiple lanes, making exact per-run reconstruction and debugging slower than required in production incidents. | Capture deterministic per-run trace bundle keyed by `run_id` including prompt/tool/schema versions, tool call envelopes, retrieval metadata, step latency/cost, and final outcome link; every run must be replay-addressable via trace receipt pointers. | 7 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-OBSERVABILITY-002.2 | existing-coverage-validated | Human + Automated Run Review Queue Contract | Quality regressions persist when only automated checks run, or only manual reviews occur without structured sampling and scoring continuity. | Implement hybrid review queue with policy-scoped sampling (for example 1-5%), SME review rubric + automated checks (schema validity, citation/tool/policy/PII checks), and deterministic review-score receipts linked to user-impact severity classes. | 7 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-OBSERVABILITY-002.3 | existing-coverage-validated | Prompt/Tool/Schema Version Pinning Contract | Regression analysis is unreliable when runs cannot be deterministically mapped to exact prompt/tool/schema versions at execution time. | Every run trace stores immutable prompt/tool/schema version IDs and allows before/after diff queries that surface regressions by version change; version-missing runs fail trace acceptance checks. | 7 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-OBSERVABILITY-002.4 | existing-coverage-validated | Cost-per-Success Outcome Ledger Contract | Token/cost dashboards are noisy when they track raw spend without tying spend/latency to successful outcomes. | Emit per-run and aggregate `cost_per_success` metrics (cost, tokens, latency vs outcome class) and flag wasteful loops under policy thresholds, with deterministic accounting receipts and trend views in observability surfaces. | 7 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-OBSERVABILITY-002.5 | existing-coverage-validated | Failure Taxonomy + Deterministic Replay Contract | Postmortems remain inconsistent when failures are not normalized into one taxonomy and replay path. | Normalize failures into governed taxonomy (`hallucination`, `tool_error`, `policy_refusal`, `partial_answer`, `loop`, etc.), support deterministic replay by `run_id`, and attach replay outcomes to incident/review artifacts with strict lineage receipts. | 7 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-OBSERVABILITY-002.6 | existing-coverage-validated | Conduit-Only Run Review/Replay Boundary + Privacy-Safe Storage | Run reviews and replay tooling can become bypass channels or privacy leaks if traces store unrestricted payloads or operate outside Safety Plane gates. | Enforce conduit-only routing for trace/review/replay operations, apply redaction policy forbidding raw private CoT and sensitive payload leakage, and fail closed with deterministic denial receipts on policy violations. | 10 | 0/1/2/client |

## Everything Claude Code Harness Source Coverage Intake (Doc `10tYR3m9Qq3Li6GhEqQZu3F1hecqxjr2A8q8_cjg0Glk`, 2026-03-11)

Source references:
- https://docs.google.com/document/d/10tYR3m9Qq3Li6GhEqQZu3F1hecqxjr2A8q8_cjg0Glk/edit?usp=sharing
- https://github.com/affaan-m/everything-claude-code

Notes:
- Primitive-first normalization: this intake extends existing skill, persistence, security, and orchestration lanes; it does not create a parallel harness runtime.
- Overlap handled explicitly:
  - skill templates, curated library, and skill evolution: `V6-SKILLS-001.1..001.14`
  - context/persistence hooks and plugin contracts: `V6-CONTEXT-001.*`, `V6-PERSIST-001.*`
  - orchestration/delegation baseline: `V6-COCKPIT-009.1`, `V6-COCKPIT-022.*`, `V6-AGENCY-001.*`
  - security scanning and governance lanes: `V6-SEC-010..017`
- Net-new focus from this source:
  - first-class skill+subagent frontmatter schema with built-in test vectors
  - session lifecycle hook profiles and disable-list controls
  - instinct-learning confidence pipeline that promotes patterns into governed skills
  - slash-command orchestration surface compiling to existing workflow primitives
  - mandatory security scan checks when onboarding/extending skills/subagents
- Default placement:
  - Layer `0`: conduit-routed skill/hook/learn/slash/scan actions + deterministic receipts
  - Layer `1`: skill frontmatter policy, hook-profile policy, instinct confidence/promotion policy, security rule policy
  - Layer `2`: hook runtime executor, instinct extractor/scorer, slash compiler/router, scan-on-install gate
  - Layer `3`: optional persona/subagent role-pack defaults only
  - `client`: thin `protheus skill|slash|learn` wrappers and observability only
  - `app`: optional `/apps/harness_console/` workflow UI
  - `adapter`: cross-runner command/profile adapters (Cursor/Codex/OpenCode classes)

Objective: strengthen the advanced harness layer so skills and subagents are faster to compose, safer to evolve, and continuously improved by governed learning loops, while preserving core authority and thin client surfaces.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-COCKPIT-020.1 | existing-coverage-validated | Skill + Subagent Frontmatter Template Contract | Skill and subagent setup is still fragmented without one canonical metadata schema covering tools/models/instructions/tests. | Add governed template schema (YAML/manifest frontmatter) for skills/subagents that includes tool/model bounds, instruction payloads, and required test vectors; `protheus skill create` and `protheus shadow create --role` generate conformant bundles with deterministic scaffold receipts. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-020.2 | existing-coverage-validated | Session Lifecycle Hook Profile Contract (`start`/`end` + disable controls) | Context/memory persistence behaviors vary by runtime path when session hooks are not standardized as first-class policy-bound profiles. | Implement hook runtime profiles for session start/end compaction/persistence with explicit environment controls (`hook profile`, disabled hook list), deterministic hook execution receipts, and fail-closed behavior on unauthorized hooks. | 10 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-020.3 | existing-coverage-validated | Instinct Learning Confidence-to-Skill Promotion Contract | Pattern learning exists, but promotion of learned patterns into reusable governed skills lacks a confidence-gated canonical path. | Add `protheus learn instinct` flow that extracts candidate patterns from conversation/git evidence, computes confidence scores, and proposes skill promotions through policy gates with deterministic propose/apply/revert receipts. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-020.4 | existing-coverage-validated | Slash Command Orchestration Compilation Contract | Workflow entrypoints are powerful but not yet normalized into concise slash-command ergonomics mapped to deterministic orchestration graphs. | Add slash command registry (for example `plan`, `tdd`, `security-scan`, `multi-plan`) that compiles commands into existing orchestration primitives with deterministic command->graph lineage receipts and bounded argument schemas. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-020.5 | existing-coverage-validated | Skill/Subagent Security Scan Gate Contract | New skills/subagents can introduce unsafe patterns unless each artifact passes mandatory policy and vulnerability scan checks before activation. | Enforce scan-on-install/update gate for skills/subagents using policy-scoped rule packs; activation is denied on critical findings and all scan decisions emit deterministic receipts linked to skill/subagent versions. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-020.6 | existing-coverage-validated | Conduit-Only Harness Boundary + Thin Client Enforcement | Harness features can drift into client-owned authority logic if hooks, learning, or slash routing are executed directly in wrappers. | Route all skill/hook/instinct/slash/scan actions through conduit with fail-closed policy checks, deterministic receipts, and bypass-rejection tests; keep client/app layers non-authoritative and UX-focused only. | 10 | 0/1/2/client |

## Agent Command Center CLI with Resumable Sessions & Live Steering Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1NmUDaVaWU3DEJV2cUpqoT33k1fQptNSWJf96u0GwoFU`, 2026-03-11)

Source references:
- https://docs.google.com/document/d/1NmUDaVaWU3DEJV2cUpqoT33k1fQptNSWJf96u0GwoFU/edit?usp=sharing
- https://x.com/elvissun/status/2031730194327982115

Notes:
- Source proposes `V6-COCKPIT-020`, but that ID is already occupied by harness intake; normalized to `V6-COCKPIT-025.*` to avoid collisions.
- Primitive-first normalization: no new core primitive is introduced; this intake extends existing gateway attach/runtime, observability, scheduler, and session continuity lanes.
- Overlap handled explicitly:
  - persistent gateway/session attach runtime: `V6-COCKPIT-012.2`, `V6-PERSIST-001.3`, `V6-COCKPIT-001`
  - command-center/dashboard surfaces: `V6-OBSERVABILITY-001.*`, `V6-SWARM-010`
  - session tenancy and lifecycle controls: `V6-COCKPIT-021.2`, `V6-PERSIST-001.8`
  - cockpit coding-mode/session control surfaces: `V6-COCKPIT-024.*`
- Placement correction from source doc:
  - source frames this as client extension; normalized placement is core-authoritative session orchestration with thin CLI/dashboard wrappers.
- Default placement:
  - Layer `0`: conduit-routed spawn/list/attach/steer/pause/kill/tail/inspect actions + deterministic receipts
  - Layer `1`: session policy, steering authorization policy, task metadata retention policy
  - Layer `2`: command center orchestrator, attach broker, lifecycle controller, tail/inspect stream router
  - Layer `3`: optional monitoring persona defaults only
  - `client`: thin command-center CLI and dashboard wrappers only
  - `app`: optional `/apps/command_center/` visual cockpit only

Objective: provide one-command, low-friction control over active agents/sessions (spawn/list/monitor/resume/steer) with deterministic governance and auditable lifecycle traces.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-COCKPIT-025.1 | existing-coverage-validated | Unified Command-Center Session Registry Contract | Operators lose control when active agents/sessions are spread across ad hoc state surfaces with inconsistent status semantics. | Add unified registry for active sessions/agents exposing status, uptime, current task, token/cost counters, and health flags through deterministic query receipts and policy-scoped visibility filters. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-025.2 | existing-coverage-validated | Instant Session Resume + Live Steering Attach Contract | Human intervention is slow without deterministic attach/resume semantics for running sessions. | Add `protheus session resume <id>` attach flow that resumes live sessions without cold-start rehydration, supports steering commands, and emits deterministic attach/intervention receipts tied to session lineage. | 10 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-025.3 | existing-coverage-validated | Lifecycle Control Surface Contract (`spawn/list/status/send/kill/tail/inspect`) | Multi-agent operations remain brittle without one canonical lifecycle command set and bounded control semantics. | Implement full lifecycle command contract with authorization gates and deterministic action receipts for spawn/list/status/send/kill/tail/inspect operations; unauthorized or stale session actions fail closed. | 9 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-COCKPIT-025.4 | existing-coverage-validated | Persistent Task/Session Metadata Ledger Contract | Session/task continuity weakens when metadata is not persisted as provenance-linked objects across daemon restarts. | Persist task/session metadata in governed Epistemic objects with restart-safe continuity, retention policy enforcement, and deterministic mutation/history receipts. | 8 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-COCKPIT-025.5 | existing-coverage-validated | Command-Center Dashboard Delta + Tail/Inspect Stream Contract | Terminal-only status checks are insufficient for rapid triage when multi-agent fleets need live drill-downs. | Add command-center dashboard mode (`protheus dashboard`, `protheus top`) showing live status grid + receipt-linked tail/inspect drill-down streams with bounded latency and role-scoped access controls. | 8 | 1/2/client/app |
| V6-COCKPIT-025.6 | existing-coverage-validated | Conduit-Only Command-Center Boundary + Thin Surface Enforcement | Session steering/lifecycle controls can bypass policy if implemented in client/app wrappers. | Enforce conduit-only routing for all command-center lifecycle and steering actions with bypass-rejection tests and deterministic denial receipts; keep client/app layers non-authoritative UX/control surfaces only. | 10 | 0/1/2/client/app |

## PRD-to-Roadmap Autonomous Software Team Planner Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1z7TGqd7-fsYwiZY66xpxbnZTRQ-s1ZpSzEKuD-64VHc`, 2026-03-11)

Source references:
- https://docs.google.com/document/d/1z7TGqd7-fsYwiZY66xpxbnZTRQ-s1ZpSzEKuD-64VHc/edit?usp=sharing
- https://x.com/Suryanshti777/status/2031312325660344779

Notes:
- Primitive-first normalization: no new core primitive is introduced; this intake extends existing research/assimilation, orchestration, workflow execution, and receipted merge lanes.

  - code-engineer/project build orchestration: `V6-APP-006.1..006.8`
  - visual/declarative workflow orchestration contracts: `V6-FLOW-001.*`
  - deep execution harness and todo/graph execution lanes: `V6-COCKPIT-022.*`
  - command center/session continuity and persistent execution: `V6-COCKPIT-025.*`, `V6-PERSIST-001.*`
- Placement correction from source doc:
  - source frames this as client extension; normalized placement is core-authoritative planning/execution flow with thin app/CLI wrappers.
- Default placement:
  - Layer `0`: conduit-routed ingest/plan/execute/merge actions + deterministic receipts
  - Layer `1`: roadmap policy (scope, risk, budget, context-window limits), merge gate policy, provenance policy
  - Layer `2`: PRD ingestor, story-point estimator, dependency batch planner, iterative execution orchestrator

Objective: produce and execute dependency-aware project roadmaps from PRD + tech-stack inputs with deterministic batching, iterative execution, and audit-ready receipts.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-WORKFLOW-003.1 | existing-coverage-validated | PRD + Tech-Stack Ingestion Contract (`.planr/`) | Planning quality degrades when PRD and stack constraints are not ingested as structured, provenance-linked objects before orchestration. | Ingest `.planr/prd.md` and `.planr/tech-stack.md` (or explicit paths) into structured Epistemic objects with deterministic parse/summary receipts and policy-bound schema validation. | 8 | 0/1/2/client/app |
| V6-WORKFLOW-003.2 | existing-coverage-validated | Story-Point + Context-Budget Batch Planner Contract | Large project plans fail when task estimates and dependency batches ignore context-window and execution-budget constraints. | Add planner that computes story-point estimates, dependency-aware batches, and phase ordering under context/token/cost policy caps; emits deterministic roadmap-generation receipts and batch manifests. | 9 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-WORKFLOW-003.3 | existing-coverage-validated | Roadmap Artifact Contract (`.planr/roadmap.md` + machine schema) | Roadmaps lose downstream reliability when only human-readable output is emitted without stable machine-readable structure. | Emit roadmap artifacts in both markdown and governed machine schema (phase, batch, deps, estimate, acceptance criteria) with deterministic artifact hashes and provenance receipts. | 8 | 1/2/client/app |
| V6-WORKFLOW-003.4 | existing-coverage-validated | Iterative Batch Execution + Merge Gate Contract | Autonomous execution loops become unsafe when implementation/merge progression is not tied to explicit gates and context refresh between batches. | Execute each planned batch through bounded implement/test/merge/update cycles with deterministic per-step receipts; progression requires merge-policy pass and explicit context refresh before next batch. | 10 | 0/1/2 |
| V6-WORKFLOW-003.5 | existing-coverage-validated | One-Command Project Planner Activation Contract | Adoption remains low if project-planning flow requires manual multi-command composition each run. | Provide thin command surface (`protheus app run project-planner --prd <path> [--stack <path>]`) that launches full governed flow end-to-end and emits deterministic run/session receipts. | 7 | 1/2/client/app |
| V6-WORKFLOW-003.6 | existing-coverage-validated | Conduit-Only Project Planner Boundary + Thin Surface Enforcement | Planning/execution logic can bypass governance if implemented directly in client/app wrappers. | Enforce conduit-only routing for ingest/estimate/plan/execute/merge actions with bypass-rejection tests; keep client/app layers non-authoritative orchestration surfaces only. | 10 | 0/1/2/client/app |

## Complete Shannon Production Framework Assimilation Source Coverage Intake (Doc `17JIvRFQ5zUh1ml3Bhu1IItY2VJIefqT4swMaQ8PmMMw`, 2026-03-17)

Source references:
- https://docs.google.com/document/d/17JIvRFQ5zUh1ml3Bhu1IItY2VJIefqT4swMaQ8PmMMw/edit?usp=sharing
- https://github.com/Kocoro-lab/Shannon

Notes:
- Primitive-first normalization: this intake extends existing workflow, swarm, memory, observability, security, and adapter lanes; it does not authorize a parallel Shannon-owned subsystem.
- Overlap handled explicitly:
  - graph, replay, and workflow-execution baselines: `V6-WORKFLOW-002.*`, `V6-WORKFLOW-003.*`
  - swarm reliability and hierarchy baselines: `V6-SWARM-*`, `V6-SWARM-033` through `V6-SWARM-038`
  - memory/session/context baselines: `V6-MEMORY-*`, `V6-CONTEXT-*`, `V6-RESEARCH-*`
  - observability/replay/tracing lanes: `V6-OBSERVABILITY-*`, `V9-AUDIT-026`
  - substrate, tenancy, and sandbox baselines: `V6-SUBSTRATE-007.*`
- Placement correction from source doc:
  - source suggests `apps/shannon/` and `apps/shannon-desktop/`; normalized placement is core-authoritative workflow/replay/memory/sandbox orchestration plus thin runtime/client shells and adapter-owned compat, sandbox, and desktop bridges. Optional desktop/demo shells may exist only as deletable surfaces.
- Default placement:
  - Layer `0`: conduit-routed pattern/replay/budget/gateway/cron/sandbox actions + deterministic receipts
  - Layer `1`: policy/budget/privacy/tenancy/receipt contracts
  - Layer `2`: workflow orchestration, replay coordination, scheduler, observability capture, P2P reliability logic
  - Layer `3`: optional persona defaults only
  - `client`: thin `infring assimilate shannon` control and optional desktop shells only
  - `adapter`: OpenAI-compat, MCP, sandbox, and desktop bridges

Objective: assimilate Shannon’s orchestration patterns, hard budget fallback, hierarchical and vector memory, deterministic replay, HITL middleware, sandbox and multi-tenant isolation, observability, OpenAI compatibility, skills/MCP, cron, desktop ergonomics, and P2P swarm reliability into InfRing by reusing existing primitives without duplicating authority.

| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

| V6-WORKFLOW-001.2 | queued | Token Budget and Auto-Fallback Guard Contract | Hard budget enforcement and fallback drift unless Shannon budget semantics stay inside existing routing and budget primitives. | Reuse current budget and routing lanes for Shannon-style hard budgets and automatic model fallback so breaches and fallback decisions emit deterministic receipts and remain fail closed. | 10 | 0/1/2/client |

| V6-WORKFLOW-001.4 | queued | Deterministic Replay Engine Contract | Time-travel and replay become untrustworthy unless Shannon replay semantics route through existing receipt and replay authority. | Reuse existing receipt and replay primitives for Shannon-style deterministic replay so exports and re-executions remain auditable and policy-governed without a separate replay authority path. | 10 | 0/1/2/client |

| V6-WORKFLOW-001.9 | queued | Skills and MCP Tool Registry Contract | Skills and MCP registration create tool sprawl unless Shannon tool semantics route through governed content-skill and adapter bridges. | Reuse content-skill and adapter bridges for Shannon-style skills and MCP tools so registration and invocation emit deterministic receipts and unsafe tool paths fail closed. | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |

| V6-WORKFLOW-006.7 | queued | Observability and Pipeline Tracing Contract | Full-company simulations are hard to trust unless MetaGPT pipeline events stream through the native observability stack. | Fold MetaGPT-style workflow tracing and debugging into native observability and receipt lanes so all simulation events stream with zero-loss capture and existing dashboards/export paths can render the evidence. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-WORKFLOW-006.8 | queued | YAML/Config and Extensibility Bridge Contract | Declarative configs and SOP extensions create operational debt unless MetaGPT config surfaces normalize through governed manifests. | Support `infring assimilate metagpt` ingestion of YAML/config assets through content-skill and adapter-owned bridges so config-driven execution emits deterministic receipts and unsupported config paths fail closed with explicit reasons. | 8 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |

## OpenAI Swarm Lightweight Handoff Orchestration Assimilation Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1rOMGeqiWLK71_J-pIgSMvAQvg8aFh-HsDpm_t7mqtbo`, 2026-03-17)

Source references:
- https://docs.google.com/document/d/1rOMGeqiWLK71_J-pIgSMvAQvg8aFh-HsDpm_t7mqtbo/edit?usp=sharing
- https://github.com/openai/swarm

Notes:
- Primitive-first normalization: this intake extends existing swarm, workflow, memory, observability, and adapter lanes; it does not authorize a parallel OpenAI-Swarm-owned subsystem.
- Overlap handled explicitly:
  - swarm messaging, hierarchy, budgets, and orchestration hardening: `V6-SWARM-*`, `V6-SWARM-033` through `V6-SWARM-038`
  - workflow planning/execution baselines: `V6-WORKFLOW-003.*`
  - memory/context/auto-recall baselines: `V6-MEMORY-*`, `V6-CONTEXT-*`, `V6-RESEARCH-*`
  - observability/evals/tracing lanes: `V6-OBSERVABILITY-*`, `V9-AUDIT-026`
  - tool/adaptation baselines: `REQ-17`, `V6-COMP-002`, `V6-SUBSTRATE-007.*`
- Placement correction from source doc:
  - source suggests `apps/swarm/`; normalized placement is core-authoritative swarm/workflow/context orchestration plus thin runtime/client shells and adapter-owned tool/polyglot bridges. Optional REPL/demo shells may exist only as deletable surfaces.
- Default placement:
  - Layer `0`: conduit-routed handoff/context/tool/stream/recovery actions + deterministic receipts
  - Layer `1`: policy/budget/privacy/receipt contracts
  - Layer `2`: swarm coordination, context propagation, multi-turn execution, streaming orchestration
  - Layer `3`: optional persona defaults only
  - `client`: thin `infring assimilate swarm` control and optional REPL/demo shells only
  - `adapter`: polyglot import and tool-schema bridges

Objective: assimilate OpenAI Swarm’s lightweight handoff orchestration, mutable context propagation, auto-tool generation, streaming delimiters, multi-turn execution, REPL ergonomics, and composable agent-network strengths into InfRing by reusing existing primitives without duplicating authority.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-WORKFLOW-007.1 | done | Agent Handoff Registry Contract | Handoff-driven orchestration remains fragmented unless Swarm-style control transfer normalizes onto one governed swarm/workflow execution surface. | Delivered receipted handoff registry and lineage-preserving transfer over the authoritative swarm runtime in `core/layer0/ops/src/swarm_runtime.rs`; verified by `core/layer0/ops/tests/v6_workflow_007_integration.rs`, `tests/client-memory-tools/swarm_workflow_007_bridge.test.js`, and `protheus-ops srs-contract-runtime run --id=V6-WORKFLOW-007.1`. | 10 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-WORKFLOW-007.2 | done | Context Variable Propagation Contract | Mutable context becomes unsafe and lossy unless Swarm-style context variables route through authoritative memory and context-budget primitives. | Delivered governed context put/get propagation with budget compaction receipts in `core/layer0/ops/src/swarm_runtime.rs` and `client/runtime/systems/autonomy/swarm_sessions_bridge.ts`; verified by `core/layer0/ops/tests/v6_workflow_007_integration.rs`, `tests/client-memory-tools/swarm_workflow_007_bridge.test.js`, and `protheus-ops srs-contract-runtime run --id=V6-WORKFLOW-007.2`. | 9 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-WORKFLOW-007.3 | done | Automatic JSON-Schema Tool Bridge Contract | Auto-generated function tools create schema and safety drift unless they normalize through governed skill and adapter bridges. | Delivered governed JSON-schema tool registration/invocation with fail-closed unsafe bridge denial in `core/layer0/ops/src/swarm_runtime.rs` and `client/runtime/systems/autonomy/swarm_sessions_bridge.ts`; verified by `core/layer0/ops/tests/v6_workflow_007_integration.rs`, `tests/client-memory-tools/swarm_workflow_007_bridge.test.js`, and `protheus-ops srs-contract-runtime run --id=V6-WORKFLOW-007.3`. | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V6-WORKFLOW-007.4 | done | Streaming with Agent Delimiters Contract | Streaming loses operator trust unless agent-boundary events and partial outputs remain visible through native observability paths. | Delivered delimited stream emit/render receipts over the native swarm runtime in `core/layer0/ops/src/swarm_runtime.rs`; verified by `core/layer0/ops/tests/v6_workflow_007_integration.rs`, `tests/client-memory-tools/swarm_workflow_007_bridge.test.js`, and `protheus-ops srs-contract-runtime run --id=V6-WORKFLOW-007.4`. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-WORKFLOW-007.5 | done | Multi-Turn Execution and Error Recovery Contract | Multi-turn execution and self-recovery drift into wrappers unless they stay inside authoritative workflow and safety lanes. | Delivered receipted multi-turn execution with retry-once recovery in `core/layer0/ops/src/swarm_runtime.rs`; verified by `core/layer0/ops/tests/v6_workflow_007_integration.rs`, `tests/client-memory-tools/swarm_workflow_007_bridge.test.js`, and `protheus-ops srs-contract-runtime run --id=V6-WORKFLOW-007.5`. | 9 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-WORKFLOW-007.6 | done | REPL and Demo Loop Ergonomics Contract | Lightweight experimentation is less usable unless REPL/demo loops exist as thin shells over the same governed runtime. | Delivered thin non-authoritative demo shell `client/runtime/systems/autonomy/swarm_repl_demo.ts` delegating every step to the governed bridge with compact JSON output; verified by `tests/client-memory-tools/swarm_repl_demo.test.js` and `protheus-ops srs-contract-runtime run --id=V6-WORKFLOW-007.6`. | 7 | 1/2/client/app |
| V6-WORKFLOW-007.7 | done | Composable Agent Networks Contract | Agent-as-node composition remains brittle unless Swarm-style network ergonomics map onto existing session, persona, and attention primitives. | Delivered receipted composable agent network creation/status over the authoritative swarm/session lanes in `core/layer0/ops/src/swarm_runtime.rs`; verified by `core/layer0/ops/tests/v6_workflow_007_integration.rs`, `tests/client-memory-tools/swarm_workflow_007_bridge.test.js`, and `protheus-ops srs-contract-runtime run --id=V6-WORKFLOW-007.7`. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |

## Mastra Lightweight TypeScript Agent Framework Assimilation Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1QDZnA7Ezfjn5Ez4xcKyz00hpfy5Rbj9FI1XW-x-OAgI`, 2026-03-17)

Source references:
- https://docs.google.com/document/d/1QDZnA7Ezfjn5Ez4xcKyz00hpfy5Rbj9FI1XW-x-OAgI/edit?usp=sharing
- https://github.com/mastra-ai/mastra

Notes:
- Primitive-first normalization: this intake extends existing workflow, swarm, memory, observability, and adapter lanes; it does not authorize a parallel Mastra-owned subsystem.
- Overlap handled explicitly:
  - workflow planning/execution baselines: `V6-WORKFLOW-003.*`
  - swarm messaging, hierarchy, budgets, and orchestration hardening: `V6-SWARM-033` through `V6-SWARM-038`
  - memory/RAG and semantic recall baselines: `V6-MEMORY-*`, `V6-RESEARCH-*`, `V6-CONTEXT-*`
  - observability, evals, and tracing lanes: `V6-OBSERVABILITY-*`, `V9-AUDIT-026`
  - MCP/interoperability and adapter/connectivity baselines: `V6-COMP-002`, `V6-SUBSTRATE-007.*`, `V6-APP-006.*`, `REQ-17`
- Placement correction from source doc:
  - source suggests `apps/mastra/` and `apps/mastra-studio/`; normalized placement is core-authoritative workflow/memory/orchestration plus thin runtime/client surfaces and adapter-owned MCP/provider bridges. Optional studio/playground apps may exist only as deletable shells.
- Default placement:
  - Layer `0`: conduit-routed workflow/agent/memory/HITL/MCP/eval actions + deterministic receipts
  - Layer `1`: policy/budget/privacy/approval/receipt contracts
  - Layer `2`: graph workflow orchestration, agents, semantic recall, routing, tracing
  - Layer `3`: optional persona defaults only
  - `client`: thin `infring assimilate mastra` control and optional studio shell surfaces only
  - `adapter`: provider, MCP, and protocol interoperability shims

Objective: assimilate Mastra’s lightweight TypeScript-first graph workflows, agent/tool loops, semantic memory, HITL suspend/resume, MCP interoperability, evals/tracing, model routing, and dev-studio/full-stack ergonomics into InfRing by reusing existing primitives without duplicating authority.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V6-WORKFLOW-011.1 | done | Graph Workflow Engine Contract | Lightweight graph-based AI workflows remain fragmented unless Mastra-style chaining, branching, and parallel flow patterns map onto one governed execution surface. | Delivered receipted Mastra graph registration and execution over the authoritative swarm/workflow lane in `core/layer0/ops/src/mastra_bridge.rs` with thin client wrappers in `client/runtime/lib/mastra_bridge.ts`; verified by `core/layer0/ops/tests/v6_workflow_011_integration.rs`, `tests/client-memory-tools/mastra_bridge.test.js`, and `protheus-ops srs-contract-runtime run --id=V6-WORKFLOW-011.1`. | 9 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-WORKFLOW-011.2 | done | Autonomous Agent and Tool Reasoning Contract | Agent loops and tool reasoning drift into non-deterministic wrappers unless they route through the authoritative swarm and persona lanes. | Delivered Mastra-style agent loop execution, bounded tool selection, and governed swarm receipts in `core/layer0/ops/src/mastra_bridge.rs` plus workflow/runtime bridges under `client/runtime/systems/workflow/mastra_bridge.ts` and `adapters/polyglot/mastra_runtime_bridge.ts`; verified by `core/layer0/ops/tests/v6_workflow_011_integration.rs`, `tests/client-memory-tools/mastra_bridge.test.js`, and `protheus-ops srs-contract-runtime run --id=V6-WORKFLOW-011.2`. | 9 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-WORKFLOW-011.3 | done | Memory, Semantic Recall, and RAG Bridge Contract | TS-first memory ergonomics become duplicated debt unless semantic recall and RAG normalize onto the existing memory runtime. | Delivered governed Mastra memory recall bridging in `core/layer0/ops/src/mastra_bridge.rs` with safe degraded receipts when ambient memory runtime support is unavailable, exposed through `client/runtime/lib/mastra_bridge.ts`; verified by `core/layer0/ops/tests/v6_workflow_011_integration.rs`, `tests/client-memory-tools/mastra_bridge.test.js`, and `protheus-ops srs-contract-runtime run --id=V6-WORKFLOW-011.3`. | 10 | 0/1/2/client/adapter |
| V6-WORKFLOW-011.4 | done | Human-in-the-Loop Suspend/Resume Contract | Workflow pauses and approvals are unreliable if state persistence and review semantics are implemented outside the existing receipt-backed runtime. | Delivered receipt-backed Mastra suspend/resume and approval checkpoint flows by persisting run snapshots and reusing approval gates in `core/layer0/ops/src/mastra_bridge.rs`; verified by `core/layer0/ops/tests/v6_workflow_011_integration.rs`, `tests/client-memory-tools/mastra_bridge.test.js`, and `protheus-ops srs-contract-runtime run --id=V6-WORKFLOW-011.4`. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-WORKFLOW-011.5 | done | MCP Server and Interoperability Bridge Contract | MCP interoperability becomes unsafe and inconsistent unless it is normalized through governed adapter-owned bridges. | Delivered adapter-owned Mastra MCP bridge registration and invocation with deterministic interoperability receipts in `core/layer0/ops/src/mastra_bridge.rs` and `adapters/protocol/mastra_mcp_bridge.ts`; verified by `core/layer0/ops/tests/v6_workflow_011_integration.rs`, `tests/client-memory-tools/mastra_bridge.test.js`, and `protheus-ops srs-contract-runtime run --id=V6-WORKFLOW-011.5`. | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V6-WORKFLOW-011.6 | done | Evals and Tracing Observability Contract | Lightweight eval/tracing value is lost if Mastra-style traces and metrics bypass the native observability stack. | Delivered Mastra eval trace recording, token telemetry, and observability-plane receipts in `core/layer0/ops/src/mastra_bridge.rs`; verified by `core/layer0/ops/tests/v6_workflow_011_integration.rs`, `tests/client-memory-tools/mastra_bridge.test.js`, and `protheus-ops srs-contract-runtime run --id=V6-WORKFLOW-011.6`. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |
| V6-WORKFLOW-011.7 | done | 40+ LLM Routing Compatibility Contract | TS-first provider ergonomics create fragmentation unless model routing compatibility is absorbed into the canonical inference path. | Delivered governed Mastra runtime bridge registration and model routing receipts with explicit pure/tiny-max degradation reasons in `core/layer0/ops/src/mastra_bridge.rs` and `adapters/polyglot/mastra_runtime_bridge.ts`; verified by `core/layer0/ops/tests/v6_workflow_011_integration.rs`, `tests/client-memory-tools/mastra_bridge.test.js`, and `protheus-ops srs-contract-runtime run --id=V6-WORKFLOW-011.7`. | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V6-WORKFLOW-011.8 | done | Dev Studio and Full-Stack Shell Contract | Developer adoption improves with studio and full-stack UI shells, but those shells must not become system authority. | Delivered non-authoritative Mastra deployment shell receipts that delegate back to `core://mastra-bridge` while keeping shell paths under `client/` or `apps/` in `core/layer0/ops/src/mastra_bridge.rs`; verified by `core/layer0/ops/tests/v6_workflow_011_integration.rs`, `tests/client-memory-tools/mastra_bridge.test.js`, and `protheus-ops srs-contract-runtime run --id=V6-WORKFLOW-011.8`. | 7 | client/app |
| V6-WORKFLOW-011.9 | done | Lightweight TypeScript Ergonomics Intake Contract | TS-first developer experience is valuable only if scaffolding and intake stay thin and do not force Node dependency into sovereign profiles. | Delivered `assimilate mastra` intake routing plus governed scaffold emission for `package.json`, `src/mastra.graph.ts`, and `README.md` in `core/layer0/ops/src/mastra_bridge.rs` and `core/layer0/ops/src/protheusctl_routes.rs`; verified by `core/layer0/ops/tests/v6_workflow_011_integration.rs`, `tests/client-memory-tools/mastra_bridge.test.js`, `core/layer0/ops/src/protheusctl_tests.rs`, and `protheus-ops srs-contract-runtime run --id=V6-WORKFLOW-011.9`. | 8 | 0/1/2/client |

## Haystack Modular Pipeline & Agent Orchestration Assimilation Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1eqzU0knBOiWTNv4MiaMD2HSIiLyKB9Jg0-CBBL8rfwI`, 2026-03-16)

Source references:
- https://docs.google.com/document/d/1eqzU0knBOiWTNv4MiaMD2HSIiLyKB9Jg0-CBBL8rfwI/edit?usp=sharing
- https://github.com/deepset-ai/haystack

Notes:
- Primitive-first normalization: this intake extends existing workflow, swarm, memory, observability, and adapter lanes; it does not authorize a parallel Haystack-owned subsystem.
- Overlap handled explicitly:
  - workflow planning/execution baselines: `V6-WORKFLOW-003.*`
  - swarm messaging, hierarchy, budgets, and orchestration hardening: `V6-SWARM-033` through `V6-SWARM-038`
  - memory/RAG and low-burn retrieval baselines: `V6-MEMORY-*`, `V6-RESEARCH-*`, `V6-CONTEXT-*`
  - observability and tracing lanes: `V6-OBSERVABILITY-*`, `V9-AUDIT-026`
  - adapter/import/connectivity baselines: `V6-COMP-002`, `V6-SUBSTRATE-007.*`, `V6-APP-006.*`
- Placement correction from source doc:
  - source suggests `apps/haystack/`; normalized placement is core-authoritative workflow/memory/orchestration plus thin client surfaces and adapter-owned integration shims, because this is system capability rather than a deletable app.
- Default placement:
  - Layer `0`: conduit-routed pipeline/agent/retrieval/eval actions + deterministic receipts
  - Layer `1`: policy/budget/privacy/connector gates and receipt contracts
  - Layer `2`: pipeline orchestration, routing, rankers, agent execution, trace emission
  - Layer `3`: optional persona defaults only
  - `client`: thin `infring assimilate haystack` control and status surfaces only
  - `adapter`: connector/document-store/model integration shims

Objective: assimilate Haystack v2.25.x strengths into InfRing by reusing existing primitives for modular pipelines, agent tool use, dynamic prompt templates, explicit RAG control, conditional routing, multimodal evaluation, tracing, and connector breadth without duplicating authority.

## CAMEL Scaling-Law Agent Society Assimilation Source Coverage Intake (Doc `18JoI69ZnPTtAqsp3QtKyLJ8NNRWDXqWcOW8RkwwgQEU`, 2026-03-17)

Source references:

- https://github.com/camel-ai/camel

Notes:
- Primitive-first normalization: this intake extends existing swarm, workflow, memory, observability, content-skill, and adapter lanes; it does not authorize a parallel CAMEL-owned society runtime.
- Overlap handled explicitly:
  - workflow and graph-execution baselines: `V6-WORKFLOW-001.*` through `V6-WORKFLOW-012.*`
  - swarm messaging, hierarchy, capabilities, and reliability baselines: `V6-SWARM-*`, `V6-SWARM-033` through `V6-SWARM-038`
  - memory/context/simulation baselines: `V6-MEMORY-*`, `V6-CONTEXT-*`, `V6-RESEARCH-*`
  - observability/eval/receipt baselines: `V9-AUDIT-026`, `V6-OBSERVABILITY-*`
- Placement correction from source doc:
  - source suggests `apps/camel/` and `apps/camel-oasis/`; normalized placement is core-authoritative society/workflow/memory orchestration plus thin runtime/client shells and adapter-owned dataset/tool bridges. Optional society/demo shells may exist only as deletable surfaces.
- Default placement:
  - Layer `0`: conduit-routed society, dataset, benchmark, and simulation actions + deterministic receipts
  - Layer `1`: policy/budget/receipt/provenance contracts
  - Layer `2`: society orchestration, role routing, world simulation coordination, scaling observability
  - Layer `3`: optional persona defaults only
  - `client`: thin `infring assimilate camel` control and optional demo shells only
  - `adapter`: dataset/tool/provider bridges

Objective: assimilate CAMEL’s role-playing societies, large-scale agent coordination, OASIS-style world simulation, synthetic datasets, code-as-prompt conversations, CRAB-style evaluations, tool ecosystem, and emergent scaling-law observability into InfRing by reusing existing primitives without duplicating authority.

## LangChain Declarative Ecosystem Assimilation Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1tghS8K-vKzMb-3-gtHo7byy_W22g92upY-BDom7SbCw`, 2026-03-17)

Source references:
- https://docs.google.com/document/d/1tghS8K-vKzMb-3-gtHo7byy_W22g92upY-BDom7SbCw/edit?usp=sharing
- https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchain

Notes:
- Primitive-first normalization: this intake extends existing workflow, swarm, memory, inference, observability, content-skill, and adapter lanes; it does not authorize a parallel LangChain-owned execution runtime.
- Overlap handled explicitly:
  - workflow and graph-execution baselines: `V6-WORKFLOW-001.*` through `V6-WORKFLOW-013.*`
  - swarm and agent-handoff baselines: `V6-SWARM-*`, `V6-SWARM-033` through `V6-SWARM-038`
  - memory/retrieval/context baselines: `V6-MEMORY-*`, `V6-CONTEXT-*`, `V6-RESEARCH-*`
  - observability/eval/receipt baselines: `V9-AUDIT-026`, `V6-OBSERVABILITY-*`
- Placement correction from source doc:
  - source suggests `apps/langchain/`; normalized placement is core-authoritative workflow/memory/integration orchestration plus thin runtime/client shells and adapter-owned provider, retriever, and loader bridges. Optional demo shells may exist only as deletable surfaces.
- Default placement:
  - Layer `0`: conduit-routed chain, agent, retrieval, integration, and trace actions + deterministic receipts
  - Layer `1`: policy/budget/receipt/provenance contracts
  - Layer `2`: workflow orchestration, declarative chaining, memory/retrieval coordination, integration routing
  - Layer `3`: optional persona defaults only
  - `client`: thin `infring assimilate langchain` control and optional demo shells only
  - `adapter`: provider/tool/retriever/loader bridges

Objective: assimilate LangChain’s LCEL chains, Runnable composability, legacy and deep agents, retrieval and memory abstractions, broad integration layer, model interoperability, prompt templating, and LangSmith-style observability into InfRing by reusing existing primitives without duplicating authority.
