| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V7-BUSINESS-001.1 | existing-coverage-validated | Business-Class Tiered Memory Taxonomy + Auto-Promotion Contract | Flat memory views degrade executive and departmental recall quality when strategic decisions and ambient notes are mixed in one undifferentiated lane. | Extend memory taxonomy to support tier classes (`node1`, `tag2`, `jot3`) with business-context tagging, heat-based auto-promotion, and query filters (`memory_search --tier=<tier> --business-context=<id>`), with deterministic classify/promote receipts. | 9 | 0/1/2/client |
| V7-BUSINESS-001.2 | existing-coverage-validated | Cross-Session Business Persona Identity + Capability Lease Persistence Contract | Multi-stakeholder operations need consistent persona behavior and capability continuity across restarts rather than session-fresh rebootstrap semantics. | Persist named shadow personas, auto-load business context registry (subsidiaries + stakeholder preferences), and support renewable 24-hour capability leases with deterministic issue/renew/revoke receipts and fail-closed expiry behavior. | 10 | 0/1/2/3/client |
| V7-BUSINESS-001.3 | existing-coverage-validated | Active Continuity Protocol (Checkpoint/Resume/Handoff) Contract | Reactive heartbeat-only continuity misses critical state transitions and makes cross-session handoff fragile for active business workflows. | Add pre-session resume, periodic/milestone checkpoints, post-session priming receipts, and async handoff queue contracts (`business checkpoint`, `business resume`, `business handoff`) with receipt-verified restore pointers and deterministic handoff lineage. | 10 | 0/1/2/client/app |
| V7-BUSINESS-001.4 | existing-coverage-validated | Stakeholder Alert Surface + Escalation Matrix Contract | Operators need deterministic escalation when ambiguity, lease expiry, checkpoint mismatch, or health degradation requires human intervention. | Implement policy-configured alert matrix (decision-required, lease-expiry, checkpoint-mismatch, async-complete, vitality-drop) with channel adapters (dashboard/Slack/email/SMS/PagerDuty), deterministic alert/ack receipts, and fail-closed escalation fallback. | 8 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V7-BUSINESS-001.5 | existing-coverage-validated | Multi-Tenant Business Context Isolation + Firewall Contract | One agent operating multiple businesses risks cross-tenant context leakage without hard isolation and business-scoped capability boundaries. | Enforce per-business memory/state namespaces, business-scoped capability grants, receipt hash-chain namespacing (`business_id`), and cross-business access denials with leakage regression tests (`--business-context=<id>` required for scoped operations). | 10 | 0/1/2/3/client |
| V7-BUSINESS-001.6 | existing-coverage-validated | External System Sync Bridge (`external_sync_eye`) Contract | Business continuity fails when local agent state diverges from Notion/wiki/CRM/calendar/email/Slack systems of record. | Add governed bidirectional sync adapters (Notion/Confluence, CRM, calendar, email, Slack) with deterministic sync receipts (`content hash`, `external id`, `timestamp`, `direction`), policy-scoped permissions, and conflict-resolution lineage artifacts. | 8 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V7-BUSINESS-001.7 | existing-coverage-validated | Business Continuity Stress-Test Harness Contract | Continuity claims are not trustworthy until restart, handoff, interruption, and multi-tenant isolation scenarios are reproducibly audited. | Ship `protheusctl continuity audit --days=<n> --report=business_format` harness covering 7-day chain integrity, cross-shadow handoff, interruption resume accuracy, and multi-business isolation, producing deterministic audit artifacts and pass/fail receipts. | 9 | 0/1/2/client |
| V7-BUSINESS-001.8 | existing-coverage-validated | Compliance-Grade Receipt Archive + Audit Export Contract | Regulated stakeholders need immutable, queryable receipt evidence with independent timestamp anchoring and audit-ready exports. | Add append-only business receipt archive, daily Merkle-root anchor publication, audit query interface (`protheusctl audit --date-range=<range> --business=<id-or-ALL>`), and PDF/structured export artifacts with deterministic hash-linked evidence receipts. | 10 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |

## Government-Grade Hardening & Certification Intake (Operator Addendum, 2026-03-13)

Source references:
- Inline operator brief: government-grade continuity/security/compliance hardening requirements (2026-03-13).

Notes:
- Primitive-first normalization: this intake extends existing identity, policy, conduit, observability, memory, audit, and enterprise lanes; it does not create a parallel authority plane.
- Overlap handled explicitly:
  - enterprise identity/compliance baselines: `V7-ENTERPRISE-001.*`, `V6-F100-043`, `V3-RACE-311`, `V3-RACE-340`
  - receipt/audit and provenance baselines: `V7-META-013`, `V8-NETWORK-002.2`, `V6-F100-042`, `V6-OBSERVABILITY-004.8`
  - continuity/failover baselines: `V6-PERSIST-001.*`, `V6-COCKPIT-025.*`
  - safety boundary baselines: `V6-CONDUIT-*`, `V8-DIRECTIVES-001.*`
- Governance target profile from source:
  - classification-aware operation (UNCLASSIFIED/CUI/CONFIDENTIAL/SECRET/TOP SECRET + compartments)
  - IL4-IL6/Secret-TS deployment constraints, air-gap support, and cross-domain transfer controls
  - formal certification path (FedRAMP/FIPS/CMMC/Common Criteria/ATO).

Objective: evolve Protheus from enterprise-grade continuity to government-grade assured operation with hardware-rooted trust, classification enforcement, legal-grade attribution, and certifiable resilience controls.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V7-GOV-001.1 | existing-coverage-validated | Hardware Root-of-Trust Identity + Receipt Signing Contract | Software-only key custody is insufficient for government attribution and tamper resistance at higher classification levels. | Integrate TPM 2.0 attestation + HSM-backed signing profiles (`protheusctl identity attestation --tpm`) and require hardware-backed receipt signatures verifiable by `protheusctl verify --hardware`, with fail-closed behavior when hardware trust signals are absent/invalid. | 10 | -1/0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V7-GOV-001.2 | existing-coverage-validated | Classification Plane + Clearance-Enforced Memory Isolation Contract | Multi-level security operation requires explicit clearance and namespace enforcement beyond standard tenant/business partitioning. | Add `planes/classification/` policy contracts, classified memory namespaces (`unclassified/cui/secret/compartment/*`), clearance-aware access checks, and receipt classification tags; deny read/write above effective clearance and require policy-approved transfer paths. | 10 | 0/1/2/3/client |
| V7-GOV-001.3 | existing-coverage-validated | Legal Non-Repudiation + Trusted Timestamp Attribution Contract | Receipt hashes alone do not satisfy legal evidentiary standards for who authorized what and when. | Bind every governed action to authenticated principal identity (PIV/CAC/PKI subject), attach cryptographic authorization signatures, use RFC 3161 timestamp authority records, and support legal-hold freezing with immutable evidence-chain receipts. | 10 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V7-GOV-001.4 | existing-coverage-validated | Air-Gap + Data-Diode Cross-Domain Transfer Contract | Secret/TS workflows require physical and protocol separation, not just logical policy controls. | Support separated high-side/low-side daemon profiles with one-way sanitized transfer (`protheusctl diode --from=<high> --to=<low> --sanitize=true`), explicit offline media ingress controls, and deterministic transfer/deny receipts proving no direct reverse path. | 10 | -1/0/1/2/adapter |
| V7-GOV-001.5 | existing-coverage-validated | Government SOC/SIEM Continuous Monitoring Contract | Operational assurance requires continuous security telemetry and anomaly detection integrated into SOC workflows. | Stream all security-relevant receipts/events to approved SIEM connectors (`Splunk/Elastic/ArcSight`) via `protheusctl soc connect`, enforce MAC-oriented policy evaluation, and emit deterministic alert/escalation receipts for policy violations and behavioral deviations. | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V7-GOV-001.6 | existing-coverage-validated | Multi-Site COOP/COG Resilience + Failover Contract | Government continuity mandates geographically distributed survivability with audited failover, not local checkpointing only. | Implement primary/secondary/tertiary site topology with Merkle-checked replication, split-brain detection, deterministic failover orchestration, site-state model (`ACTIVE/STANDBY/COLD/FAILED`), and 30-day offline operation profile receipts. | 10 | 0/1/2/3/adapter/client |
| V7-GOV-001.7 | existing-coverage-validated | Formal Verification + Information-Flow Assurance Contract | Unit/integration testing alone is insufficient for high-assurance government accreditation boundaries. | Extend machine-checked proofs for security properties and classification information-flow isolation (Lean/Coq artifacts under `proofs/layer0/` and `proofs/layer1/`), prohibit unsafe Rust in privileged boundaries, and produce repeatable proof receipts for release gates. | 10 | 0/1/2 |
| V7-GOV-001.8 | existing-coverage-validated | Standards-First Interoperability Profile Contract (PKI/SAML/OIDC/SMIME/IPv6/DNSSEC/OAuth2) | Government adoption requires certified interoperability patterns over standard protocols rather than bespoke integration paths. | Ship governed interoperability profiles for identity, messaging, schema validation, network hardening, and API auth using approved standards with deterministic conformance receipts and fail-closed downgrade rejection (for example no plain HTTP paths). | 8 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V7-GOV-001.9 | existing-coverage-validated | ATO Documentation + Certification Evidence Pack Contract | Authority to Operate requires structured control documentation and auditable evidence bundles, not implementation status alone. | Add `docs/government/` package (`SYSTEM_SECURITY_PLAN`, `CONTINGENCY_PLAN`, `INCIDENT_RESPONSE_PLAN`, `PIA`, `CONFIGURATION_MANAGEMENT_PLAN`, `TEST_PLAN_AND_RESULTS`) plus NIST 800-53 control mapping and exportable certification evidence receipts for FedRAMP/CMMC/CC workflows. | 9 | 1/2/client |

## Banking-Grade Financial Integrity & Regulatory Operations Intake (Operator Addendum, 2026-03-13)

Source references:
- Inline operator brief: bank-specific hardening requirements beyond government baseline (2026-03-13).

Notes:
- Primitive-first normalization: this intake extends existing identity/compliance/observability/network/economy lanes with financial-grade transaction and regulatory control contracts; it does not create a parallel safety authority.
- Overlap handled explicitly:
  - government-grade trust/compliance baseline: `V7-GOV-001.*`
  - economy/trading/network baselines: `V6-ECONOMY-001.*`, `V6-ECONOMY-002.*`, `V8-NETWORK-002.*`
  - observability/audit lineage baselines: `V6-OBSERVABILITY-001.*`, `V6-F100-042`, `V7-META-013`
  - continuity/failover baselines: `V6-PERSIST-001.*`, `V7-GOV-001.6`
- Required finance posture from source:
  - transaction correctness is atomic and ledger-balanced (debit+credit or no commit)
  - regulator-facing evidence (SR 11-7, AML/KYC, BCBS 239, filing automation) is first-class and receipt-linked
  - uptime and settlement reliability targets align with 5-nines expectations.

Objective: achieve bank-grade operational correctness where money movement, risk decisions, and regulatory reporting are provably consistent, low-latency, and audit-ready.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V7-BANK-001.1 | existing-coverage-validated | Financial Transaction Plane Contract (ACID + Double-Entry + Settlement Rails) | Receipt logging is insufficient for banking unless transaction state is atomic, ledger-balanced, and reconcilable across payment rails. | Add `planes/finance/` transaction contracts for ACID commit/rollback, double-entry invariants, settlement connectors (SWIFT/ACH/RTP/Fedwire), FX conversion, and end-of-day reconciliation; emit `financial_transaction` receipts including deterministic `atomic_commit_hash` and fail-closed rollback receipts. | 10 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V7-BANK-001.2 | existing-coverage-validated | Model Risk Governance Registry Contract (SR 11-7) | Banking AI decisions require documented validation, backtesting, explainability, and independent model risk workflow evidence. | Add model inventory + validation/backtesting registry (`models/inventory.json`, validation evidence, drift metrics), require pre-deployment validation gates, independent review workflow hooks, and deterministic model-governance receipts for approve/reject/promote/deprecate actions. | 10 | 0/1/2/client |
| V7-BANK-001.3 | existing-coverage-validated | AML Transaction Monitoring + SAR/CTR Case Management Contract | Banks must detect and report suspicious activity with deterministic evidence, not ad hoc alerting. | Add AML rules engine (structuring/layering/integration), sanctions/watchlist screening (OFAC/PEP), risk scoring, SAR/CTR generation workflows, and case lifecycle management with deterministic alert/escalation/filing receipts and retention policy enforcement. | 10 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V7-BANK-001.4 | existing-coverage-validated | KYC/CIP/CDD/EDD Lifecycle Contract | Customer onboarding and lifecycle risk controls require identity proofing and periodic revalidation under strict auditability. | Implement KYC pipeline covering identity verification, CIP checks, CDD/EDD risk classification, periodic refresh, adverse-media monitoring, and tokenized PII storage with encrypted evidence access logs and deterministic verification/deny receipts. | 10 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V7-BANK-001.5 | existing-coverage-validated | Financial Eye Real-Time Market + Portfolio Risk Sensor Contract | Financial operations need live market/risk signals to govern decisions under fast-changing exposure conditions. | Add `finance_eye` sensory lane with live market feeds, sentiment ingestion, portfolio/P&L/Greeks tracking, VaR/CVaR/stress metrics, and deterministic feed/compute receipts persisted under governed memory nodes for downstream policy/routing use. | 8 | 0/1/2/3/adapter/client |
| V7-BANK-001.6 | existing-coverage-validated | BCBS 239 Risk Data Aggregation + Lineage Warehouse Contract | Regulatory risk reporting requires complete, timely, lineage-proven aggregation across risk classes and business lines. | Deliver risk data warehouse contracts for market/credit/operational risk with end-to-end lineage/provenance, stress-test scenario execution, and deterministic report-ready aggregation receipts suitable for BCBS 239 evidence audits. | 10 | 0/1/2/client |
| V7-BANK-001.7 | existing-coverage-validated | Digital Asset Custody + Proof-of-Reserves Contract | Modern banking requires secure crypto/tokenized asset custody with key ceremony controls and verifiable reserve evidence. | Add `planes/finance/digital_assets/` contracts for multi-sig custody, hot/cold wallet policy, HSM key custody, chain-node integration, tokenized deposit/stablecoin flows, and proof-of-reserves attestations with deterministic custody/move/sign/attest receipts. | 9 | -1/0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V7-BANK-001.8 | existing-coverage-validated | Zero-Trust Financial Operations Contract (mTLS + JIT + Micro-Segmentation) | Financial blast radius must remain minimal under assumed breach conditions and continuous verification requirements. | Enforce finance-lane zero-trust posture with mTLS mutual auth, least-privilege JIT grants, micro-segmented service policies, continuous behavior verification, and deterministic authz/deny/re-auth receipts across all internal/external finance actions. | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V7-BANK-001.9 | existing-coverage-validated | Five-Nines Availability + Deterministic Failover Contract | Banking platforms require near-continuous availability and second-level failover, not minute-level checkpoint recovery. | Implement active-active multi-AZ topology, synchronous state replication, automatic failover/orchestration, blue-green deployment controls, chaos validation lanes, and uptime SLO receipts proving <=5.26 minutes annual downtime equivalent. | 10 | 0/1/2/3/adapter/client |
| V7-BANK-001.10 | existing-coverage-validated | Regulatory Reporting Automation Contract (FR Y-14/FFIEC/SAR/CTR/Basel) | Manual reporting is error-prone and non-scalable for bank regulatory obligations and internal risk governance cadence. | Add report extraction/template/validation pipelines for required filings (FR Y-14, FFIEC 031, SAR, CTR, Basel liquidity metrics), automated filing adapters where allowed, and deterministic generation/approval/submission audit receipts with immutable evidence bundles. | 9 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |

## Healthcare-Grade Clinical Safety, PHI Governance & Care Operations Intake (Operator Addendum, 2026-03-13)

Source references:
- Inline operator brief: hospital-specific requirements beyond government/banking profiles (2026-03-13).

Notes:
- Primitive-first normalization: this intake extends identity, memory, policy, observability, emergency access, and interoperability lanes with patient-safety-first healthcare contracts.
- Overlap handled explicitly:
  - government-grade trust/compliance baseline: `V7-GOV-001.*`
  - business continuity/audit baseline: `V7-BUSINESS-001.*`
  - observability/alerting baseline: `V6-OBSERVABILITY-001.*`
  - cognition/memory continuity baseline: `V6-COGNITION-*`, `V6-MEMORY-*`
  - emergency override governance baseline: `V7-GOV-001.3`, `V7-GOV-001.4`
- Required healthcare posture from source:
  - first-do-no-harm safety gates on clinical decision surfaces
  - PHI-centric access logging and patient-visible auditability
  - interoperability with healthcare standards (HL7/FHIR/DICOM) and regulated workflow evidence.

Objective: achieve hospital-grade safe operation where patient identity, PHI access, clinical decision support, and emergency workflows are deterministic, auditable, and constrained by clinical safety policy.

| ID | Status | Upgrade | Why | Exit Criteria | Impact (1-10) | Layer Map |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V7-HEALTH-001.1 | existing-coverage-validated | Patient Identity Plane Contract (MPI + MRN + Consent-Scoped UUID Context) | Clinical safety and continuity require deterministic patient matching and consent-aware context without broad PHI exposure. | Add `planes/healthcare/patient/` contracts for master patient index, MRN mapping, FHIR Patient linkage, probabilistic duplicate matching, de-identification support, and consent-state policy checks; emit deterministic patient-context/consent receipts without raw PHI leakage. | 10 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V7-HEALTH-001.2 | existing-coverage-validated | HIPAA PHI Access Audit Contract (Minimum Necessary + Patient Disclosure Log) | Healthcare legal posture requires who/what/when/why PHI access evidence, including break-glass events and patient-requestable access history. | Implement PHI access logging with user identity (NPI/role), purpose-of-use tags (treatment/payment/operations/research), minimum-necessary checks, break-glass justification capture, anomaly detection, and patient-facing disclosure export receipts. | 10 | 0/1/2/client |
| V7-HEALTH-001.3 | existing-coverage-validated | Clinical Decision Support Contract (CDS Hooks + Medication/Allergy/Dosing Safety Gates) | Clinical AI without explicit safety checks can cause direct patient harm via missed interactions, allergy conflicts, or dosing errors. | Add `planes/healthcare/cds/` rules engine supporting drug-drug/allergy checks, dosing guidance, guideline reminders, sepsis/critical warning triggers, and CDS Hooks-compatible trigger/output contracts with deterministic alert/override rationale receipts. | 10 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V7-HEALTH-001.4 | existing-coverage-validated | Medical Device Interoperability Contract (HL7/FHIR/DICOM/IEEE 11073) | ICU and inpatient workflows depend on real-time device and EHR interoperability, not manual data re-entry. | Add `planes/healthcare/devices/` integration contracts for HL7 v2 message types, FHIR R4 endpoints, DICOM imaging flows, IEEE 11073 device telemetry ingestion, and deterministic device-event receipts with strict source provenance and safety bounds. | 9 | -1/0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V7-HEALTH-001.5 | existing-coverage-validated | Structured Clinical Documentation Contract (SOAP + SNOMED/ICD-10/CPT/LOINC) | Free-text-only notes reduce clinical reliability, coding quality, and downstream interoperability for care/billing/reporting. | Add `planes/healthcare/documentation/` contracts for SOAP note structure, coded terminology normalization (SNOMED/ICD-10/CPT/LOINC), completeness checks, and deterministic coding suggestion/accept/reject receipts with audit-safe provenance links. | 9 | 0/1/2/client |
| V7-HEALTH-001.6 | existing-coverage-validated | Alert Fatigue Mitigation + Clinical Escalation Contract | Excessive low-value alerts reduce clinician response to true critical events and increase patient risk. | Implement tiered alerting, deduplication, context/time-aware suppression, role-aware routing/escalation, acknowledgement/action/resolution lifecycle tracking, and deterministic alert-value metrics receipts to support sustained reduction of non-actionable alerts. | 10 | 0/1/2/client/app |
| V7-HEALTH-001.7 | existing-coverage-validated | Care Coordination & Handoff Contract (SBAR + Med Reconciliation) | Patient safety depends on reliable transitions across teams/units with structured handoff and reconciliation guarantees. | Add `planes/healthcare/coordination/` contracts for SBAR handoffs, multidisciplinary care-plan updates, secure care-team messaging, discharge planning continuity, and medication/allergy/test-result reconciliation with deterministic transition receipts. | 9 | 0/1/2/client/app |
| V7-HEALTH-001.8 | existing-coverage-validated | Clinical Trials Integration Contract (Eligibility + Consent + Protocol/AE Tracking) | Trial operations require governed enrollment/consent/protocol adherence and adverse-event timelines alongside clinical care workflows. | Add `planes/healthcare/trials/` contracts for inclusion/exclusion screening, consent versioning, protocol adherence checks, SAE reporting timers, and de-identified research extraction with deterministic screening/enroll/deviation/report receipts. | 8 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V7-HEALTH-001.9 | existing-coverage-validated | Medical Imaging Plane Contract (DICOM/PACS + AI Pre-Read + Critical Findings Routing) | Imaging-heavy workflows require standard ingestion, comparative review, and critical result escalation with traceable interpretation steps. | Add `planes/healthcare/imaging/` contracts for DICOM ingestion, PACS integration, prior-study comparison metadata, AI pre-read annotation traces, structured report generation, and deterministic critical-finding alert receipts linked to ordering provider workflow. | 8 | 0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V7-HEALTH-001.10 | existing-coverage-validated | Break-Glass Emergency Access + Post-Incident Review Contract | Emergencies require temporary override access, but every override must be justified, time-bounded, and audited to prevent misuse. | Add `planes/healthcare/emergency/` break-glass contracts with justification capture, temporary privilege escalation TTL, compliance auto-notification, quarterly review board export artifacts, and deterministic override/use/expiry/audit receipts. | 10 | 0/1/2/client |

## Cross-Industry Verticalization Intake (Operator Addendum, 2026-03-13)

Source references:
- Inline operator brief: manufacturing/energy/aerospace/automotive/telecom/retail/education/legal/gaming/agriculture/construction/logistics/pharma requirements (2026-03-13).

Notes:
- Primitive-first normalization: this intake adds domain-specific contracts on top of existing safety, identity, receipt, policy, and continuity planes.
- Overlap handled explicitly:
  - government/bank/healthcare baselines: `V7-GOV-001.*`, `V7-BANK-001.*`, `V7-HEALTH-001.*`
  - substrate/sensory/observability baselines: `V6-SUBSTRATE-*`, `V6-OBSERVABILITY-*`
  - conduit/receipt/audit baselines: `V6-CONDUIT-*`, `V7-META-013`, `V6-F100-042`
- Required cross-domain posture from source:
  - each vertical must define entity model, regulatory controls, protocol integrations, safety/reliability class, and real-time SLO contracts with deterministic evidence.

Objective: enable deterministic, domain-safe adaptation to high-impact industries without duplicating core authority paths.

| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

| V7-VERTICAL-001.2 | existing-coverage-validated | Energy/Grid Critical Infrastructure Plane Contract (NERC CIP + DER + Outage Management) | Grid operations require real-time balancing, critical infrastructure controls, and outage restoration workflows with strict reliability guarantees. | Add `planes/grid/` contracts for NERC CIP policy controls, dispatch/load-balancing algorithms, DER orchestration, outage/GIS workflows, and AMI metering with IEEE 2030.5/OpenADR/Green Button adapter receipts. | 10 | -1/0/1/2/adapter/client |
| V7-VERTICAL-001.3 | existing-coverage-validated | Aerospace/Avionics High-Assurance Plane Contract (DO-178C + ARINC + Deterministic Runtime) | Flight-critical systems require certifiable determinism and avionics protocol conformance with very low tolerance for runtime nondeterminism. | Add `planes/avionics/` contracts for DO-178C evidence workflows, ARINC 429/664 + MIL-STD-1553 adapters, hard real-time deterministic execution profile, and airworthiness artifact pack generation receipts. | 10 | -1/0/1/2/adapter/client |
